text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt with Jacob, each with his household:
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of the Sons of Israel, who came into Egypt, with Jacob, did each man and his household come in:—
text.KJV: Now these are the names of the children of Israel, which came into Egypt; every man and his household came with Jacob.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the sons of Israel who, accompanied by their households, entered into Egypt with Jacob:
text.DRB: These be the names of the children of Israel, that entered into Ægypt with Jacob: they did enter in every one with their houses,

text.NRSV: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,
text.Rotherham: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah;
text.KJV: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,
text.NABRE: Reuben, Simeon, Levi and Judah;
text.DRB: Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Judas,

text.NRSV: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,
text.Rotherham: Issachar, Zebulon, and Benjamin;
text.KJV: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,
text.NABRE: Issachar, Zebulun and Benjamin;
text.DRB: Issachar, Zabulon, & Benjamin,

text.NRSV: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
text.Rotherham: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
text.KJV: Dan, and Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
text.NABRE: Dan and Naphtali; Gad and Asher.
text.DRB: Dan, and Nephthali, Gad, and Aser.

text.NRSV: The total number of people born to Jacob was seventy. Joseph was already in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that all the persons who were descended from Jacob were seventy souls,—but, Joseph, was already in Egypt.
text.KJV: And all the souls that came out of the loins of Jacob were seventy souls: for Joseph was in Egypt already.
text.NABRE: The total number of Jacob’s direct descendants was seventy. Joseph was already in Egypt.
text.DRB: Therefore all the souls that came out of Jacob's thigh were seventy: and Joseph was in Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph died, and all his brothers, and that whole generation.
text.Rotherham: So then Joseph died and all his brethren, and all that generation.
text.KJV: And Joseph died, and all his brethren, and all that generation.
text.NABRE: Now Joseph and all his brothers and that whole generation died.
text.DRB: Who being dead, and all his brethren, and all that generation,

text.NRSV: But the Israelites were fruitful and prolific; they multiplied and grew exceedingly strong, so that the land was filled with them.
text.Rotherham: But the sons of Israel, were fruitful and swarmed and multiplied and waxed mighty, with exceeding vigour,—so that the land was filled with them.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and waxed exceeding mighty; and the land was filled with them.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites were fruitful and prolific. They multiplied and became so very numerous that the land was filled with them.
text.DRB: the children of Israel increased, and as it were springing up did multiply, and growing strong exceedingly, filled the land.

text.NRSV: Now a new king arose over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
text.Rotherham: Then arose a new king over Egypt,—who had not known Joseph.
text.KJV: Now there arose up a new king over Egypt, which knew not Joseph.
text.NABRE: The Oppression. Then a new king, who knew nothing of Joseph, rose to power in Egypt.
text.DRB: In the mean time there arose a new king over Ægypt, that knew not Joseph:

text.NRSV: He said to his people, “Look, the Israelite people are more numerous and more powerful than we.
text.Rotherham: So he said, unto his people, Lo! the people of the sons of Israel are too many and mighty for us!
text.KJV: And he said unto his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we:
text.NABRE: He said to his people, “See! The Israelite people have multiplied and become more numerous than we are!
text.DRB: and he said to his people: Behold the people of the children of Israel is much, and stronger than we.

text.NRSV: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, or they will increase and, in the event of war, join our enemies and fight against us and escape from the land.”
text.Rotherham: Come on! let us shew ourselves wise with regard to them, lest they so multiply that it shall come to pass when war befalleth us, that they also, shall join themselves unto them who hate us, and shall make war upon us. and then go up out of the land.
text.KJV: Come on, let us deal wisely with them; lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out any war, they join also unto our enemies, and fight against us, and so get them up out of the land.
text.NABRE: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them to stop their increase; otherwise, in time of war they too may join our enemies to fight against us, and so leave the land.”
text.DRB: Come, let us wisely oppress the same, lest perhaps it multiply: and if there shall be any war against us, it joined with our enemies, and we being overthrown, they depart out of the land.

text.NRSV: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to oppress them with forced labor. They built supply cities, Pithom and Rameses, for Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: So they set over them chiefs of tribute, to the end they might humiliate them with their burdens,—and they built store-cities for Pharaoh, even Pithom and Raamses.
text.KJV: Therefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh treasure cities, Pithom and Raamses.
text.NABRE: Accordingly, they set supervisors over the Israelites to oppress them with forced labor. Thus they had to build for Pharaoh the garrison cities of Pithom and Raamses.
text.DRB: Therefore he set over them masters of the works, to afflict them with burdens: and they built unto Pharao cities of tabernacles, Phithom, and Ramesses.

text.NRSV: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread, so that the Egyptians came to dread the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: But the more they were humiliating them, the more, were they multiplying, and, the more, were they breaking forth, so they were filled with alarm because of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and grew. And they were grieved because of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Yet the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread, so that the Egyptians began to loathe the Israelites.
text.DRB: And the more they did oppress them, so much the more they multiplied and increased:

text.NRSV: The Egyptians became ruthless in imposing tasks on the Israelites,
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians rigorously made the sons of Israel serve;
text.KJV: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour:
text.NABRE: So the Egyptians reduced the Israelites to cruel slavery,
text.DRB: and the Ægyptians hated the children of Israel, and deriding afflicted them:

text.NRSV: and made their lives bitter with hard service in mortar and brick and in every kind of field labor. They were ruthless in all the tasks that they imposed on them.
text.Rotherham: and embittered their lives with harsh service, in clay and in bricks, and in all manner of service in the field,—all their service, wherein they rigorously made them serve.
text.KJV: And they made their lives bitter with hard bondage, in morter, and in brick, and in all manner of service in the field: all their service, wherein they made them serve, was with rigour.
text.NABRE: making life bitter for them with hard labor, at mortar and brick and all kinds of field work—cruelly oppressed in all their labor.
text.DRB: and they brought their life into bitterness with the hard works of clay and brick, & with all service, wherewith they were pressed in the works of the earth.

text.NRSV: The king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, one of whom was named Shiphrah and the other Puah,
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Egypt to the Hebrew midwives,—of whom, the name of the one was, Shiphrah, and, the name of the other Puah;—
text.KJV: And the king of Egypt spake to the Hebrew midwives, of which the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other Puah:
text.NABRE: Command to the Midwives. The king of Egypt told the Hebrew midwives, one of whom was called Shiphrah and the other Puah,
text.DRB: And the king of Ægypt said to the midwives of the Hebrews, of whom one was called Sephora, the other Phua,

text.NRSV: “When you act as midwives to the Hebrew women, and see them on the birthstool, if it is a boy, kill him; but if it is a girl, she shall live.”
text.Rotherham: then he said—When ye act as midwives unto the Hebrew women, then shall ye look out for the sex,—If it is, a son, then shall ye kill it, But, if it is, a daughter, then shall it live.
text.KJV: And he said, When ye do the office of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them upon the stools; if it be a son, then ye shall kill him: but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.
text.NABRE: “When you act as midwives for the Hebrew women, look on the birthstool: if it is a boy, kill him; but if it is a girl, she may live.”
text.DRB: commanding them: When you shall be midwives to the Hebrew women, and the time of delivery is come, if it be a man-child, kill it: if a woman, reserve her.

text.NRSV: But the midwives feared God; they did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but they let the boys live.
text.Rotherham: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt spake unto them,—but suffered the male children to live.
text.KJV: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the men children alive.
text.NABRE: The midwives, however, feared God; they did not do as the king of Egypt had ordered them, but let the boys live.
text.DRB: But the midwives feared God, and did not according to the commandment of the king of Ægypt, but preserved the men-children.

text.NRSV: So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and said to them, “Why have you done this, and allowed the boys to live?”
text.Rotherham: Then called the king of Egypt for the midwives, and said to them—Wherefore have ye done this thing, that ye should let the male children live?
text.KJV: And the king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said unto them, Why have ye done this thing, and have saved the men children alive?
text.NABRE: So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and asked them, “Why have you done this, allowing the boys to live?”
text.DRB: To whom being called unto him, the king said: What is this that you meant to do, that you would save the men-children?

text.NRSV: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwife comes to them.”
text.Rotherham: And the midwives said unto Pharaoh. Because not like the Egyptian women, are the Hebrew women, for they are full of life, ere yet the midwife can come in unto them, they have given birth,
text.KJV: And the midwives said unto Pharaoh, Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwives come in unto them.
text.NABRE: The midwives answered Pharaoh, “The Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women. They are robust and give birth before the midwife arrives.”
text.DRB: Who answered: The Hebrew women are not as the Ægyptian women: for they have the knowledge to play the midwife themselves, and before we come to them, they are delivered.

text.NRSV: So God dealt well with the midwives; and the people multiplied and became very strong.
text.Rotherham: So then God dealt well with the midwives,—and the people multiplied, and waxed exceeding mighty.
text.KJV: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives: and the people multiplied, and waxed very mighty.
text.NABRE: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives; and the people multiplied and grew very numerous.
text.DRB: God therefore did well to the midwives: and the people increased, and became strong exceedingly.

text.NRSV: And because the midwives feared God, he gave them families.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, because the midwives feared God, that he made for them households.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, because the midwives feared God, that he made them houses.
text.NABRE: And because the midwives feared God, God built up families for them.
text.DRB: And because the midwives feared God, he built them houses.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh commanded all his people, “Every boy that is born to the Hebrews you shall throw into the Nile, but you shall let every girl live.”
text.Rotherham: Then gave Pharaoh command to all his people saying—Every son that is born to the Hebrews, into the river, shall ye cast him; But every daughter, shall ye suffer to live.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river, and every daughter ye shall save alive.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh then commanded all his people, “Throw into the Nile every boy that is born, but you may let all the girls live.”
text.DRB: Pharao therefore commanded all his people, saying: Whatsoever shall be born of the male-sex cast it into the river: whatsoever of the female, reserve it.

text.NRSV: Now a man from the house of Levi went and married a Levite woman.
text.Rotherham: And there went a man of the house of Levi,—and took a daughter of Levi.
text.KJV: And there went a man of the house of Levi, and took to wife a daughter of Levi.
text.NABRE: Now a man of the house of Levi married a Levite woman,
text.DRB: After these things there came forth a man of the house of Levi, and he took a wife of his own stock.

text.NRSV: The woman conceived and bore a son; and when she saw that he was a fine baby, she hid him three months.
text.Rotherham: And the woman conceived and bare a son,—and she saw him, that he was a goodly child, so she hid him three months.
text.KJV: And the woman conceived, and bare a son: and when she saw him that he was a goodly child, she hid him three months.
text.NABRE: and the woman conceived and bore a son. Seeing what a fine child he was, she hid him for three months.
text.DRB: Who conceived, and bare a son: & seeing him a goodly one, hid him three months.

text.NRSV: When she could hide him no longer she got a papyrus basket for him, and plastered it with bitumen and pitch; she put the child in it and placed it among the reeds on the bank of the river.
text.Rotherham: And when she could no longer hide him, she took for him an ark of paper-reed, and covered it over with bitumen, and with pitch,—and put therein the child, and laid it among the rushes upon the bank of the river.
text.KJV: And when she could not longer hide him, she took for him an ark of bulrushes, and daubed it with slime and with pitch, and put the child therein; and she laid it in the flags by the river’s brink.
text.NABRE: But when she could no longer hide him, she took a papyrus basket, daubed it with bitumen and pitch, and putting the child in it, placed it among the reeds on the bank of the Nile.
text.DRB: And when now she could not conceal him, she took a basket made of bulrushes, and daubed it with bitume and pitch: and put within it the little infant, and laid him in a sedgy place by the river's brink,

text.NRSV: His sister stood at a distance, to see what would happen to him.
text.Rotherham: Then did his sister station herself at a distance,—to see what would be done to him.
text.KJV: And his sister stood afar off, to wit what would be done to him.
text.NABRE: His sister stationed herself at a distance to find out what would happen to him.
text.DRB: his sister standing a far off, and considering the event of the thing.

text.NRSV: The daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the river, while her attendants walked beside the river. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid to bring it.
text.Rotherham: So then the daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe by the river, and her, maidens, were walking by the side of the river,—when she saw the ark in the midst of the rushes, and sent her handmaid, and fetched it.
text.KJV: And the daughter of Pharaoh came down to wash herself at the river; and her maidens walked along by the river’s side; and when she saw the ark among the flags, she sent her maid to fetch it.
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh’s daughter came down to bathe at the Nile, while her attendants walked along the bank of the Nile. Noticing the basket among the reeds, she sent her handmaid to fetch it.
text.DRB: And behold the daughter of Pharao came down to be washed in the river, and her maids walked by the river's brink. Who when she saw the basket in the sedges, she sent one of her handmaids: and when it was brought,

text.NRSV: When she opened it, she saw the child. He was crying, and she took pity on him, “This must be one of the Hebrews’ children,” she said.
text.Rotherham: And she opened and beheld it—even the child, and lo! a boy weeping,—so she took pity on him, and said, Of the children of the Hebrews, is this.
text.KJV: And when she had opened it, she saw the child: and, behold, the babe wept. And she had compassion on him, and said, This is one of the Hebrews’ children.
text.NABRE: On opening it, she looked, and there was a baby boy crying! She was moved with pity for him and said, “It is one of the Hebrews’ children.”
text.DRB: opening it, and seeing with it an infant crying, having pity on it, said: This is one of the infants of the Hebrews.

text.NRSV: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and get you a nurse from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?”
text.Rotherham: Then said his sister unto Pharaoh’s daughter, Shall I go and call thee a nurse, of the Hebrew women,—that she may nurse for thee the child?
text.KJV: Then said his sister to Pharaoh’s daughter, Shall I go and call to thee a nurse of the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for thee?
text.NABRE: Then his sister asked Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and summon a Hebrew woman to nurse the child for you?”
text.DRB: To whom the child's sister said: Wilt thou that I go, and call to thee an Hebrew woman, that may nurse the little infant?

text.NRSV: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Yes.” So the girl went and called the child’s mother.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh’s daughter said to her—Go. So the maid went, and called the mother of the child.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, Go. And the maid went and called the child’s mother.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh’s daughter answered her, “Go.” So the young woman went and called the child’s own mother.
text.DRB: She answered: Go. The maid went and called her mother.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child and nurse it for me, and I will give you your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed it.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh’s daughter said to her—Take this child, and nurse it for me, and, I, will give thee thy wages. So the woman took the child, and nursed it.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh’s daughter said unto her, Take this child away, and nurse it for me, and I will give thee thy wages. And the woman took the child, and nursed it.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child and nurse him for me, and I will pay your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed him.
text.DRB: To whom Pharao's daughter speaking: Take, quoth she, this child, and nurse him for me: I will give thee thy hire. The woman took and nursed the child: and when he was grown, delivered him to Pharao's daughter.

text.NRSV: When the child grew up, she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and she took him as her son. She named him Moses, “because,” she said, “I drew him out of the water.”
text.Rotherham: And the child grew, and she brought him in to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses, and said—For out of the water, I drew him.
text.KJV: And the child grew, and she brought him unto Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses: and she said, Because I drew him out of the water.
text.NABRE: When the child grew, she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses; for she said, “I drew him out of the water.”
text.DRB: Whom she adopted into the place of a son, and called him Moyses, saying: Because from the water I did take him.

text.NRSV: One day, after Moses had grown up, he went out to his people and saw their forced labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his kinsfolk.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in those days when Moses grew up, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens,—and saw, an Egyptian smiting a Hebrew—of his brethren,
text.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens: and he spied an Egyptian smiting an Hebrew, one of his brethren.
text.NABRE: Moses’ Flight to Midian. On one occasion, after Moses had grown up, when he had gone out to his kinsmen and witnessed their forced labor, he saw an Egyptian striking a Hebrew, one of his own kinsmen.
text.DRB: In those days after that Moyses was grown, he went forth to his brethren: and he saw their affliction, and a man that was an Ægyptian striking one of the Hebrews his brethren.

text.NRSV: He looked this way and that, and seeing no one he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
text.Rotherham: So he turned this way and that, and when he saw that there was no man, he smote the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
text.KJV: And he looked this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no man, he slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
text.NABRE: Looking about and seeing no one, he struck down the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
text.DRB: And when he had looked about hither and thither, and saw no man present, he struck the Ægyptian, and hid him in the sand.

text.NRSV: When he went out the next day, he saw two Hebrews fighting; and he said to the one who was in the wrong, “Why do you strike your fellow Hebrew?”
text.Rotherham: And he went out on the second day, and lo! two Hebrews, striving together,—so he said to him that did the wrong, Wherefore shouldest thou smite thy fellow?
text.KJV: And when he went out the second day, behold, two men of the Hebrews strove together: and he said to him that did the wrong, Wherefore smitest thou thy fellow?
text.NABRE: The next day he went out again, and now two Hebrews were fighting! So he asked the culprit, “Why are you striking your companion?”
text.DRB: And going forth another day, he saw two Hebrews brawling; and he said to him that did the wrong: Why strikest thou thy neighbour?

text.NRSV: He answered, “Who made you a ruler and judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and thought, “Surely the thing is known.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Who hath set thee for a chief and a judge over us? To slay me, art thou thinking, as thou didst slay the Egyptian? Then was Moses afraid, and said—Surely the thing is, known!
text.KJV: And he said, Who made thee a prince and a judge over us? intendest thou to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? And Moses feared, and said, Surely this thing is known.
text.NABRE: But he replied, “Who has appointed you ruler and judge over us? Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses became afraid and thought, “The affair must certainly be known.”
text.DRB: Who answered: Who hath appointed thee Prince and Judge over us? Wilt thou kill me, as yesterday thou didst the Ægyptian? Moyses feared, and said: How is this thing come abroad?

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh heard of it, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh. He settled in the land of Midian, and sat down by a well.
text.Rotherham: So Pharaoh heard this thing, and sought to slay Moses,—and Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian and sat down by the well.
text.KJV: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian: and he sat down by a well.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh heard of the affair, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to the land of Midian. There he sat down by a well.
text.DRB: And Pharao heard of this talk, and sought to kill Moyses: who flying from his sight, abode in the Land of Madian, and sat beside a well.

text.NRSV: The priest of Midian had seven daughters. They came to draw water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
text.Rotherham: Now, the priest of Midian, had seven daughters,—and they came and drew and filled the troughs, to water their fathers flock.
text.KJV: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters: and they came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
text.NABRE: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters, and they came to draw water and fill the troughs to water their father’s flock.
text.DRB: And the Priest of Madian had seven daughters, which were come to draw water: and when the troughs were filled, they desired to water their father's flocks.

text.NRSV: But some shepherds came and drove them away. Moses got up and came to their defense and watered their flock.
text.Rotherham: Then came the shepherds, and drave them away,—so Moses rose up, and succoured them, and watered their flock,
text.KJV: And the shepherds came and drove them away: but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
text.NABRE: But shepherds came and drove them away. So Moses rose up in their defense and watered their flock.
text.DRB: The shepherds came upon them, and drove them away: and Moyses arose, and defending the maids, watered their sheep.

text.NRSV: When they returned to their father Reuel, he said, “How is it that you have come back so soon today?”
text.Rotherham: Then came they in unto Reuel their father,—and he said, Wherefore have ye so early come in to-day?
text.KJV: And when they came to Reuel their father, he said, How is it that ye are come so soon to day?
text.NABRE: When they returned to their father Reuel, he said to them, “How is it you have returned so soon today?”
text.DRB: Who being returned to Raguel their father, he said to them: Why are you come sooner than you were wont?

text.NRSV: They said, “An Egyptian helped us against the shepherds; he even drew water for us and watered the flock.”
text.Rotherham: And they said, An Egyptian, delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds,—Moreover also he drew, for us, and watered the flock.
text.KJV: And they said, An Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and also drew water enough for us, and watered the flock.
text.NABRE: They answered, “An Egyptian delivered us from the shepherds. He even drew water for us and watered the flock!”
text.DRB: They answered: A certain man an Ægyptian delivered us from the hand of the shepherds: moreover also he drew water with us, and gave the sheep to drink.

text.NRSV: He said to his daughters, “Where is he? Why did you leave the man? Invite him to break bread.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto his daughters—And where is he? wherefore is it that ye left the man? Call him that he may eat bread.
text.KJV: And he said unto his daughters, And where is he? why is it that ye have left the man? call him, that he may eat bread.
text.NABRE: “Where is he?” he asked his daughters. “Why did you leave the man there? Invite him to have something to eat.”
text.DRB: But he said: Where is he? Why have you let the man go? Call him that he may eat bread.

text.NRSV: Moses agreed to stay with the man, and he gave Moses his daughter Zipporah in marriage.
text.Rotherham: And Moses was well-pleased to dwell with the man,—and he gave Zipporah his daughter to Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses was content to dwell with the man: and he gave Moses Zipporah his daughter.
text.NABRE: Moses agreed to stay with him, and the man gave Moses his daughter Zipporah in marriage.
text.DRB: Therefore Moyses sware that he would dwell with him. And he took Sephora his daughter to wife:

text.NRSV: She bore a son, and he named him Gershom; for he said, “I have been an alien residing in a foreign land.”
text.Rotherham: And she bare a son, and he called his name Gershom,—for he said, A, sojourner, I am, in a strange land.
text.KJV: And she bare him a son, and he called his name Gershom: for he said, I have been a stranger in a strange land.
text.NABRE: She conceived and bore a son, whom he named Gershom; for he said, “I am a stranger residing in a foreign land.”
text.DRB: Who bare him a son, whom he called Gersam, saying: I have been a stranger in a foreign country. And she bare another, whom he called Eliezer, saying: for the God of my father my helper hath delivered me out of the hand of Pharao.

text.NRSV: After a long time the king of Egypt died. The Israelites groaned under their slavery, and cried out. Out of the slavery their cry for help rose up to God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, during those many days, that the king of Egypt died, and the sons of Israel sighed by reason of the servitude and lamented,—and their cry for help went up to God, by reason of the servitude.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in process of time, that the king of Egypt died: and the children of Israel sighed by reason of the bondage, and they cried, and their cry came up unto God by reason of the bondage.
text.NABRE: A long time passed, during which the king of Egypt died. The Israelites groaned under their bondage and cried out, and from their bondage their cry for help went up to God.
text.DRB: But after much time the king of Ægypt died: and the children of Israel groaning, cried out because of the works: and their cry ascended unto God from the works.

text.NRSV: God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text.Rotherham: And God heard their groaning,—and God remembered his covenant, with Abraham, with Isaac and with Jacob;
text.KJV: And God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
text.NABRE: God heard their moaning and God was mindful of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.
text.DRB: And he heard their groaning, and remembered the covenant which he made with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

text.NRSV: God looked upon the Israelites, and God took notice of them.
text.Rotherham: so then, God looked upon the sons of Israel,—and God regarded.
text.KJV: And God looked upon the children of Israel, and God had respect unto them.
text.NABRE: God saw the Israelites, and God knew….
text.DRB: And our Lord looked upon the children of Israel and knew them.

text.NRSV: Moses was keeping the flock of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian; he led his flock beyond the wilderness, and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.
text.Rotherham: Now, Moses, was shepherding the flock of Jethro his father-in-law priest of Midian,—so he led forth the flock behind the desert, and came in unto the mountain of God to Horeb.
text.KJV: Now Moses kept the flock of Jethro his father in law, the priest of Midian: and he led the flock to the backside of the desert, and came to the mountain of God, even to Horeb.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile Moses was tending the flock of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian. Leading the flock beyond the wilderness, he came to the mountain of God, Horeb.
text.DRB: And Moyses fed the sheep of Jethro his father in law the Priest of Madian: and having driven the flock to the inner parts of the desert, he came to the mountain of God, Horeb.

text.NRSV: There the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a flame of fire out of a bush; he looked, and the bush was blazing, yet it was not consumed.
text.Rotherham: Then appeared the messenger of Yahweh unto him, in a flame of fire from the midst of the thorn-bush,—so he looked and lo! the thorn-bush burning with fire and yet the thorn-bush, was not consumed.
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush: and he looked, and, behold, the bush burned with fire, and the bush was not consumed.
text.NABRE: There the angel of the Lord appeared to him as fire flaming out of a bush. When he looked, although the bush was on fire, it was not being consumed.
text.DRB: And our Lord appeared to him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush: and he saw that the bush was on fire and was not burnt.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said, “I must turn aside and look at this great sight, and see why the bush is not burned up.”
text.Rotherham: And, Moses said, Let me turn aside, pray, and see this great sight,—why the thorn-bush, doth not burn up.
text.KJV: And Moses said, I will now turn aside, and see this great sight, why the bush is not burnt.
text.NABRE: So Moses decided, “I must turn aside to look at this remarkable sight. Why does the bush not burn up?”
text.DRB: Moyses therefore said: I will go, and see this great vision, why the bush is not burnt.

text.NRSV: When the Lord saw that he had turned aside to see, God called to him out of the bush, “Moses, Moses!” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh saw, that he turned aside to see,—so God called unto him out of the midst of the thorn-bush, and said—Moses! Moses! And he said—Behold me!
text.KJV: And when the Lord saw that he turned aside to see, God called unto him out of the midst of the bush, and said, Moses, Moses. And he said, Here am I.
text.NABRE: When the Lord saw that he had turned aside to look, God called out to him from the bush: Moses! Moses! He answered, “Here I am.”
text.DRB: And our Lord seeing that he went forward to see, he called him out of the midst of the bush, and said: Moyses, Moyses. Who answered: Here I am.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Come no closer! Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place on which you are standing is holy ground.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Do not draw nigh hither,—put off thy sandals from thy feet, for, the place whereon thou art standing is holy ground.
text.KJV: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.
text.NABRE: God said: Do not come near! Remove your sandals from your feet, for the place where you stand is holy ground.
text.DRB: But he said: Approach not hither, loose off thy shoe from thy feet: for the place wherein thou standest is holy ground.

text.NRSV: He said further, “I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.” And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.
text.Rotherham: And he said—I, am the God of thy father, God of Abraham, God of Isaac and God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look upon God.
text.KJV: Moreover he said, I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look upon God.
text.NABRE: I am the God of your father, he continued, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.
text.DRB: And he said: I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Moyses hid his face; for he durst not look against God.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “I have observed the misery of my people who are in Egypt; I have heard their cry on account of their taskmasters. Indeed, I know their sufferings,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh, I have, seen, the humiliation of my people, who are in Egypt,—and their outcry, have I heard, by reason of their task-masters, for I know their pains;
text.KJV: And the Lord said, I have surely seen the affliction of my people which are in Egypt, and have heard their cry by reason of their taskmasters; for I know their sorrows;
text.NABRE: The Call and Commission of Moses. But the Lord said: I have witnessed the affliction of my people in Egypt and have heard their cry against their taskmasters, so I know well what they are suffering.
text.DRB: To whom our Lord said: I have seen the affliction of my people in Ægypt, and I have heard their cry because of their rigour that oversee the works:

text.NRSV: and I have come down to deliver them from the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land to a good and broad land, a land flowing with milk and honey, to the country of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.Rotherham: therefore have I come down to deliver them from the hand of the Egyptians, and to take them up out of that land, into a land good and large, into a land flowing with milk and honey,—into the place of the Canaanite and the Hittite, and the Amorite and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
text.KJV: And I am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land unto a good land and a large, unto a land flowing with milk and honey; unto the place of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.NABRE: Therefore I have come down to rescue them from the power of the Egyptians and lead them up from that land into a good and spacious land, a land flowing with milk and honey, the country of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Girgashites, the Hivites and the Jebusites.
text.DRB: and knowing their sorrow, I am descended to deliver them out of the hands of the Ægyptians, and to bring them out of that land into a land good and large, into a land that floweth with milk and honey, to the place of the Chananeite, and Hetheite, and Amorrheite, and Pherezeite, and Heveite, & Jebuseite.

text.NRSV: The cry of the Israelites has now come to me; I have also seen how the Egyptians oppress them.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the outcry of the sons of Israel, hath come in unto me,—moreover also, I have seen the oppression, wherewith the Egyptians are oppressing them.
text.KJV: Now therefore, behold, the cry of the children of Israel is come unto me: and I have also seen the oppression wherewith the Egyptians oppress them.
text.NABRE: Now indeed the outcry of the Israelites has reached me, and I have seen how the Egyptians are oppressing them.
text.DRB: Therefore the cry of the children of Israel is come unto me: and I have seen their affliction wherewith they are oppressed by the Ægyptians.

text.NRSV: So come, I will send you to Pharaoh to bring my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, come thou! that I may send thee unto Pharaoh,—and bring thou forth my people—the sons of Israel—out of Egypt.
text.KJV: Come now therefore, and I will send thee unto Pharaoh, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Now, go! I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt.
text.DRB: But come, and I will send thee to Pharao, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to God, “Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh, and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto God, Who am, I, that I should go unto Pharaoh,—and that I should bring forth the sons of Israel out of Egypt?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto God, Who am I, that I should go unto Pharaoh, and that I should bring forth the children of Israel out of Egypt?
text.NABRE: But Moses said to God, “Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to God: Who am I that I shall go to Pharao, and bring forth the children of Israel out of Ægypt?

text.NRSV: He said, “I will be with you; and this shall be the sign for you that it is I who sent you: when you have brought the people out of Egypt, you shall worship God on this mountain.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—I will be with thee, and, this, to thee, [shall be] the sign, that, I have sent thee,—When thou bringest forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall do service unto God, upon this mountain.
text.KJV: And he said, Certainly I will be with thee; and this shall be a token unto thee, that I have sent thee: When thou hast brought forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall serve God upon this mountain.
text.NABRE: God answered: I will be with you; and this will be your sign that I have sent you. When you have brought the people out of Egypt, you will serve God at this mountain.
text.DRB: Who said to him: I will be with thee; and this thou shalt have for a sign that I have sent thee: When thou shalt have brought my people out of Ægypt, thou shalt sacrifice to God upon this mountain.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to God, “If I come to the Israelites and say to them, ‘The God of your ancestors has sent me to you,’ and they ask me, ‘What is his name?’ what shall I say to them?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto God—Lo! as surely as, I, go in unto the sons of Israel, and say unto them, The God of your fathers, hath sent me unto you, So surely will they say unto me—What is his name? What shall I say unto them?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto God, Behold, when I come unto the children of Israel, and shall say unto them, The God of your fathers hath sent me unto you; and they shall say to me, What is his name? what shall I say unto them?
text.NABRE: “But,” said Moses to God, “if I go to the Israelites and say to them, ‘The God of your ancestors has sent me to you,’ and they ask me, ‘What is his name?’ what do I tell them?”
text.DRB: Moyses said to God: Lo I shall go to the children of Israel, and say to them: The God of your fathers hath sent me to you. If they shall say to me: What is his name: What shall I say to them?

text.NRSV: God said to Moses, “I am who I am.” He said further, “Thus you shall say to the Israelites, ‘I am has sent me to you.’”
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Moses, I Will Become whatsoever I please, And he said—Thus, shalt thou say to the sons of Israel, I Will Become hath sent me unto you.
text.KJV: And God said unto Moses, I AM THAT I AM: and he said, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, I AM hath sent me unto you.
text.NABRE: God replied to Moses: I am who I am. Then he added: This is what you will tell the Israelites: I AM has sent me to you.
text.DRB: God said to Moyses: I am Which AM. He said: Thus shalt thou say to the children of Israel: HE Which IS, hath sent me to you.

text.NRSV: God also said to Moses, “Thus you shall say to the Israelites, ‘The Lord, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you’: This is my name forever, and this my title for all generations.
text.Rotherham: And God said yet further unto Moses—Thus shalt thou say unto the sons of Israel, Yahweh God of your fathers. God of Abraham God of Isaac and God of Jacob, hath sent me unto you. This, is my name to times age-abiding, And, this, my memorial to generation after generation.
text.KJV: And God said moreover unto Moses, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath sent me unto you: this is my name for ever, and this is my memorial unto all generations.
text.NABRE: God spoke further to Moses: This is what you will say to the Israelites: The Lord, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you. This is my name forever; this is my title for all generations.
text.DRB: And God said again to Moyses: These things shalt thou say to the children of Israel: The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob hath sent me to you: this is my name for ever, and this is my memorial into generation & to generation.

text.NRSV: Go and assemble the elders of Israel, and say to them, ‘The Lord, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, has appeared to me, saying: I have given heed to you and to what has been done to you in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Go—and thou shalt gather together the elders of Israel and shalt say unto them-Yahweh God of your fathers, hath appeared unto me, the God of Abraham Isaac, and Jacob saying,—I am, concerned, for you, and for what is done to you in Egypt;
text.KJV: Go, and gather the elders of Israel together, and say unto them, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, appeared unto me, saying, I have surely visited you, and seen that which is done to you in Egypt:
text.NABRE: Go and gather the elders of the Israelites, and tell them, The Lord, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, has appeared to me and said: I have observed you and what is being done to you in Egypt;
text.DRB: Go, and gather together the Ancients of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: The Lord God of your fathers hath appeared to me, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, saying: Visiting I have visited you: and I have seen all things that have chanced to you in Ægypt:

text.NRSV: I declare that I will bring you up out of the misery of Egypt, to the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, a land flowing with milk and honey.’
text.Rotherham: therefore do I say—I will bring you up out of the humiliation of Egypt, into the land of the Canaanite and the Hittite, and the Amorite and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite,—into a land flowing with milk and honey.
text.KJV: And I have said, I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt unto the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, unto a land flowing with milk and honey.
text.NABRE: so I have decided to lead you up out of your affliction in Egypt into the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Girgashites, the Hivites and the Jebusites, a land flowing with milk and honey.
text.DRB: and I have said the word to bring you forth out of the affliction of Ægypt, into the land of the Chananeite, and Hetheite, and Amorrheite, and Pherezeite, and Heveite, and Jebuseite, to a Land that floweth with milk and honey.

text.NRSV: They will listen to your voice; and you and the elders of Israel shall go to the king of Egypt and say to him, ‘The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us; let us now go a three days’ journey into the wilderness, so that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.’
text.Rotherham: So will they hearken to thy voice,—and thou shalt go in—thou, and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt and ye shall say unto him—Yahweh, God of the Hebrews, hath fallen in with us, Now, therefore let us go, we pray thee, a journey of three days into the desert, that we may sacrifice to Yahweh, our God.
text.KJV: And they shall hearken to thy voice: and thou shalt come, thou and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt, and ye shall say unto him, The Lord God of the Hebrews hath met with us: and now let us go, we beseech thee, three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.
text.NABRE: They will listen to you. Then you and the elders of Israel will go to the king of Egypt and say to him: The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, has come to meet us. So now, let us go a three days’ journey in the wilderness to offer sacrifice to the Lord, our God.
text.DRB: And they shall hear thy voice: and thou shalt enter in, thou and the ancients of Israel to the king of Ægypt, and thou shalt say to him: The Lord God of the Hebrews hath called us: We will go three days' journey into the wilderness to sacrifice unto the Lord our God.

text.NRSV: I know, however, that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless compelled by a mighty hand.
text.Rotherham: But, I, know, that the king of Egypt will not suffer you to go,—not even by a firm hand.
text.KJV: And I am sure that the king of Egypt will not let you go, no, not by a mighty hand.
text.NABRE: Yet I know that the king of Egypt will not allow you to go unless his hand is forced.
text.DRB: But I know that the king of Ægypt will not dismiss you to go but by mighty hand.

text.NRSV: So I will stretch out my hand and strike Egypt with all my wonders that I will perform in it; after that he will let you go.
text.Rotherham: So then I will put forth my hand and smite Egypt, with all my wonders, which I will do in the midst thereof,—and, after that, he will send you forth.
text.KJV: And I will stretch out my hand, and smite Egypt with all my wonders which I will do in the midst thereof: and after that he will let you go.
text.NABRE: So I will stretch out my hand and strike Egypt with all the wondrous deeds I will do in its midst. After that he will let you go.
text.DRB: For I will stretch forth my hand, and will strike Ægypt in all my marvels, which I will do in the midst of them: after these he will dismiss you.

text.NRSV: I will bring this people into such favor with the Egyptians that, when you go, you will not go empty-handed;
text.Rotherham: Thus will I give favour to this people in the eyes of the Egyptians,—so shall it come to pass that when ye do go, ye shall not go empty;
text.KJV: And I will give this people favour in the sight of the Egyptians: and it shall come to pass, that, when ye go, ye shall not go empty:
text.NABRE: I will even make the Egyptians so well-disposed toward this people that, when you go, you will not go empty-handed.
text.DRB: And I will give grace to this people in the sight of the Ægyptians: and when you shall go forth, you shall not depart empty:

text.NRSV: each woman shall ask her neighbor and any woman living in the neighbor’s house for jewelry of silver and of gold, and clothing, and you shall put them on your sons and on your daughters; and so you shall plunder the Egyptians.”
text.Rotherham: but every woman shall ask of her neighbour, and of her that sojourneth in her house, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold and mantles,—and ye shall put them upon your sons and upon your daughters, so shall ye spoil the Egyptians.
text.KJV: But every woman shall borrow of her neighbour, and of her that sojourneth in her house, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment: and ye shall put them upon your sons, and upon your daughters; and ye shall spoil the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: Every woman will ask her neighbor and the resident alien in her house for silver and gold articles and for clothing, and you will put them on your sons and daughters. So you will plunder the Egyptians.
text.DRB: but each woman shall ask of her neighbour and of her that is in house with her vessels of silver and of gold, and raiment: and you shall lay it upon your sons and daughters, and shall spoil Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Moses answered, “But suppose they do not believe me or listen to me, but say, ‘The Lord did not appear to you.’”
text.Rotherham: Then responded Moses, and said, And lo! they will not believe me, neither hearken to my voice,—for they will say, Yahweh, hath not appeared unto thee.
text.KJV: And Moses answered and said, But, behold, they will not believe me, nor hearken unto my voice: for they will say, The Lord hath not appeared unto thee.
text.NABRE: “But,” objected Moses, “suppose they do not believe me or listen to me? For they may say, ‘The Lord did not appear to you.’”
text.DRB: Moyses answering said: They will not believe me, nor hear my voice, but they will say: Our Lord hath not appeared to thee.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “What is that in your hand?” He said, “A staff.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—What is this in thy hand? And he said—A staff.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, What is that in thine hand? And he said, A rod.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to him: What is in your hand? “A staff,” he answered.
text.DRB: Therefore he said to him: What is that thou holdest in thy hand? He answered: A rod.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Throw it on the ground.” So he threw the staff on the ground, and it became a snake; and Moses drew back from it.
text.Rotherham: Then said he—Cast it to the earth. And he cast it to the earth, and it became a serpent,—and Moses fled from the face thereof.
text.KJV: And he said, Cast it on the ground. And he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from before it.
text.NABRE: God said: Throw it on the ground. So he threw it on the ground and it became a snake, and Moses backed away from it.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Cast it upon the ground. He did cast it, and it was turned into a serpent, so that Moyses fled.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Reach out your hand, and seize it by the tail”—so he reached out his hand and grasped it, and it became a staff in his hand—
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Put forth thy hand, and take it by its tail: So he put forth his hand, and laid hold of it, and it became a staff in his hand:—
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Put forth thine hand, and take it by the tail. And he put forth his hand, and caught it, and it became a rod in his hand:
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Now stretch out your hand and take hold of its tail. So he stretched out his hand and took hold of it, and it became a staff in his hand.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Stretch thy hand, & catch the tail thereof. He stretched it forth, & took hold of it, and it was turned into a rod.

text.NRSV: “so that they may believe that the Lord, the God of their ancestors, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you.”
text.Rotherham: that they may believe that Yahweh, God of their fathers,—God of Abraham, God of Isaac, and God of Jacob hath appeared unto thee.
text.KJV: That they may believe that the Lord God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.
text.NABRE: That is so they will believe that the Lord, the God of their ancestors, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, did appear to you.
text.DRB: That they may believe, quoth he, that the Lord God of their fathers hath appeared to thee, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, & the God of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Again, the Lord said to him, “Put your hand inside your cloak.” He put his hand into his cloak; and when he took it out, his hand was leprous, as white as snow.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto him, yet again—Bring, I pray thee, thy hand, into thy bosom. So he brought his hand, into his bosom,—and then took it out, and lo! his hand, was leprous,—like snow,
text.KJV: And the Lord said furthermore unto him, Put now thine hand into thy bosom. And he put his hand into his bosom: and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous as snow.
text.NABRE: Again the Lord said to him: Put your hand into the fold of your garment. So he put his hand into the fold of his garment, and when he drew it out, there was his hand covered with scales, like snowflakes.
text.DRB: And our Lord said again: Put thy hand into thy bosom. Which when he had put into his bosom, he brought it forth full of leprosy like snow.

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Put your hand back into your cloak”—so he put his hand back into his cloak, and when he took it out, it was restored like the rest of his body—
text.Rotherham: And he said—Put back thy hand into thy bosom: So he put back his hand into his bosom,—and then took it out of his bosom, and lo it had come again like his own flesh:
text.KJV: And he said, Put thine hand into thy bosom again. And he put his hand into his bosom again; and plucked it out of his bosom, and, behold, it was turned again as his other flesh.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Put your hand back into the fold of your garment. So he put his hand back into the fold of his garment, and when he drew it out, there it was again like his own flesh.
text.DRB: Draw back, quoth he, thy hand into thy bosom. He drew it back, and brought it forth again, & it was like the other flesh.

text.NRSV: “If they will not believe you or heed the first sign, they may believe the second sign.
text.Rotherham: thus shall it come to pass, if they will not believe thee, nor hearken to the voice of the first sign, then will they believe the voice of the next sign;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe thee, neither hearken to the voice of the first sign, that they will believe the voice of the latter sign.
text.NABRE: If they do not believe you or pay attention to the message of the first sign, they should believe the message of the second sign.
text.DRB: If they will not believe thee, quoth he, nor hear the word of the former sign, they will believe the word of the sign following.

text.NRSV: If they will not believe even these two signs or heed you, you shall take some water from the Nile and pour it on the dry ground; and the water that you shall take from the Nile will become blood on the dry ground.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall come to pass if they will not believe even these two signs nor hearken to thy voice, then shalt thou take of the water of the river, and pour it out on the dry land,—so shall the water which thou hast taken from the river, become, yea it shall become, blood, on the dry land.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe also these two signs, neither hearken unto thy voice, that thou shalt take of the water of the river, and pour it upon the dry land: and the water which thou takest out of the river shall become blood upon the dry land.
text.NABRE: And if they do not believe even these two signs and do not listen to you, take some water from the Nile and pour it on the dry land. The water you take from the Nile will become blood on the dry land.
text.DRB: And if so be they will believe neither of these two signs, nor hear thy voice, take water of the river, & pour it out upon the dry land, and whatsoever thou drawest of the river shall be turned into blood.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Lord, “O my Lord, I have never been eloquent, neither in the past nor even now that you have spoken to your servant; but I am slow of speech and slow of tongue.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh—Pardon, O My Lord! not a man of words, am I, neither heretofore, nor since thou hast spoken unto thy servant,—for heavy of mouth and heavy of tongue, I am.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the Lord, O my Lord, I am not eloquent, neither heretofore, nor since thou hast spoken unto thy servant: but I am slow of speech, and of a slow tongue.
text.NABRE: Aaron’s Office as Assistant. Moses, however, said to the Lord, “If you please, my Lord, I have never been eloquent, neither in the past nor now that you have spoken to your servant; but I am slow of speech and tongue.”
text.DRB: Moyses said: I beseech thee, Lord, I am not eloquent from yesterday and the day before: and since thou hast spoken to thy servant, I have more impediment & slowness of tongue.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Who gives speech to mortals? Who makes them mute or deaf, seeing or blind? Is it not I, the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh said unto him—Who appointed a mouth for man, or who appointeth him to be dumb, or deaf, or seeing or blind? Is it not I, Yahweh,?
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, Who hath made man’s mouth? or who maketh the dumb, or deaf, or the seeing, or the blind? have not I the Lord?
text.NABRE: The Lord said to him: Who gives one person speech? Who makes another mute or deaf, seeing or blind? Is it not I, the Lord?
text.DRB: Our Lord said to him: Who made the mouth of man; or who framed the dumb and deaf, the seeing and the blind? Did not I?

text.NRSV: Now go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you are to speak.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore go,—and, I, will be with thy mouth, so will I direct thee, what thou shalt speak.
text.KJV: Now therefore go, and I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee what thou shalt say.
text.NABRE: Now go, I will assist you in speaking and teach you what you are to say.
text.DRB: Go on therefore, and I will be in thy mouth; & will teach thee what thou shalt speak.

text.NRSV: But he said, “O my Lord, please send someone else.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Pardon, O My Lord! send, I pray thee by the hand of him whom thou wilt send—
text.KJV: And he said, O my Lord, send, I pray thee, by the hand of him whom thou wilt send.
text.NABRE: But he said, “If you please, my Lord, send someone else!”
text.DRB: But he said: I beseech thee, Lord, send whom thou wilt send.

text.NRSV: Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses and he said, “What of your brother Aaron, the Levite? I know that he can speak fluently; even now he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you his heart will be glad.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh against Moses, and he said—Is there not Aaron, thy brother, the Levite? I know that he, can speak. Moreover also lo! he himself is coming forth to meet thee, as surely as he seeth thee, so surely will he rejoice in his heart.
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses, and he said, Is not Aaron the Levite thy brother? I know that he can speak well. And also, behold, he cometh forth to meet thee: and when he seeth thee, he will be glad in his heart.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord became angry with Moses and said: I know there is your brother, Aaron the Levite, who is a good speaker; even now he is on his way to meet you. When he sees you, he will truly be glad.
text.DRB: Our Lord being angry at Moyses, said: Aaron thy brother the Levite, I know that he is eloquent: behold he cometh forth to meet thee, & seeing thee shall be glad at the heart.

text.NRSV: You shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth; and I will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and will teach you what you shall do.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou speak unto him, and shalt put the words in his mouth,—and, I, will be with thy mouth and with his mouth, so will I direct you what ye shall do.
text.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
text.NABRE: You will speak to him and put the words in his mouth. I will assist both you and him in speaking and teach you both what you are to do.
text.DRB: Speak to him, and put my words in his mouth; & I will be in thy mouth, and in his mouth, and will shew you what ye must do.

text.NRSV: He indeed shall speak for you to the people; he shall serve as a mouth for you, and you shall serve as God for him.
text.Rotherham: And, he, shall speak for thee unto the people,—so shall it come to pass that he shall become thy mouth, and thou shalt become to him as God.
text.KJV: And he shall be thy spokesman unto the people: and he shall be, even he shall be to thee instead of a mouth, and thou shalt be to him instead of God.
text.NABRE: He will speak to the people for you: he will be your spokesman, and you will be as God to him.
text.DRB: He shall speak in thy stead to the people, and shall be thy mouth: but thou shalt be to him in those things that pertain to God.

text.NRSV: Take in your hand this staff, with which you shall perform the signs.”
text.Rotherham: And this staff, take thou in thy hand,—wherewith thou shalt do the signs.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take this rod in thine hand, wherewith thou shalt do signs.
text.NABRE: Take this staff in your hand; with it you are to perform the signs.
text.DRB: This rod also take in thy hand, wherewith thou shalt do the signs.

text.NRSV: Moses went back to his father-in-law Jethro and said to him, “Please let me go back to my kindred in Egypt and see whether they are still living.” And Jethro said to Moses, “Go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: So Moses went and returned unto Jethro his father-in-law and said to him—Let me go, I pray thee, and return unto my brethren who are in Egypt, that I may see whether they are yet alive. And Jethro said to Moses: Go and prosper.
text.KJV: And Moses went and returned to Jethro his father in law, and said unto him, Let me go, I pray thee, and return unto my brethren which are in Egypt, and see whether they be yet alive. And Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.
text.NABRE: Moses’ Return to Egypt. After this Moses returned to Jethro his father-in-law and said to him, “Let me return to my kindred in Egypt, to see whether they are still living.” Jethro replied to Moses, “Go in peace.”
text.DRB: Moyses went his way, & returned to Jethro his father in law, and said to him: I will go and return to my brethren in Ægypt, that I may see if they be yet alive. To whom Jethro said: Go in peace:

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses in Midian, “Go back to Egypt; for all those who were seeking your life are dead.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses in Midian, Go, return to Egypt,—for all the men are dead who were seeking thy life.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses in Midian, Go, return into Egypt: for all the men are dead which sought thy life.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses in Midian: Return to Egypt, for all those who sought your life are dead.
text.DRB: Therefore our Lord said to Moyses in Madian: Go, and return into Ægypt: for they are all dead that sought thy life.

text.NRSV: So Moses took his wife and his sons, put them on a donkey and went back to the land of Egypt; and Moses carried the staff of God in his hand.
text.Rotherham: So Moses took his wife and his sons, and caused them to ride upon the ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt,—and Moses took the staff of God in his hand.
text.KJV: And Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them upon an ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt: and Moses took the rod of God in his hand.
text.NABRE: So Moses took his wife and his sons, mounted them on the donkey, and started back to the land of Egypt. Moses took the staff of God with him.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore took his wife, and his children, and set them upon an ass: and returned into Ægypt, carrying the rod of God in his hand.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, “When you go back to Egypt, see that you perform before Pharaoh all the wonders that I have put in your power; but I will harden his heart, so that he will not let the people go.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, When thou goest to return to Egypt, see as touching all the wonders which I have put in thy hand, that thou do them before Pharaoh—but, I, will let his heart wax bold, and he will not suffer the people to go.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, When thou goest to return into Egypt, see that thou do all those wonders before Pharaoh, which I have put in thine hand: but I will harden his heart, that he shall not let the people go.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: On your return to Egypt, see that you perform before Pharaoh all the wonders I have put in your power. But I will harden his heart and he will not let the people go.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him returning into Ægypt: See that thou do all the wonders which I have put in thy hand, before Pharao: I will indurate his heart, and he will not dismiss the people.

text.NRSV: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord: Israel is my firstborn son.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say unto Pharaoh, Thus, saith Yahweh—My son—my firstborn, is Israel;
text.KJV: And thou shalt say unto Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord, Israel is my son, even my firstborn:
text.NABRE: So you will say to Pharaoh, Thus says the Lord: Israel is my son, my firstborn.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to him: This saith the Lord: My first-begotten son is Israel.

text.NRSV: I said to you, “Let my son go that he may worship me.” But you refused to let him go; now I will kill your firstborn son.’”
text.Rotherham: Therefore I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me, But if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I, am going to slay, thy son, thy firstborn.
text.KJV: And I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
text.NABRE: I said to you: Let my son go, that he may serve me. Since you refused to let him go, I will kill your son, your firstborn.
text.DRB: Isaid to thee: dismiss my son that he may serve me, and thou wouldst not dismiss him: behold I will kill thy first-begotten son.

text.NRSV: On the way, at a place where they spent the night, the Lord met him and tried to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the way, in the resting-place for the night, that Yahweh fell upon him, and sought to kill him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass by the way in the inn, that the Lord met him, and sought to kill him.
text.NABRE: On the journey, at a place where they spent the night, the Lord came upon Moses and sought to put him to death.
text.DRB: And when he was in his journey, in the Inn, our Lord met him, and would have killed him.

text.NRSV: But Zipporah took a flint and cut off her son’s foreskin, and touched Moses’ feet with it, and said, “Truly you are a bridegroom of blood to me!”
text.Rotherham: So Zipporah took a sharp stone and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it down at his feet,—and said—Surely, a bridegroom by rites of blood, art thou to me!
text.KJV: Then Zipporah took a sharp stone, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet, and said, Surely a bloody husband art thou to me.
text.NABRE: But Zipporah took a piece of flint and cut off her son’s foreskin and, touching his feet, she said, “Surely you are a spouse of blood to me.”
text.DRB: Sephora by and by took a very sharp stone, and circumcised the prepuce of her son, and touched his feet, and said: A bloody spouse thou art to me.

text.NRSV: So he let him alone. It was then she said, “A bridegroom of blood by circumcision.”
text.Rotherham: So he let him go, Then, it was that she said, A bridegroom by rites of blood—with regard to the rites of circumcision.
text.KJV: So he let him go: then she said, A bloody husband thou art, because of the circumcision.
text.NABRE: So God let Moses alone. At that time she said, “A spouse of blood,” in regard to the circumcision.
text.DRB: And he let him go after she had said, A bloody spouse thou art to me, because of the circumcision.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Aaron, “Go into the wilderness to meet Moses.” So he went; and he met him at the mountain of God and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Aaron, Go thy way to meet Moses, towards the desert. So he went his way, and fell in with him in the mountain of God and kissed him.
text.KJV: And the Lord said to Aaron, Go into the wilderness to meet Moses. And he went, and met him in the mount of God, and kissed him.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Aaron: Go into the wilderness to meet Moses. So he went; when meeting him at the mountain of God, he kissed him.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Aaron: Go to Moyses into the desert. Who went forth to meet him unto the Mountain of God, and kissed him.

text.NRSV: Moses told Aaron all the words of the Lord with which he had sent him, and all the signs with which he had charged him.
text.Rotherham: And Moses told Aaron, all the words of Yahweh wherewith he had sent him,—and all the signs wherewith he had charged him.
text.KJV: And Moses told Aaron all the words of the Lord who had sent him, and all the signs which he had commanded him.
text.NABRE: Moses told Aaron everything the Lord had sent him to say, and all the signs he had commanded him to do.
text.DRB: And Moyses told Aaron all the words of our Lord, by which he had sent him, and the signs that he had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron went and assembled all the elders of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: So Moses and Aaron went their way,—and gathered together all the elders of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: Then Moses and Aaron went and gathered all the elders of the Israelites.
text.DRB: And they came together, and gathered together all the ancients of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Aaron spoke all the words that the Lord had spoken to Moses, and performed the signs in the sight of the people.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron spake all the words which Yahweh had spoken unto Moses,—and did the signs before the eyes of the people,
text.KJV: And Aaron spake all the words which the Lord had spoken unto Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.
text.NABRE: Aaron told them everything the Lord had said to Moses, and he performed the signs before the people.
text.DRB: And Aaron spake all the words which our Lord had said to Moyses: and he wrought the signs before the people,

text.NRSV: The people believed; and when they heard that the Lord had given heed to the Israelites and that he had seen their misery, they bowed down and worshiped.
text.Rotherham: And the people believed,—and when they heard that Yahweh had concerned himself for the sons of Israel, and that he had looked upon their humiliation, then bent they their heads and bowed themselves down.
text.KJV: And the people believed: and when they heard that the Lord had visited the children of Israel, and that he had looked upon their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.
text.NABRE: The people believed, and when they heard that the Lord had observed the Israelites and had seen their affliction, they knelt and bowed down.
text.DRB: and the people believed. And they heard that our Lord had visited the children of Israel, and that he had looked upon their affliction; and they adored prostrate.

text.NRSV: Afterward Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Let my people go, so that they may celebrate a festival to me in the wilderness.’”
text.Rotherham: And afterwards, Moses and Aaron went in and said unto Pharaoh,—Thus, saith Yahweh God of Israel, Let my people go, that they may hold a festival to me in the desert.
text.KJV: And afterward Moses and Aaron went in, and told Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Let my people go, that they may hold a feast unto me in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Afterwards, Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Let my people go, that they may hold a feast for me in the wilderness.”
text.DRB: After these things Moyses and Aaron went in, and said to Pharao: This saith the Lord God of Israel: dismiss my people that they may sacrifice to me in the desert.

text.NRSV: But Pharaoh said, “Who is the Lord, that I should heed him and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and I will not let Israel go.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Pharaoh, Who is Yahweh that I should hearken to his voice, to let Israel go? I know not Yahweh, and certainly. Israel, will I not let go!
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said, Who is the Lord, that I should obey his voice to let Israel go? I know not the Lord, neither will I let Israel go.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh answered, “Who is the Lord, that I should obey him and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and I will not let Israel go.”
text.DRB: But he answered: Who is the Lord, that I should hear his voice, & dismiss Israel? I know not the Lord, and Israel I will not dismiss.

text.NRSV: Then they said, “The God of the Hebrews has revealed himself to us; let us go a three days’ journey into the wilderness to sacrifice to the Lord our God, or he will fall upon us with pestilence or sword.”
text.Rotherham: And they said, the God of the Hebrews, hath met with us,—let us go, we pray thee, a journey of three days into the desert and sacrifice to Yahweh our God, lest he fall upon us with pestilence or with sword.
text.KJV: And they said, The God of the Hebrews hath met with us: let us go, we pray thee, three days’ journey into the desert, and sacrifice unto the Lord our God; lest he fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword.
text.NABRE: They replied, “The God of the Hebrews has come to meet us. Let us go a three days’ journey in the wilderness, that we may offer sacrifice to the Lord, our God, so that he does not strike us with the plague or the sword.”
text.DRB: And they said: The God of the Hebrews hath called us to go three days' journey into the wilderness, & to sacrifice to the Lord our God: lest perhaps there chance to us pestilence or sword.

text.NRSV: But the king of Egypt said to them, “Moses and Aaron, why are you taking the people away from their work? Get to your labors!”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Egypt said unto them, Wherefore O Moses and Aaron should ye loose the people from their works? Get you to your burdens.
text.KJV: And the king of Egypt said unto them, Wherefore do ye, Moses and Aaron, let the people from their works? get you unto your burdens.
text.NABRE: The king of Egypt answered them, “Why, Moses and Aaron, do you make the people neglect their work? Off to your labors!”
text.DRB: The king of Ægypt said to them: Why do you Moyses and Aaron solicit the people from their works? Go you to your burdens.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh continued, “Now they are more numerous than the people of the land and yet you want them to stop working!”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said, Lo! many—now, are the people of the land,—and are ye to make them rest from their burdens?
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said, Behold, the people of the land now are many, and ye make them rest from their burdens.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh continued, “Look how they are already more numerous than the people of the land, and yet you would give them rest from their labors!”
text.DRB: And Pharao said: The people of the land is much: you see that the multitude is secretly increased: how much more if you give them rest from their works?

text.NRSV: That same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people, as well as their supervisors,
text.Rotherham: So then Pharaoh gave command on that day, unto the taskmasters over the people and unto their overseers—saying:
text.KJV: And Pharaoh commanded the same day the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying,
text.NABRE: That very day Pharaoh gave the taskmasters of the people and their foremen this order:
text.DRB: Therefore he commanded in that day the overseers of the works and the exactors of the people, saying:

text.NRSV: “You shall no longer give the people straw to make bricks, as before; let them go and gather straw for themselves.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall no more give straw unto the people, for making the bricks as heretofore: Let them, go, themselves, so shall they gather for themselves straw:
text.KJV: Ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore: let them go and gather straw for themselves.
text.NABRE: “You shall no longer supply the people with straw for their brickmaking as before. Let them go and gather their own straw!
text.DRB: You shall no more give straw to the people for to make bricks, as before; but let themselves go and gather straw.

text.NRSV: But you shall require of them the same quantity of bricks as they have made previously; do not diminish it, for they are lazy; that is why they cry, ‘Let us go and offer sacrifice to our God.’
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, the fixed number of bricks, which they have been making heretofore, shall ye lay upon them, ye shall not diminish therefrom,—for idle, they are, on this account, it is that they are making an outcry, saying, We must go our way, we must sacrifice to our God.
text.KJV: And the tale of the bricks, which they did make heretofore, ye shall lay upon them; ye shall not diminish ought thereof: for they be idle; therefore they cry, saying, Let us go and sacrifice to our God.
text.NABRE: Yet you shall levy upon them the same quota of bricks as they made previously. Do not reduce it. They are lazy; that is why they are crying, ‘Let us go to offer sacrifice to our God.’
