text.DRB: And the task of bricks, which they did before, you shall put upon them, neither shall you diminish any thing: for they are idle, and therefore they cry, saying: Let us go, and sacrifice to our God.

text.NRSV: Let heavier work be laid on them; then they will labor at it and pay no attention to deceptive words.”
text.Rotherham: The service must be made heavy on the men that they may labour on it,—and let them not pay regard to words of falsehood.
text.KJV: Let there more work be laid upon the men, that they may labour therein; and let them not regard vain words.
text.NABRE: Increase the work for the men, so that they attend to it and not to deceitful words.”
text.DRB: Let them be oppressed with works, and let them accomplish them: that they hearken not to lying words.

text.NRSV: So the taskmasters and the supervisors of the people went out and said to the people, “Thus says Pharaoh, ‘I will not give you straw.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth the taskmasters of the people and their overseers, and spake unto the people,—saying,—Thus, saith Pharaoh, I am not going to give you straw,
text.KJV: And the taskmasters of the people went out, and their officers, and they spake to the people, saying, Thus saith Pharaoh, I will not give you straw.
text.NABRE: So the taskmasters of the people and their foremen went out and told the people, “Thus says Pharaoh, ‘I will not provide you with straw.
text.DRB: Therefore the overseers of the works and the exactors going forth said unto the people: Thus saith Pharao: I allow you no straw:

text.NRSV: Go and get straw yourselves, wherever you can find it; but your work will not be lessened in the least.’”
text.Rotherham: Ye yourselves go fetch you straw, of any ye can find,—yet hath there not been diminished from your service a single thing.
text.KJV: Go ye, get you straw where ye can find it: yet not ought of your work shall be diminished.
text.NABRE: Go and get your own straw from wherever you can find it. But there will not be the slightest reduction in your work.’”
text.DRB: go, and gather if you can find any-where: neither shall any thing of your work be diminished.

text.NRSV: So the people scattered throughout the land of Egypt, to gather stubble for straw.
text.Rotherham: So the people dispersed themselves throughout all the land of Egypt,—to gather stubble for straw:
text.KJV: So the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble instead of straw.
text.NABRE: The people, then, scattered throughout the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw,
text.DRB: And the people was dispersed through all the Land of Ægypt to gather straw.

text.NRSV: The taskmasters were urgent, saying, “Complete your work, the same daily assignment as when you were given straw.”
text.Rotherham: the taskmasters, meanwhile, being urgent saying,—Complete your works, The task of a day, in its day, as when the straw was provided.
text.KJV: And the taskmasters hasted them, saying, Fulfil your works, your daily tasks, as when there was straw.
text.NABRE: while the taskmasters kept driving them on, saying, “Finish your work, the same daily amount as when the straw was supplied to you.”
text.DRB: And the overseers of the works were instant, saying: Finish your work every day, as before you were wont to when straw was given unto you.

text.NRSV: And the supervisors of the Israelites, whom Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, and were asked, “Why did you not finish the required quantity of bricks yesterday and today, as you did before?”
text.Rotherham: Then were beaten the overseers of the sons of Israel, whom the taskmasters of Pharaoh had set over them saying: Wherefore have ye not completed your task for making bricks, as heretofore, so also, yesterday and, to-day?
text.KJV: And the officers of the children of Israel, which Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, and demanded, Wherefore have ye not fulfilled your task in making brick both yesterday and to day, as heretofore?
text.NABRE: The Israelite foremen, whom the taskmasters of Pharaoh had placed over them, were beaten, and were asked, “Why have you not completed your prescribed amount of bricks yesterday and today, as before?”
text.DRB: And the overseers of the works of the children of Israel were scourged of Pharao's exactors, saying: Why do you not make up the task of bricks as before, neither yesterday, nor today?

text.NRSV: Then the Israelite supervisors came to Pharaoh and cried, “Why do you treat your servants like this?
text.Rotherham: Then came in the overseers of the sons of Israel, and made outcry unto Pharaoh, saying, Wherefore shouldst thou do thus to thy servants?
text.KJV: Then the officers of the children of Israel came and cried unto Pharaoh, saying, Wherefore dealest thou thus with thy servants?
text.NABRE: Complaint of the Foremen. Then the Israelite foremen came and cried out to Pharaoh: “Why do you treat your servants in this manner?
text.DRB: And the overseers of the children of Israel came, and cried out to Pharao, saying: Why dealest thou so against thy servants?

text.NRSV: No straw is given to your servants, yet they say to us, ‘Make bricks!’ Look how your servants are beaten! You are unjust to your own people.”
text.Rotherham: No straw, is given to thy servants, yet bricks,—they keep saying to us—make ye! And lo, thy servants, are beaten—thou dost, therefore, wrong thy people.
text.KJV: There is no straw given unto thy servants, and they say to us, Make brick: and, behold, thy servants are beaten; but the fault is in thine own people.
text.NABRE: No straw is supplied to your servants, and still we are told, ‘Make bricks!’ Look how your servants are beaten! It is you who are at fault.”
text.DRB: Straw is not given us, and bricks are commanded us in like sort: behold we thy servants are beaten with whips, and thy people is unjustly dealt withal.

text.NRSV: He said, “You are lazy, lazy; that is why you say, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to the Lord.’
text.Rotherham: But he said: Idle, ye are—idle, on this account, are, ye, saying, We must take our journey, we must sacrifice to Yahweh.
text.KJV: But he said, Ye are idle, ye are idle: therefore ye say, Let us go and do sacrifice to the Lord.
text.NABRE: He answered, “Lazy! You are lazy! That is why you keep saying, ‘Let us go and offer sacrifice to the Lord.’
text.DRB: Who said: You are idle, and therefore you say: Let us go and sacrifice to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Go now, and work; for no straw shall be given you, but you shall still deliver the same number of bricks.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, go labour, but, straw, shall not be given you,—yet the tale of bricks, shall ye render.
text.KJV: Go therefore now, and work; for there shall no straw be given you, yet shall ye deliver the tale of bricks.
text.NABRE: Now off to work! No straw will be supplied to you, but you must supply your quota of bricks.”
text.DRB: Go therefore, and work: straw shall not be given you, and you shall give up the accustomed number of bricks.

text.NRSV: The Israelite supervisors saw that they were in trouble when they were told, “You shall not lessen your daily number of bricks.”
text.Rotherham: Then did the overseers of the sons of Israel see themselves to be in an evil case, when it was said,—Ye shall not diminish from your bricks—the task of a day, in its day
text.KJV: And the officers of the children of Israel did see that they were in evil case, after it was said, Ye shall not minish ought from your bricks of your daily task.
text.NABRE: The Israelite foremen realized they were in trouble, having been told, “Do not reduce your daily amount of bricks!”
text.DRB: And the overseers of the children of Israel saw themselves in hard case, because it was said unto them: There shall not a whit be diminished of the bricks for every day.

text.NRSV: As they left Pharaoh, they came upon Moses and Aaron who were waiting to meet them.
text.Rotherham: So they fell upon Moses and upon Aaron, stationing themselves to meet them,—as they came forth from Pharaoh;
text.KJV: And they met Moses and Aaron, who stood in the way, as they came forth from Pharaoh:
text.NABRE: So when they left Pharaoh they assailed Moses and Aaron, who were waiting to meet them,
text.DRB: And they met Moyses and Aaron, who stood over-against them, coming forth from Pharao.

text.NRSV: They said to them, “The Lord look upon you and judge! You have brought us into bad odor with Pharaoh and his officials, and have put a sword in their hand to kill us.”
text.Rotherham: and they said unto them, Yahweh look upon you and judge,—in that ye have made us odious in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants, putting a sword into their hand to slay us.
text.KJV: And they said unto them, The Lord look upon you, and judge; because ye have made our savour to be abhorred in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants, to put a sword in their hand to slay us.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “The Lord look upon you and judge! You have made us offensive to Pharaoh and his servants, putting a sword into their hands to kill us.”
text.DRB: and they said to them: Our Lord see and judge, because you have made our savour to stink before Pharao and his servants, and you have given him a sword for to kill us.

text.NRSV: Then Moses turned again to the Lord and said, “O Lord, why have you mistreated this people? Why did you ever send me?
text.Rotherham: And Moses returned unto Yahweh and said,—O My Lord wherefore hast thou caused harm to this people? Wherefore now didst thou send me?
text.KJV: And Moses returned unto the Lord, and said, Lord, wherefore hast thou so evil entreated this people? why is it that thou hast sent me?
text.NABRE: Renewal of God’s Promise. Then Moses again had recourse to the Lord and said, “Lord, why have you treated this people badly? And why did you send me?
text.DRB: And Moyses returned to our Lord, and said: Lord Why hast thou afflicted this people? Wherefore hast thou sent me?

text.NRSV: Since I first came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has mistreated this people, and you have done nothing at all to deliver your people.”
text.Rotherham: seeing that from what time I came in unto Pharaoh to speak in thy name, he hath done harm to this people, and thou hast not delivered, thy people,
text.KJV: For since I came to Pharaoh to speak in thy name, he hath done evil to this people; neither hast thou delivered thy people at all.
text.NABRE: From the time I went to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has treated this people badly, and you have done nothing to rescue your people.”
text.DRB: For since the time that I entered in to Pharao to speak in thy name, he hath afflicted thy people: and thou hast not delivered them.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh: Indeed, by a mighty hand he will let them go; by a mighty hand he will drive them out of his land.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Now, shalt thou see, what I will do to Pharaoh, for with a strong hand, shall he let them go, and with a strong hand, shall he drive them out from his land.
text.KJV: Then the Lord said unto Moses, Now shalt thou see what I will do to Pharaoh: for with a strong hand shall he let them go, and with a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: Now you will see what I will do to Pharaoh. For by a strong hand, he will let them go; by a strong hand, he will drive them from his land.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses. Now thou shalt see what things I will do to Pharao: for by a mighty hand shall he dismiss them, and in a strong hand shall he cast them out of his land.

text.NRSV: God also spoke to Moses and said to him: “I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And God spake unto Moses,—and said unto him, I, am Yahweh:
text.KJV: And God spake unto Moses, and said unto him, I am the Lord:
text.NABRE: Confirmation of the Promise to the Ancestors. Then God spoke to Moses, and said to him: I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying: I am the Lord,

text.NRSV: I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty, but by my name ‘The Lord’ I did not make myself known to them.
text.Rotherham: I appeared, therefore, unto Abraham unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, as GOD Almighty,—although, by my name Yahweh was I not made known to them;
text.KJV: And I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, by the name of God Almighty, but by my name Jehovah was I not known to them.
text.NABRE: As God the Almighty I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, but by my name, Lord, I did not make myself known to them.
text.DRB: that appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, as God Almighty: and my name Adonai I did not shew them.

text.NRSV: I also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land in which they resided as aliens.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also I established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan,—even the land of their sojournings wherein they sojourned.
text.KJV: And I have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, wherein they were strangers.
text.NABRE: I also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land in which they were residing as aliens.
text.DRB: And I made a covenant with them, to give them the Land of Chanaan, the land of their pilgrimage, wherein they were strangers.

text.NRSV: I have also heard the groaning of the Israelites whom the Egyptians are holding as slaves, and I have remembered my covenant.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, I heard the groaning of the sons of Israel, whom the Egyptians, were holding in servitude, So then I remembered my covenant.
text.KJV: And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage; and I have remembered my covenant.
text.NABRE: Now that I have heard the groaning of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians have reduced to slavery, I am mindful of my covenant.
text.DRB: And I have heard the groaning of the children of Israel, wherewith the Ægyptians have oppressed them: & I have remembered my covenant.

text.NRSV: Say therefore to the Israelites, ‘I am the Lord, and I will free you from the burdens of the Egyptians and deliver you from slavery to them. I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with mighty acts of judgment.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say thou to the sons of Israel-I, am Yahweh, Therefore will I bring you forth from under the burdens of Egypt, And will deliver you out of their service, And will redeem you with a stretched-out arm, and with great judgments;
text.KJV: Wherefore say unto the children of Israel, I am the Lord, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with a stretched out arm, and with great judgments:
text.NABRE: Therefore, say to the Israelites: I am the Lord. I will free you from the burdens of the Egyptians and will deliver you from their slavery. I will redeem you by my outstretched arm and with mighty acts of judgment.
text.DRB: Therefore say to the children of Israel: I the Lord who will bring you forth out of the work-prison of the Ægyptians, and will deliver you from servitude: and redeem you in a high arm, and great judgments.

text.NRSV: I will take you as my people, and I will be your God. You shall know that I am the Lord your God, who has freed you from the burdens of the Egyptians.
text.Rotherham: And will take you to myself for a people, And will become your God,—And ye shall know that, I Yahweh, am your God, who brought you forth from under the burdens of Egypt:
text.KJV: And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God: and ye shall know that I am the Lord your God, which bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: I will take you as my own people, and I will be your God; and you will know that I, the Lord, am your God who has freed you from the burdens of the Egyptians
text.DRB: And I will take you to me for my people, and I will be your God: and you shall know that I am the Lord your God, that brought you forth out of the work-prison of the Ægyptians:

text.NRSV: I will bring you into the land that I swore to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; I will give it to you for a possession. I am the Lord.’”
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you into the land as to which I uplifted my hand to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob,—and will give it to you as an inheritance: I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will bring you in unto the land, concerning the which I did swear to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it you for an heritage: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: and I will bring you into the land which I swore to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I will give it to you as your own possession—I, the Lord!
text.DRB: and brought you into the land, over which I lifted up my hand to give it to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: and I will give it you to possess: I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Moses told this to the Israelites; but they would not listen to Moses, because of their broken spirit and their cruel slavery.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake thus unto the sons of Israel, but they hearkened not unto Moses, through shortness of spirit, and through hard service.
text.KJV: And Moses spake so unto the children of Israel: but they hearkened not unto Moses for anguish of spirit, and for cruel bondage.
text.NABRE: But when Moses told this to the Israelites, they would not listen to him because of their dejection and hard slavery.
text.DRB: Moyses then told all to the children of Israel: who did not hearken unto him, for anguish of spirit, and most painful work.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and tell Pharaoh king of Egypt to let the Israelites go out of his land.”
text.Rotherham: Go in speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the sons of Israel go out of his land.
text.KJV: Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.
text.NABRE: Go, tell Pharaoh, king of Egypt, to let the Israelites leave his land.
text.DRB: Go in, and speak to Pharao the king of Ægypt, that he dismiss the children of Israel out of his land.

text.NRSV: But Moses spoke to the Lord, “The Israelites have not listened to me; how then shall Pharaoh listen to me, poor speaker that I am?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake before Yahweh, saying,—Lo! the sons of Israel have not hearkened unto me, how then shall Pharaoh, hear, I, also being of uncircumcised lips.
text.KJV: And Moses spake before the Lord, saying, Behold, the children of Israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall Pharaoh hear me, who am of uncircumcised lips?
text.NABRE: However, Moses protested to the Lord, “If the Israelites did not listen to me, how is it possible that Pharaoh will listen to me, poor speaker that I am!”
text.DRB: And Moyses answered before our Lord: Behold the children of Israel hear me not: and how will Pharao hear, especially whereas I am of uncircumcised lips?

text.NRSV: Thus the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, and gave them orders regarding the Israelites and Pharaoh king of Egypt, charging them to free the Israelites from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Thus then did Yahweh speak unto Moses and unto Aaron, yea he gave them a charge unto the sons of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, for the bringing forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: But the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron regarding the Israelites and Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and charged them to bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses and Aaron, and he gave them commandment unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharao the king of Ægypt, that they should bring forth the children of Israel out of the land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The following are the heads of their ancestral houses: the sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi; these are the families of Reuben.
text.Rotherham: These, are the heads of their ancestral house: The sons of Reuben the first-born of Israel, Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi. These are the families of, Reuben.
text.KJV: These be the heads of their fathers’ houses: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel; Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi: these be the families of Reuben.
text.NABRE: Genealogy of Moses and Aaron. These are the heads of their ancestral houses. The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron and Carmi; these are the clans of Reuben.
text.DRB: These are Princes of their houses by their families. The sons of Ruben the first-begotten of Israel: Henoch and Phallu, Hesron and Charmi.

text.NRSV: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman; these are the families of Simeon.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Simeon, Jemuel and Jamin and Ohad and Jachin and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanitess. These, are the families of, Simeon.
text.KJV: And the sons of Simeon; Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanitish woman: these are the families of Simeon.
text.NABRE: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman; these are the clans of Simeon.
text.DRB: These are the kindreds of Ruben. The sons of Simeon: Iamuel and Jamin, and Ahod, and Jachin, and Soar, and Saul the sons of the Chananitess, these are the progenies of Simeon.

text.NRSV: The following are the names of the sons of Levi according to their genealogies: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari, and the length of Levi’s life was one hundred thirty-seven years.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the names of the sons of Levi by their generations, Gershon, and Kohath and Merari, And, the years of the life of Levi, were a hundred and thirty-seven years.
text.KJV: And these are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations; Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari: and the years of the life of Levi were an hundred thirty and seven years.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the sons of Levi, in their genealogical order: Gershon, Kohath and Merari. Levi lived one hundred and thirty-seven years.
text.DRB: And these are the names of the sons of Levi by their kindreds: Gerson and Caath, and Merari. And the years of the life of Levi were an hundred thirty seven.

text.NRSV: The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, by their families.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Gershon, Libni and Shimei by their families.
text.KJV: The sons of Gershon; Libni, and Shimi, according to their families.
text.NABRE: The sons of Gershon, by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
text.DRB: The sons of Gerson: Lobni and Semi, by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, and the length of Kohath’s life was one hundred thirty-three years.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Kohath, Amram and Izhar, and Hebron and Uzziel. And the years of the life of Kohath, were a hundred and thirty-three years.
text.KJV: And the sons of Kohath; Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel: and the years of the life of Kohath were an hundred thirty and three years.
text.NABRE: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel. Kohath lived one hundred and thirty-three years.
text.DRB: The sons of Caath: Amram, and Isaar, and Hebron and Oziel. The years also of Caath's life, were an hundred thirty three.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their genealogies.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi. These, are the families of the Levites by their generations.
text.KJV: And the sons of Merari; Mahali and Mushi: these are the families of Levi according to their generations.
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of Levi in their genealogical order.
text.DRB: The sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi, these be the kindreds of Levi by their families.

text.NRSV: Amram married Jochebed his father’s sister and she bore him Aaron and Moses, and the length of Amram’s life was one hundred thirty-seven years.
text.Rotherham: So then, Amram took Jochebed, his father’s sister to himself to wife, and she bare to him—Aaron and Moses. And, the years of the life of Amram, were a hundred and thirty-seven.
text.KJV: And Amram took him Jochebed his father’s sister to wife; and she bare him Aaron and Moses: and the years of the life of Amram were an hundred and thirty and seven years.
text.NABRE: Amram married his aunt Jochebed, who bore him Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. Amram lived one hundred and thirty-seven years.
text.DRB: And Amram took to wife Iocabed his aunt by the father's side: who bare him Aaron & Moyses. And the years of Amram's life were an hundred thirty seven.

text.NRSV: The sons of Izhar: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Izhar, Korah and Nepheg, and Zichri.
text.KJV: And the sons of Izhar; Korah, and Nepheg, and Zichri.
text.NABRE: The sons of Izhar: Korah, Nepheg and Zichri.
text.DRB: The sons also of Isaar: Core, & Nepheg, & Zechri.

text.NRSV: The sons of Uzziel: Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Uzziel, Mishael and Elzaphan, and Sithri.
text.KJV: And the sons of Uzziel; Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Zithri.
text.NABRE: The sons of Uzziel: Mishael, Elzaphan and Sithri.
text.DRB: The sons also of Oziel: Mizael, & Elizaphan, & Sethri.

text.NRSV: Aaron married Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab and sister of Nahshon, and she bore him Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron took Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon, to himself to wife,—and she bare to him—Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.KJV: And Aaron took him Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Naashon, to wife; and she bare him Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.NABRE: Aaron married Elisheba, Amminadab’s daughter, the sister of Nahshon; she bore him Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
text.DRB: And Aaron took to wife Elizabeth the daughter of Aminadab, sister of Nahason, who bare him Nadab, & Abiu, & Eleazar, & Ithamar.

text.NRSV: The sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are the families of the Korahites.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Korah, Assir and Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These are the families of the Korahites.
text.KJV: And the sons of Korah; Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph: these are the families of the Korhites.
text.NABRE: The sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah and Abiasaph. These are the clans of the Korahites.
text.DRB: The sons also of Core: Aser, & Elcana, & Abiasaph, these be the kindreds of the Corites.

text.NRSV: Aaron’s son Eleazar married one of the daughters of Putiel, and she bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the ancestral houses of the Levites by their families.
text.Rotherham: And Eleazar, son of Aaron took to himself [one] of the daughters of Putiel, to himself to wife, and she bare to him Phinehas. These, are the heads of the fathers of the Levites by their families.
text.KJV: And Eleazar Aaron’s son took him one of the daughters of Putiel to wife; and she bare him Phinehas: these are the heads of the fathers of the Levites according to their families.
text.NABRE: Eleazar, Aaron’s son, married one of Putiel’s daughters, who bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the ancestral houses of the Levites by their clans.
text.DRB: But Eleazar the son of Aaron took a wife of the daughters of Phutiel: who bare him Phinees. These are the heads of the Levitical families by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: It was this same Aaron and Moses to whom the Lord said, “Bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt, company by company.”
text.Rotherham: The same, Aaron and Moses,—to whom said Yahweh, Bring ye forth the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, according to their hosts:
text.KJV: These are that Aaron and Moses, to whom the Lord said, Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their armies.
text.NABRE: These are the Aaron and the Moses to whom the Lord said, “Bring the Israelites out from the land of Egypt, company by company.”
text.DRB: This is Aaron and Moyses, whom our Lord commanded that they should bring forth the children of Israel out of the land of Ægypt by their troops.

text.NRSV: It was they who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring the Israelites out of Egypt, the same Moses and Aaron.
text.Rotherham: They who were to speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring forth the sons of Israel out of Egypt: The same, Moses and Aaron.
text.KJV: These are they which spake to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt: these are that Moses and Aaron.
text.NABRE: They are the ones who spoke to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, to bring the Israelites out of Egypt—the same Moses and Aaron.
text.DRB: These are they that spake to Pharao the king of Ægypt, that they might bring forth the children of Israel out of Ægypt: this is Moyses, & Aaron,

text.NRSV: On the day when the Lord spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: Thus came it to pass, that on a certain day, Yahweh spake unto Moses in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the day when the Lord spake unto Moses in the land of Egypt,
text.NABRE: When the Lord spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt
text.DRB: in the day when our Lord spake to Moyses in the land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: he said to him, “I am the Lord; tell Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I am speaking to you.”
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—I, am Yahweh: Speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, all that, I, am speaking unto thee.
text.KJV: That the Lord spake unto Moses, saying, I am the Lord: speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say unto thee.
text.NABRE: the Lord said to Moses: I am the Lord. Say to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, all that I tell you.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying: I the Lord: speak to Pharao the king of Ægypt, all things which I speak to thee.

text.NRSV: But Moses said in the Lord’s presence, “Since I am a poor speaker, why would Pharaoh listen to me?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said before Yahweh,—Lo! I, am of uncircumcised lips, how then will Pharaoh hearken unto me?
text.KJV: And Moses said before the Lord, Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh hearken unto me?
text.NABRE: But Moses protested to the Lord, “Since I am a poor speaker, how is it possible that Pharaoh will listen to me?”
text.DRB: And Moyses said before our Lord: Lo I am of uncircumcised lips, how will Pharao hear me?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “See, I have made you like God to Pharaoh, and your brother Aaron shall be your prophet.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—See! I have appointed thee to be God unto Pharaoh,—and, Aaron thy brother, shall be thy prophet!
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, See, I have made thee a god to Pharaoh: and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: See! I have made you a god to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother will be your prophet.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Behold I have appointed thee the God of Pharao: and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.

text.NRSV: You shall speak all that I command you, and your brother Aaron shall tell Pharaoh to let the Israelites go out of his land.
text.Rotherham: Thou, shalt speak all that which I may command thee, and, Aaron thy brother, shall speak unto Pharaoh, and he shall let the sons of Israel go out of his land.
text.KJV: Thou shalt speak all that I command thee: and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he send the children of Israel out of his land.
text.NABRE: You will speak all that I command you. In turn, your brother Aaron will tell Pharaoh to let the Israelites go out of his land.
text.DRB: Thou shalt speak to him all things that I command thee: and he shall speak to Pharao that he dismiss the children of Israel out of his land.

text.NRSV: But I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and I will multiply my signs and wonders in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: But, I, will suffer Pharaoh to harden his heart,—so will I multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Yet I will make Pharaoh so headstrong that, despite the many signs and wonders that I work in the land of Egypt,
text.DRB: But I will indurate his heart, and will multiply my signs and wonders in the Land of Ægypt,

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh does not listen to you, I will lay my hand upon Egypt and bring my people the Israelites, company by company, out of the land of Egypt by great acts of judgment.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh will not hearken unto you, so will I lay my hand on Egypt,—and bring forth my hosts—my people the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, with great judgments.
text.KJV: But Pharaoh shall not hearken unto you, that I may lay my hand upon Egypt, and bring forth mine armies, and my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh will not listen to you. Therefore I will lay my hand on Egypt and with mighty acts of judgment I will bring my armies, my people the Israelites, out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: and he will not hear you: and I will put in my hand upon Ægypt, and will bring forth my army and people the children of Israel out of the Land of Ægypt, by very great judgments.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I stretch out my hand against Egypt and bring the Israelites out from among them.”
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians shall know that I, am Yahweh, when I have stretched forth my hand over Egypt,—and brought forth the sons of Israel out of their midst.
text.KJV: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I stretch forth mine hand upon Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.
text.NABRE: All Egyptians will know that I am the Lord, when I stretch out my hand against Egypt and bring the Israelites out of their midst.
text.DRB: And the Ægyptians shall know that I am the Lord, which have stretched forth my hand upon Ægypt, and have brought forth the children of Israel out of the midst of them.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron did so; they did just as the Lord commanded them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Aaron did [so],—as Yahweh commanded them, so, did they.
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron did as the Lord commanded them, so did they.
text.NABRE: This, then, is what Moses and Aaron did. They did exactly as the Lord had commanded them.
text.DRB: Therefore Moyses and Aaron did as our Lord had commanded: so did they.

text.NRSV: Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three when they spoke to Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: Now Moses, was eighty years old, and, Aaron, eighty-three years old,—when they spake unto Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And Moses was fourscore years old, and Aaron fourscore and three years old, when they spake unto Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three, when they spoke to Pharaoh.
text.DRB: And Moyses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty three, when they spake to Pharao.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: “When Pharaoh says to you, ‘Perform a wonder,’ then you shall say to Aaron, ‘Take your staff and throw it down before Pharaoh, and it will become a snake.’”
text.Rotherham: When Pharaoh shall speak unto you saying, Shew for yourselves a wonder, then shalt thou say unto Aaron, Take thy staff, and cast it down before Pharaoh let it become a sea-serpent.
text.KJV: When Pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying, Shew a miracle for you: then thou shalt say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and cast it before Pharaoh, and it shall become a serpent.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh demands of you, “Produce a sign or wonder,” you will say to Aaron: “Take your staff and throw it down before Pharaoh, and it will turn into a serpent.”
text.DRB: When Pharao shall say unto you, Shew signs, thou shalt say to Aaron: Take thy rod, and cast it before Pharao, and it shall be turned into a serpent.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did as the Lord had commanded; Aaron threw down his staff before Pharaoh and his officials, and it became a snake.
text.Rotherham: So Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and did so as Yahweh had commanded,—and Aaron cast down his staff before Pharaoh and before his servants and it became a sea-serpent,
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and they did so as the Lord had commanded: and Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh, and before his servants, and it became a serpent.
text.NABRE: Then Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did just as the Lord had commanded. Aaron threw his staff down before Pharaoh and his servants, and it turned into a serpent.
text.DRB: Therefore Moyses and Aaron going in unto Pharao, did as our Lord had commanded. And Aaron took the rod before Pharao and his servants, the which was turned into a serpent.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh summoned the wise men and the sorcerers; and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did the same by their secret arts.
text.Rotherham: Then called Pharaoh also for the wise men and for the magicians,—and they too, the sacred scribes of Egypt with their secret arts, did in like manner;
text.KJV: Then Pharaoh also called the wise men and the sorcerers: now the magicians of Egypt, they also did in like manner with their enchantments.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh, in turn, summoned the wise men and the sorcerers, and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did the same thing by their magic arts.
text.DRB: And Pharao called the wise men and the enchanters: and they also by Ægyptian enchantments and certain secrecies did in like manner.

text.NRSV: Each one threw down his staff, and they became snakes; but Aaron’s staff swallowed up theirs.
text.Rotherham: yea they cast down each man his staff, and they became sea-serpents,—but Aaron’s staff swallowed up their staves.
text.KJV: For they cast down every man his rod, and they became serpents: but Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.
text.NABRE: Each one threw down his staff, and they turned into serpents. But Aaron’s staff swallowed their staffs.
text.DRB: And every one did cast forth their rods, the which were turned into dragons: but Aaron's rod devoured their rods.

text.NRSV: Still Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, as the Lord had said.
text.Rotherham: Then waxed bold the heart of Pharaoh, and he hearkened not unto them,—as spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he hardened Pharaoh’s heart, that he hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh, however, hardened his heart and would not listen to them, just as the Lord had foretold. First Plague: Water Turned into Blood.
text.DRB: And Pharao's heart was indurate, and he heard them not, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Pharaoh’s heart is hardened; he refuses to let the people go.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Dull, is the heart of Pharaoh,—he hath refused to let the people go.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Pharaoh’s heart is hardened, he refuseth to let the people go.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Pharaoh is obstinate in refusing to let the people go.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Pharao's heart is aggravated, he will not dismiss the people.

text.NRSV: Go to Pharaoh in the morning, as he is going out to the water; stand by at the river bank to meet him, and take in your hand the staff that was turned into a snake.
text.Rotherham: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning—lo! he is coming out to the waters, therefore shalt thou station thyself to meet him on the bank of the river,—and the staff which was turned into a serpent, shalt thou take in thy hand.
text.KJV: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning; lo, he goeth out unto the water; and thou shalt stand by the river’s brink against he come; and the rod which was turned to a serpent shalt thou take in thine hand.
text.NABRE: In the morning, just when he sets out for the water, go to Pharaoh and present yourself by the bank of the Nile, holding in your hand the staff that turned into a snake.
text.DRB: Go to him in the morning, behold he will go forth to the waters: and thou shalt stand to meet him upon the bank of the river: and the rod that was turned into a dragon, thou shalt take in thy hand.

text.NRSV: Say to him, ‘The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, sent me to you to say, “Let my people go, so that they may worship me in the wilderness.” But until now you have not listened.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say unto him—Yahweh God of the Hebrews hath sent me unto thee saying,—Let my people go, that they may serve me in the desert,—And lo! thou hast not hearkened hitherto.
text.KJV: And thou shalt say unto him, The Lord God of the Hebrews hath sent me unto thee, saying, Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness: and, behold, hitherto thou wouldest not hear.
text.NABRE: Say to him: The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, sent me to you with the message: Let my people go to serve me in the wilderness. But as yet you have not listened.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to him: The Lord God of the Hebrews sent me to thee, saying: Dismiss my people to sacrifice unto me in the desert: and until this present thou wouldst not hear.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, “By this you shall know that I am the Lord.” See, with the staff that is in my hand I will strike the water that is in the Nile, and it shall be turned to blood.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, Hereby, shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh,—Lo! I, am smiting with the staff that is in my hand upon the waters that are in the river and they shall be turned to blood;
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, In this thou shalt know that I am the Lord: behold, I will smite with the rod that is in mine hand upon the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: This is how you will know that I am the Lord. With the staff here in my hand, I will strike the water in the Nile and it will be changed into blood.
text.DRB: This therefore saith our Lord: In this thou shalt know that I am the Lord: behold I will strike with the rod that is in my hand, the water of the river, and it shall be turned into blood.

text.NRSV: The fish in the river shall die, the river itself shall stink, and the Egyptians shall be unable to drink water from the Nile.’”
text.Rotherham: and, the fish that is in the river shall die and the river shall be loathsome,—and the Egyptians, shall disdain, to drink water out of the river.
text.KJV: And the fish that is in the river shall die, and the river shall stink; and the Egyptians shall lothe to drink of the water of the river.
text.NABRE: The fish in the Nile will die, and the Nile itself will stink so that the Egyptians will be unable to drink water from the Nile.
text.DRB: The fishes also, that are in the river, shall die, and the waters shall putrefy, and the Ægyptians shall be afflicted drinking the water of the river.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Take your staff and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt—over its rivers, its canals, and its ponds, and all its pools of water—so that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout the whole land of Egypt, even in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses,—Say unto Aaron—Take thy staff, and stretch forth thy hand over the waters of Egypt, over their streams over their rivers, and over their pools and over all their collections of water—that they may become blood,—so shall there be blood in all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and stretch out thine hand upon the waters of Egypt, upon their streams, upon their rivers, and upon their ponds, and upon all their pools of water, that they may become blood; and that there may be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood, and in vessels of stone.
text.NABRE: The Lord then spoke to Moses: Speak to Aaron: Take your staff and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt—its streams, its canals, its ponds, and all its supplies of water—that they may become blood. There will be blood throughout the land of Egypt, even in the wooden pails and stone jars.
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses: Say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and stretch forth thy hand upon the waters of Ægypt, and upon their floods, and rivers, and pools, and all the lakes of waters, that they may be turned into blood: and be there blood in all the Land of Ægypt, as well in the vessels of wood as of stone.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron did just as the Lord commanded. In the sight of Pharaoh and of his officials he lifted up the staff and struck the water in the river, and all the water in the river was turned into blood,
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Aaron, did so, as Yahweh commanded, and he lifted high the staff, and smote the waters which were in the river, before the eyes of Pharaoh, and before the eyes of his servants,—and all the waters which were in the river were turned to blood;
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron did so, as the Lord commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.
text.NABRE: This, then, is what Moses and Aaron did, exactly as the Lord had commanded. Aaron raised his staff and struck the waters in the Nile in full view of Pharaoh and his servants, and all the water in the Nile was changed into blood.
text.DRB: And Moyses and Aaron did as our Lord had commanded: and lifting up the rod he struck the water of the river before Pharao and his servants: which was turned into blood.

text.NRSV: and the fish in the river died. The river stank so that the Egyptians could not drink its water, and there was blood throughout the whole land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: and the fish that was in the river, died and the river became loathsome, so that the Egyptians could not drink water out of the river,—thus came it to pass that blood was in all the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And the fish that was in the river died; and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink of the water of the river; and there was blood throughout all the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: The fish in the Nile died, and the Nile itself stank so that the Egyptians could not drink water from it. There was blood throughout the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And the fishes that were in the river died, and the river putrefied, and the Ægyptians could not drink the water of the river, and there was blood in the whole land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: But the magicians of Egypt did the same by their secret arts; so Pharaoh’s heart remained hardened, and he would not listen to them; as the Lord had said.
text.Rotherham: And the sacred scribes of Egypt did in like manner with their secret arts,—so the heart of Pharaoh waxed bold and he hearkened not unto them, as spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the magicians of Egypt did so with their enchantments: and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, neither did he hearken unto them; as the Lord had said.
text.NABRE: But the Egyptian magicians did the same by their magic arts. So Pharaoh hardened his heart and would not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.
text.DRB: And the enchanters of the Ægyptians with their enchantments did in like manner and Pharao's heart was indurate, neither did he hear them, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he did not take even this to heart.
text.Rotherham: Then Pharaoh, turned away, and entered into his house—neither applied he his heart, even to this.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, neither did he set his heart to this also.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh turned away and went into his house, with no concern even for this.
text.DRB: And he turned away himself, and went into his house, neither did he yet set his heart to it this time also.

text.NRSV: And all the Egyptians had to dig along the Nile for water to drink, for they could not drink the water of the river.
text.Rotherham: And all the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink,—for they could not drink of the water of the river.
text.KJV: And all the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.
text.NABRE: All the Egyptians had to dig round about the Nile for drinking water, since they could not drink any water from the Nile.
text.DRB: And all the Ægyptians digged round about the river for water to drink: for they could not drink of the water of the river.

text.NRSV: Seven days passed after the Lord had struck the Nile.
text.Rotherham: And seven days were fulfilled,—after Yahweh had smitten the river.
text.KJV: And seven days were fulfilled, after that the Lord had smitten the river.
text.NABRE: Second Plague: The Frogs. Seven days passed after the Lord had struck the Nile.
text.DRB: And seven days were fully ended, after that our Lord struck the river.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go to Pharaoh and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: Let my people go, so that they may worship me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh,—then shalt thou say unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh,—Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, Go unto Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.NABRE: The Lord then spoke to Moses: Speak to Aaron: Stretch out your hand with your staff over the streams, the canals, and the ponds, and make frogs overrun the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses: Go in to Pharao, and thou shalt say unto him: This saith the Lord: Dismiss my people for to sacrifice unto me.

text.NRSV: If you refuse to let them go, I will plague your whole country with frogs.
text.Rotherham: But if thou art, refusing, to let them go, lo! I, am plaguing all thy boundaries with frogs;
text.KJV: And if thou refuse to let them go, behold, I will smite all thy borders with frogs:
text.NABRE: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: but if thou wilt not dismiss them, behold I will strike all thy coasts with frogs.

text.NRSV: The river shall swarm with frogs; they shall come up into your palace, into your bedchamber and your bed, and into the houses of your officials and of your people, and into your ovens and your kneading bowls.
text.Rotherham: so shall the river swarm with frogs, and they shall come up, and enter into thy house, and into thy bedchamber and upon thy couch,—and into the house of thy servants and among thy people, and into thine ovens and into thy kneading-troughs:
text.KJV: And the river shall bring forth frogs abundantly, which shall go up and come into thine house, and into thy bedchamber, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneadingtroughs:
text.NABRE: But the magicians did the same by their magic arts and made frogs overrun the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And the river shall bubble with frogs, which shall come up, and enter into thy house, and thy bed-chamber, and upon thy bed, and into the houses of thy servants, and unto thy people, and into thy ovens, and into the remains of thy meats:

text.NRSV: The frogs shall come up on you and on your people and on all your officials.’”
text.Rotherham: both against thee, and against thy people, and against all thy servants, shall come up the frogs.
text.KJV: And the frogs shall come up both on thee, and upon thy people, and upon all thy servants.
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “Pray to the Lord to remove the frogs from me and my people, and I will let the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
text.DRB: and unto thee, and to thy people, and to all thy servants shall the frogs enter.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Stretch out your hand with your staff over the rivers, the canals, and the pools, and make frogs come up on the land of Egypt.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Say unto Aaron—Stretch forth thy hand with thy staff, over the streams over the rivers and over the pools,—and bring up the frogs, over the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch forth thine hand with thy rod over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Moses answered Pharaoh, “Please designate for me the time when I am to pray for you and your servants and your people, to get rid of the frogs from you and your houses. They will be left only in the Nile.”
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Say unto Aaron, Stretch forth thy hand upon the floods, and upon the rivers and the pools, and bring forth frogs upon the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So Aaron stretched forth his hand, over the waters of Egypt,—and the frog came up, and covered the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: “Tomorrow,” he said. Then Moses replied, “It will be as you have said, so that you may know that there is none like the Lord, our God.
text.DRB: And Aaron stretched forth his hand upon the waters of Ægypt, and the frogs came up, and covered the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: But the magicians did the same by their secret arts, and brought frogs up on the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And the sacred scribes did in like manner, with their secret arts,—and brought up frogs over the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And the magicians did so with their enchantments, and brought up frogs upon the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: The frogs will leave you and your houses, your servants and your people; they will be left only in the Nile.”
text.DRB: And the enchanters also by their enchantments did in like manner, and they brought forth frogs upon the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron, and said, “Pray to the Lord to take away the frogs from me and my people, and I will let the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then called Pharaoh for Moses and for Aaron and said—Make entreaty unto Yahweh, that he take away the frogs from me and from my people,—and I must let the people go that they may sacrifice to Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, Intreat the Lord, that he may take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may do sacrifice unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: After Moses and Aaron left Pharaoh’s presence, Moses cried out to the Lord on account of the frogs that he had inflicted on Pharaoh;
text.DRB: And Pharao called Moyses and Aaron, and said to them: Pray ye to the Lord to take away the frogs from me & from my people and I will dismiss the people to sacrifice unto the Lord.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Pharaoh, “Kindly tell me when I am to pray for you and for your officials and for your people, that the frogs may be removed from you and your houses and be left only in the Nile.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said to Pharaoh—Explain thyself unto me: For what time, shall I make entreaty for thee and for thy servants and for thy people, to cause the frogs to be cut off, from thee and from thy houses,—only in the river, shall they remain?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Pharaoh, Glory over me: when shall I intreat for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, to destroy the frogs from thee and thy houses, that they may remain in the river only?
text.NABRE: and the Lord did as Moses had asked. The frogs died off in the houses, the courtyards, and the fields.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to Pharao: Appoint me when I shall pray for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, that the frogs may be driven away from thee and from thy house, and from thy servants, and from thy people, and may remain only in the river.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Tomorrow.” Moses said, “As you say! So that you may know that there is no one like the Lord our God,
text.Rotherham: And he said—For to-morrow. And he said—According to thy word! that thou mayest know that there is none like Yahweh, our God:
text.KJV: And he said, To morrow. And he said, Be it according to thy word: that thou mayest know that there is none like unto the Lord our God.
text.NABRE: Heaps of them were piled up, and the land stank.
text.DRB: Who answered: Tomorrow. But he said: According to thy word will I do; that thou mayst know that there is not the like to the Lord our God.

text.NRSV: the frogs shall leave you and your houses and your officials and your people; they shall be left only in the Nile.”
text.Rotherham: so the frogs shall depart—from thee and from thy houses and from thy servants and from thy people,—only in the river, shall they remain.
text.KJV: And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy servants, and from thy people; they shall remain in the river only.
text.NABRE: But when Pharaoh saw there was a respite, he became obstinate and would not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.
text.DRB: And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy house, and from thy servants, and from thy people: and shall remain only in the river.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh; and Moses cried out to the Lord concerning the frogs that he had brought upon Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth Moses and Aaron from Pharaoh,—and Moses made outcry unto Yahweh, over the matter of the frogs which he had appointed for Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh: and Moses cried unto the Lord because of the frogs which he had brought against Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: Third Plague: The Gnats. Thereupon the Lord spoke to Moses: Speak to Aaron: Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, and it will turn into gnats throughout the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And Moyses and Aaron went forth from Pharao: and Moyses cried to our Lord for the promise, concerning the frogs, which he had agreed to Pharao.

text.NRSV: And the Lord did as Moses requested: the frogs died in the houses, the courtyards, and the fields.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh did according to the word of Moses,—and the frogs died, out of the houses and out of the yards, and out of the fields;
text.KJV: And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the villages, and out of the fields.
text.NABRE: They did so. Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth, and gnats came upon human being and beast alike. All the dust of the earth turned into gnats throughout the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And our Lord did according to the word of Moyses: and the frogs died out of the houses, and out of the villages, and out of the fields:

text.NRSV: And they gathered them together in heaps, and the land stank.
text.Rotherham: and they piled them up, heaps—heaps,—and the land became loathsome.
text.KJV: And they gathered them together upon heaps: and the land stank.
text.NABRE: Though the magicians did the same thing to produce gnats by their magic arts, they could not do so. The gnats were on human being and beast alike,
text.DRB: and they gathered them together into huge heaps, and the earth did rot.

text.NRSV: But when Pharaoh saw that there was a respite, he hardened his heart, and would not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.
text.Rotherham: But when Pharaoh saw that there had come a respite, then made he his heart dull and hearkened not unto them,—as spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: But when Pharaoh saw that there was respite, he hardened his heart, and hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
text.NABRE: and the magicians said to Pharaoh, “This is the finger of God.” Yet Pharaoh hardened his heart and would not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.
text.DRB: And Pharao seeing that rest was given he hardened his own heart; and heard them not, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, so that it may become gnats throughout the whole land of Egypt.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Say unto Aaron—Stretch forth thy staff, and smite the dust of the land,—so shall it become gnats in all the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch out thy rod, and smite the dust of the land, that it may become lice throughout all the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Fourth Plague: The Flies. Then the Lord spoke to Moses: Early tomorrow morning present yourself to Pharaoh when he sets out toward the water, and say to him: Thus says the Lord: Let my people go to serve me.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Speak to Aaron: Stretch forth thy rod, and strike the dust of the earth: and be there sciniphs in the whole Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: And they did so; Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth, and gnats came on humans and animals alike; all the dust of the earth turned into gnats throughout the whole land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And they did so, and Aaron stretched forth his hand with his staff and smote the dust of the land, and there canto to be gnats, among men and among beasts,—all the dust of the land, became gnats, in all the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And they did so; for Aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and smote the dust of the earth, and it became lice in man, and in beast; all the dust of the land became lice throughout all the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: For if you do not let my people go, I will send swarms of flies upon you and your servants and your people and your houses. The houses of the Egyptians and the very ground on which they stand will be filled with swarms of flies.
text.DRB: And they did so. And Aaron stretched forth his hand, holding the rod: and he struck the dust of the earth, and there were made sciniphs on men and on beasts: all the dust of the earth was turned into sciniphs through the whole Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The magicians tried to produce gnats by their secret arts, but they could not. There were gnats on both humans and animals.
text.Rotherham: And the sacred scribes did in like manner with their secret arts, to bring forth the gnats, but they could not. So there came to be gnats, among men and among beasts.
text.KJV: And the magicians did so with their enchantments to bring forth lice, but they could not: so there were lice upon man, and upon beast.
text.NABRE: But on that day I will make an exception of the land of Goshen, where my people are, and no swarms of flies will be there, so that you may know that I the Lord am in the midst of the land.
text.DRB: And the enchanters with their enchantments practised in like manner to bring forth sciniphs, and they could not: and there were sciniphs as well on men as on beasts.

text.NRSV: And the magicians said to Pharaoh, “This is the finger of God!” But Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.
text.Rotherham: Then said the sacred scribes unto Pharaoh, The finger of God, it is! But the heart of Pharaoh waxed bold and he hearkened not unto them, as spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then the magicians said unto Pharaoh, This is the finger of God: and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
text.NABRE: I will make a distinction between my people and your people. This sign will take place tomorrow.
text.DRB: And the enchanters said to Pharao: This is the finger of God. And Pharao's heart was indurate, and he heard them not as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Rise early in the morning and present yourself before Pharaoh, as he goes out to the water, and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: Let my people go, so that they may worship me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Rise thou early in the morning, and station thyself before Pharaoh, lo! he is coming forth to the waters,—then shalt thou say unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh: Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh; lo, he cometh forth to the water; and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.NABRE: This the Lord did. Thick swarms of flies entered the house of Pharaoh and the houses of his servants; throughout Egypt the land was devastated on account of the swarms of flies.
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses: Arise early, and stand before Pharao: for he will go forth to the waters: & thou shalt say to him: This saith our Lord: Dismiss my people to sacrifice unto me.

text.NRSV: For if you will not let my people go, I will send swarms of flies on you, your officials, and your people, and into your houses; and the houses of the Egyptians shall be filled with swarms of flies; so also the land where they live.
text.Rotherham: But if thou art not letting my people go, behold me! sending forth—against thee, and against thy servants, and against thy people, and against thy houses the gad-fly,—and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of the gad-fly, moreover also the ground whereon, they are:
text.KJV: Else, if thou wilt not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies upon thee, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thy houses: and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground whereon they are.
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “Go sacrifice to your God within the land.”
text.DRB: And if thou wilt not dismiss them, behold I will send in upon thee & upon thy servants, & upon thy people, and upon thy houses all kind of flies: and the houses of Ægypt shall be filled with flies of diverse kinds, and the whole land wherein they shall be.

text.NRSV: But on that day I will set apart the land of Goshen, where my people live, so that no swarms of flies shall be there, that you may know that I the Lord am in this land.
text.Rotherham: then will I make to differ on that day, the land of Goshen wherein my people do dwell, so that there shall not be there a gad-fly! in order that thou mayest know, that I, Yahweh, am in the midst of the land;
text.KJV: And I will sever in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there; to the end thou mayest know that I am the Lord in the midst of the earth.
text.NABRE: But Moses replied, “It is not right to do so, for what we sacrifice to the Lord, our God, is abhorrent to the Egyptians. If we sacrifice what is abhorrent to the Egyptians before their very eyes, will they not stone us?
text.DRB: And I will make the Land of Gessen marvelous in that day, wherein my people is, so that flies shall not be there: & thou shalt know that I am the Lord in the midst of the earth.

text.NRSV: Thus I will make a distinction between my people and your people. This sign shall appear tomorrow.’”
text.Rotherham: so will I put a separation betwixt my people and thy people. By to-morrow, shall come to pass this sign.
text.KJV: And I will put a division between my people and thy people: to morrow shall this sign be.
text.NABRE: We must go a three days’ journey in the wilderness and sacrifice to the Lord, our God, as he commands us.”
text.DRB: And I will put a division between my people & thy people: tomorrow shall this sign be.

text.NRSV: The Lord did so, and great swarms of flies came into the house of Pharaoh and into his officials’ houses; in all of Egypt the land was ruined because of the flies.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh did so, and brought a grievous gad-fly unto the house of Pharaoh and the houses of his servants and in all the land of Egypt, and the land was laid waste by reason of the gad-fly.
text.KJV: And the Lord did so; and there came a grievous swarm of flies into the house of Pharaoh, and into his servants’ houses, and into all the land of Egypt: the land was corrupted by reason of the swarm of flies.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh said, “I will let you go to sacrifice to the Lord, your God, in the wilderness, provided that you do not go too far away. Pray for me.”
text.DRB: And our Lord did so. And there came a very grievous fly into the houses of Pharao & of his servants, & into all the Land of Ægypt: & the Land was corrupted by such kind of flies.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron, and said, “Go, sacrifice to your God within the land.”
text.Rotherham: Then did Pharaoh cry out—unto Moses and to Aaron,—and said-Go your way, sacrifice to your God, in the land.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh called for Moses and for Aaron, and said, Go ye, sacrifice to your God in the land.
text.NABRE: Moses answered, “As soon as I leave you I will pray to the Lord that the swarms of flies may depart tomorrow from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people. Pharaoh, however, must not act deceitfully again and refuse to let the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
text.DRB: And Pharao called Moyses & Aaron, and said to them. Go and sacrifice to your God in this land.

text.NRSV: But Moses said, “It would not be right to do so; for the sacrifices that we offer to the Lord our God are offensive to the Egyptians. If we offer in the sight of the Egyptians sacrifices that are offensive to them, will they not stone us?
text.Rotherham: And Moses said: Not meet, is it, to do thus, for, an abomination to the Egyptians, we should sacrifice to Yahweh our God. Lo! could we sacrifice that which is an abomination to the Egyptians before their eyes and they not stone us?
text.KJV: And Moses said, It is not meet so to do; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to the Lord our God: lo, shall we sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, and will they not stone us?
text.NABRE: When Moses left Pharaoh, he prayed to the Lord;
text.DRB: And Moyses said: It cannot so be done: for if we shall offer the abominations of the Ægyptians to the Lord our God, and we kill those things which the Ægyptians do worship before them, they will beat us down with stones.

text.NRSV: We must go a three days’ journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the Lord our God as he commands us.”
text.Rotherham: A journey of three days, must we go, in the desert,—then will we sacrifice to Yahweh our God, as, Yahweh, hath said unto us.
text.KJV: We will go three days’ journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to the Lord our God, as he shall command us.
text.NABRE: and the Lord did as Moses had asked, removing the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people. Not one remained.
text.DRB: We will go forth three days' journey into the wilderness: and we will sacrifice unto the Lord our God, as he hath commanded us.

text.NRSV: So Pharaoh said, “I will let you go to sacrifice to the Lord your God in the wilderness, provided you do not go very far away. Pray for me.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Pharaoh: I, will let you go—so shall ye sacrifice to Yahweh your God in the desert, only ye shall not go a, very long, journey,—make ye entreaty for me.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said, I will let you go, that ye may sacrifice to the Lord your God in the wilderness; only ye shall not go very far away: intreat for me.
text.NABRE: But once more Pharaoh became obstinate and would not let the people go.
text.DRB: And Pharao said: I will dismiss you to sacrifice to the Lord your God in the desert: but go no farther: pray for me.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said, “As soon as I leave you, I will pray to the Lord that the swarms of flies may depart tomorrow from Pharaoh, from his officials, and from his people; only do not let Pharaoh again deal falsely by not letting the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said: Lo! I, am going out from thee and will make entreaty unto Yahweh, so will he take away the gad-fly—from Pharaoh from his servants, and from his people tomorrow,—only let not Pharaoh again deceive, in not letting the people go, to sacrifice to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses said, Behold, I go out from thee, and I will intreat the Lord that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, to morrow: but let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.
text.DRB: And Moyses said: Being gone forth from thee, I will pray to our Lord: and the fly shall depart from Pharao, and from his servants, and from his people tomorrow: but deceive no more so, that thou wilt not dismiss the people to sacrifice unto our Lord.

text.NRSV: So Moses went out from Pharaoh and prayed to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So Moses went out from Pharaoh,—and made entreaty unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses went out from Pharaoh, and intreated the Lord.
text.DRB: And Moyses being gone forth from Pharao, prayed our Lord.

text.NRSV: And the Lord did as Moses asked: he removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his officials, and from his people; not one remained.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh did according to the word of Moses, and took away the gad-fly, from Pharaoh from his servants and from his people,—there remained not, one.
text.KJV: And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; and he removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; there remained not one.
text.DRB: Who did according to his word: and he took away the flies from Pharao, and from his servants, and from his people: there was left not so much as one.

text.NRSV: But Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and would not let the people go.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh made his heart dull, this time also,—and did not let the people go.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh hardened his heart at this time also, neither would he let the people go.
text.DRB: And Pharao's heart was hardened, so that neither this time would he dismiss the people.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go to Pharaoh, and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord, the God of the Hebrews: Let my people go, so that they may worship me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh,—then shalt thou say unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.KJV: Then the Lord said unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh, and tell him, Thus saith the Lord God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Go to Pharaoh and tell him: Thus says the Lord, the God of the Hebrews: Let my people go to serve me.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Go into Pharao, and speak to him: This saith our Lord, the God of the Hebrews: dismiss my people to sacrifice unto me.

text.NRSV: For if you refuse to let them go and still hold them,
text.Rotherham: But, if, refusing, thou art to let them go,—and still art holding them fast,
text.KJV: For if thou refuse to let them go, and wilt hold them still,
text.NABRE: For if you refuse to let them go and persist in holding them,
text.DRB: And if thou refuse, and holdest them,

text.NRSV: the hand of the Lord will strike with a deadly pestilence your livestock in the field: the horses, the donkeys, the camels, the herds, and the flocks.
text.Rotherham: lo! the hand of Yahweh, is coming on thy cattle which are in the field, on the horses, on the asses on the camels, on the herds and on the flocks,—a very grievous pestilence;
text.KJV: Behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thy cattle which is in the field, upon the horses, upon the asses, upon the camels, upon the oxen, and upon the sheep: there shall be a very grievous murrain.
text.NABRE: the hand of the Lord will strike your livestock in the field—your horses, donkeys, camels, herds and flocks—with a very severe pestilence.
text.DRB: behold my hand shall be upon thy fields: and upon thy horses, and asses, and camels, and oxen, and sheep, a very sore pestilence.

text.NRSV: But the Lord will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt, so that nothing shall die of all that belongs to the Israelites.’”
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh will make a difference between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of the Egyptians,—so that there shall not die from among all that pertaineth to the sons of Israel, a thing!
text.KJV: And the Lord shall sever between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt: and there shall nothing die of all that is the children’s of Israel.
text.NABRE: But the Lord will distinguish between the livestock of Israel and that of Egypt, so that nothing belonging to the Israelites will die.
text.DRB: And our Lord will make a marvel between the possessions of Israel and the possessions of the Ægyptians, that nothing at all perish of those things that pertain to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord set a time, saying, “Tomorrow the Lord will do this thing in the land.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appointed a set time—saying, To-morrow, will Yahweh do this thing, in the land.
text.KJV: And the Lord appointed a set time, saying, To morrow the Lord shall do this thing in the land.
text.NABRE: And the Lord set a definite time, saying: Tomorrow the Lord will do this in the land.
text.DRB: And our Lord hath appointed a time, saying: Tomorrow will our Lord do this thing in the land.

text.NRSV: And on the next day the Lord did so; all the livestock of the Egyptians died, but of the livestock of the Israelites not one died.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh did this thing, on the morrow, and all the cattle of the Egyptians died,—but of the cattle of the sons of Israel, died not one.
text.KJV: And the Lord did that thing on the morrow, and all the cattle of Egypt died: but of the cattle of the children of Israel died not one.
text.NABRE: And on the next day the Lord did it. All the livestock of the Egyptians died, but not one animal belonging to the Israelites died.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore did this thing the next day: and all the beasts of the Ægyptians died, but of the beasts of the children of Israel nothing at all perished.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh inquired and found that not one of the livestock of the Israelites was dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he would not let the people go.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh sent, and lo! there had not died of the cattle of Israel, so much as one. But Pharaoh’s heart became dull, and he did not let the people go.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh sent, and, behold, there was not one of the cattle of the Israelites dead. And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.
