text.NRSV: Then he summoned Moses and Aaron in the night, and said, “Rise up, go away from my people, both you and the Israelites! Go, worship the Lord, as you said.
text.Rotherham: So he called for Moses and for Aaron by night and said—Rise ye, go forth out of the midst of my people, both ye and the sons of Israel,—and go serve Yahweh according to your word;
text.KJV: And he called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, Rise up, and get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the Lord, as ye have said.
text.NABRE: Permission to Depart. During the night Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “Leave my people at once, you and the Israelites! Go and serve the Lord as you said.
text.DRB: And Pharao calling Moyses & Aaron in the night, said: Arise & go forth from my people, you and the children of Israel: go, sacrifice to the Lord as you say.

text.NRSV: Take your flocks and your herds, as you said, and be gone. And bring a blessing on me too!”
text.Rotherham: also, your flocks and your herds, take ye, as ye spake and go your way,—so shall ye bless, even me.
text.KJV: Also take your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.
text.NABRE: Take your flocks, too, and your herds, as you said, and go; and bless me, too!”
text.DRB: Your sheep & herds take you as you demanded, and departing bless me.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians urged the people to hasten their departure from the land, for they said, “We shall all be dead.”
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians became urgent with the people, to send them in haste out of the land,—for they said—We all, are dying.
text.KJV: And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, that they might send them out of the land in haste; for they said, We be all dead men.
text.NABRE: The Egyptians, in a hurry to send them away from the land, urged the people on, for they said, “All of us will die!”
text.DRB: And the Ægyptians urged the people to go forth out of the land quickly, saying: We shall all die.

text.NRSV: So the people took their dough before it was leavened, with their kneading bowls wrapped up in their cloaks on their shoulders.
text.Rotherham: So the people took up their dough, ere yet it was leavened,—with their kneading-bowls, bound up in their mantles on their shoulders,
text.KJV: And the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneadingtroughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
text.NABRE: The people, therefore, took their dough before it was leavened, in their kneading bowls wrapped in their cloaks on their shoulders.
text.DRB: The people therefore took dough before it was leavened: and tying it in their cloaks, put it upon their shoulders.

text.NRSV: The Israelites had done as Moses told them; they had asked the Egyptians for jewelry of silver and gold, and for clothing,
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel, did according to the word of Moses,—and asked of the Egyptians, articles of silver and articles of gold and mantles.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment:
text.NABRE: And the Israelites did as Moses had commanded: they asked the Egyptians for articles of silver and gold and for clothing.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did as Moyses had commanded: & they asked of the Ægyptians vessels of silver and gold, and very much raiment.

text.NRSV: and the Lord had given the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. And so they plundered the Egyptians.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, gave the people favour in the eyes of the Egyptians and they gave them gladly,—so they spoiled the Egyptians.
text.KJV: And the Lord gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they lent unto them such things as they required. And they spoiled the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: Indeed the Lord had made the Egyptians so well-disposed toward the people that they let them have whatever they asked for. And so they despoiled the Egyptians.
text.DRB: And our Lord gave grace to the people before the Ægyptians that they did lend them: & they spoiled the Ægyptians.

text.NRSV: The Israelites journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides children.
text.Rotherham: Then did the sons of Israel break up from Rameses, towards Succoth,—about six hundred thousand foot of men grown, besides little ones.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot that were men, beside children.
text.NABRE: Departure from Egypt. The Israelites set out from Rameses for Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, not counting the children.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel set forward from Ramesse into Socoth, almost six hundred thousand of foot men, beside little ones.

text.NRSV: A mixed crowd also went up with them, and livestock in great numbers, both flocks and herds.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, a mixed multitude, came up with them,—flocks also, and herds, exceeding much cattle,
text.KJV: And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.
text.NABRE: A crowd of mixed ancestry also went up with them, with livestock in great abundance, both flocks and herds.
text.DRB: But also the common people of all sorts innumerable went up with them, sheep and herds and beasts of diverse kinds exceeding many.

text.NRSV: They baked unleavened cakes of the dough that they had brought out of Egypt; it was not leavened, because they were driven out of Egypt and could not wait, nor had they prepared any provisions for themselves.
text.Rotherham: Then baked they the dough which they had brought out of Egypt—into round unleavened cakes for it had not been leavened,—for they had been driven out of Egypt and could not tarry, and indeed no provisions, had they made ready for themselves.
text.KJV: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.
text.NABRE: The dough they had brought out of Egypt they baked into unleavened loaves. It was not leavened, because they had been driven out of Egypt and could not wait. They did not even prepare food for the journey.
text.DRB: And they baked the meal, which a little before they had taken out of Ægypt tempered: and made hearth cakes unleavened: for it could not be leavened, the Ægyptians urging them to depart, & not suffering them to make any tarriance: neither did they think upon preparing any meat.

text.NRSV: The time that the Israelites had lived in Egypt was four hundred thirty years.
text.Rotherham: Now, the time during which the sons of Israel dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years.
text.KJV: Now the sojourning of the children of Israel, who dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years.
text.NABRE: The time the Israelites had stayed in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
text.DRB: And the dwelling of the children of Israel that they abode in Ægypt, was four hundred thirty years.

text.NRSV: At the end of four hundred thirty years, on that very day, all the companies of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at the end of four hundred and thirty years, yea it came to pass, on this self-same day, that all the hosts of Yahweh had come forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the hosts of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: At the end of four hundred and thirty years, on this very date, all the armies of the Lord left the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: The which being expired, the same day all the army of our Lord went forth out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: That was for the Lord a night of vigil, to bring them out of the land of Egypt. That same night is a vigil to be kept for the Lord by all the Israelites throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: A night of solemn observances, it is unto Yahweh, for bringing them forth out of the land of Egypt,—this same night, pertaineth to Yahweh, for solemn observances by all the sons of Israel to their generations.
text.KJV: It is a night to be much observed unto the Lord for bringing them out from the land of Egypt: this is that night of the Lord to be observed of all the children of Israel in their generations.
text.NABRE: This was a night of vigil for the Lord, when he brought them out of the land of Egypt; so on this night all Israelites must keep a vigil for the Lord throughout their generations.
text.DRB: This is the observable night of our Lord, when he brought them forth out of the Land of Ægypt: this night all the children of Israel must observe in their generations.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron: This is the ordinance for the passover: no foreigner shall eat of it,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses and Aaron: This, is the statute of the passover,—No stranger, shall eat thereof;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses and Aaron, This is the ordinance of the passover: There shall no stranger eat thereof:
text.NABRE: Law of the Passover. The Lord said to Moses and Aaron: This is the Passover statute. No foreigner may eat of it.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses and Aaron: This is the religion of the Phase: No alien shall eat of it.

text.NRSV: but any slave who has been purchased may eat of it after he has been circumcised;
text.Rotherham: but, every man’s servant bought with silver,—when thou hast circumcised him, then, shall he eat thereof:
text.KJV: But every man’s servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
text.NABRE: However, every slave bought for money you will circumcise; then he may eat of it.
text.DRB: And every bought servant shall be circumcised, and so shall eat.

text.NRSV: no bound or hired servant may eat of it.
text.Rotherham: An alien or a hireling, shall not eat thereof.
text.KJV: A foreigner and an hired servant shall not eat thereof.
text.NABRE: But no tenant or hired worker may eat of it.
text.DRB: The stranger and the hireling shall not eat thereof.

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten in one house; you shall not take any of the animal outside the house, and you shall not break any of its bones.
text.Rotherham: In one house, shall it be eaten, thou shalt not take forth out of the house any of the flesh outside; and a, bone thereof, shalt thou not break.
text.KJV: In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth ought of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
text.NABRE: It must be eaten in one house; you may not take any of its meat outside the house. You shall not break any of its bones.
text.DRB: In one house shall it be eaten, neither shall you carry forth of the flesh thereof out of the house, neither shall you break a bone thereof.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation of Israel shall celebrate it.
text.Rotherham: All the assembly of Israel, shall keep it.
text.KJV: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
text.NABRE: The whole community of Israel must celebrate this feast.
text.DRB: All the assembly of the children of Israel shall make it.

text.NRSV: If an alien who resides with you wants to celebrate the passover to the Lord, all his males shall be circumcised; then he may draw near to celebrate it; he shall be regarded as a native of the land. But no uncircumcised person shall eat of it;
text.Rotherham: And when there may sojourn with thee a sojourner who may wish to keep a passover to Yahweh, there must be circumcised to him every male and then, may he draw near to keep it, so shall he be as a native of the land,—but no uncircumcised male, shall eat thereof.
text.KJV: And when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the Lord, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land: for no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
text.NABRE: If any alien residing among you would celebrate the Passover for the Lord, all his males must be circumcised, and then he may join in its celebration just like the natives. But no one who is uncircumcised may eat of it.
text.DRB: And if any of the sojourners be willing to dwell among you, and make the Phase of the Lord, first all the male that he hath shall be circumcised, and then shall he celebrate it according to the rite: & he shall be as he that is born in the land: but if there be any man uncircumcised, he shall not eat thereof.

text.NRSV: there shall be one law for the native and for the alien who resides among you.
text.Rotherham: One law, shall there be for the native, and for the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst.
text.KJV: One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.
text.NABRE: There will be one law for the native and for the alien residing among you.
text.DRB: All one law shall be to him that is born in the land and to the Proselyte that sojourneth with you.

text.NRSV: All the Israelites did just as the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron.
text.Rotherham: Thus did all the sons of Israel,—as Yahweh had commanded Moses and Aaron, so, did they.
text.KJV: Thus did all the children of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
text.NABRE: All the Israelites did exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron.
text.DRB: And all the children of Israel did as our Lord had commanded Moyses and Aaron.

text.NRSV: That very day the Lord brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt, company by company.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on this self-same day, that Yahweh brought forth the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, by their hosts.
text.KJV: And it came to pass the selfsame day, that the Lord did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies.
text.NABRE: On that same day the Lord brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt company by company.
text.DRB: And the same day our Lord brought forth the children of Israel out of the Land of Ægypt by their troops.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses and said:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Consecrate to me all the firstborn; whatever is the first to open the womb among the Israelites, of human beings and animals, is mine.
text.Rotherham: Hallow to me every firstborn, that which any female first beareth among the sons of Israel, among men and among beasts,—mine! it is.
text.KJV: Sanctify unto me all the firstborn, whatsoever openeth the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast: it is mine.
text.NABRE: Consecrate to me every firstborn; whatever opens the womb among the Israelites, whether of human being or beast, belongs to me.
text.DRB: Sanctify unto me every first-born that openeth the matrice in the children of Israel, as well of men as of beasts; for they are all mine.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the people, “Remember this day on which you came out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery, because the Lord brought you out from there by strength of hand; no leavened bread shall be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the people—[Ye are] to remember this day, on which ye came forth out of Egypt out of the house of slaves, that with strength of hand, did Yahweh bring you forth from hence,—there shall not, therefore be eaten anything leavened.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the people, Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the Lord brought you out from this place: there shall no leavened bread be eaten.
text.NABRE: Moses said to the people, “Remember this day on which you came out of Egypt, out of a house of slavery. For it was with a strong hand that the Lord brought you out from there. Nothing made with leaven may be eaten.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to the people: Remember this day in the which you went forth out of Ægypt, & out of the house of servitude, because with a strong hand hath our Lord brought you forth out of this place; that you eat not leavened bread.

text.NRSV: Today, in the month of Abib, you are going out.
text.Rotherham: To-day, are, ye, coming forth,—in the month Abib.
text.KJV: This day came ye out in the month Abib.
text.NABRE: This day on which you are going out is in the month of Abib.
text.DRB: This day you go forth in the month of new corn.

text.NRSV: When the Lord brings you into the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he swore to your ancestors to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, you shall keep this observance in this month.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when Yahweh shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanite and the Hittite and the Amorite, and the Hivite and the Jebusite, which he sware to thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, then shalt thou serve with this service, in this month.
text.KJV: And it shall be when the Lord shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he sware unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.
text.NABRE: Therefore, when the Lord, your God, has brought you into the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perrizites, the Girgashites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he swore to your ancestors to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, you will perform the following service in this month.
text.DRB: And when our Lord shall have brought thee into the Land of Chananeite and Hetheite & Amorrheite & Heveite & Jebuseite, which he sware to thy fathers that he would give thee, a land that floweth with milk and honey, thou shalt celebrate this manner of sacred rites in this month.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a festival to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shalt thou eat unleavened cakes,—and on the seventh day, [shall be] a festival to Yahweh.
text.KJV: Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the Lord.
text.NABRE: For seven days you will eat unleavened bread, and the seventh day will also be a festival to the Lord.
text.DRB: Seven days shalt thou eat azymes: and in the seventh day shall be the solemnity of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Unleavened bread shall be eaten for seven days; no leavened bread shall be seen in your possession, and no leaven shall be seen among you in all your territory.
text.Rotherham: Unleavened cakes, shall be eaten for the seven days,—neither shall there be seen with thee anything leavened, nor shall there be seen with thee leaven, within any of thy bounds,
text.KJV: Unleavened bread shall be eaten seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee in all thy quarters.
text.NABRE: Unleavened bread may be eaten during the seven days, but nothing leavened and no leaven may be found in your possession in all your territory.
text.DRB: Azymes shall you eat seven days: there shall not be seen any leavened thing with thee, nor in all thy coasts.

text.NRSV: You shall tell your child on that day, ‘It is because of what the Lord did for me when I came out of Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt tell thy son, on that day, saying,—[This is] because, of that which Yahweh did for me, when he brought me forth out of Egypt.
text.KJV: And thou shalt shew thy son in that day, saying, This is done because of that which the Lord did unto me when I came forth out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: And on that day you will explain to your son, ‘This is because of what the Lord did for me when I came out of Egypt.’
text.DRB: And thou shalt tell thy son in that day, saying: This is that which our Lord did to me when I came forth out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: It shall serve for you as a sign on your hand and as a reminder on your forehead, so that the teaching of the Lord may be on your lips; for with a strong hand the Lord brought you out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be to thee for a sign upon thy hand and for a memorial between thine eyes, in order that the law of Yahweh may be in thy mouth,—for with a strong hand, did Yahweh bring thee forth out of Egypt.
text.KJV: And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thine hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the Lord’s law may be in thy mouth: for with a strong hand hath the Lord brought thee out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: It will be like a sign on your hand and a reminder on your forehead, so that the teaching of the Lord will be on your lips: with a strong hand the Lord brought you out of Egypt.
text.DRB: And it shall be as a sign in thy hand, and as a monument before thine eyes: and that the law of our Lord be always in the mouth, for in a strong hand our Lord hath brought thee out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: You shall keep this ordinance at its proper time from year to year.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou observe this statute in its appointed time,—from year to year.
text.KJV: Thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in his season from year to year.
text.NABRE: You will keep this statute at its appointed time from year to year.
text.DRB: Thou shalt keep this observation at the set time from days to days.

text.NRSV: “When the Lord has brought you into the land of the Canaanites, as he swore to you and your ancestors, and has given it to you,
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when Yahweh shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanite, as he sware to thee and to thy fathers,—and shalt give it thee,
text.KJV: And it shall be when the Lord shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, as he sware unto thee and to thy fathers, and shall give it thee,
text.NABRE: “When the Lord, your God, has brought you into the land of the Canaanites, just as he swore to you and your ancestors, and gives it to you,
text.DRB: And when our Lord shall have brought thee into the Land of the Chananeite, as he sware to thee and thy fathers, and shall give it thee,

text.NRSV: you shall set apart to the Lord all that first opens the womb. All the firstborn of your livestock that are males shall be the Lord’s.
text.Rotherham: that thou shalt set apart whatsoever is born first, to Yahweh; and, of all firstlings of beasts which thou shalt have, the males, shall pertain to Yahweh.
text.KJV: That thou shalt set apart unto the Lord all that openeth the matrix, and every firstling that cometh of a beast which thou hast; the males shall be the Lord’s.
text.NABRE: you will dedicate to the Lord every newborn that opens the womb; and every firstborn male of your animals will belong to the Lord.
text.DRB: thou shalt separate all that openeth the matrice unto our Lord, and all that is brought forth in thy cattle: whatsoever thou shalt have of male sex thou shalt consecrate to our Lord.

text.NRSV: But every firstborn donkey you shall redeem with a sheep; if you do not redeem it, you must break its neck. Every firstborn male among your children you shall redeem.
text.Rotherham: But every firstling of an ass, shalt thou redeem with a lamb or if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break its neck,—but every firstborn of men among thy sons, shalt thou redeem.
text.KJV: And every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break his neck: and all the firstborn of man among thy children shalt thou redeem.
text.NABRE: Every firstborn of a donkey you will ransom with a sheep. If you do not ransom it, you will break its neck. Every human firstborn of your sons you must ransom.
text.DRB: The first-born of an ass thou shall change for a sheep: and if thou do not redeem it, thou shalt kill it. And every first-born of men among thy children, thou shalt redeem with a price.

text.NRSV: When in the future your child asks you, ‘What does this mean?’ you shall answer, ‘By strength of hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt, from the house of slavery.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when thy son shall ask thee in time to come, saying, What is this? Then shalt thou say unto him: With strength of hand, did Yahweh bring us forth out of Egypt out of the house of slaves.
text.KJV: And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What is this? that thou shalt say unto him, By strength of hand the Lord brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage:
text.NABRE: And when your son asks you later on, ‘What does this mean?’ you will tell him, ‘With a strong hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt, out of a house of slavery.
text.DRB: And when thy son shall ask thee tomorrow saying: What is this? Thou shalt answer him: With a strong hand did our Lord bring us forth out of the land of Ægypt, out of the house of servitude.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from human firstborn to the firstborn of animals. Therefore I sacrifice to the Lord every male that first opens the womb, but every firstborn of my sons I redeem.’
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Pharaoh had shown himself too hardened to let us go, then did Yahweh slay every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of men even unto the firstborn of beasts. For this cause, am, I, sacrificing to Yahweh whatsoever is born first, of the males, and every firstborn of my sons, must I redeem.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh would hardly let us go, that the Lord slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man, and the firstborn of beast: therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all that openeth the matrix, being males; but all the firstborn of my children I redeem.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord killed every firstborn in the land of Egypt, the firstborn of human being and beast alike. That is why I sacrifice to the Lord every male that opens the womb, and why I ransom every firstborn of my sons.’
text.DRB: For when Pharao's heart was indurate, and would not dismiss us, our Lord slew every first-born in the Land of Ægypt, from the first-born of man to the first-born of beasts: therefore I sacrifice to our Lord all that openeth the matrice of the male sex, and all the first-born of my sons I do redeem.

text.NRSV: It shall serve as a sign on your hand and as an emblem on your forehead that by strength of hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall be for a sign upon thy hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes,—that, with strength of hand, did Yahweh bring us forth out of Egypt.
text.KJV: And it shall be for a token upon thine hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes: for by strength of hand the Lord brought us forth out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: It will be like a sign on your hand and a band on your forehead that with a strong hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt.”
text.DRB: It shall be therefore as a sign in thy hand, and as a thing hanged before thine eyes, for a remembrance: because our Lord by a strong hand hath brought us forth out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was nearer; for God thought, “If the people face war, they may change their minds and return to Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God did not lead by the land-way of the Philistines, although it was near,—for God said—Lest the people be dismayed when they see war and turn back to Egypt.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God led them not through the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said, Lest peradventure the people repent when they see war, and they return to Egypt:
text.NABRE: Now, when Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the Philistines’ land, though this was the nearest; for God said: If the people see that they have to fight, they might change their minds and return to Egypt.
text.DRB: Therefore when Pharao had sent forth the people, our Lord led them not by the way of the Philisthiims' country which is near: thinking lest perhaps it would repent them, if they should see wars arise against them, and would return into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: So God led the people by the roundabout way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea. The Israelites went up out of the land of Egypt prepared for battle.
text.Rotherham: So God took the people round the desert-way of the Red Sea,—and the sons of Israel went up armed, out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: But God led the people about, through the way of the wilderness of the Red sea: and the children of Israel went up harnessed out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Instead, God rerouted them toward the Red Sea by way of the wilderness road, and the Israelites went up out of the land of Egypt arrayed for battle.
text.DRB: But he led them about by the way of the desert, which is besides the Red sea: and the children of Israel went up out of the Land of Ægypt armed.

text.NRSV: And Moses took with him the bones of Joseph who had required a solemn oath of the Israelites, saying, “God will surely take notice of you, and then you must carry my bones with you from here.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the bones of Joseph, with him,—for he had, taken an oath, of the sons of Israel saying, God will, surely visit, you, so then ye shall carry up my bones from hence with you.
text.KJV: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him: for he had straitly sworn the children of Israel, saying, God will surely visit you; and ye shall carry up my bones away hence with you.
text.NABRE: Moses also took Joseph’s bones with him, for Joseph had made the Israelites take a solemn oath, saying, “God will surely take care of you, and you must bring my bones up with you from here.”
text.DRB: Moyses also took Joseph's bones with him: because he had adjured the children of Israel, saying: God shall visit you, carry out my bones from hence with you.

text.NRSV: They set out from Succoth, and camped at Etham, on the edge of the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Succoth,—and encamped in Etham, at the edge of the desert.
text.KJV: And they took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, in the edge of the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Succoth, they camped at Etham near the edge of the wilderness.
text.DRB: And marching from Socoth they camped in Etham in the utmost coasts of the wilderness.

text.NRSV: The Lord went in front of them in a pillar of cloud by day, to lead them along the way, and in a pillar of fire by night, to give them light, so that they might travel by day and by night.
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, was going before them—by day, in a pillar of cloud to lead them the way, and by night, in a pillar of fire to give them light,—that they might journey day and night:
text.KJV: And the Lord went before them by day in a pillar of a cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; to go by day and night:
text.NABRE: The Lord preceded them, in the daytime by means of a column of cloud to show them the way, and at night by means of a column of fire to give them light. Thus they could travel both day and night.
text.DRB: And our Lord went before them to shew the way by day in a pillar of a cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire: that he might be the guide of their journey both times.

text.NRSV: Neither the pillar of cloud by day nor the pillar of fire by night left its place in front of the people.
text.Rotherham: the pillar of cloud by day ceased not, nor the pillar of fire by night,—from before the people.
text.KJV: He took not away the pillar of the cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, from before the people.
text.NABRE: Neither the column of cloud by day nor the column of fire by night ever left its place in front of the people.
text.DRB: There never failed the pillar of the cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, before the people.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: “Tell the Israelites to turn back and camp in front of Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, in front of Baal-zephon; you shall camp opposite it, by the sea.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, that they turn back and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea,—before Baal-zephon, over against it, shall ye encamp, by the sea.
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi–hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, over against Baal–zephon: before it shall ye encamp by the sea.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites: Let them turn about and camp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea. Camp in front of Baal-zephon, just opposite, by the sea.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: Let them return and camp over against Phihahiroth which is between Magdal and the sea against Beelsephon: in the sight thereof you shall camp upon the sea.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh will say of the Israelites, ‘They are wandering aimlessly in the land; the wilderness has closed in on them.’
text.Rotherham: Then will Pharaoh say, of the sons of Israel, Entangled, they are in the land,—the desert hath closed in upon them.
text.KJV: For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, They are entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh will then say, “The Israelites are wandering about aimlessly in the land. The wilderness has closed in on them.”
text.DRB: And Pharao will say concerning the children of Israel: They are straightened in the land, the desert hath shut them in.

text.NRSV: I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and he will pursue them, so that I will gain glory for myself over Pharaoh and all his army; and the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord.” And they did so.
text.Rotherham: So will I let the heart of Pharaoh wax bold, and he will pursue them, that I may get me honour over Pharaoh, and over all his forces, and the Egyptians shall know, that I, am Yahweh. And they did so.
text.KJV: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, that he shall follow after them; and I will be honoured upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host; that the Egyptians may know that I am the Lord. And they did so.
text.NABRE: I will so harden Pharaoh’s heart that he will pursue them. Thus I will receive glory through Pharaoh and all his army, and the Egyptians will know that I am the Lord. This the Israelites did.
text.DRB: And I will indurate his heart, and he will pursue you: and I will be glorified in Pharao, and in all his army: and the Ægyptians shall know that I am the Lord. And they did so.

text.NRSV: When the king of Egypt was told that the people had fled, the minds of Pharaoh and his officials were changed toward the people, and they said, “What have we done, letting Israel leave our service?”
text.Rotherham: And it was told the king of Egypt, that the people had fled,—and the heart of Pharaoh and his servants was turned against the people, and they said—What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?
text.KJV: And it was told the king of Egypt that the people fled: and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was turned against the people, and they said, Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from serving us?
text.NABRE: When it was reported to the king of Egypt that the people had fled, Pharaoh and his servants had a change of heart about the people. “What in the world have we done!” they said. “We have released Israel from our service!”
text.DRB: And it was told the king of Ægyptians that the people was fled: and the heart of Pharao and of his servants was changed toward the people, & they said: What meant we to do, that we dismissed Israel from serving us?

text.NRSV: So he had his chariot made ready, and took his army with him;
text.Rotherham: So he yoked his chariot,—and his people, took he with him.
text.KJV: And he made ready his chariot, and took his people with him:
text.NABRE: So Pharaoh harnessed his chariots and took his army with him.
text.DRB: Therefore he made ready his chariot, and took all his people with him.

text.NRSV: he took six hundred picked chariots and all the other chariots of Egypt with officers over all of them.
text.Rotherham: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt,—with warriors in every one.
text.KJV: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over every one of them.
text.NABRE: He took six hundred select chariots and all the chariots of Egypt, with officers on all of them.
text.DRB: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots that were in Ægypt, and Captains of the whole army.

text.NRSV: The Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt and he pursued the Israelites, who were going out boldly.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh let the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt wax bold, and he pursued the sons of Israel,—when the sons of Israel, were going out with a high hand.
text.KJV: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel: and the children of Israel went out with an high hand.
text.NABRE: The Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, so that he pursued the Israelites while they were going out in triumph.
text.DRB: And our Lord hardened Pharao's heart the king of Ægypt, & he pursued the children of Israel: but they went forth in a mighty hand.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians pursued them, all Pharaoh’s horses and chariots, his chariot drivers and his army; they overtook them camped by the sea, by Pi-hahiroth, in front of Baal-zephon.
text.Rotherham: So the Egyptians pursued them and overtook them, encamping by the sea—all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his forces,—by Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
text.KJV: But the Egyptians pursued after them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pi–hahiroth, before Baal–zephon.
text.NABRE: The Egyptians pursued them—all Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, his horsemen, and his army—and caught up with them as they lay encamped by the sea, at Pi-hahiroth, in front of Baal-zephon.
text.DRB: And when the Ægyptians pursued their steps going before, they found them encamped at the sea side: all Pharao's horse and chariots, & the whole army were in Phihahiroth against Beelsephon.

text.NRSV: As Pharaoh drew near, the Israelites looked back, and there were the Egyptians advancing on them. In great fear the Israelites cried out to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now when, Pharaoh, had drawn near, the sons of Israel lifted up their eyes, and lo! the Egyptians, moving along after them and they feared greatly, and the sons of Israel made outcry unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and they were sore afraid: and the children of Israel cried out unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Crossing the Red Sea. Now Pharaoh was near when the Israelites looked up and saw that the Egyptians had set out after them. Greatly frightened, the Israelites cried out to the Lord.
text.DRB: And when Pharao approached, the children of Israel lifting up their eyes, saw the Ægyptians behind them: and they feared exceedingly, and cried to our Lord.

text.NRSV: They said to Moses, “Was it because there were no graves in Egypt that you have taken us away to die in the wilderness? What have you done to us, bringing us out of Egypt?
text.Rotherham: Then said they unto Moses, Was it because there were no graves in Egypt, that thou didst fetch us to die in the desert? What is this thou hast done to us, bringing us forth out of Egypt?
text.KJV: And they said unto Moses, Because there were no graves in Egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to carry us forth out of Egypt?
text.NABRE: To Moses they said, “Were there no burial places in Egypt that you brought us to die in the wilderness? What have you done to us, bringing us out of Egypt?
text.DRB: and said to Moyses: Perhaps there were no graves in Ægypt, therefore thou hast taken us thence to die in the wilderness: why wouldst thou do this, in bringing us out of Ægypt?

text.NRSV: Is this not the very thing we told you in Egypt, ‘Let us alone and let us serve the Egyptians’? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”
text.Rotherham: Is not this the word which we spake unto thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians,—for it is better for us, to serve the Egyptians, than to die in the desert?
text.KJV: Is not this the word that we did tell thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For it had been better for us to serve the Egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Did we not tell you this in Egypt, when we said, ‘Leave us alone that we may serve the Egyptians’? Far better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”
text.DRB: Is not this the word that we spake to thee in Ægypt, saying: Depart from us, that we may serve the Ægyptians? For it was much better to serve them, than to die in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the people, “Do not be afraid, stand firm, and see the deliverance that the Lord will accomplish for you today; for the Egyptians whom you see today you shall never see again.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the people—Do not fear, stand still and see the salvation of Yahweh, which he will work for you to-day,—for as for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to-day, ye shall not again see them any more to times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the people, Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will shew to you to day: for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.
text.NABRE: But Moses answered the people, “Do not fear! Stand your ground and see the victory the Lord will win for you today. For these Egyptians whom you see today you will never see again.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to the people: Fear not; stand, and see the great wonders of our Lord that he will do this day: for the Ægyptians, whom now you see, you shall no more see for ever.

text.NRSV: The Lord will fight for you, and you have only to keep still.”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will fight for you,—and, ye, shall forbear.
text.KJV: The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
text.NABRE: The Lord will fight for you; you have only to keep still.”
text.DRB: Our Lord will fight for you and you shall hold your peace.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Why do you cry out to me? Tell the Israelites to go forward.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, What outcry wouldst thou make unto me? Speak unto the sons of Israel, that they go forward;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward:
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Why are you crying out to me? Tell the Israelites to set out.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Why cryest thou to me? Speak to the children of Israel that they go forward.

text.NRSV: But you lift up your staff, and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, that the Israelites may go into the sea on dry ground.
text.Rotherham: and, thou, lift high thy staff and stretch forth thy hand over the sea and cleave it asunder,—that the sons of Israel may enter into the midst of the sea on dry ground;
text.KJV: But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea.
text.NABRE: And you, lift up your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea, and split it in two, that the Israelites may pass through the sea on dry land.
text.DRB: But thou lift up thy rod, and stretch forth thy hand upon the sea, and divide it: that the children of Israel may go in the midst of the sea by dry ground.

text.NRSV: Then I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they will go in after them; and so I will gain glory for myself over Pharaoh and all his army, his chariots, and his chariot drivers.
text.Rotherham: and, I, behold me letting the heart of the Egyptians wax bold, that they may enter after them,—that I may get me honour over Pharaoh and over his forces, over his chariots, and over his horsemen,
text.KJV: And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall follow them: and I will get me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
text.NABRE: But I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they will go in after them, and I will receive glory through Pharaoh and all his army, his chariots and his horsemen.
text.DRB: And I will indurate the heart of the Ægyptians to pursue you: and I will be glorified in Pharao, and in all his host, and in his chariots, and in his horsemen.

text.NRSV: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I have gained glory for myself over Pharaoh, his chariots, and his chariot drivers.”
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians shall know, that, I, am Yahweh,—when I have gotten me honour over Pharaoh, over his chariots and over his horsemen.
text.KJV: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I have gotten me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
text.NABRE: The Egyptians will know that I am the Lord, when I receive glory through Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.
text.DRB: And the Ægyptians shall know that I am the Lord when I shall be glorified in Pharao, and in his chariots and in his horsemen.

text.NRSV: The angel of God who was going before the Israelite army moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud moved from in front of them and took its place behind them.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of God who was going on before the camp of Israel removed, and came on behind them,—and the pillar of cloud removed from before them, and stood behind them;
text.KJV: And the angel of God, which went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of the cloud went from before their face, and stood behind them:
text.NABRE: The angel of God, who had been leading Israel’s army, now moved and went around behind them. And the column of cloud, moving from in front of them, took up its place behind them,
text.DRB: And the Angel of God that went before the camp of Israel, removing himself, went behind them: & together with him the pillar of the cloud, leaving the forward,

text.NRSV: It came between the army of Egypt and the army of Israel. And so the cloud was there with the darkness, and it lit up the night; one did not come near the other all night.
text.Rotherham: so it came in between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel, so it became a cloud and darkness, and yet lighted up the night,—so that the one came not near unto the other, all the night.
text.KJV: And it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel; and it was a cloud and darkness to them, but it gave light by night to these: so that the one came not near the other all the night.
text.NABRE: so that it came between the Egyptian army and that of Israel. And when it became dark, the cloud illumined the night; and so the rival camps did not come any closer together all night long.
text.DRB: stood behind, between the Ægyptians' camp & the camp of Israel: & it was a dark cloud, & lightening the night, so that they could not come to each other the whole night time.

text.NRSV: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea. The Lord drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night, and turned the sea into dry land; and the waters were divided.
text.Rotherham: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and Yahweh carried away the sea by a mighty east wind all the night, and turned the sea into dry land,—and the waters were cloven asunder.
text.KJV: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.
text.NABRE: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord drove back the sea with a strong east wind all night long and turned the sea into dry ground. The waters were split,
text.DRB: And when Moyses had stretched forth his hand upon the sea, our Lord took it away, a vehement and burning wind blowing all the night, and turned it into dry ground: and the water was divided.

text.NRSV: The Israelites went into the sea on dry ground, the waters forming a wall for them on their right and on their left.
text.Rotherham: Then went the sons of Israel into the midst of the sea on the dry ground,—and, the waters, to them, were a wall, on their right hand and on their left.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground: and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
text.NABRE: so that the Israelites entered into the midst of the sea on dry land, with the water as a wall to their right and to their left.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel went through the midst of the dry sea: for the water was as it were a wall on their right hand and their left.

text.NRSV: The Egyptians pursued, and went into the sea after them, all of Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, and chariot drivers.
text.Rotherham: And the Egyptians pursued, and entered after them—all the horses of Pharaoh his chariots and his horsemen,—into the midst of the sea.
text.KJV: And the Egyptians pursued, and went in after them to the midst of the sea, even all Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
text.NABRE: Rout of the Egyptians. The Egyptians followed in pursuit after them—all Pharaoh’s horses and chariots and horsemen—into the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: And the Ægyptians pursuing went in after them, and all Pharao's horses, his chariots and horsemen through the midst of the sea.

text.NRSV: At the morning watch the Lord in the pillar of fire and cloud looked down upon the Egyptian army, and threw the Egyptian army into panic.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the morning watch, that Yahweh looked down into the camp of the Egyptians, through the pillar of fire and cloud, and confused the camp of the Egyptians;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that in the morning watch the Lord looked unto the host of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and of the cloud, and troubled the host of the Egyptians,
text.NABRE: But during the watch just before dawn, the Lord looked down from a column of fiery cloud upon the Egyptian army and threw it into a panic;
text.DRB: And now the morning watch was come, and behold our Lord looking upon the Ægyptians' camp through the pillar of fire and the cloud, slew their army:

text.NRSV: He clogged their chariot wheels so that they turned with difficulty. The Egyptians said, “Let us flee from the Israelites, for the Lord is fighting for them against Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: and he took off the wheels of their chariots, and made them drive heavily,—so that the Egyptians said—We must flee from the face of Israel, for Yahweh, is fighting for them against the Egyptians.
text.KJV: And took off their chariot wheels, that they drave them heavily: so that the Egyptians said, Let us flee from the face of Israel; for the Lord fighteth for them against the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: and he so clogged their chariot wheels that they could drive only with difficulty. With that the Egyptians said, “Let us flee from Israel, because the Lord is fighting for them against Egypt.”
text.DRB: and overthrew the wheels of the chariots, and they were borne into the depth. The Ægyptians therefore said: Let us fly from Israel: for the Lord fighteth for them against us.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the sea, so that the water may come back upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots and chariot drivers.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Stretch forth thy hand over the sea,—that the waters may return upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord spoke to Moses: Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the water may flow back upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots and their horsemen.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Stretch forth thy hand upon the sea, that the waters may return to the Ægyptians upon their chariots and horsemen.

text.NRSV: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and at dawn the sea returned to its normal depth. As the Egyptians fled before it, the Lord tossed the Egyptians into the sea.
text.Rotherham: So Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea and the sea returned, towards morning, to its steady flow, when the Egyptians, were fleeing to meet it,—thus Yahweh shook off the Egyptians into the midst of the sea;
text.KJV: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to his strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it; and the Lord overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
text.NABRE: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and at daybreak the sea returned to its normal flow. The Egyptians were fleeing head on toward it when the Lord cast the Egyptians into the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: And when Moyses had stretched forth his hand against the sea, it returned in the first break of day to the former place: and the Ægyptians flying away, the waters came upon them, and our Lord enwrapt them in the midst of the waves.

text.NRSV: The waters returned and covered the chariots and the chariot drivers, the entire army of Pharaoh that had followed them into the sea; not one of them remained.
text.Rotherham: thus the waters returned and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, even all the forces of Pharaoh that were entering after them into the sea,—there was not left remaining among them so much as one.
text.KJV: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, and all the host of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them; there remained not so much as one of them.
text.NABRE: As the water flowed back, it covered the chariots and the horsemen. Of all Pharaoh’s army which had followed the Israelites into the sea, not even one escaped.
text.DRB: And the waters returned, and overwhelmed the chariots & the horsemen of all Pharao's army, who following were entered into the sea, neither did there so much as one of them remain.

text.NRSV: But the Israelites walked on dry ground through the sea, the waters forming a wall for them on their right and on their left.
text.Rotherham: But the sons of Israel, had gone their way on dry ground in the saidst of the sea,—and the waters had been to them a wall, on their right hand and on their left.
text.KJV: But the children of Israel walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites had walked on dry land through the midst of the sea, with the water as a wall to their right and to their left.
text.DRB: But the children of Israel marched through the midst of the dry sea, and the waters were unto them as instead of a wall on the right hand and on the left:

text.NRSV: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day from the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Yahweh, on that day, save Israel, out of the hand of the Egyptians,—and Israel saw the Egyptians, dead on the shore of the sea.
text.KJV: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore.
text.NABRE: Thus the Lord saved Israel on that day from the power of Egypt. When Israel saw the Egyptians lying dead on the seashore
text.DRB: & our Lord delivered Israel in that day out of the hand of the Ægyptians.

text.NRSV: Israel saw the great work that the Lord did against the Egyptians. So the people feared the Lord and believed in the Lord and in his servant Moses.
text.Rotherham: And when Israel saw the mighty hand wherewith Yahweh had wrought against the Egyptians, then did the people revere Yahweh,—and they put their trust in Yahweh, and in Moses, his servant.
text.KJV: And Israel saw that great work which the Lord did upon the Egyptians: and the people feared the Lord, and believed the Lord, and his servant Moses.
text.NABRE: and saw the great power that the Lord had shown against Egypt, the people feared the Lord. They believed in the Lord and in Moses his servant.
text.DRB: And they saw the Ægyptians dead upon the sea shore, & the mighty hand that our Lord had exercised against them: & the people feared our Lord, and they believed our Lord, and Moyses his servant.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the Lord: “I will sing to the Lord, for he has triumphed gloriously; horse and rider he has thrown into the sea.
text.Rotherham: Then, sang Moses, and the sons of Israel, this song unto Yahweh, and they spake, saying,—I will sing to Yahweh, for he is exalted, exalted,—The horse and his rider, hath he cast into the sea.
text.KJV: Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto the Lord, and spake, saying, I will sing unto the Lord, for he hath triumphed gloriously: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
text.NABRE: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the Lord: I will sing to the Lord, for he is gloriously triumphant; horse and chariot he has cast into the sea.
text.DRB: Then sang Moyses and the children of Israel this song to our Lord, and said: Let us sing to our Lord: for he is gloriously magnified, the horse and the rider he hath thrown into the sea.

text.NRSV: The Lord is my strength and my might, and he has become my salvation; this is my God, and I will praise him, my father’s God, and I will exalt him.
text.Rotherham: My might and melody, is Yah, And he became mine, by salvation,—This, is my GOD, and I will glorify him, The God of my father and I will set him on high.
text.KJV: The Lord is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father’s God, and I will exalt him.
text.NABRE: My strength and my refuge is the Lord, and he has become my savior. This is my God, I praise him; the God of my father, I extol him.
text.DRB: My strength, and my praise is our Lord, and he is made unto me a salvation: this is my God, and I will glorify him: the God of my father, and I will exalt him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is a warrior; the Lord is his name.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh is a warlike one,—Yahweh, is his name.
text.KJV: The Lord is a man of war: the Lord is his name.
text.NABRE: The Lord is a warrior, Lord is his name!
text.DRB: Our Lord is a man of war, Omnipotent is his name.

text.NRSV: “Pharaoh’s chariots and his army he cast into the sea; his picked officers were sunk in the Red Sea.
text.Rotherham: The chariots of Pharaoh and his train, hath he cast into the sea,—Yea, the choice of his warriors, were sunk in the Sedgy Sea.
text.KJV: Pharaoh’s chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea: his chosen captains also are drowned in the Red sea.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh’s chariots and army he hurled into the sea; the elite of his officers were drowned in the Red Sea.
text.DRB: Pharao's chariots and his army he hath cast into the sea: his chosen Princes are drowned in the red sea.

text.NRSV: The floods covered them; they went down into the depths like a stone.
text.Rotherham: Roaring deeps, covered them,—They went down in the raging depths like a stone.
text.KJV: The depths have covered them: they sank into the bottom as a stone.
text.NABRE: The flood waters covered them, they sank into the depths like a stone.
text.DRB: The depths have overwhelmed them, they are sunk into the bottom like a stone.

text.NRSV: Your right hand, O Lord, glorious in power— your right hand, O Lord, shattered the enemy.
text.Rotherham: Thy right hand, O Yahweh is splendid in power,—Thy right hand, O Yahweh dasheth in pieces a foe;
text.KJV: Thy right hand, O Lord, is become glorious in power: thy right hand, O Lord, hath dashed in pieces the enemy.
text.NABRE: Your right hand, O Lord, magnificent in power, your right hand, O Lord, shattered the enemy.
text.DRB: Thy right hand, o Lord, is magnified in strength: thy right hand, o Lord, hath strucken the enemy.

text.NRSV: In the greatness of your majesty you overthrew your adversaries; you sent out your fury, it consumed them like stubble.
text.Rotherham: And in the greatness of thine exaltation, dost thou tear down thine opposers,—Thou dost send forth thy wrath, it consumeth them as straw;
text.KJV: And in the greatness of thine excellency thou hast overthrown them that rose up against thee: thou sentest forth thy wrath, which consumed them as stubble.
text.NABRE: In your great majesty you overthrew your adversaries; you loosed your wrath to consume them like stubble.
text.DRB: And in the multitude of thy glory thou hast put down thy adversaries: thou hast sent thy wrath, which hath devoured them like stubble.

text.NRSV: At the blast of your nostrils the waters piled up, the floods stood up in a heap; the deeps congealed in the heart of the sea.
text.Rotherham: And with the blast of thy nostrils, heaped up are the waters, Upreared like a mound, are the flowing waves,—Roaring deeps are congealed in the heart of the sea.
text.KJV: And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were gathered together, the floods stood upright as an heap, and the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.
text.NABRE: At the blast of your nostrils the waters piled up, the flowing waters stood like a mound, the flood waters foamed in the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: And in the spirit of thy fury were the waters gathered together: the flowing water stood, the depths were gathered together in the midst of the sea.

text.NRSV: The enemy said, ‘I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil, my desire shall have its fill of them. I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’
text.Rotherham: Said the foe—I will pursue—overtake divide spoil,—Take her fill of them—shall my soul, I will bare my sword, root them out—shall my hand.
text.KJV: The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.
text.NABRE: The enemy boasted, “I will pursue and overtake them; I will divide the spoils and have my fill of them; I will draw my sword; my hand will despoil them!”
text.DRB: The enemy said: I will pursue and overtake, I will divide the spoils, my soul shall have his fill: I will draw forth my sword, my hand shall kill them.

text.NRSV: You blew with your wind, the sea covered them; they sank like lead in the mighty waters.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst blow with thy wind they were covered by the sea,—They rolled like lead, into the waters so wide.
text.KJV: Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them: they sank as lead in the mighty waters.
text.NABRE: When you blew with your breath, the sea covered them; like lead they sank in the mighty waters.
text.DRB: The spirit blew and the sea overwhelmed them: they sank as lead in the vehement waters.

text.NRSV: “Who is like you, O Lord, among the gods? Who is like you, majestic in holiness, awesome in splendor, doing wonders?
text.Rotherham: Who is, like unto thee, among the mighty O Yahweh? Who is, like unto thee, Majestic in holiness,—To be revered in praises, Doing a marvellous thing?
text.KJV: Who is like unto thee, O Lord, among the gods? who is like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?
text.NABRE: Who is like you among the gods, O Lord? Who is like you, magnificent among the holy ones? Awe-inspiring in deeds of renown, worker of wonders,
text.DRB: Who is like to thee among the strong, o Lord? Who is like to thee, magnifical in sanctity, terrible and laudable, doing marvels?

text.NRSV: You stretched out your right hand, the earth swallowed them.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst stretch forth thy right hand, earth swallowed them up.
text.KJV: Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, the earth swallowed them.
text.NABRE: when you stretched out your right hand, the earth swallowed them!
text.DRB: Thou didst stretch forth thy hand, and the earth devoured them.

text.NRSV: “In your steadfast love you led the people whom you redeemed; you guided them by your strength to your holy abode.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast led forth in thy lovingkindness, the people which thou hast redeemed,—Thou hast guided them in thy might into the home of thy holiness.
text.KJV: Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people which thou hast redeemed: thou hast guided them in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.
text.NABRE: In your love you led the people you redeemed; in your strength you guided them to your holy dwelling.
text.DRB: Thou hast in thy mercy been a guide to the people which thou hast redeemed: & in thy strength thou hast carried them unto thy holy habitation.

text.NRSV: The peoples heard, they trembled; pangs seized the inhabitants of Philistia.
text.Rotherham: Peoples have heard they tremble,—A pang, hath seized the dwellers of Philistia:
text.KJV: The people shall hear, and be afraid: sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of Palestina.
text.NABRE: The peoples heard and quaked; anguish gripped the dwellers in Philistia.
text.DRB: Nations rose up, and were angry: sorrows possessed the inhabiters of Philisthiim.

text.NRSV: Then the chiefs of Edom were dismayed; trembling seized the leaders of Moab; all the inhabitants of Canaan melted away.
text.Rotherham: Then, were amazed the chiefs of Edom, The mighty ones of Moab, there seizeth them, a trembling,—Melted away, have all the dwellers of Canaan:
text.KJV: Then the dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab, trembling shall take hold upon them; all the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away.
text.NABRE: Then were the chieftains of Edom dismayed, the nobles of Moab seized by trembling; All the inhabitants of Canaan melted away;
text.DRB: Then were the Princes of Edom troubled, trembling ceased on the sturdy of Moab: all the inhabiters of Chanaan were stark.

text.NRSV: Terror and dread fell upon them; by the might of your arm, they became still as a stone until your people, O Lord, passed by, until the people whom you acquired passed by.
text.Rotherham: There falleth upon them a terror and dread, With the greatness of thine arm, are they struck dumb as a stone,—Till thy people pass over O Yahweh, Till the people pass over, which thou hast made thine own:—
text.KJV: Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of thine arm they shall be as still as a stone; till thy people pass over, O Lord, till the people pass over, which thou hast purchased.
text.NABRE: terror and dread fell upon them. By the might of your arm they became silent like stone, while your people, Lord, passed over, while the people whom you created passed over.
text.DRB: Let fear and dread fall upon them, in the greatness of thy arm: Let them become unmovable as a stone, until thy people, o Lord, shall pass, until thy people shall pass, this which thou hast possessed.

text.NRSV: You brought them in and planted them on the mountain of your own possession, the place, O Lord, that you made your abode, the sanctuary, O Lord, that your hands have established.
text.Rotherham: Thou bringest them in and plantest them, In the mountain of thine inheritance, Thy dwelling-place to abide in which thou hast made, O Yahweh,—A hallowed place O My Lord which thy hands have established.
text.KJV: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, in the place, O Lord, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, in the Sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.
text.NABRE: You brought them in, you planted them on the mountain that is your own— The place you made the base of your throne, Lord, the sanctuary, Lord, your hands established.
text.DRB: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thy inheritance, in thy most firm habitation, which thou hast wrought, o Lord: thy sanctuary Lord, which thy hands have confirmed.

text.NRSV: The Lord will reign forever and ever.”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, shall reign unto times age-abiding and beyond.
text.KJV: The Lord shall reign for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: May the Lord reign forever and ever!
text.DRB: Our Lord shall reign for ever and evermore.

text.NRSV: When the horses of Pharaoh with his chariots and his chariot drivers went into the sea, the Lord brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground.
text.Rotherham: For the horses of Pharaoh, with his chariots and his horsemen, entered into the sea, Then did Yahweh bring back upon them the waters of the sea,—But the sons of Israel, had gone their way on the dry ground through the midst of the sea.
text.KJV: For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the Lord brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land in the midst of the sea.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh’s horses and chariots and horsemen entered the sea, the Lord made the waters of the sea flow back upon them, though the Israelites walked on dry land through the midst of the sea.
text.DRB: For Pharao on horseback entered in with his chariots & horsemen into the sea: and our Lord brought back upon them the waters of the sea: but the children of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Miriam, Aaron’s sister, took a tambourine in her hand; and all the women went out after her with tambourines and with dancing.
text.Rotherham: Then took Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron the timbrel in her hand,—and all the women came forth after her with timbrels and dances,
text.KJV: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
text.NABRE: Then the prophet Miriam, Aaron’s sister, took a tambourine in her hand, while all the women went out after her with tambourines, dancing;
text.DRB: Mary therefore the Prophetess, Aaron's sister, took a timbrel in her hand: and all the women went forth after her with timbrels and dances,

text.NRSV: And Miriam sang to them: “Sing to the Lord, for he has triumphed gloriously; horse and rider he has thrown into the sea.”
text.Rotherham: And Miriam responded to the men, Sing to Yahweh for he is exalted—exalted, The horse and his rider, hath he cast into the sea.
text.KJV: And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to the Lord, for he hath triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
text.NABRE: and she responded to them: Sing to the Lord, for he is gloriously triumphant; horse and chariot he has cast into the sea.
text.DRB: to whom she began the song, saying: Let us sing to our Lord, for he is gloriously magnified, the horse and his rider he hath cast into the sea.

text.NRSV: Then Moses ordered Israel to set out from the Red Sea, and they went into the wilderness of Shur. They went three days in the wilderness and found no water.
text.Rotherham: And Moses caused Israel to break up from the Red Sea, and they went out into the desert of Shur,—and journeyed three days in the desert and found no water;
text.KJV: So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.
text.NABRE: Then Moses led Israel forward from the Red Sea, and they marched out to the wilderness of Shur. After traveling for three days through the wilderness without finding water,
text.DRB: And Moyses removed Israel from the red sea, and they went forth into the desert Sur: and they walked three days through the wilderness, and found not water.

text.NRSV: When they came to Marah, they could not drink the water of Marah because it was bitter. That is why it was called Marah.
text.Rotherham: and, when they came in towards Marah they could not drink the waters of Marah, for they were bitter,—for this cause, was the name thereof called Marah.
text.KJV: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.
text.NABRE: they arrived at Marah, where they could not drink its water, because it was too bitter. Hence this place was called Marah.
text.DRB: And they came into Mara, neither could they drink the waters of Mara, because they were bitter: whereupon he gave a name also agreeable to the place, calling it Mara, that is, bitterness.

text.NRSV: And the people complained against Moses, saying, “What shall we drink?”
text.Rotherham: So the people murmured against Moses—saying, What are we to drink?
text.KJV: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?
text.NABRE: As the people grumbled against Moses, saying, “What are we to drink?”
text.DRB: And the people murmured against Moyses, saying: What shall we drink?

text.NRSV: He cried out to the Lord; and the Lord showed him a piece of wood; he threw it into the water, and the water became sweet. There the Lord made for them a statute and an ordinance and there he put them to the test.
text.Rotherham: And he made outcry unto Yahweh, and Yahweh pointed out a tree, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters became sweet,—There, he appointed him a statute and regulation And, there, he put him to the proof.
text.KJV: And he cried unto the Lord; and the Lord shewed him a tree, which when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet: there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them,
text.NABRE: he cried out to the Lord, who pointed out to him a piece of wood. When he threw it into the water, the water became fresh. It was here that God, in making statutes and ordinances for them, put them to the test.
text.DRB: But he cried to our Lord. Who did shew him a piece of wood: which when he had cast into the waters, they were turned into sweetness. There he appointed him precepts, and judgments, and there he proved him,

text.NRSV: He said, “If you will listen carefully to the voice of the Lord your God, and do what is right in his sight, and give heed to his commandments and keep all his statutes, I will not bring upon you any of the diseases that I brought upon the Egyptians; for I am the Lord who heals you.”
text.Rotherham: And he said: If thou, wilt indeed hearken, to the voice of Yahweh thy God, And the thing that is right in his eyes, thou wilt do, And so give ear to his commandments, And keep all his statutes, None of the sicknesses which I laid on the Egyptians, will I lay upon thee, For, I, am Yahweh, thy physician.
text.KJV: And said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the Lord thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon the Egyptians: for I am the Lord that healeth thee.
text.NABRE: He said: If you listen closely to the voice of the Lord, your God, and do what is right in his eyes: if you heed his commandments and keep all his statutes, I will not afflict you with any of the diseases with which I afflicted the Egyptians; for I, the Lord, am your healer.
text.DRB: saying: If thou wilt hear the voice of the Lord thy God, and do that is right before him, and obey his commandments, and keep all his precepts, none of the maladies, that I laid upon Ægypt, will I bring upon thee: for I am the Lord God thy curer.

text.NRSV: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees; and they camped there by the water.
text.Rotherham: Then came they in to Elim, and there, were twelve fountains of water and seventy palm-trees,—so they encamped there, by the waters.
text.KJV: And they came to Elim, where were twelve wells of water, and threescore and ten palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters.
text.NABRE: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there near the water.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel came into Elim, where there were twelve fountains of water, and seventy palm trees: and they camped beside the waters.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation of the Israelites set out from Elim; and Israel came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had departed from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then moved they on from Elim, and all the assembly of the sons of Israel entered into the desert of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month, by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Having set out from Elim, the whole Israelite community came into the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departure from the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And they set forward from Elim, and all the multitude of the children of Israel came into the desert Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai: the fifteenth day of the second month, after they came forth out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation of the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the assembly of the sons of Israel murmur against Moses and against Aaron in the desert;
text.KJV: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:
text.NABRE: Here in the wilderness the whole Israelite community grumbled against Moses and Aaron.
text.DRB: And all the assembly of the children of Israel murmured against Moyses & Aaron in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: The Israelites said to them, “If only we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the fleshpots and ate our fill of bread; for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger.”
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Israel said unto them—Oh that we had died by the hand of Yahweh, in the land of Egypt, when we remained by the pot of flesh, when we did eat bread to the full,—for ye have brought us forth into this desert, to cause all this multitude to die by famine.
