text.KJV: And the children of Israel said unto them, Would to God we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh pots, and when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.
text.NABRE: The Israelites said to them, “If only we had died at the Lord’s hand in the land of Egypt, as we sat by our kettles of meat and ate our fill of bread! But you have led us into this wilderness to make this whole assembly die of famine!”
text.DRB: And the children of Israel said to them: Would to God we had died by the hand of our Lord in the Land of Ægypt, when we sat over the flesh pots, and did eat bread our fill: why have you brought us into this desert, that you might kill all the multitude with famine?

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “I am going to rain bread from heaven for you, and each day the people shall go out and gather enough for that day. In that way I will test them, whether they will follow my instruction or not.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Behold me! raining down for you bread out of heaven,—and the people shall go out and gather the portion for a day, on its day, that I may prove them whether they will walk in my law, or not.
text.KJV: Then said the Lord unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.
text.NABRE: The Quail and the Manna. Then the Lord said to Moses: I am going to rain down bread from heaven for you. Each day the people are to go out and gather their daily portion; thus will I test them, to see whether they follow my instructions or not.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Behold I will rain you bread from heaven: let the people go forth, and gather that sufficeth for every day: that I may prove them whether they will walk in my law, or no.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it will be twice as much as they gather on other days.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass on the sixth day, that they shall settle what they shall bring in,—and it shall be twice as much as they gather day by day.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that on the sixth day they shall prepare that which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.
text.NABRE: On the sixth day, however, when they prepare what they bring in, let it be twice as much as they gather on the other days.
text.DRB: But the sixth day let them provide for to bring in: and let it be double to that they were wont to gather every day.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Aaron said to all the Israelites, “In the evening you shall know that it was the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: So Moses and Aaron said unto all the sons of Israel,—At eventide, then shall ye know that it was, Yahweh, who brought you forth, out of the land of Egypt;
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the Lord hath brought you out from the land of Egypt:
text.NABRE: So Moses and Aaron told all the Israelites, “At evening you will know that it was the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt;
text.DRB: And Moyses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel: At even you shall know that our Lord hath brought you forth out of the land of Ægypt;

text.NRSV: and in the morning you shall see the glory of the Lord, because he has heard your complaining against the Lord. For what are we, that you complain against us?”
text.Rotherham: and, in the morning, then shall ye see the glory of Yahweh, in that he hath heard your murmurings against Yahweh,—what then are, we, that ye should murmur against, us?
text.KJV: And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the Lord; for that he heareth your murmurings against the Lord: and what are we, that ye murmur against us?
text.NABRE: and in the morning you will see the glory of the Lord, when he hears your grumbling against him. But who are we that you should grumble against us?”
text.DRB: and in the morning you shall see the glory of our Lord: for he hath heard your murmuring against our Lord. But as for us, what are we, that you mutter against us?

text.NRSV: And Moses said, “When the Lord gives you meat to eat in the evening and your fill of bread in the morning, because the Lord has heard the complaining that you utter against him—what are we? Your complaining is not against us but against the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—In that Yahweh giveth you in the evening flesh to eat, and bread in the morning, to the full, in that Yahweh heareth your murmurings wherewith ye are murmuring against him, what then are, we? Not against us, do ye murmur but against Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses said, This shall be, when the Lord shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the Lord heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him: and what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but against the Lord.
text.NABRE: And Moses said, “When the Lord gives you meat to eat in the evening and in the morning your fill of bread, and hears the grumbling you utter against him, who then are we? Your grumbling is not against us, but against the Lord.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said: At even our Lord will give you flesh to eat, and in the morning bread your fill: for he hath heard your murmurings, which you have murmured against him, for what are we? Neither is your murmuring against us, but against our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Say to the whole congregation of the Israelites, ‘Draw near to the Lord, for he has heard your complaining.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron, Say unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, Draw near, before Yahweh,—for he hath heard your murmurings.
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto Aaron, Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, Come near before the Lord: for he hath heard your murmurings.
text.NABRE: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Tell the whole Israelite community: Approach the Lord, for he has heard your grumbling.”
text.DRB: Moyses also said to Aaron: Say to the whole assembly of the children of Israel: Approach you before our Lord: for he hath heard your murmuring.

text.NRSV: And as Aaron spoke to the whole congregation of the Israelites, they looked toward the wilderness, and the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Aaron spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, then turned they towards the desert,—and lo! the glory of Yahweh, had appeared in the cloud,
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as Aaron spake unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
text.NABRE: But while Aaron was speaking to the whole Israelite community, they turned in the direction of the wilderness, and there the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud!
text.DRB: And when Aaron spake to all the assembly of the children of Israel, they looked toward the wilderness: and behold the glory of our Lord appeared in a cloud.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and said,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—saying,
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: “I have heard the complaining of the Israelites; say to them, ‘At twilight you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall have your fill of bread; then you shall know that I am the Lord your God.’”
text.Rotherham: I have heard the murmurings of the sons of Israel,—speak unto them, saying—Between the two evenings, shall ye eat flesh, and in the morning, shall ye be filled with bread,—so shall ye know that I Yahweh am your God.
text.KJV: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak unto them, saying, At even ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: I have heard the grumbling of the Israelites. Tell them: In the evening twilight you will eat meat, and in the morning you will have your fill of bread, and then you will know that I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel, say to them: At even you shall eat flesh, and in the morning you shall have your fill of bread: and you shall know that I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: In the evening quails came up and covered the camp; and in the morning there was a layer of dew around the camp.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the evening, that there came up quail, and covered the camp,—and in the morning was the outpouring of dew, round about the camp;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that at even the quails came up, and covered the camp: and in the morning the dew lay round about the host.
text.NABRE: In the evening, quail came up and covered the camp. In the morning there was a layer of dew all about the camp,
text.DRB: Therefore it came to pass at even, & the quail rose, and covered the camp: in the morning also a dew lay round about the camp.

text.NRSV: When the layer of dew lifted, there on the surface of the wilderness was a fine flaky substance, as fine as frost on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and when the outpouring of dew went up, then lo! on the face of the desert, a thin flake, thin as hoar-frost on the earth,
text.KJV: And when the dew that lay was gone up, behold, upon the face of the wilderness there lay a small round thing, as small as the hoar frost on the ground.
text.NABRE: and when the layer of dew evaporated, fine flakes were on the surface of the wilderness, fine flakes like hoarfrost on the ground.
text.DRB: And when it had covered the face of the earth, it appeared in the wilderness small, and as it were beaten with a pestle like unto the hoar frost on the ground.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites saw it, they said to one another, “What is it?” For they did not know what it was. Moses said to them, “It is the bread that the Lord has given you to eat.
text.Rotherham: And when the sons of Israel saw it they said one to another—What is that? For they knew not what "that" was. Then said Moses unto them, "That" is the bread which Yahweh hath given you to eat.
text.KJV: And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another, It is manna: for they wist not what it was. And Moses said unto them, This is the bread which the Lord hath given you to eat.
text.NABRE: On seeing it, the Israelites asked one another, “What is this?” for they did not know what it was. But Moses told them, “It is the bread which the Lord has given you to eat.
text.DRB: Which when the children of Israel had seen, they said one to other: Man-hu! Which signifieth: What is this! For they knew not what it was. To whom Moyses said: This is the bread, which our Lord hath given you to eat.

text.NRSV: This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Gather as much of it as each of you needs, an omer to a person according to the number of persons, all providing for those in their own tents.’”
text.Rotherham: This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, Gather ye thereof, each man what he needeth for eating,—an omer a head, by the number of your souls, each man—for them who are in his tent, shall ye take.
text.KJV: This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded, Gather of it every man according to his eating, an omer for every man, according to the number of your persons; take ye every man for them which are in his tents.
text.NABRE: Regulations Regarding the Manna. “Now, this is what the Lord has commanded. Gather as much of it as each needs to eat, an omer for each person for as many of you as there are, each of you providing for those in your own tent.”
text.DRB: This is the word, that our Lord hath commanded: Let every one gather of it so much as sufficeth to eat: a gomor every man, according to the number of your souls that dwell in a tent so shall you take up.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so, some gathering more, some less.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel did so,—and gathered, [some] more, and [some] less;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some more, some less.
text.NABRE: The Israelites did so. Some gathered a large and some a small amount.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did so: and they gathered, one more, another less.

text.NRSV: But when they measured it with an omer, those who gathered much had nothing over, and those who gathered little had no shortage; they gathered as much as each of them needed.
text.Rotherham: and when they measured it with the omer, he who had gathered more had nothing over, and he who had gathered less had no lack: each man what he needed for eating, did they gather.
text.KJV: And when they did mete it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.
text.NABRE: But when they measured it out by the omer, the one who had gathered a large amount did not have too much, and the one who had gathered a small amount did not have too little. They gathered as much as each needed to eat.
text.DRB: And they measured by the measure of a gomor: neither he that gathered more, had above: nor he that provided less, found under: but every one gathered according to that which they were able to eat.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to them, “Let no one leave any of it over until morning.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Let, no man leave thereof, until morning.
text.KJV: And Moses said, Let no man leave of it till the morning.
text.NABRE: Moses said to them, “Let no one leave any of it over until morning.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to them: Let no man leave thereof till the morning.

text.NRSV: But they did not listen to Moses; some left part of it until morning, and it bred worms and became foul. And Moses was angry with them.
text.Rotherham: But they hearkened not unto Moses, but certain men left thereof until morning, and it swarmed with worms and become loathsome,—and Moses was wroth with them.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and stank: and Moses was wroth with them.
text.NABRE: But they did not listen to Moses, and some kept a part of it over until morning, and it became wormy and stank. Therefore Moses was angry with them.
text.DRB: Who heard him not, but certain of them left until the morning, and it began to be full of worms, and it putrefied. And Moyses was angry against them.

text.NRSV: Morning by morning they gathered it, as much as each needed; but when the sun grew hot, it melted.
text.Rotherham: Thus then they gathered it morning by morning, each man what he needed for eating, and as soon as the sun waxed hot, so soon it melted.
text.KJV: And they gathered it every morning, every man according to his eating: and when the sun waxed hot, it melted.
text.NABRE: Morning after morning they gathered it, as much as each needed to eat; but when the sun grew hot, it melted away.
text.DRB: And every one of them gathered in the morning so much as might suffice to eat: and after the sun waxed hot, it melted.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day they gathered twice as much food, two omers apiece. When all the leaders of the congregation came and told Moses,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the sixth day, that they gathered food twice as much, two omers for each one,—so all the princes of the assembly came in, and told Moses.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one man: and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.
text.NABRE: On the sixth day they gathered twice as much food, two omers for each person. When all the leaders of the community came and reported this to Moses,
text.DRB: But in the sixth day they gathered double portions, that is, two gomors every man: and all the Princes of the multitude came, and told Moyses.

text.NRSV: he said to them, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Tomorrow is a day of solemn rest, a holy sabbath to the Lord; bake what you want to bake and boil what you want to boil, and all that is left over put aside to be kept until morning.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—That is what Yahweh spake. The observance of a holy sabbath to Yahweh, is to-morrow,—what ye would bake, bake and what ye would boil, boil, and all that is left over, put by you to keep until the morning.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, This is that which the Lord hath said, To morrow is the rest of the holy sabbath unto the Lord: bake that which ye will bake to day, and seethe that ye will seethe; and that which remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.
text.NABRE: he told them, “That is what the Lord has prescribed. Tomorrow is a day of rest, a holy sabbath of the Lord. Whatever you want to bake, bake; whatever you want to boil, boil; but whatever is left put away and keep until the morning.”
text.DRB: Who said to them: This is it which our Lord hath spoken: The Sabbath's rest is sanctified unto our Lord tomorrow. Whatsoever is to be wrought, do it: and the meats that are to be made ready, make them ready: and whatsoever shall remain, lay it up until the morning.

text.NRSV: So they put it aside until morning, as Moses commanded them; and it did not become foul, and there were no worms in it.
text.Rotherham: So they put it by until the morning, as Moses had commanded,—and it gave no unpleasant odour, neither was there a worm, therein.
text.KJV: And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.
text.NABRE: When they put it away until the morning, as Moses commanded, it did not stink nor were there worms in it.
text.DRB: And they did so as Moyses had commanded, and it putrefied not, neither was there worm found in it.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Eat it today, for today is a sabbath to the Lord; today you will not find it in the field.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses—Eat ye it to-day, for a sabbath, is to-day, unto Yahweh,—to-day, ye shall not find it in the field.
text.KJV: And Moses said, Eat that to day; for to day is a sabbath unto the Lord: to day ye shall not find it in the field.
text.NABRE: Moses then said, “Eat it today, for today is the sabbath of the Lord. Today you will not find any in the field.
text.DRB: And Moyses said: Eat it today, because it is the Sabbath of our Lord: today it shall not be found in the field.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is a sabbath, there will be none.”
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall ye gather it,—but on the seventh day, a sabbath, it shall not be therein.
text.KJV: Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is the sabbath, in it there shall be none.
text.NABRE: Six days you will gather it, but on the seventh day, the sabbath, it will not be there.”
text.DRB: Gather it six days: but in the seventh day is the Sabbath of our Lord, therefore it shall not be found.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day some of the people went out to gather, and they found none.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the seventh day, that there went forth some of the people to gather,—but they found not.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that there went out some of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.
text.NABRE: Still, on the seventh day some of the people went out to gather it, but they did not find any.
text.DRB: And the seventh day came: and some of the people going forth to gather, found not.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “How long will you refuse to keep my commandments and instructions?
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses,—How long have ye refused to keep my commandments and my laws?
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: How long will you refuse to keep my commandments and my instructions?
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: How long will you not keep my commandments, and my law?

text.NRSV: See! The Lord has given you the sabbath, therefore on the sixth day he gives you food for two days; each of you stay where you are; do not leave your place on the seventh day.”
text.Rotherham: See because Yahweh hath given you the sabbath, for this cause, is, he, giving you on the sixth day, food for two days,—abide ye every man in his place, let no man go forth from his dwelling, on the seventh day,
text.KJV: See, for that the Lord hath given you the sabbath, therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.
text.NABRE: Take note! The Lord has given you the sabbath. That is why on the sixth day he gives you food for two days. Each of you stay where you are and let no one go out on the seventh day.
text.DRB: See that our Lord hath given you a Sabbath, and for this cause on the sixth day he giveth you double portions: let each man tarry with himself, and let none go forth out of his place the seventh day.

text.NRSV: So the people rested on the seventh day.
text.Rotherham: So the people rested on the seventh day.
text.KJV: So the people rested on the seventh day.
text.NABRE: After that the people rested on the seventh day.
text.DRB: And the people kept the Sabbath on the seventh day.

text.NRSV: The house of Israel called it manna; it was like coriander seed, white, and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
text.Rotherham: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna,—and the same, was like coriander seed white, and the taste thereof, like flat-cake with honey,
text.KJV: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it was like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
text.NABRE: The house of Israel named this food manna. It was like coriander seed, white, and it tasted like wafers made with honey.
text.DRB: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: which was as it were coriander seed white, and the taste thereof like to flour with honey.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Let an omer of it be kept throughout your generations, in order that they may see the food with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, Fill an omer therefrom as a thing to be preserved to your generations,—that they may see the bread wherewith I fed you in the desert, when I brought you forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And Moses said, This is the thing which the Lord commandeth, Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I have fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Moses said, “This is what the Lord has commanded. Keep a full omer of it for your future generations, so that they may see the food I gave you to eat in the wilderness when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said: This is the word, which our Lord hath commanded: Fill a gomor of it, and let it be kept unto the generations to come hereafter; that they may know the bread, wherewith I fed you in the wilderness, when you were brought forth out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron, “Take a jar, and put an omer of manna in it, and place it before the Lord, to be kept throughout your generations.”
text.Rotherham: So Moses said unto Aaron—Take a single basket, and put therein an omer-full of manna,—and set it down before Yahweh, as a thing to be preserved to your generations.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a pot, and put an omer full of manna therein, and lay it up before the Lord, to be kept for your generations.
text.NABRE: Moses then told Aaron, “Take a jar and put a full omer of manna in it. Then place it before the Lord to keep it for your future generations.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to Aaron: Take one vessel, and put Manna into it, so much as a gomor can hold: and lay it up before our Lord to keep unto your generations:

text.NRSV: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron placed it before the covenant, for safekeeping.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh gave command unto Moses, so did Aaron set it down before the testimony, as a thing to be preserved,
text.KJV: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.
text.NABRE: As the Lord had commanded Moses, Aaron placed it in front of the covenant to keep it.
text.DRB: as our Lord commanded Moyses. And Aaron put it in the tabernacle to be reserved.

text.NRSV: The Israelites ate manna forty years, until they came to a habitable land; they ate manna, until they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Israel did eat the manna forty years, until they entered into the land wherein they were to dwell,—the manna, they did eat until they entered into the bounds of the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did eat manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: The Israelites ate the manna for forty years, until they came to settled land; they ate the manna until they came to the borders of Canaan.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did eat Manna forty years, till they came into the habitable land: with this meat were they fed, until they touched the borders of the land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: An omer is a tenth of an ephah.
text.Rotherham: Now, an omer, is, the tenth of the ephah.
text.KJV: Now an omer is the tenth part of an ephah.
text.NABRE: (An omer is one tenth of an ephah.)
text.DRB: And a gomor is the tenth part an ephi.

text.NRSV: From the wilderness of Sin the whole congregation of the Israelites journeyed by stages, as the Lord commanded. They camped at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.
text.Rotherham: And all the assembly of the sons of Israel set forward out of the desert of Sin, by their removings, at the bidding of Yahweh,—and encamped in Rephidim, and there was no water for the people to drink.
text.KJV: And all the congregation of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin, after their journeys, according to the commandment of the Lord, and pitched in Rephidim: and there was no water for the people to drink.
text.NABRE: From the wilderness of Sin the whole Israelite community journeyed by stages, as the Lord directed, and encamped at Rephidim. But there was no water for the people to drink,
text.DRB: Therefore all the multitude of the children of Israel setting forward from the desert Sin, by their mansions, according to the word of our Lord, camped in Raphidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.

text.NRSV: The people quarreled with Moses, and said, “Give us water to drink.” Moses said to them, “Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And the people found fault with Moses, and said—Give us water, that we may drink. And Moses said to them, Why should ye find fault with me? Why should ye put Yahweh to the proof?
text.KJV: Wherefore the people did chide with Moses, and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said unto them, Why chide ye with me? wherefore do ye tempt the Lord?
text.NABRE: and so they quarreled with Moses and said, “Give us water to drink.” Moses replied to them, “Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you put the Lord to a test?”
text.DRB: Who chiding against Moyses, said: Give us water, that we may drink. To whom Moyses answered: Why chide you against me? Wherefore do you tempt our Lord?

text.NRSV: But the people thirsted there for water; and the people complained against Moses and said, “Why did you bring us out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and livestock with thirst?”
text.Rotherham: So the people thirsted, there for water, and the people murmured against Moses,—and said—Wherefore is it that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill me and my sons and my cattle, with thirst?
text.KJV: And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, Wherefore is this that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?
text.NABRE: Here, then, in their thirst for water, the people grumbled against Moses, saying, “Why then did you bring us up out of Egypt? To have us die of thirst with our children and our livestock?”
text.DRB: The people therefore was thirsty there for lack of water, & murmured against Moyses, saying: Why didst thou make us go forth out of Ægypt to kill us, and our children, & our beasts with thirst:

text.NRSV: So Moses cried out to the Lord, “What shall I do with this people? They are almost ready to stone me.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses made outcry unto Yahweh saying, What am I to do, with this people? Yet a little, and they will stone me.
text.KJV: And Moses cried unto the Lord, saying, What shall I do unto this people? they be almost ready to stone me.
text.NABRE: So Moses cried out to the Lord, “What shall I do with this people? A little more and they will stone me!”
text.DRB: And Moyses cried to our Lord, saying: What shall I do to this people? Yet a little while, and they will stone me.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go on ahead of the people, and take some of the elders of Israel with you; take in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Pass over before the people, and take with thee some of the elders of Israel,—thy staff also wherewith thou didst smite the river, take thou in thy hand, so shalt thou go thy way.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thine hand, and go.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: Go on ahead of the people, and take along with you some of the elders of Israel, holding in your hand, as you go, the staff with which you struck the Nile.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Go before the people, and take with thee of the ancients of Israel: and the rod wherewith thou didst strike the river, take in thy hand, and go.

text.NRSV: I will be standing there in front of you on the rock at Horeb. Strike the rock, and water will come out of it, so that the people may drink.” Moses did so, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! standing before thee, there upon the rock in Horeb, then shalt thou smite the rock and there shall come forth therefrom water, and the people shall drink. And Moses did so, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.KJV: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.NABRE: I will be standing there in front of you on the rock in Horeb. Strike the rock, and the water will flow from it for the people to drink. Moses did this, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
text.DRB: Behold I will stand there before thee, upon the rock Horeb: and thou shalt strike the rock, & water shall go out thereof, that the people may drink. Moyses did so before the ancients of Israel:

text.NRSV: He called the place Massah and Meribah, because the Israelites quarreled and tested the Lord, saying, “Is the Lord among us or not?”
text.Rotherham: So he called the name of the place, Massah, and Meribah,—because of the fault-finding of the sons of Israel, and because of their putting Yahweh to the proof, saying, Is Yahweh in our midst or is he not?
text.KJV: And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the Lord, saying, Is the Lord among us, or not?
text.NABRE: The place was named Massah and Meribah, because the Israelites quarreled there and tested the Lord, saying, “Is the Lord in our midst or not?”
text.DRB: and he called the name of that place, Temptation, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and for that they tempted our Lord, saying: Is the Lord among us or not?

text.NRSV: Then Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
text.Rotherham: Then came in Amalek,—and fought with Israel, in Rephidim.
text.KJV: Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel in Rephidim.
text.NABRE: Battle with Amalek. Then Amalek came and waged war against Israel in Rephidim.
text.DRB: And Amalec came, and fought against Israel in Raphidim.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Joshua, “Choose some men for us and go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the staff of God in my hand.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Joshua—Choose for us men, and go forth, fight with Amalek,—to-morrow, am, I, stationing myself upon the top of the hill, with the staff of God in my hand.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Joshua, Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek: to morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in mine hand.
text.NABRE: So Moses said to Joshua, “Choose some men for us, and tomorrow go out and engage Amalek in battle. I will be standing on top of the hill with the staff of God in my hand.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to Josue: Choose out men, and go forth & fight against Amalec: tomorrow I will stand in the top of the hill having the rod of God in my hand.

text.NRSV: So Joshua did as Moses told him, and fought with Amalek, while Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, to fight with Amalek,—and Moses, Aaron and Hur, went up to the top of the hill.
text.KJV: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, and fought with Amalek: and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
text.NABRE: Joshua did as Moses told him: he engaged Amalek in battle while Moses, Aaron, and Hur climbed to the top of the hill.
text.DRB: Josue did as Moyses had spoken, and he fought against Amalec: but Moyses and Aaron and Hur went up upon the top of the Hill.

text.NRSV: Whenever Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; and whenever he lowered his hand, Amalek prevailed.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when Moses shall lift on high his hands, then shall Israel prevail, but when he shall let down his hands, then shall Amalek prevail.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed: and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.
text.NABRE: As long as Moses kept his hands raised up, Israel had the better of the fight, but when he let his hands rest, Amalek had the better of the fight.
text.DRB: And when Moyses lifted up his hands, Israel overcame: but if he did let them down a little, Amalec overcame.

text.NRSV: But Moses’ hands grew weary; so they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one on one side, and the other on the other side; so his hands were steady until the sun set.
text.Rotherham: But, the hands of Moses, were weary, so they took a stone, and put under him, and he sat thereupon,—and, Aaron and Hur, upheld his hands—on this side, one and on that side, one, and so his hands became steady, until the going in of the sun.
text.KJV: But Moses’ hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat thereon; and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.
text.NABRE: Moses’ hands, however, grew tired; so they took a rock and put it under him and he sat on it. Meanwhile Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one on one side and one on the other, so that his hands remained steady until sunset.
text.DRB: And the hands of Moyses were heavy: therefore they took a stone, and put under him, whereupon he sat: and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands on both sides. And it came to pass that his hands were not weary until sun set.

text.NRSV: And Joshua defeated Amalek and his people with the sword.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua overthrew Amalek and his people, with the edge of the sword.
text.KJV: And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.
text.NABRE: And Joshua defeated Amalek and his people with the sword.
text.DRB: And Josue put Amalec to flight, & his people by the edge of the sword.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Write this as a reminder in a book and recite it in the hearing of Joshua: I will utterly blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Write this as a remembrancer in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua—that I will, wipe out, the remembrance of Amalek, from under the heavens.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua: for I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Write this down in a book as something to be remembered, and recite it to Joshua: I will completely blot out the memory of Amalek from under the heavens.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Write this for a monument in a book, & deliver it to the ears of Josue: for I will destroy the memory of Amalec from under heaven.

text.NRSV: And Moses built an altar and called it, The Lord is my banner.
text.Rotherham: And Moses built an altar,—and called the name thereof, Yahweh-nissi.
text.KJV: And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it JEHOVAH–nissi:
text.NABRE: Moses built an altar there, which he named Yahweh-nissi;
text.DRB: And Moyses builded an Altar, and called the name thereof, Our Lord my exaltation, saying:

text.NRSV: He said, “A hand upon the banner of the Lord! The Lord will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Because of a hand against the throne of Yah, Yahweh hath war with Amalek,—from generation to generation!
text.KJV: For he said, Because the Lord hath sworn that the Lord will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: for he said, “Take up the banner of the Lord! The Lord has a war against Amalek through the ages.”
text.DRB: Because the hand of our Lord's throne, and the war of our Lord shall be against Amalec, from generation unto generation.

text.NRSV: Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for his people Israel, how the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro, priest of Midian, father-in-law of Moses, heard all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people,—in that Yahweh had brought forth Israel out of Egypt.
text.KJV: When Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father in law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, and that the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt;
text.NABRE: Now Moses’ father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for his people Israel: how the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt.
text.DRB: And When Jethro the Priest of Madian, the allied of Moyses, had heard all the things, that God had done to Moyses, and to Israel his people, and that our Lord had brought forth Israel out of Ægypt;

text.NRSV: After Moses had sent away his wife Zipporah, his father-in-law Jethro took her back,
text.Rotherham: So Jethro, Moses father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses wife,—after she had been sent home;
text.KJV: Then Jethro, Moses’ father in law, took Zipporah, Moses’ wife, after he had sent her back,
text.NABRE: So his father-in-law Jethro took along Zipporah, Moses’ wife—now this was after Moses had sent her back—
text.DRB: he took Sephora the wife of Moyses whom he had sent back:

text.NRSV: along with her two sons. The name of the one was Gershom (for he said, “I have been an alien in a foreign land”),
text.Rotherham: and her two sons,—of whom, the name of the one, was Gershom, for, said he, A sojourner, am I in a strange land,
text.KJV: And her two sons; of which the name of the one was Gershom; for he said, I have been an alien in a strange land:
text.NABRE: and her two sons. One of these was named Gershom; for he said, “I am a resident alien in a foreign land.”
text.DRB: and her two sons, of which one was called Gersam, his father saying: I have been a stranger in a foreign country;

text.NRSV: and the name of the other, Eliezer (for he said, “The God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh”).
text.Rotherham: and, the name of the other, Eliezer, for the God of my father, was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And the name of the other was Eliezer; for the God of my father, said he, was mine help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh:
text.NABRE: The other was named Eliezer; for he said, “The God of my father is my help; he has rescued me from Pharaoh’s sword.”
text.DRB: And the other Eliezer: for the God of my father, quoth he, is my helper, and hath delivered me from Pharao's sword.

text.NRSV: Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, came into the wilderness where Moses was encamped at the mountain of God, bringing Moses’ sons and wife to him.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro, Moses father-in-law, came in, with his two sons and his wife, unto Moses,—into the desert where, he, was encamping, the mountain of God;
text.KJV: And Jethro, Moses’ father in law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness, where he encamped at the mount of God:
text.NABRE: Together with Moses’ wife and sons, then, his father-in-law Jethro came to him in the wilderness where he was encamped at the mountain of God,
text.DRB: Jethro therefore the allied of Moyses came and his sons, and his wife to Moyses into the desert, where he was camped beside the mountain of God.

text.NRSV: He sent word to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you, with your wife and her two sons.”
text.Rotherham: and said unto Moses, I, thy father-in-law, Jethro, am coming in unto thee,—and thy wife, and her two sons, with her.
text.KJV: And he said unto Moses, I thy father in law Jethro am come unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.
text.NABRE: and he sent word to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you, along with your wife and her two sons.”
text.DRB: And he sent word to Moyses, saying: I Jethro thy allied come to thee, and thy wife, and thy two children with her.

text.NRSV: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law; he bowed down and kissed him; each asked after the other’s welfare, and they went into the tent.
text.Rotherham: So Moses went forth to meet his father-in-law, and bowed himself down to him and kissed him, and they asked each other of their welfare,—and came into the tent.
text.KJV: And Moses went out to meet his father in law, and did obeisance, and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.
text.NABRE: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, bowed down, and then kissed him. Having greeted each other, they went into the tent.
text.DRB: Who going forth to meet his allied, adored, and kissed him: and they saluted one another with words of peace. And when he was entered into the tent,

text.NRSV: Then Moses told his father-in-law all that the Lord had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the hardship that had beset them on the way, and how the Lord had delivered them.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses recounted to his father-in-law, all that Yahweh had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians, for Israel’s sake,—all the hardship which had befallen them by the way, and how Yahweh had delivered them.
text.KJV: And Moses told his father in law all that the Lord had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, and all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the Lord delivered them.
text.NABRE: Moses then told his father-in-law of all that the Lord had done to Pharaoh and the Egyptians for the sake of Israel, and of all the hardships that had beset them on their journey, and how the Lord had rescued them.
text.DRB: Moyses told his allied all things that our Lord had done to Pharao, and the Ægyptians for Israel: and the whole travail which had chanced to them in the journey, and that our Lord had delivered them.

text.NRSV: Jethro rejoiced for all the good that the Lord had done to Israel, in delivering them from the Egyptians.
text.Rotherham: Then did Jethro rejoice, for all the good which Yahweh had done to Israel,—whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
text.KJV: And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the Lord had done to Israel, whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: Jethro rejoiced over all the goodness that the Lord had shown Israel in rescuing them from the power of the Egyptians.
text.DRB: And Jethro rejoiced for all the good things, that our Lord had done to Israel, because he had delivered them out of the hands of the Ægyptians,

text.NRSV: Jethro said, “Blessed be the Lord, who has delivered you from the Egyptians and from Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And Jethro said—Blessed be Yahweh, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharaoh,—Who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians:
text.KJV: And Jethro said, Blessed be the Lord, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh, who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: “Blessed be the Lord,” he said, “who has rescued you from the power of the Egyptians and of Pharaoh.
text.DRB: and he said: Blessed is the Lord, that hath delivered you out of the hand of the Ægyptians, and out of the hand of Pharao, that hath delivered his people out of the hand of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all gods, because he delivered the people from the Egyptians, when they dealt arrogantly with them.”
text.Rotherham: Now, I know, that, greater, is Yahweh than all the gods,—Even in the thing wherein they were arrogant over them.
text.KJV: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all gods: for in the thing wherein they dealt proudly he was above them.
text.NABRE: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all the gods; for he rescued the people from the power of the Egyptians when they treated them arrogantly.”
text.DRB: Now do I know, that the Lord is great above all gods: for because they dealt proudly against them.

text.NRSV: And Jethro, Moses’ father-in-law, brought a burnt offering and sacrifices to God; and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law in the presence of God.
text.Rotherham: Then took Jethro father-in-law of Moses, an ascending-offering and sacrifices to God,—and Aaron and all the elders of Israel came in to eat bread with the father-in-law of Moses before God.
text.KJV: And Jethro, Moses’ father in law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God: and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’ father in law before God.
text.NABRE: Then Jethro, the father-in-law of Moses, brought a burnt offering and sacrifices for God, and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to share with Moses’ father-in-law in the meal before God.
text.DRB: Jethro therefore the allied of Moyses offered holocausts and hosts to God: and Aaron and all the ancients of Israel came, to eat bread with him before God.

text.NRSV: The next day Moses sat as judge for the people, while the people stood around him from morning until evening.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat, to judge the people,—and the people stood by Moses, from the morning, until the evening.
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people: and the people stood by Moses from the morning unto the evening.
text.NABRE: Appointment of Minor Judges. The next day Moses sat in judgment for the people, while they stood around him from morning until evening.
text.DRB: And the next day Moyses sat to judge the people, who stood by Moyses from morning until night.

text.NRSV: When Moses’ father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, “What is this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, while all the people stand around you from morning until evening?”
text.Rotherham: And when Moses father-in-law saw all that he, was doing for the people, he said—What is this thing which, thou, art doing for the people? Wherefore art, thou, sitting alone, with all the people, stationed by thee, from morning until evening?
text.KJV: And when Moses’ father in law saw all that he did to the people, he said, What is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand by thee from morning unto even?
text.NABRE: When Moses’ father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he asked, “What is this business that you are conducting for the people? Why do you sit alone while all the people have to stand about you from morning till evening?”
text.DRB: Which thing when his allied had seen, to wit, all things that he did in the people, he said: What is this that thou doest in the people? Why sittest thou alone, and all the people attendeth from morning until night?

text.NRSV: Moses said to his father-in-law, “Because the people come to me to inquire of God.
text.Rotherham: And Moses said to his father-in-law,—Because the people come in unto me to seek God:
text.KJV: And Moses said unto his father in law, Because the people come unto me to enquire of God:
text.NABRE: Moses answered his father-in-law, “The people come to me to consult God.
text.DRB: To whom Moyses answered: The people cometh to me seeking the sentence of God.

text.NRSV: When they have a dispute, they come to me and I decide between one person and another, and I make known to them the statutes and instructions of God.”
text.Rotherham: When they have a matter, they come in unto me, then do I judge between a man and his neighbour,—and make known the statutes of God and his laws.
text.KJV: When they have a matter, they come unto me; and I judge between one and another, and I do make them know the statutes of God, and his laws.
text.NABRE: Whenever they have a disagreement, they come to me to have me settle the matter between them and make known to them God’s statutes and instructions.”
text.DRB: And when any controversy chanceth among them, they come unto me to judge between them, and to shew the precepts of God, and his laws.

text.NRSV: Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “What you are doing is not good.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses’ father-in-law unto him,—Not good, is the thing that thou art doing.
text.KJV: And Moses’ father in law said unto him, The thing that thou doest is not good.
text.NABRE: “What you are doing is not wise,” Moses’ father-in-law replied.
text.DRB: But he said: Thou doest not well:

text.NRSV: You will surely wear yourself out, both you and these people with you. For the task is too heavy for you; you cannot do it alone.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt get quite worn out, both thou and this people that is with thee,—for the thing is too heavy for thee, thou canst not do it, alone.
text.KJV: Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee: for this thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.
text.NABRE: “You will surely wear yourself out, both you and these people with you. The task is too heavy for you; you cannot do it alone.
text.DRB: Thou art tired with foolish labour, both thou, and this people that is with thee, the business is above thy strength, thou alone canst not sustain it.

text.NRSV: Now listen to me. I will give you counsel, and God be with you! You should represent the people before God, and you should bring their cases before God;
text.Rotherham: Now, hearken thou to my voice—let me counsel thee, and may God be with thee: Be, thou, for the people, in front of God, so shalt, thou, bring the matters unto God;
text.KJV: Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God shall be with thee: Be thou for the people to God-ward, that thou mayest bring the causes unto God:
text.NABRE: Now, listen to me, and I will give you some advice, and may God be with you. Act as the people’s representative before God, and bring their disputes to God.
text.DRB: But hear my words and councils, and God shall be with thee. Be thou to the people in those things that pertain to God, to report their words unto him:

text.NRSV: teach them the statutes and instructions and make known to them the way they are to go and the things they are to do.
text.Rotherham: and shalt cause to shine upon them, the statutes and the laws,—and make known to them the way wherein they should go, and the work they should do.
text.KJV: And thou shalt teach them ordinances and laws, and shalt shew them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.
text.NABRE: Enlighten them in regard to the statutes and instructions, showing them how they are to conduct themselves and what they are to do.
text.DRB: & to shew to the people the ceremonies & rite of worshipping, and the way wherein they ought to walk, and the work that they ought to do.

text.NRSV: You should also look for able men among all the people, men who fear God, are trustworthy, and hate dishonest gain; set such men over them as officers over thousands, hundreds, fifties and tens.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore shalt look out, from among all the people—men of ability, reverers of God, men of fidelity, haters of extortion,—and place them over [them] as rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
text.KJV: Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, and rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens:
text.NABRE: But you should also look among all the people for able and God-fearing men, trustworthy men who hate dishonest gain, and set them over the people as commanders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
text.DRB: And provide out of all the people men that are wise, & do fear God, in whom there is truth, and that do hate avarice, and appoint of them tribunes, and centurions, and quinquagenarians, and deans,

text.NRSV: Let them sit as judges for the people at all times; let them bring every important case to you, but decide every minor case themselves. So it will be easier for you, and they will bear the burden with you.
text.Rotherham: So shall they judge the people at any time, and it shall be, all the great matters, shall they bring in unto thee, but all the small matters, shall, they themselves, judge,—so, lighten thou the burden for thyself, and let them bear it with thee.
text.KJV: And let them judge the people at all seasons: and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge: so shall it be easier for thyself, and they shall bear the burden with thee.
text.NABRE: Let these render decisions for the people in all routine cases. Every important case they should refer to you, but every lesser case they can settle themselves. Lighten your burden by letting them bear it with you!
text.DRB: which may judge the people at all times: and what great matter soever shall fall out, let them refer it to thee, & let them judge the less matters only: & so it may be lighter for thee, the burden being imparted unto others.

text.NRSV: If you do this, and God so commands you, then you will be able to endure, and all these people will go to their home in peace.”
text.Rotherham: If, this thing, thou wilt do, and God shall command thee, then shalt thou be able to endure,—yea, moreover, all this people, shall go in, unto their dwellings, contented.
text.KJV: If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace.
text.NABRE: If you do this, and God so commands you, you will be able to stand the strain, and all these people, too, will go home content.”
text.DRB: If thou doest this, thou shalt fulfill the commandment of God, & shalt be able to bear his precepts: and all this people shall return to their places with peace.

text.NRSV: So Moses listened to his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
text.Rotherham: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said;
text.KJV: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father in law, and did all that he had said.
text.NABRE: Moses listened to his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
text.DRB: Which things when Moyses heard, he did all things that he had suggested unto him.

text.NRSV: Moses chose able men from all Israel and appointed them as heads over the people, as officers over thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
text.Rotherham: and Moses chose men of ability out of all Israel, and set them to be heads over the people,—rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
text.KJV: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
text.NABRE: He picked out able men from all Israel and put them in charge of the people as commanders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
text.DRB: And choosing substantial men out of all Israel, he appointed them Princes of the people, tribunes, and centurions, and quinquagenarians, and deans.

text.NRSV: And they judged the people at all times; hard cases they brought to Moses, but any minor case they decided themselves.
text.Rotherham: And they shall judge the people, at any time,—the hard matters, shall they bring in unto Moses, but all the small matters, shall, they themselves, judge.
text.KJV: And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.
text.NABRE: They rendered decisions for the people in all routine cases. The more difficult cases they referred to Moses, but all the lesser cases they settled themselves.
text.DRB: Who judged the people at all time: and whatsoever was of greater difficulty they referred to him, themselves judging the easier cases only.

text.NRSV: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went off to his own country.
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent forth his father-in-law,—and he went his way by himself unto his own land.
text.KJV: And Moses let his father in law depart; and he went his way into his own land.
text.NABRE: Then Moses said farewell to his father-in-law, who went off to his own country.
text.DRB: And he dismissed his allied: who returning went into his country.

text.NRSV: On the third new moon after the Israelites had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that very day, they came into the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: In the third month, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, on this day, came they into the desert of Sinai:
text.KJV: In the third month, when the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.
text.NABRE: In the third month after the Israelites’ departure from the land of Egypt, on the first day, they came to the wilderness of Sinai.
text.DRB: In the third month of the departure of Israel out of the Land of Ægypt, this day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.

text.NRSV: They had journeyed from Rephidim, entered the wilderness of Sinai, and camped in the wilderness; Israel camped there in front of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: then was it that they brake up out of Rephidim and came into the desert of Sinai, and encamped in the desert,—yea Israel encamped there before the mountain.
text.KJV: For they were departed from Rephidim, and were come to the desert of Sinai, and had pitched in the wilderness; and there Israel camped before the mount.
text.NABRE: After they made the journey from Rephidim and entered the wilderness of Sinai, they then pitched camp in the wilderness. While Israel was encamped there in front of the mountain,
text.DRB: For departing out of Raphidim, and coming to the desert of Sinai, they camped in the same place, & there Israel pitched their tents over against the mountain.

text.NRSV: Then Moses went up to God; the Lord called to him from the mountain, saying, “Thus you shall say to the house of Jacob, and tell the Israelites:
text.Rotherham: And when Moses, had gone up unto God, then called Yahweh unto him out of the mountain saying, Thus, shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, And tell the sons of Israel:
text.KJV: And Moses went up unto God, and the Lord called unto him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;
text.NABRE: Moses went up to the mountain of God. Then the Lord called to him from the mountain, saying: This is what you will say to the house of Jacob; tell the Israelites:
text.DRB: And Moyses went up to God: and our Lord called him from the mountain, and said: This shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and shalt tell the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: You have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself.
text.Rotherham: Ye, have seen what I did unto the Egyptians,—And how I bare you upon wings of eagles, And brought you in unto myself:—
text.KJV: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and how I bare you on eagles’ wings, and brought you unto myself.
text.NABRE: You have seen how I treated the Egyptians and how I bore you up on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself.
text.DRB: Yourselves have seen what I have done to the Ægyptians, how I have carried you upon the wings of eagles, and have taken you unto me.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, if you obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession out of all the peoples. Indeed, the whole earth is mine,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if ye will, indeed hearken, to my voice, And keep my covenant, Then shall ye be mine as a treasure beyond all the peoples, For, mine, is all the earth;
text.KJV: Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth is mine:
text.NABRE: Now, if you obey me completely and keep my covenant, you will be my treasured possession among all peoples, though all the earth is mine.
text.DRB: If therefore you will hear my voice, and keep my covenant, you shall be my peculiar of all peoples: for all the earth is mine.

text.NRSV: but you shall be for me a priestly kingdom and a holy nation. These are the words that you shall speak to the Israelites.”
text.Rotherham: But, ye, shall be mine, As a kingdom of priests, And a holy nation. These, are the words, which thou shalt speak unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: You will be to me a kingdom of priests, a holy nation. That is what you must tell the Israelites.
text.DRB: And you shall be unto me a Priestly Kingdom, and a holy nation: these are the words that thou shalt speak to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Moses came, summoned the elders of the people, and set before them all these words that the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: Then came Moses, and called for the elders of the people,—and put before them all these words which Yahweh had commanded him.
text.KJV: And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and laid before their faces all these words which the Lord commanded him.
text.NABRE: So Moses went and summoned the elders of the people. When he set before them all that the Lord had ordered him to tell them,
text.DRB: Moyses came: and calling together the nations of the people, he declared all the words which our Lord had commanded him.

text.NRSV: The people all answered as one: “Everything that the Lord has spoken we will do.” Moses reported the words of the people to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And all the people responded together and said, All that Yahweh hath spoken, will we do. And Moses took back the words of the people, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And all the people answered together, and said, All that the Lord hath spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: all the people answered together, “Everything the Lord has said, we will do.” Then Moses brought back to the Lord the response of the people.
text.DRB: And all the people answered together. All things that our Lord hath spoken, we will do. And when Moyses had reported the people's words to our Lord,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “I am going to come to you in a dense cloud, in order that the people may hear when I speak with you and so trust you ever after.” When Moses had told the words of the people to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses: Lo! I, am coming unto thee in the veiling of cloud, in order that the people may hear when I speak with thee, moreover also, that, in thee, they may trust to times age-abiding. Then told Moses the words of the people unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: I am coming to you now in a dense cloud, so that when the people hear me speaking with you, they will also remain faithful to you. When Moses, then, had reported the response of the people to the Lord,
text.DRB: our Lord said to him: Now presently will I come to thee in the darkness of a cloud, that the people may hear me speaking to thee, and may believe thee for ever. Moyses therefore told the people's words to our Lord.

text.NRSV: the Lord said to Moses: “Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow. Have them wash their clothes
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses—Go unto the people, and thou shalt hallow them to-day, and to-morrow,—and they shall wash their clothes;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes,
text.NABRE: the Lord said to Moses: Go to the people and have them sanctify themselves today and tomorrow. Have them wash their garments
text.DRB: Who said to him: Go to the people, and sanctify them today, and tomorrow, and let them wash their garments.

text.NRSV: and prepare for the third day, because on the third day the Lord will come down upon Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.
text.Rotherham: and shall be ready, by the third day,—for on the third day, will Yahweh come down in the sight of all the people upon Mount Sinai.
text.KJV: And be ready against the third day: for the third day the Lord will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.
text.NABRE: and be ready for the third day; for on the third day the Lord will come down on Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.
text.DRB: And let them be ready against the third day: for in the third day the Lord will descend in the sight of all the people upon the mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: You shall set limits for the people all around, saying, ‘Be careful not to go up the mountain or to touch the edge of it. Any who touch the mountain shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt set bounds for the people round about saying, Take heed to yourselves—that ye go not up into the mountain, nor touch the boundary thereof, whosoever toucheth the mountain, shall, surely die.
text.KJV: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:
text.NABRE: Set limits for the people all around, saying: Take care not to go up the mountain, or even to touch its edge. All who touch the mountain must be put to death.
text.DRB: And thou shalt appoint certain limits to the people in circuit, and shalt say to them: Beware ye ascend not into the mount, & that you touch not the ends thereof: every one that toucheth the mount, dying shall die.

text.NRSV: No hand shall touch them, but they shall be stoned or shot with arrows; whether animal or human being, they shall not live.’ When the trumpet sounds a long blast, they may go up on the mountain.”
text.Rotherham: No hand shall touch it but he shall be, surely stoned, or be, surely shot, whether beast or man, he shall not live,—When the ram’s horn soundeth, they themselves, shall come up within the mount,
text.KJV: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live: when the trumpet soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.
text.NABRE: No hand shall touch them, but they must be stoned to death or killed with arrows. Whether human being or beast, they must not be allowed to live. Only when the ram’s horn sounds may they go up on the mountain.
text.DRB: Hand shall not touch him, but he shall be stoned to death, or shall be shot through with arrows: whether it be beast, or man, it shall not live. When the trumpet shall begin to sound, then let them ascend into the mount.

text.NRSV: So Moses went down from the mountain to the people. He consecrated the people, and they washed their clothes.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses went down out of the mount, unto the people,—and hallowed the people, and they washed their clothes.
text.KJV: And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.
text.NABRE: Then Moses came down from the mountain to the people and had them sanctify themselves, and they washed their garments.
text.DRB: And Moyses came down from the mount to the people, and sanctified them. And when they had washed their garments,

text.NRSV: And he said to the people, “Prepare for the third day; do not go near a woman.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto the people, Be ready, by the third day,—do not approach a woman.
text.KJV: And he said unto the people, Be ready against the third day: come not at your wives.
text.NABRE: He said to the people, “Be ready for the third day. Do not approach a woman.”
text.DRB: he said to them: Be ready against the third day, and come not near your wives.

text.NRSV: On the morning of the third day there was thunder and lightning, as well as a thick cloud on the mountain, and a blast of a trumpet so loud that all the people who were in the camp trembled.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the third day, when the morning had come, that there were thunderings and lightnings, and a heavy cloud upon the mount, and the sound of a horn, loud exceedingly,—and all the people who were in the camp trembled.
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the third day in the morning, that there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of the trumpet exceeding loud; so that all the people that was in the camp trembled.
text.NABRE: The Great Theophany. On the morning of the third day there were peals of thunder and lightning, and a heavy cloud over the mountain, and a very loud blast of the shofar, so that all the people in the camp trembled.
text.DRB: And now the third day was come, and the morning appeared: and behold thunders began to be heard, and lightnings to flash, and a very thick cloud to cover the mount, & the noise of the trumpet sounded exceedingly: and the people, that was in the camp, feared.

text.NRSV: Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet God. They took their stand at the foot of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And Moses brought forth the people, to meet God out of the camp,—and they stationed themselves in the lower part of the mount,
text.KJV: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet with God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
text.NABRE: But Moses led the people out of the camp to meet God, and they stationed themselves at the foot of the mountain.
text.DRB: And when Moyses had brought them forth to meet with God from the place of the camp, they stood at the bottom of the mount.

text.NRSV: Now Mount Sinai was wrapped in smoke, because the Lord had descended upon it in fire; the smoke went up like the smoke of a kiln, while the whole mountain shook violently.
text.Rotherham: And Mount Sinai, smoked, all over, because Yahweh had come down thereon in fire,—and the smoke thereof went up as the smoke of a furnace, and all the mountain trembled exceedingly.
text.KJV: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the Lord descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
text.NABRE: Now Mount Sinai was completely enveloped in smoke, because the Lord had come down upon it in fire. The smoke rose from it as though from a kiln, and the whole mountain trembled violently.
text.DRB: And all the mount Sinai smoked: for because our Lord was descended upon it in fire, and the smoke arose from it as out of a furnace: and all the mount was terrible.

text.NRSV: As the blast of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moses would speak and God would answer him in thunder.
text.Rotherham: And as oft as the sound of the horn went on and became exceeding loud, Moses, spake and, God, responded to him with a voice.
text.KJV: And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.
text.NABRE: The blast of the shofar grew louder and louder, while Moses was speaking and God was answering him with thunder.
text.DRB: And the sound of the trumpet grew louder by little and little, and was drawn out a length: Moyses spake, and God answered him.

text.NRSV: When the Lord descended upon Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain, the Lord summoned Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.
text.Rotherham: Thus came Yahweh down upon Mount Sinai unto the top of the mount,—and Yahweh called Moses unto the top of the mount, and Moses went up.
text.KJV: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount: and the Lord called Moses up to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.
text.NABRE: When the Lord came down upon Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain, the Lord summoned Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.
text.DRB: And our Lord descended upon the mount Sinai in the very top of the mount, and he called Moyses into the top thereof. Whither when he was ascended,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go down and warn the people not to break through to the Lord to look; otherwise many of them will perish.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Go down, adjure the people,—lest they press through unto Yahweh to see, and so there fall from among them a multitude.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the Lord to gaze, and many of them perish.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord told Moses: Go down and warn the people not to break through to the Lord in order to see him; otherwise many of them will be struck down.
text.DRB: he said unto him: Go down, and charge the people, lest perhaps they will pass their limits to see the Lord, and a very great multitude of them perish.

text.NRSV: Even the priests who approach the Lord must consecrate themselves or the Lord will break out against them.”
text.Rotherham: Yea, even the priests who do approach unto Yahweh, must hallow themselves,—lest Yahweh break in upon them.
text.KJV: And let the priests also, which come near to the Lord, sanctify themselves, lest the Lord break forth upon them.
text.NABRE: For their part, the priests, who approach the Lord must sanctify themselves; else the Lord will break out in anger against them.
text.DRB: The Priests also that come to the Lord, let them be sanctified, lest he strike them.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the Lord, “The people are not permitted to come up to Mount Sinai; for you yourself warned us, saying, ‘Set limits around the mountain and keep it holy.’”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh, The people cannot come up into Mount Sinai,—for, thou thyself, hast adjured us saying: Set bounds to the mountain and hallow it.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the Lord, The people cannot come up to mount Sinai: for thou chargedst us, saying, Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.
text.NABRE: But Moses said to the Lord, “The people cannot go up to Mount Sinai, for you yourself warned us, saying: Set limits around the mountain to make it sacred.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to our Lord: The common people cannot ascend into the mount Sinai: for thou didst charge, and command, saying: Put limits about the mount, and sanctify it.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “Go down, and come up bringing Aaron with you; but do not let either the priests or the people break through to come up to the Lord; otherwise he will break out against them.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—Away, down, then shalt thou come up, thou and Aaron with thee,—but as for the priests and the people, let it not be that they press through to come up unto Yahweh lest he break in upon them.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, Away, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee: but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the Lord, lest he break forth upon them.
text.NABRE: So the Lord said to him: Go down and come up along with Aaron. But do not let the priests and the people break through to come up to the Lord; else he will break out against them.”
text.DRB: To whom our Lord said: Go, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, and Aaron with thee: but the Priests and the people let them not pass the limits, nor ascend to the Lord, lest perhaps he kill them.

text.NRSV: So Moses went down to the people and told them.
text.Rotherham: So Moses went down unto the people,—and said [these things] unto them.
text.KJV: So Moses went down unto the people, and spake unto them.
text.NABRE: So Moses went down to the people and spoke to them.
text.DRB: And Moyses went down to the people, and told them all.

text.NRSV: Then God spoke all these words:
text.Rotherham: And God spake all these words saying:—
text.KJV: And God spake all these words, saying,
text.NABRE: Then God spoke all these words:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake all these words.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery;
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh thy God,—who have brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of servants:—
text.KJV: I am the Lord thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
text.NABRE: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
text.DRB: I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee forth out of the Land of Ægypt, out of the house of servitude.

text.NRSV: you shall have no other gods before me.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not have other gods besides me.
text.KJV: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
text.NABRE: You shall not have other gods beside me.
