text.DRB: Thou shalt not have strange gods before me.

text.NRSV: You shall not make for yourself an idol, whether in the form of anything that is in heaven above, or that is on the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not make to thee an image, or any form, that is in the heavens above,—or that is in the earth beneath,—or that is in the waters beneath the earth:
text.KJV: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth:
text.NABRE: You shall not make for yourself an idol or a likeness of anything in the heavens above or on the earth below or in the waters beneath the earth;
text.DRB: Thou shalt not make to thee a graven thing, nor any similitude that is in heaven above, & that is in the earth beneath, neither of those things that are in the waters under the earth.

text.NRSV: You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I the Lord your God am a jealous God, punishing children for the iniquity of parents, to the third and the fourth generation of those who reject me,
text.Rotherham: thou shalt not bow thyself down to them nor be led to serve them—For, I, Yahweh, thy God, am a jealous GOD, visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons, unto three [generations] and, unto four, of them that hate me;
text.KJV: Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the Lord thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me;
text.NABRE: you shall not bow down before them or serve them. For I, the Lord, your God, am a jealous God, inflicting punishment for their ancestors’ wickedness on the children of those who hate me, down to the third and fourth generation;
text.DRB: Thou shalt not adore them, nor serve them: I am the Lord thy God mighty, jealous, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, upon the third and fourth generation of them that hate me:

text.NRSV: but showing steadfast love to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
text.Rotherham: but shewing lovingkindness unto thousands [of generations],—of them who love me, and keep my commandments.
text.KJV: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me, and keep my commandments.
text.NABRE: but showing love down to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
text.DRB: and doing mercy upon thousands to them that love me, and keep my precepts.

text.NRSV: You shall not make wrongful use of the name of the Lord your God, for the Lord will not acquit anyone who misuses his name.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not utter the name of Yahweh thy God for falsehood, for Yahweh will not let him go unpunished who uttereth his name for falsehood.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
text.NABRE: You shall not invoke the name of the Lord, your God, in vain. For the Lord will not leave unpunished anyone who invokes his name in vain.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain. For the Lord will not hold him innocent that shall take the name of the Lord his God vainly.

text.NRSV: Remember the sabbath day, and keep it holy.
text.Rotherham: Remember the sabbath day, to hallow it:
text.KJV: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
text.NABRE: Remember the sabbath day—keep it holy.
text.DRB: Remember that thou sanctify the Sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall labor and do all your work.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;
text.KJV: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:
text.NABRE: Six days you may labor and do all your work,
text.DRB: Six days shalt thou work, and shalt do all thy works.

text.NRSV: But the seventh day is a sabbath to the Lord your God; you shall not do any work—you, your son or your daughter, your male or female slave, your livestock, or the alien resident in your towns.
text.Rotherham: but, the seventh day, is a sabbath unto Yahweh thy God,—thou shalt do no work, thou nor thy son nor thy daughter nor thy servant, nor thy handmaid, nor thy beast, nor thy sojourner who is within thy gates,
text.KJV: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the Lord thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates:
text.NABRE: but the seventh day is a sabbath of the Lord your God. You shall not do any work, either you, your son or your daughter, your male or female slave, your work animal, or the resident alien within your gates.
text.DRB: But on the seventh day is the sabbath of the Lord thy God: thou shalt do no work in it, thou and thy son, and thy daughter, thy man servant, and thy woman servant, thy beast, and the stranger that is within thy gates.

text.NRSV: For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, but rested the seventh day; therefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day and consecrated it.
text.Rotherham: For, in six days, did Yahweh make the heavens and the earth, [and] the sea—and all that in them is, and rested on the seventh day,—for this cause, Yahweh blessed the sabbath day and hallowed it.
text.KJV: For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested the seventh day: wherefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
text.NABRE: For in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them; but on the seventh day he rested. That is why the Lord has blessed the sabbath day and made it holy.
text.DRB: For six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and the sea, and all things that are in them, and rested in the seventh day, therefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day, and sanctified it.

text.NRSV: Honor your father and your mother, so that your days may be long in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
text.Rotherham: Honour thy father, and thy mother,—that thy days may be prolonged upon the soil, which Yahweh thy God is about to give unto thee.
text.KJV: Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
text.NABRE: Honor your father and your mother, that you may have a long life in the land the Lord your God is giving you.
text.DRB: Honour thy father and thy mother, that thou mayst be long lived upon the earth, which the Lord thy God will give thee.

text.NRSV: You shall not murder.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not commit murder.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not kill.
text.NABRE: You shall not kill.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not murder.

text.NRSV: You shall not commit adultery.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not commit adultery.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not commit adultery.
text.NABRE: You shall not commit adultery.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

text.NRSV: You shall not steal.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not steal.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not steal.
text.NABRE: You shall not steal.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not steal.

text.NRSV: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not testify against thy neighbour, with a witness of falsehood.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not speak against thy neighbour false testimony.

text.NRSV: You shall not covet your neighbor’s house; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or male or female slave, or ox, or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not covet, thy neighbour’s house,—thou shalt not covet, thy neighbour’s wife nor his servant nor his handmaid nor his ox, nor his ass, nor anything which belongeth unto thy neighbour.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.
text.NABRE: You shall not covet your neighbor’s house. You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, his male or female slave, his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house: neither shalt thou desire his wife, nor servant, nor handmaid, nor ox, nor ass, nor any thing that is his.

text.NRSV: When all the people witnessed the thunder and lightning, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking, they were afraid and trembled and stood at a distance,
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, were witnessing the voices and the torches, and the sound of the horn, and the mountain smoking,—so then the people were struck with awe and shrank back, and stood afar off.
text.KJV: And all the people saw the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the noise of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking: and when the people saw it, they removed, and stood afar off.
text.NABRE: Moses Accepted as Mediator. Now as all the people witnessed the thunder and lightning, the blast of the shofar and the mountain smoking, they became afraid and trembled. So they took up a position farther away
text.DRB: And all the people saw the voices, and the flames, and the sound of the trumpet, and the mount smoking: and being frightened and strucken with fear they stood a far off,

text.NRSV: and said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak to us, or we will die.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto Moses, Speak, thou, with us, and we will hear,—but let not God speak with us lest we die.
text.KJV: And they said unto Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear: but let not God speak with us, lest we die.
text.NABRE: and said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak to us, or we shall die.”
text.DRB: saying to Moyses: Speak thou to us, and we will hear: let not our Lord Speak to us, lest perhaps we die.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the people, “Do not be afraid; for God has come only to test you and to put the fear of him upon you so that you do not sin.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the people—Do not fear, for to the end he may prove you, hath God come,—and to the end the reverence of him may be upon your faces that ye sin not.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the people, Fear not: for God is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before your faces, that ye sin not.
text.NABRE: Moses answered the people, “Do not be afraid, for God has come only to test you and put the fear of him upon you so you do not sin.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to the people: Fear not: for God came to prove you, and that his terror might be in you, and you should not sin.

text.NRSV: Then the people stood at a distance, while Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was.
text.Rotherham: So then the people stood afar off, and, Moses, approached unto the thick gloom, where God was.
text.KJV: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.
text.NABRE: So the people remained at a distance, while Moses approached the dark cloud where God was.
text.DRB: And the people stood a far off. But Moyses went unto the dark cloud wherein God was.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Thus you shall say to the Israelites: “You have seen for yourselves that I spoke with you from heaven.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Thus, shalt thou say, unto the sons of Israel,—Ye yourselves, have seen that out of the heavens, have I spoken with you.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel, Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
text.NABRE: The Covenant Code. The Lord said to Moses: This is what you will say to the Israelites: You have seen for yourselves that I have spoken to you from heaven.
text.DRB: Our Lord said moreover to Moyses: This shalt thou say to the children of Israel: You have seen that from heaven I have spoken to you.

text.NRSV: You shall not make gods of silver alongside me, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not make [aught] beside me,—neither gods of silver, nor gods of gold, shall ye make to yourselves.
text.KJV: Ye shall not make with me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold.
text.NABRE: You shall not make alongside of me gods of silver, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.
text.DRB: You shall not make gods of silver, nor gods of gold shall you make to you.

text.NRSV: You need make for me only an altar of earth and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your offerings of well-being, your sheep and your oxen; in every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.
text.Rotherham: An altar of earth, shalt thou make to me, then shalt thou offer thereupon thine ascending-sacrifices and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen. In every place where I may mention my name, will I come in unto thee and will bless thee.
text.KJV: An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt offerings, and thy peace offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen: in all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee.
text.NABRE: An altar of earth make for me, and sacrifice upon it your burnt offerings and communion sacrifices, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause my name to be invoked I will come to you and bless you.
text.DRB: An altar of earth you shall make to me, and you shall offer upon it your holocausts and pacifics, your sheep and oxen in every place where the memory of my name shall be: I will come to thee, and will bless thee.

text.NRSV: But if you make for me an altar of stone, do not build it of hewn stones; for if you use a chisel upon it you profane it.
text.Rotherham: But if, an altar of stones, thou wilt make to me, thou shalt not build them hewn,—for as soon as thou hast lifted, thy sharp tool, thereupon, thou hast profaned it;
text.KJV: And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it.
text.NABRE: But if you make an altar of stone for me, do not build it of cut stone, for by putting a chisel to it you profane it.
text.DRB: And if thou make an Altar of stone unto me, thou shalt not build it of hewed stones: for if thou lift up thy knife over it, it shall be polluted.

text.NRSV: You shall not go up by steps to my altar, so that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.”
text.Rotherham: neither shalt thou go up, by steps unto mine altar,—lest by any means thy shame be discovered thereupon.
text.KJV: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.
text.NABRE: You shall not ascend to my altar by steps, lest your nakedness be exposed.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not go up by grises unto mine Altar, lest thy turpitude be discovered.

text.NRSV: These are the ordinances that you shall set before them:
text.Rotherham: Now, these are the regulations which thou shalt put before them.
text.KJV: Now these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.
text.NABRE: These are the ordinances you shall lay before them.
text.DRB: These are the judgments which thou shalt propose to them.

text.NRSV: When you buy a male Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, but in the seventh he shall go out a free person, without debt.
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt acquire a servant who is a Hebrew, six years, shall he serve,—but in the seventh shall he go out freely—for nought.
text.KJV: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
text.NABRE: When you purchase a Hebrew slave, he is to serve you for six years, but in the seventh year he shall leave as a free person without any payment.
text.DRB: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years shall he serve thee: in the seventh he shall go out free gratis.

text.NRSV: If he comes in single, he shall go out single; if he comes in married, then his wife shall go out with him.
text.Rotherham: If, by himself, he came in, by himself, shall he go out,—if he was married, then shall his wife go out with him.
text.KJV: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.
text.NABRE: If he comes into service alone, he shall leave alone; if he comes with a wife, his wife shall leave with him.
text.DRB: With the raiment he entered in, with the like let him go out: if having a wife, his wife also shall go out with him.

text.NRSV: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master’s and he shall go out alone.
text.Rotherham: If his lord gave him a wife, and she have borne him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her lords, and, he shall go out by himself.
text.KJV: If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
text.NABRE: But if his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the woman and her children belong to her master and the man shall leave alone.
text.DRB: But if his Lord give him a wife, and she bear sons & daughters, the woman and her children shall be her Lord's; but himself shall go out with his raiment.

text.NRSV: But if the slave declares, “I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out a free person,”
text.Rotherham: But, if the servant shall plainly say, I love my lord, and my wife and my sons,—I will not go out free,
text.KJV: And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free:
text.NABRE: If, however, the slave declares, ‘I love my master and my wife and children; I will not leave as a free person,’
text.DRB: And if the servant say: I love my Lord and wife & children, I will not go out free:

text.NRSV: then his master shall bring him before God. He shall be brought to the door or the doorpost; and his master shall pierce his ear with an awl; and he shall serve him for life.
text.Rotherham: then shall his lord bring him near unto God, and shall bring him near unto the door, or unto the door-post,—and his lord shall pierce his ear with an awl, so shall he serve him all his life.
text.KJV: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever.
text.NABRE: his master shall bring him to God and there, at the door or doorpost, he shall pierce his ear with an awl, thus keeping him as his slave forever.
text.DRB: his lord shall present him to the gods, and he shall be set to the door and the posts, and he shall bore his ear through with an awl: and he shall be his bond man for ever.

text.NRSV: When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she shall not go out as the male slaves do.
text.Rotherham: And when a man shall sell his daughter to be a handmaid, she shall not go out according to the out-going of the men-servants,
text.KJV: And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.
text.NABRE: When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she shall not go free as male slaves do.
text.DRB: If any man sell his daughter to be a servant, she shall not go out, as bondwomen are wont to go out.

text.NRSV: If she does not please her master, who designated her for himself, then he shall let her be redeemed; he shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt unfairly with her.
text.Rotherham: If she is uncomely in the eyes of her lord, who hath not assigned her in marriage, then shall he suffer her to be redeemed: to a strange people, shall he not have power to sell her in that he hath dealt treacherously with her.
text.KJV: If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.
text.NABRE: But if she displeases her master, who had designated her for himself, he shall let her be redeemed. He has no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has broken faith with her.
text.DRB: If she mislike the eyes of her master to whom she was delivered, he shall dismiss her: but he shall not have authority to sell her unto a strange people, if he despise her.

text.NRSV: If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her as with a daughter.
text.Rotherham: And if, to his son, he assign her, according to the custom for daughters, shall he do for her.
text.KJV: And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
text.NABRE: If he designates her for his son, he shall treat her according to the ordinance for daughters.
text.DRB: But if he despouse her to his son, he shall do to her after the manner of daughters.

text.NRSV: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish the food, clothing, or marital rights of the first wife.
text.Rotherham: If he take to himself another, her food her clothing, and her marriage-right, shall he not withdraw.
text.KJV: If he take him another wife; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.
text.NABRE: If he takes another wife, he shall not withhold her food, her clothing, or her conjugal rights.
text.DRB: And if he take another wife for him, he shall provide her a marriage, and raiment, and the price of her chastity he shall not deny.

text.NRSV: And if he does not do these three things for her, she shall go out without debt, without payment of money.
text.Rotherham: But if these three, he will not do for her, then shall she go out for nought, without silver.
text.KJV: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money.
text.NABRE: If he does not do these three things for her, she may leave without cost, without any payment.
text.DRB: If he do not these three things, she shall go out gratis without money.

text.NRSV: Whoever strikes a person mortally shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall surely be put to death.
text.KJV: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.
text.NABRE: Personal Injury. Whoever strikes someone a mortal blow must be put to death.
text.DRB: He that striketh a man willfully to kill him, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: If it was not premeditated, but came about by an act of God, then I will appoint for you a place to which the killer may flee.
text.Rotherham: But he who hath not lain in wait, but, God, hath occasioned him to come to his hand, then will I appoint for thee a place, whither he may flee.
text.KJV: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
text.NABRE: However, regarding the one who did not hunt another down, but God caused death to happen by his hand, I will set apart for you a place to which that one may flee.
text.DRB: But he that did not lie in wait for him, but God delivered him into his hands, I will appoint thee a place whereunto he ought to fly.

text.NRSV: But if someone willfully attacks and kills another by treachery, you shall take the killer from my altar for execution.
text.Rotherham: But, when a man shall act presumptuously against his neighbour to slay him with guile, from mine altar, shalt thou take him to die.
text.KJV: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
text.NABRE: But when someone kills a neighbor after maliciously scheming to do so, you must take him even from my altar and put him to death.
text.DRB: If a man of set purpose kill his neighbour, and by lying in wait for him, thou shalt pluck him out from mine Altar, that he may die.

text.NRSV: Whoever strikes father or mother shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, he that smiteth his father or his mother, shall, surely be put to death.
text.KJV: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever strikes father or mother shall be put to death.
text.DRB: He that striketh his father or mother, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: Whoever kidnaps a person, whether that person has been sold or is still held in possession, shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, he that stealeth a man of the sons of Israel, and selleth him, or he be found in his hand, shall, surely be put to death.
text.KJV: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: A kidnapper, whether he sells the person or the person is found in his possession, shall be put to death.
text.DRB: He that shall steal a man, and sell him, being convicted of the trespass, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: Whoever curses father or mother shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And he who revileth his father or his mother, shall, surely be put to death.
text.KJV: And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever curses father or mother shall be put to death.
text.DRB: He that curseth his father, or mother, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: When individuals quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or fist so that the injured party, though not dead, is confined to bed,
text.Rotherham: And when men strive together, and one shall smite the other, with a stone or with his fist,—and he die not, but shall fall to his bed;—
text.KJV: And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keepeth his bed:
text.NABRE: When men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist, not mortally, but enough to put him in bed,
text.DRB: If men fall at words, and the one strike his neighbour with a stone or with his fist, and he die not, but lie in his bed:

text.NRSV: but recovers and walks around outside with the help of a staff, then the assailant shall be free of liability, except to pay for the loss of time, and to arrange for full recovery.
text.Rotherham: if he rise and shall walk abroad on his staff, then shall he that smote him be acquitted,—only, for his loss of time, he shall pay, and, shall surely heal, him.
text.KJV: If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.
text.NABRE: the one who struck the blow shall be acquitted, provided the other can get up and walk around with the help of his staff. Still, he must compensate him for his recovery time and make provision for his complete healing.
text.DRB: if he rise, and walk abroad upon his staff, he that did strike shall be quit, yet so that he make restitution for his work, and for his expenses upon the physicians.

text.NRSV: When a slaveowner strikes a male or female slave with a rod and the slave dies immediately, the owner shall be punished.
text.Rotherham: And when a man shall smite his servant or his handmaid with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall, surely be avenged;
text.KJV: And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished.
text.NABRE: When someone strikes his male or female slave with a rod so that the slave dies under his hand, the act shall certainly be avenged.
text.DRB: He that striketh his man or maid-servant with a rod, and they die in his hands, he shall be guilty of the crime.

text.NRSV: But if the slave survives a day or two, there is no punishment; for the slave is the owner’s property.
text.Rotherham: nevertheless if, for a day or for two days, he continue, he shall not be avenged, for, his silver, he is.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he is his money.
text.NABRE: If, however, the slave survives for a day or two, he is not to be punished, since the slave is his own property.
text.DRB: But if the party remain alive a day or two, he shall not be subject to punishment, because it is his money.

text.NRSV: When people who are fighting injure a pregnant woman so that there is a miscarriage, and yet no further harm follows, the one responsible shall be fined what the woman’s husband demands, paying as much as the judges determine.
text.Rotherham: And when men strive together and push against a woman with child, and she miscarry, but there is no other mischief, he shall, surely be fined, according as the woman’s husband shall lay upon him, but he shall give it through judges.
text.KJV: If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart from her, and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman’s husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
text.NABRE: When men have a fight and hurt a pregnant woman, so that she suffers a miscarriage, but no further injury, the guilty one shall be fined as much as the woman’s husband demands of him, and he shall pay in the presence of the judges.
text.DRB: If certain fall at words, and one strike a woman with child, and she indeed abort, but herself live; he shall be subject to so much damage as the woman's husband shall require, and as arbiters shall award.

text.NRSV: If any harm follows, then you shall give life for life,
text.Rotherham: But, if mischief follow, then shalt thou give life for life;
text.KJV: And if any mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,
text.NABRE: But if injury ensues, you shall give life for life,
text.DRB: But if her death do ensue thereupon, he shall render life for life,

text.NRSV: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
text.Rotherham: eye for eye, tooth for tooth,—hand for hand, foot for foot,
text.KJV: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
text.NABRE: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
text.DRB: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

text.NRSV: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
text.Rotherham: brand for brand, wound for wound,—stripe for stripe.
text.KJV: Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
text.NABRE: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
text.DRB: adustion for adustion, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

text.NRSV: When a slaveowner strikes the eye of a male or female slave, destroying it, the owner shall let the slave go, a free person, to compensate for the eye.
text.Rotherham: And, when a man smiteth the eye of his servant, or the eye of his handmaid, and destroyeth it, he shall send him forth, free, for his eye;
text.KJV: And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
text.NABRE: When someone strikes his male or female slave in the eye and destroys the use of the eye, he shall let the slave go free in compensation for the eye.
text.DRB: If any man strike the eye of his man-servant or maid-servant, and leave them but one eye, he shall make them free for the eye which he put out.

text.NRSV: If the owner knocks out a tooth of a male or female slave, the slave shall be let go, a free person, to compensate for the tooth.
text.Rotherham: or, if, the tooth of his servant or the tooth of his handmaid, he knock out, he shall send him forth, free, for his tooth.
text.KJV: And if he smite out his manservant’s tooth, or his maidservant’s tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
text.NABRE: If he knocks out a tooth of his male or female slave, he shall let the slave go free in compensation for the tooth.
text.DRB: Also if he strike out a tooth of his man-servant or maid-servant, he shall in like manner make them free.

text.NRSV: When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall not be liable.
text.Rotherham: And when an ox goreth a man or a woman, and death ensueth, the ox shall, surely be stoned and his flesh shall not be eaten, and the owner of the ox, shall be quit.
text.KJV: If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
text.NABRE: When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox must be stoned; its meat may not be eaten. The owner of the ox, however, shall be free of blame.
text.DRB: If an ox with his horn strike a man or a woman, and they die, he shall be stoned: and his flesh shall not be eaten, the owner also of the ox shall be quit.

text.NRSV: If the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has been warned but has not restrained it, and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: But, if, the ox, was wont to gore before that time and it hath been attested to his owner, and he hath not proceeded to put him under guard, and he causeth the death of man or woman, the ox, shall be stoned, and, his owner also, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past, and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: But if an ox was previously in the habit of goring people and its owner, though warned, would not watch it; should it then kill a man or a woman, not only must the ox be stoned, but its owner also must be put to death.
text.DRB: But if the ox were wont to strike from yesterday and the day before, and they warned his master, neither did he shut him up, and he kill a man or a woman; both the ox shall be stoned, & they shall put to death his owner also.

text.NRSV: If a ransom is imposed on the owner, then the owner shall pay whatever is imposed for the redemption of the victim’s life.
text.Rotherham: If, a sin-covering, be laid on him, then shall he give a ransom for his life, according to whatsoever may be laid on him:
text.KJV: If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
text.NABRE: If, however, a fine is imposed on him, he must pay in ransom for his life whatever amount is imposed on him.
text.DRB: And if they set a price upon him, he shall give for his life whatsoever he is asked.

text.NRSV: If it gores a boy or a girl, the owner shall be dealt with according to this same rule.
text.Rotherham: if, a son, he gore or, a daughter, he gore, according to this regulation, shall it be done to him;
text.KJV: Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
text.NABRE: This ordinance applies if it is a boy or a girl that the ox gores.
text.DRB: Also if with his horn he strike a son, or a daughter, he shall be subject to the like sentence.

text.NRSV: If the ox gores a male or female slave, the owner shall pay to the slaveowner thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
text.Rotherham: if, a servant the ox gore, or a handmaid, thirty shekels of silver, shall he give to his lord, and, the ox, shall be stoned.
text.KJV: If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
text.NABRE: But if it is a male or a female slave that it gores, he must pay the owner of the slave thirty shekels of silver, and the ox must be stoned.
text.DRB: If he invade a bondman or bondwoman, he shall give thirty sicles of silver to their master, but the ox shall be stoned.

text.NRSV: If someone leaves a pit open, or digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
text.Rotherham: And when a man openeth a pit, or when a man diggeth a pit, and doth not cover it,—and there falleth thereinto an ox or an ass,
text.KJV: And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;
text.NABRE: Property Damage. When someone uncovers or digs a cistern and does not cover it over again, should an ox or a donkey fall into it,
text.DRB: If a man open a cistern, & dig one, & do not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall into it,

text.NRSV: the owner of the pit shall make restitution, giving money to its owner, but keeping the dead animal.
text.Rotherham: the owner of the pit, shall make it good, silver, shall he pay back to the owner thereof,—and the dead beast, shall be his.
text.KJV: The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.
text.NABRE: the owner of the cistern must make good by restoring the value of the animal to its owner, but the dead animal he may keep.
text.DRB: the owner of the cistern shall pay the price of the beasts: and that which died shall be his own.

text.NRSV: If someone’s ox hurts the ox of another, so that it dies, then they shall sell the live ox and divide the price of it; and the dead animal they shall also divide.
text.Rotherham: And when the ox of one man thrusteth the ox of his neighbour so that it dieth, then shall they sell the live ox and divide the silver thereof, and the dead ox also, shall they divide,
text.KJV: And if one man’s ox hurt another’s, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead ox also they shall divide.
text.NABRE: When one man’s ox hurts another’s ox and it dies, they shall sell the live ox and divide this money as well as the dead animal equally between them.
text.DRB: If one man's ox gore another man's ox, & he die, they shall sell the ox that liveth, and shall divide the price, and the carcass of that which died they shall part between them.

text.NRSV: But if it was known that the ox was accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has not restrained it, the owner shall restore ox for ox, but keep the dead animal.
text.Rotherham: Or if it was known that, an ox, was, wont to gore, before that time, and his owner proceeded not to put him under guard, he shall surely make good,—an ox for the ox, and, the dead one, shall be his.
text.KJV: Or if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past, and his owner hath not kept him in; he shall surely pay ox for ox; and the dead shall be his own.
text.NABRE: But if it was known that the ox was previously in the habit of goring and its owner would not watch it, he must make full restitution, an ox for an ox; but the dead animal he may keep.
text.DRB: But if he knew that his ox was wont to strike from yesterday and the day before, and his master did not keep him in, he shall render ox for ox, and shall take the carcass whole.

text.NRSV: When someone steals an ox or a sheep, and slaughters it or sells it, the thief shall pay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep. The thief shall make restitution, but if unable to do so, shall be sold for the theft.
text.Rotherham: When a man stealeth an ox or a sheep, and slayeth it, or selleth it, with five of the herd, shall he make good—for the ox, or with four of the flock, for the sheep.
text.KJV: If a man shall steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.
text.NABRE: [If a thief is caught in the act of housebreaking and beaten to death, there is no bloodguilt involved.
text.DRB: If any man steal an ox or a sheep, and kill or sell it: he shall restore five oxen for one ox, and four sheep for one sheep.

text.NRSV: If a thief is found breaking in, and is beaten to death, no bloodguilt is incurred;
text.Rotherham: If, in the place of breaking in, the thief be found and he is smitten so that he dieth, there is due for him no shedding of blood:
text.KJV: If a thief be found breaking up, and be smitten that he die, there shall no blood be shed for him.
text.NABRE: But if after sunrise he is thus beaten, there is bloodguilt.] He must make full restitution. If he has nothing, he shall be sold to pay for his theft.
text.DRB: If the thief be found breaking up the house or undermining it, and taking a wound die; the striker shall not be guilty of blood.

text.NRSV: but if it happens after sunrise, bloodguilt is incurred.
text.Rotherham: should the sun have arisen upon him, there would be shedding of blood due for him,—he is surely to make restitution, if he hath nothing, then is he to be sold, for his theft.
text.KJV: If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be blood shed for him; for he should make full restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
text.NABRE: If what he stole is found alive in his possession, be it an ox, a donkey or a sheep, he shall make twofold restitution.
text.DRB: But if he do this when the sun is risen, he hath committed manslaughter, and himself shall die. If he have not wherewith to make restitution for the theft, himself shall be sold.

text.NRSV: When the animal, whether ox or donkey or sheep, is found alive in the thief’s possession, the thief shall pay double.
text.Rotherham: If the thing stolen is found in his hand—whether ox or ass or sheep, alive, with two, shall he make restitution.
text.KJV: If the theft be certainly found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep; he shall restore double.
text.NABRE: When someone causes a field or a vineyard to be grazed over, by sending his cattle to graze in another’s field, he must make restitution with the best produce of his own field or vineyard.
text.DRB: If that which he stole be found with him alive, either ox, or ass, or sheep; he shall restore double.

text.NRSV: When someone causes a field or vineyard to be grazed over, or lets livestock loose to graze in someone else’s field, restitution shall be made from the best in the owner’s field or vineyard.
text.Rotherham: When a man causeth a field or a vineyard to be depastured, or hath sent in his own cattle and stripped the field of another, he shall, surely make restitution, out of his own field, according to the yield thereof; or if, all the field, he depasture, with the best of his own field, or with the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.
text.KJV: If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall put in his beast, and shall feed in another man’s field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.
text.NABRE: If a fire breaks out, catches on to thorn bushes, and consumes shocked grain, standing grain, or the field itself, the one who started the fire must make full restitution.
text.DRB: If any man hurt a field or a vineyard, and let go his beast to feed upon that which is other men's; the best of whatsoever he hath in his own field, or in his vineyard, he shall restore, according to the estimation of the damage.

text.NRSV: When fire breaks out and catches in thorns so that the stacked grain or the standing grain or the field is consumed, the one who started the fire shall make full restitution.
text.Rotherham: When a fire breaketh out and hath come upon thorns and so there is consumed a stack of sheaves, or the standing corn, or the field, he that kindled the fire, shall surely make restitution.
text.KJV: If fire break out, and catch in thorns, so that the stacks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field, be consumed therewith; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.
text.NABRE: Trusts and Loans. When someone gives money or articles to another for safekeeping and they are stolen from the latter’s house, the thief, if caught, must make twofold restitution.
text.DRB: If fire breaking forth light upon the thorns, and catch stacks of corn, or corn standing in the fields, he shall render the damage that kindled the fire.

text.NRSV: When someone delivers to a neighbor money or goods for safekeeping, and they are stolen from the neighbor’s house, then the thief, if caught, shall pay double.
text.Rotherham: When a man giveth unto his neighbour silver or jewelry, to keep, and it is stolen out of the house of the man:
text.KJV: If a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief be found, let him pay double.
text.NABRE: If the thief is not caught, the owner of the house shall be brought to God, to swear that he himself did not lay hands on his neighbor’s property.
text.DRB: If a man commit money, or a vessel unto his friend to keep, & they be stolen away from him that received them; if the thief be found, he shall restore double:

text.NRSV: If the thief is not caught, the owner of the house shall be brought before God, to determine whether or not the owner had laid hands on the neighbor’s goods.
text.Rotherham: if the thief be found, he shall give in restitution double, if the thief be not found, then shall the owner of the house be brought near unto God to swear that he hath not laid his hand on the property of his neighbour.
text.KJV: If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges, to see whether he have put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods.
text.NABRE: In every case of dishonest appropriation, whether it be about an ox, or a donkey, or a sheep, or a garment, or anything else that has disappeared, where another claims that the thing is his, the claim of both parties shall be brought before God; the one whom God convicts must make twofold restitution to the other.
text.DRB: If the thief be not known, the master of the house shall be brought to the gods, and shall swear that he did not extend his hand upon his neighbour's good,

text.NRSV: In any case of disputed ownership involving ox, donkey, sheep, clothing, or any other loss, of which one party says, “This is mine,” the case of both parties shall come before God; the one whom God condemns shall pay double to the other.
text.Rotherham: For any affair of trespass—for an ox, for an ass, for a sheep, for a mantle for anything lost as to which one could say—This is it, unto God, shall come the affair of them both,—he whom God shall condemn, shall make restitution of double to his neighbour.
text.KJV: For all manner of trespass, whether it be for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, or for any manner of lost thing, which another challengeth to be his, the cause of both parties shall come before the judges; and whom the judges shall condemn, he shall pay double unto his neighbour.
text.NABRE: When someone gives an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any other animal to another for safekeeping, if it dies, or is maimed or snatched away, without anyone witnessing the fact,
text.DRB: to do any fraud, as well in ox as in ass, and sheep and raiment, and whatsoever may bring damage: the cause of both parties shall come to the gods: and if they give judgment, he shall restore double to his neighbour.

text.NRSV: When someone delivers to another a donkey, ox, sheep, or any other animal for safekeeping, and it dies or is injured or is carried off, without anyone seeing it,
text.Rotherham: When a man delivereth unto his neighbour, an ass, or an ox or a sheep, or any beast, to keep,—and it dieth or is fractured, or is driven away, no one beholding,
text.KJV: If a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep; and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing it:
text.NABRE: there shall be an oath before the Lord between the two of them that the guardian did not lay hands on his neighbor’s property; the owner must accept the oath, and no restitution is to be made.
text.DRB: If a man commit ass, ox, sheep, or any beast, to his neighbour's custody, and it die, or be hurt, or be taken of enemies, and no man saw;

text.NRSV: an oath before the Lord shall decide between the two of them that the one has not laid hands on the property of the other; the owner shall accept the oath, and no restitution shall be made.
text.Rotherham: the oath of Yahweh, shall come between them both, That he hath not laid his hand on the property of his neighbour,—then shall the owner thereof accept it and he shall not make restitution.
text.KJV: Then shall an oath of the Lord be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods; and the owner of it shall accept thereof, and he shall not make it good.
text.NABRE: But if the guardian has actually stolen from it, then he must make restitution to the owner.
text.DRB: there shall be an oath between them, that he did not put forth his hand to his neighbour's good: and the owner shall admit the oath, and he shall not be compelled to make restitution.

text.NRSV: But if it was stolen, restitution shall be made to its owner.
text.Rotherham: But, if it be verily stolen, from him, he shall make restitution to the owner thereof.
text.KJV: And if it be stolen from him, he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof.
text.NABRE: If it has been killed by a wild beast, let him bring it as evidence; he need not make restitution for the mangled animal.
text.DRB: But if it were taken away by stealth, he shall restore the damage to the owner.

text.NRSV: If it was mangled by beasts, let it be brought as evidence; restitution shall not be made for the mangled remains.
text.Rotherham: If it be, verily torn in pieces, he shall bring it in as a witness—for that which was torn, he shall not make restitution.
text.KJV: If it be torn in pieces, then let him bring it for witness, and he shall not make good that which was torn.
text.NABRE: When someone borrows an animal from a neighbor, if it is maimed or dies while the owner is not present, that one must make restitution.
text.DRB: If it were eaten of a beast, let him bring unto him that which was slain, and he shall not make restitution.

text.NRSV: When someone borrows an animal from another and it is injured or dies, the owner not being present, full restitution shall be made.
text.Rotherham: And when a man asketh aught of his neighbour, and it is fractured or dieth, its owner, not being with it, he shall, surely make restitution.
text.KJV: And if a man borrow ought of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof being not with it, he shall surely make it good.
text.NABRE: But if the owner is present, that one need not make restitution. If it was hired, this was covered by the price of its hire.
text.DRB: He that asketh of his neighbour to borrow any of these things, and it be hurt or dead, the owner being not present, he shall be compelled to make restitution.

text.NRSV: If the owner was present, there shall be no restitution; if it was hired, only the hiring fee is due.
text.Rotherham: If, its owner, was with it, he shall not make restitution,—if it is, hired, it cometh into its hire.
text.KJV: But if the owner thereof be with it, he shall not make it good: if it be an hired thing, it came for his hire.
text.NABRE: Social Laws. When a man seduces a virgin who is not betrothed, and lies with her, he shall make her his wife by paying the bride price.
text.DRB: But if the owner be present, he shall not make restitution, especially if it were hired and came for the hire of the same.

text.NRSV: When a man seduces a virgin who is not engaged to be married, and lies with her, he shall give the bride-price for her and make her his wife.
text.Rotherham: And, when a man enticeth a virgin who is not betrothed, and lieth with her, he shall, surely pay a purchase-price, that she may be his wife.
text.KJV: And if a man entice a maid that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely endow her to be his wife.
text.NABRE: If her father refuses to give her to him, he must still pay him the bride price for virgins.
text.DRB: If a man seduce a virgin being not yet despoused, and lie with her; he shall endow her, and have her to wife.

text.NRSV: But if her father refuses to give her to him, he shall pay an amount equal to the bride-price for virgins.
text.Rotherham: If her father utterly refuse, to give her to him, silver, shall he weigh out, according to the purchase-price of virgins.
text.KJV: If her father utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.
text.NABRE: You shall not let a woman who practices sorcery live.
text.DRB: If the virgin's father will not give her, he shall give money according to the manner of the dowry, which virgins are wont to receive.

text.NRSV: You shall not permit a female sorcerer to live.
text.Rotherham: A sorceress, shalt thou not suffer to live.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.
text.NABRE: Anyone who lies with an animal shall be put to death.
text.DRB: Enchanters thou shalt not suffer to live.

text.NRSV: Whoever lies with an animal shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.
text.KJV: Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever sacrifices to any god, except to the Lord alone, shall be put under the ban.
text.DRB: He that lieth with a brute beast, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: Whoever sacrifices to any god, other than the Lord alone, shall be devoted to destruction.
text.Rotherham: He that sacrificeth to the gods shall be devoted to destruction,—except [he sacrificeth] to Yahweh alone.
text.KJV: He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the Lord only, he shall be utterly destroyed.
text.NABRE: You shall not oppress or afflict a resident alien, for you were once aliens residing in the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: He that sacrificeth to gods, shall be put to death, but to the Lord only.

text.NRSV: You shall not wrong or oppress a resident alien, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And, a sojourner, shalt thou not tread down neither shalt thou drive him away,—for sojourners, ye became in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: You shall not wrong any widow or orphan.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not molest a stranger, nor afflict him: for yourselves also were strangers in the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: You shall not abuse any widow or orphan.
text.Rotherham: Neither widow nor fatherless, shalt thou humiliate:
text.KJV: Ye shall not afflict any widow, or fatherless child.
text.NABRE: If ever you wrong them and they cry out to me, I will surely listen to their cry.
text.DRB: Awidow and an orphan you shall not hurt.

text.NRSV: If you do abuse them, when they cry out to me, I will surely heed their cry;
text.Rotherham: if thou, do humiliate, him, when he in anywise crieth out unto me, I will, surely hear, his outcry:
text.KJV: If thou afflict them in any wise, and they cry at all unto me, I will surely hear their cry;
text.NABRE: My wrath will flare up, and I will kill you with the sword; then your own wives will be widows, and your children orphans.
text.DRB: If you hurt them, they will cry out to me, and I will hear their cry:

text.NRSV: my wrath will burn, and I will kill you with the sword, and your wives shall become widows and your children orphans.
text.Rotherham: so shall kindle mine anger, and I will slay you with the sword,—and your wives, shall become widows, and your, sons fatherless.
text.KJV: And my wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.
text.NABRE: If you lend money to my people, the poor among you, you must not be like a money lender; you must not demand interest from them.
text.DRB: and my fury shall take indignation, and I will strike you with the sword, and your wives shall be widows, and your children orphans.

text.NRSV: If you lend money to my people, to the poor among you, you shall not deal with them as a creditor; you shall not exact interest from them.
text.Rotherham: If, silver, thou wilt lend unto my people—unto the humbled one by thee, thou shalt not be to him like one that lendeth on interest,—thou shalt not lay upon him interest.
text.KJV: If thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee, thou shalt not be to him as an usurer, neither shalt thou lay upon him usury.
text.NABRE: If you take your neighbor’s cloak as a pledge, you shall return it to him before sunset;
text.DRB: If thou lend money to my people being poor, that dwelleth with thee, thou shalt not urge them as an exactor, nor oppress them with usuries.

text.NRSV: If you take your neighbor’s cloak in pawn, you shall restore it before the sun goes down;
text.Rotherham: If thou, do take in pledge, the mantle of thy neighbour, by the going in of the sun, shalt thou restore it to him;
text.KJV: If thou at all take thy neighbour’s raiment to pledge, thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down:
text.NABRE: for this is his only covering; it is the cloak for his body. What will he sleep in? If he cries out to me, I will listen; for I am compassionate.
text.DRB: If thou take of thy neighbour a garment in pledge, thou shalt give it him again before sun set.

text.NRSV: for it may be your neighbor’s only clothing to use as cover; in what else shall that person sleep? And if your neighbor cries out to me, I will listen, for I am compassionate.
text.Rotherham: for that is his only covering, that, is his mantle, for his skin,—wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass when he maketh outcry unto me, then will I hear because, gracious, I am.
text.KJV: For that is his covering only, it is his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.
text.NABRE: You shall not despise God, nor curse a leader of your people.
text.DRB: For that same is the only thing wherewith he is covered, the clothing of his body, neither hath he other to sleep in: if he cry to me, I will hear him, because I am merciful.

text.NRSV: You shall not revile God, or curse a leader of your people.
text.Rotherham: God, shalt thou not revile,—and a prince among thy people, shalt thou not curse.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not revile the gods, nor curse the ruler of thy people.
text.NABRE: You shall not delay the offering of your harvest and your press. You shall give me the firstborn of your sons.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not detract from the gods, and the prince of thy people thou shalt not curse.

text.NRSV: You shall not delay to make offerings from the fullness of your harvest and from the outflow of your presses. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.
text.Rotherham: Thy fulness and thine outflow, shalt thou not delay,—the firstborn of thy sons, shalt thou give to me.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not delay to offer the first of thy ripe fruits, and of thy liquors: the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.
text.NABRE: You must do the same with your oxen and your sheep; for seven days the firstling may stay with its mother, but on the eighth day you must give it to me.
text.DRB: Thy tithes and thy first fruits thou shalt not slack to pay, the first-born of thy sons thou shalt give me.

text.NRSV: You shall do the same with your oxen and with your sheep: seven days it shall remain with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shalt thou do with thine ox with thy sheep,—seven days, shall it be with its dam, on the eighth day, shalt thou give it to me.
text.KJV: Likewise shalt thou do with thine oxen, and with thy sheep: seven days it shall be with his dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it me.
text.NABRE: You shall be a people sacred to me. Flesh torn to pieces in the field you shall not eat; you must throw it to the dogs.
text.DRB: Of thy oxen also & sheep thou shalt do in like manner: seven days let it be with the dam, the eighth day thou shalt render it to me.

text.NRSV: You shall be people consecrated to me; therefore you shall not eat any meat that is mangled by beasts in the field; you shall throw it to the dogs.
text.Rotherham: Holy men, therefore shall ye truly become to me,—and flesh in the field torn to pieces, shall ye not eat, to the dogs, shall ye cast it.
text.KJV: And ye shall be holy men unto me: neither shall ye eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs.
text.DRB: Holy men you shall be to me: the flesh that beasts have tasted of before, you shall not eat, but shall cast it to the dogs.

text.NRSV: You shall not spread a false report. You shall not join hands with the wicked to act as a malicious witness.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not utter a false report. Do not lay thy hand with a lawless man, to become a witness helping forward violence and wrong.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not raise a false report: put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.
text.NABRE: Do not join your hand with the wicked to be a witness supporting violence.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not admit a lying voice: neither shalt thou join thy hand to say false testimony for a wicked person.

text.NRSV: You shall not follow a majority in wrongdoing; when you bear witness in a lawsuit, you shall not side with the majority so as to pervert justice;
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not follow multitudes to commit wickedness,—neither shalt thou answer in a quarrel, so as to turn away after multitudes to mislead,
text.KJV: Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil; neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest judgment:
text.NABRE: You shall not follow the crowd in doing wrong. When testifying in a lawsuit, you shall not follow the crowd in perverting justice.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not follow the multitude to do evil: neither shalt thou in judgement agree to the sentence of the most part, to stray from the truth.

text.NRSV: nor shall you be partial to the poor in a lawsuit.
text.Rotherham: Even a poor man, shalt thou not prefer in his quarrel.
text.KJV: Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause.
text.NABRE: You shall not favor the poor in a lawsuit.
text.DRB: The poor man also thou shalt not pity in judgment.

text.NRSV: When you come upon your enemy’s ox or donkey going astray, you shall bring it back.
text.Rotherham: When thou meetest the ox of thine enemy, or his ass, going astray, thou shalt, surely bring it back, to him.
text.KJV: If thou meet thine enemy’s ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.
text.NABRE: When you come upon your enemy’s ox or donkey going astray, you must see to it that it is returned.
text.DRB: If thou meet thy enemy's ox or ass going astray, bring it back to him.

text.NRSV: When you see the donkey of one who hates you lying under its burden and you would hold back from setting it free, you must help to set it free.
text.Rotherham: When thou seest the ass of him that hateth thee sinking down under his burden, and mightest have foreborne to unload him, thou shalt surely help him to unload,
text.KJV: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, and wouldest forbear to help him, thou shalt surely help with him.
text.NABRE: When you notice the donkey of one who hates you lying down under its burden, you should not desert him; you must help him with it.
text.DRB: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lie underneath his burden, thou shalt not pass by, but shalt lift him up with the same.

text.NRSV: You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in their lawsuits.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not pervert the vindication of thy needy one in his quarrel.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.
text.NABRE: You shall not pervert justice for the needy among you in a lawsuit.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not decline the poor man's judgment.

text.NRSV: Keep far from a false charge, and do not kill the innocent and those in the right, for I will not acquit the guilty.
text.Rotherham: From a matter of falsehood, thou shalt keep far away,—and the innocent one and the righteous, do not thou slay, for I will not justify a lawless man.
text.KJV: Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.
text.NABRE: You shall keep away from anything dishonest. The innocent and the just you shall not put to death, for I will not acquit the guilty.
text.DRB: Alie thou shalt avoid. The innocent and just person thou shalt not put to death: because I abhor the impious man.

text.NRSV: You shall take no bribe, for a bribe blinds the officials, and subverts the cause of those who are in the right.
text.Rotherham: And, a bribe, shalt thou not take,—for the bribe, blindeth the clear-sighted, and perverteth the words of the righteous.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take no gift: for the gift blindeth the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous.
text.NABRE: Never take a bribe, for a bribe blinds the clear-sighted and distorts the words of the just.
text.DRB: Neither shalt thou take bribes, which do blind also the wise, and pervert the words of the just.

text.NRSV: You shall not oppress a resident alien; you know the heart of an alien, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And a sojourner, shalt thou not drive away,—seeing that, ye yourselves, know the, soul of a sojourner; for sojourners, became ye in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: Also thou shalt not oppress a stranger: for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: You shall not oppress a resident alien; you well know how it feels to be an alien, since you were once aliens yourselves in the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: The stranger thou shalt not molest, for you know the hearts of strangers: because yourselves also were strangers in the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: For six years you shall sow your land and gather in its yield;
text.Rotherham: And six years, shalt thou sow thy land,—and shalt gather the yield thereof;
text.KJV: And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and shalt gather in the fruits thereof:
text.NABRE: Religious Laws. For six years you may sow your land and gather in its produce.
text.DRB: Six years thou shalt sow thy ground, and shalt gather the corn thereof.

text.NRSV: but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, so that the poor of your people may eat; and what they leave the wild animals may eat. You shall do the same with your vineyard, and with your olive orchard.
text.Rotherham: but the seventh year, shalt thou let it rest and be still so shall the needy of thy people eat, and what they leave, shall the wild-beast of the field eat,—in like manner, shalt thou deal with thy vineyard with thine oliveyard.
text.KJV: But the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still; that the poor of thy people may eat: and what they leave the beasts of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thy oliveyard.
text.NABRE: But the seventh year you shall let the land lie untilled and fallow, that the poor of your people may eat of it and their leftovers the wild animals may eat. So also shall you do in regard to your vineyard and your olive grove.
text.DRB: But the seventh year thou shalt let it alone, and make it to rest, that the poor of thy people may eat, and whatsoever shall be left, let the beasts of the field eat it: so shalt thou do in thy vineyard and thy olivet.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall do your work, but on the seventh day you shall rest, so that your ox and your donkey may have relief, and your homeborn slave and the resident alien may be refreshed.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shalt thou do thy work, but on the seventh day, shalt thou keep sabbath,—that thine ox may rest and thine ass, and that the son of thy handmaid and the sojourner may be refreshed.
text.KJV: Six days thou shalt do thy work, and on the seventh day thou shalt rest: that thine ox and thine ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed.
text.NABRE: For six days you may do your work, but on the seventh day you must rest, that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and that the son of your maidservant and the resident alien may be refreshed.
text.DRB: Six days thou shalt work: the seventh day thou shalt cease, that thy ox may rest and thine ass: and the son of thy handmaid may be refreshed, and the stranger.

text.NRSV: Be attentive to all that I have said to you. Do not invoke the names of other gods; do not let them be heard on your lips.
text.Rotherham: And, in all that I have said unto thee, shalt thou take heed to thyself,—and the name of other gods, shalt thou not mention, it shall not be heard upon thy mouth.
text.KJV: And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect: and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: Give heed to all that I have told you. You shall not mention the name of any other god; it shall not be heard from your lips.
text.DRB: Keep all things that I have said to you. And by the name of foreign gods you shall not swear, neither shall it be heard out of your mouth.

text.NRSV: Three times in the year you shall hold a festival for me.
text.Rotherham: Three times, shalt thou keep festival to me, in the year.
text.KJV: Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year.
text.NABRE: Three times a year you shall celebrate a pilgrim feast to me.
text.DRB: Three times every year you shall celebrate feasts to me.

text.NRSV: You shall observe the festival of unleavened bread; as I commanded you, you shall eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in it you came out of Egypt. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: The festival of unleavened cakes, shalt thou keep,—seven days, shalt thou eat unleavened cakes, as I commanded thee at the appointed time of the month Abib; for, therein, camest thou forth out of Egypt,—and they shall not see my face, empty-handed.
text.KJV: Thou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread: (thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days, as I commanded thee, in the time appointed of the month Abib; for in it thou camest out from Egypt: and none shall appear before me empty:)
text.NABRE: You shall keep the feast of Unleavened Bread. As I have commanded you, you must eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for it was then that you came out of Egypt. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
text.DRB: Thou shalt keep the solemnity of Azymes. Seven days shalt thou eat azymes, as I commanded thee, in time of the month of new corn, when thou didst come forth out of Ægypt: thou shalt not appear in my sight empty.

text.NRSV: You shall observe the festival of harvest, of the first fruits of your labor, of what you sow in the field. You shall observe the festival of ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather in from the field the fruit of your labor.
text.Rotherham: And the festival of harvest with the firstfruits of thy labours, which thou shalt sow in the field, And the festival of ingathering—at the outgoing of the year, when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field.
text.KJV: And the feast of harvest, the firstfruits of thy labours, which thou hast sown in the field: and the feast of ingathering, which is in the end of the year, when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field.
text.NABRE: You shall also keep the feast of the grain harvest with the first fruits of the crop that you sow in the field; and finally, the feast of Ingathering at the end of the year, when you collect your produce from the fields.
text.DRB: And the solemnity of the harvest of the first fruits of thy work, whatsoever thou didst sow in the field. The solemnity also in the end of the year, when thou hast gathered all thy corn out of the field.

text.NRSV: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Three times in the year, shall all thy males see the face of the Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: Three times in the year all thy males shall appear before the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Three times a year shall all your men appear before the Lord God.
text.DRB: Thrice a year shall all thy male sex appear before the Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened, or let the fat of my festival remain until the morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not offer with anything leavened, the blood of my sacrifice,—neither shall the fat of my festival-sacrifice remain until morning.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning.
text.NABRE: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened; nor shall the fat of my feast be kept overnight till the next day.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not sacrifice the blood of my victim upon leaven, neither shall the fat of the solemnity remain until the morning.

text.NRSV: The choicest of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother’s milk.
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the firstfruits of thy ground, shalt thou bring into the house of Yahweh thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of its dam.
text.KJV: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
text.NABRE: The choicest first fruits of your soil you shall bring to the house of the Lord, your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.
text.DRB: The first fruits of the corn of thy ground thou shalt carry into the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of his dam.

text.NRSV: I am going to send an angel in front of you, to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place that I have prepared.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I, am sending a messenger before thee, to guard thee in the way,—and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.
text.KJV: Behold, I send an Angel before thee, to keep thee in the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.
text.NABRE: Reward of Fidelity. See, I am sending an angel before you, to guard you on the way and bring you to the place I have prepared.
text.DRB: Behold I will send mine Angel, which shall go before thee, and keep thee in thy journey, and bring thee into the place that I have prepared.

text.NRSV: Be attentive to him and listen to his voice; do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression; for my name is in him.
text.Rotherham: Take thou heed of his presence, and hearken to his voice—do not vex him,-for he will not pardon your transgression, for, my name, is within him.
text.KJV: Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not; for he will not pardon your transgressions: for my name is in him.
text.NABRE: Be attentive to him and obey him. Do not rebel against him, for he will not forgive your sin. My authority is within him.
text.DRB: Observe him, and hear his voice, neither do thou think him one to be contemned: for he will not forgive when thou hast sinned, and my name is in him.

text.NRSV: But if you listen attentively to his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and a foe to your foes.
text.Rotherham: But if thou wilt indeed hearken, to his voice, and so wilt do all that I may speak, then will I be an enemy to thine enemies, and an adversary to thine adversaries;
text.KJV: But if thou shalt indeed obey his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.
text.NABRE: If you obey him and carry out all I tell you, I will be an enemy to your enemies and a foe to your foes.
text.DRB: But if thou wilt hear his voice, and do all that I speak, I will be enemy to thine enemies, & will afflict them that afflict thee.

text.NRSV: When my angel goes in front of you, and brings you to the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, and I blot them out,
text.Rotherham: for my messenger shall go before thee, and bring thee in—unto the Amorite and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Canaanite, the Hivite and the Jebusite,—so will I destroy them.
text.KJV: For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites: and I will cut them off.
text.NABRE: My angel will go before you and bring you to the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites and Jebusites; and I will wipe them out.
text.DRB: And mine Angel shall go before thee, and shall bring thee in unto the Amorrheite, and Hetheite, & Pherezeite, and Chananeite, and Heveite, & Jebuseite, whom I will destroy.

text.NRSV: you shall not bow down to their gods, or worship them, or follow their practices, but you shall utterly demolish them and break their pillars in pieces.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not bow thyself down to their gods neither shalt thou be led to serve them, neither shalt thou do according to their works,—but thou shalt, verily overthrow, them, and, completely break in pieces, their pillars.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works: but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and quite break down their images.
text.NABRE: Therefore, you shall not bow down to their gods and serve them, nor shall you act as they do; rather, you must demolish them and smash their sacred stones.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not adore their gods, nor serve them. Thou shalt not do their works, but shalt destroy them, and break their statues.

text.NRSV: You shall worship the Lord your God, and I will bless your bread and your water; and I will take sickness away from among you.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye serve Yahweh your God, and he will bless thy bread and thy water,—and I will take away sickness out of thy midst.
text.KJV: And ye shall serve the Lord your God, and he shall bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee.
text.NABRE: You shall serve the Lord, your God; then he will bless your food and drink, and I will remove sickness from your midst;
text.DRB: And you shall serve the Lord your God, that I may bless your bread & waters, and may take away infirmity from the midst of thee.

text.NRSV: No one shall miscarry or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
text.Rotherham: There shall be nothing casting its young or barren in thy land,—the number of thy days, will I make full.
text.KJV: There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land: the number of thy days I will fulfil.
text.NABRE: no woman in your land will be barren or miscarry; and I will give you a full span of life.
text.DRB: There shall not be a fruitless nor barren body in thy land: I will fill the number of thy days.

text.NRSV: I will send my terror in front of you, and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
text.Rotherham: My terror, will I send before thee, and will confound all the people amongst whom thou shalt come, and will deliver up all thine enemies unto thee as they flee.
text.KJV: I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.
text.NABRE: I will have the terror of me precede you, so that I will throw into panic every nation you reach. I will make all your enemies turn from you in flight,
