text.DRB: I will send my terror to run before thee, and will kill all people, to whom thou shalt enter: and will turn the backs of all thine enemies before thee:

text.NRSV: And I will send the pestilence in front of you, which shall drive out the Hivites, the Canaanites, and the Hittites from before you.
text.Rotherham: And I will send the hornet, before thee,—and it shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
text.KJV: And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
text.NABRE: and ahead of you I will send hornets to drive the Hivites, Canaanites and Hittites out of your way.
text.DRB: sending forth hornets before, that shall chase away the Heveite, and Chananeite, & Hetheite, before thou enter.

text.NRSV: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, or the land would become desolate and the wild animals would multiply against you.
text.Rotherham: I will not drive them out from before thee, in one year,—lest the land should become a desolation, so would the wild-beast of the field multiply over thee.
text.KJV: I will not drive them out from before thee in one year; lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.
text.NABRE: But I will not drive them all out before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the wild animals multiply against you.
text.DRB: I will not cast them out from thy face in one year: lest the land be brought into a wilderness, and beasts increase against thee.

text.NRSV: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you have increased and possess the land.
text.Rotherham: Little by little, will I drive them out from before thee. until thou become fruitful, then shalt thou take possession of the land.
text.KJV: By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.
text.NABRE: Little by little I will drive them out before you, until you have grown numerous enough to take possession of the land.
text.DRB: By little and little I will expel them from thy sight, till thou be increased, and dost possess the Land.

text.NRSV: I will set your borders from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the Euphrates; for I will hand over to you the inhabitants of the land, and you shall drive them out before you.
text.Rotherham: So will I set thy bounds from the Red Sea even unto the sea of the Philistines and from the desert unto the River (Euphrates),—for I will deliver into your hand the inhabitants of the land, so shalt thou drive them out from before thee.
text.KJV: And I will set thy bounds from the Red sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the desert unto the river: for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.
text.NABRE: I will set your boundaries from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the Euphrates; all who dwell in this land I will hand over to you and you shall drive them out before you.
text.DRB: And I will set thy bounds from the Red sea unto the sea of the Palestines, and from the desert unto the river: I will deliver the inhabitants of the Land in your hands, and will cast them out from your sight.

text.NRSV: You shall make no covenant with them and their gods.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not make with them or with their gods, a covenant:
text.KJV: Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.
text.NABRE: You shall not make a covenant with them or their gods.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not enter league with them, nor with their gods.

text.NRSV: They shall not live in your land, or they will make you sin against me; for if you worship their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.
text.Rotherham: they shall not dwell in thy land, lest they cause thee to sin against me,—for thou mightest serve their gods, verily! it would become to thee a snare!
text.KJV: They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me: for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee.
text.NABRE: They must not live in your land. For if you serve their gods, this will become a snare to you.
text.DRB: Let them not dwell in thy land, lest perhaps they make thee to sin against me, if thou serve their gods: which undoubtedly will be a scandal to thee.

text.NRSV: Then he said to Moses, “Come up to the Lord, you and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel, and worship at a distance.
text.Rotherham: And unto Moses, he said—Come up unto Yahweh—thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel, so shall ye bow yourselves down from afar.
text.KJV: And he said unto Moses, Come up unto the Lord, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off.
text.NABRE: Moses himself was told: Come up to the Lord, you and Aaron, with Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel. You shall bow down at a distance.
text.DRB: To Moyses also he said: Go up to the Lord, thou, and Aaron, Nadab and Abiu, and seventy Ancients of Israel, and you shall adore a far off.

text.NRSV: Moses alone shall come near the Lord; but the others shall not come near, and the people shall not come up with him.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses alone shall draw near unto Yahweh, but, they shall not draw near, and, the people, shall not come up with him.
text.KJV: And Moses alone shall come near the Lord: but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people go up with him.
text.NABRE: Moses alone is to come close to the Lord; the others shall not come close, and the people shall not come up with them.
text.DRB: And Moyses only shall ascend to the Lord, and they shall not approach: neither shall the people ascend with him.

text.NRSV: Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, “All the words that the Lord has spoken we will do.”
text.Rotherham: So Moses came, and recounted to the people all the words of Yahweh, and all the regulations, and all the people responded with one voice and said, All the words which Yahweh hath spoken, will we do.
text.KJV: And Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words which the Lord hath said will we do.
text.NABRE: When Moses came to the people and related all the words and ordinances of the Lord, they all answered with one voice, “We will do everything that the Lord has told us.”
text.DRB: Moyses therefore came and told the people all the words of our Lord, & the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice: All the words of our Lord, which he hath spoken, we will do.

text.NRSV: And Moses wrote down all the words of the Lord. He rose early in the morning, and built an altar at the foot of the mountain, and set up twelve pillars, corresponding to the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then wrote Moses all the words of Yahweh, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar, under the mountain, and twelve pillars, for the twelve tribes of Israel:
text.KJV: And Moses wrote all the words of the Lord, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: Moses then wrote down all the words of the Lord and, rising early in the morning, he built at the foot of the mountain an altar and twelve sacred stones for the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.DRB: And Moyses wrote all the words of our Lord: and rising in the morning he builded an Altar at the foot of the mount, & twelve titles according to the twelve tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: He sent young men of the people of Israel, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed oxen as offerings of well-being to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and he sent young men of the sons of Israel, and they caused to go up, ascending-sacrifices,—and slew peace-offerings to Yahweh, of oxen.
text.KJV: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, which offered burnt offerings, and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then, having sent young men of the Israelites to offer burnt offerings and sacrifice young bulls as communion offerings to the Lord,
text.DRB: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, & they offered holocausts, & sacrificed pacific victims to our Lord, calves.

text.NRSV: Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins, and half of the blood he dashed against the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then took Moses half of the blood, and put it in basins, and half of the blood, dashed he over the altar:
text.KJV: And Moses took half of the blood, and put it in basons; and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.
text.NABRE: Moses took half of the blood and put it in large bowls; the other half he splashed on the altar.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore took the half part of the blood, and put it into bowls: and the residue he poured upon the Altar.

text.NRSV: Then he took the book of the covenant, and read it in the hearing of the people; and they said, “All that the Lord has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.”
text.Rotherham: then took he the book of the covenant, and read in the ears of the people. And they said, All that Yahweh hath spoken, will we do, and will hearken,
text.KJV: And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the Lord hath said will we do, and be obedient.
text.NABRE: Taking the book of the covenant, he read it aloud to the people, who answered, “All that the Lord has said, we will hear and do.”
text.DRB: And taking the volume of the covenant, he read the people hearing it: Who said, All things that our Lord hath spoken, we will do, and we will be obedient.

text.NRSV: Moses took the blood and dashed it on the people, and said, “See the blood of the covenant that the Lord has made with you in accordance with all these words.”
text.Rotherham: Then Moses took the blood, and dashed over the people, and said, Lo! the blood of the covenant which Yahweh hath solemnised with you, over all these words.
text.KJV: And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant, which the Lord hath made with you concerning all these words.
text.NABRE: Then he took the blood and splashed it on the people, saying, “This is the blood of the covenant which the Lord has made with you according to all these words.”
text.DRB: And he took the blood, and sprinkled it upon the people, and said: This is the blood of the Covenant which our Lord hath made with you upon all these words.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up,
text.Rotherham: Then went up Moses and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel;
text.KJV: Then went up Moses, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel:
text.NABRE: Moses then went up with Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders of Israel,
text.DRB: And there went up Moyses and Aaron, Nadab and Abiu, and seventy of the Ancients of Israel:

text.NRSV: and they saw the God of Israel. Under his feet there was something like a pavement of sapphire stone, like the very heaven for clearness.
text.Rotherham: and they saw the God of Israel,—and under his feet, like a pavement of sapphire, and like the very heavens for brightness; and
text.KJV: And they saw the God of Israel: and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone, and as it were the body of heaven in his clearness.
text.NABRE: and they beheld the God of Israel. Under his feet there appeared to be sapphire tilework, as clear as the sky itself.
text.DRB: and they saw the God of Israel: and under his feet as it were a work of sapphire stone, and as the heaven, when it is clear.

text.NRSV: God did not lay his hand on the chief men of the people of Israel; also they beheld God, and they ate and drank.
text.Rotherham: against the nobles of the sons of Israel, put he not forth his hand,—so then they had vision of God, and did eat and drink,
text.KJV: And upon the nobles of the children of Israel he laid not his hand: also they saw God, and did eat and drink.
text.NABRE: Yet he did not lay a hand on these chosen Israelites. They saw God, and they ate and drank.
text.DRB: Neither did he set his hand upon those of the children of Israel, that retired far off, and they saw God, and did eat, and drink.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Come up to me on the mountain, and wait there; and I will give you the tablets of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Come thou up unto me in the mountain and remain thou there,—for I must give thee tables of stone and the law and the commandment, which I have written to direct them.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and a law, and commandments which I have written; that thou mayest teach them.
text.NABRE: Moses on the Mountain. The Lord said to Moses: Come up to me on the mountain and, while you are there, I will give you the stone tablets on which I have written the commandments intended for their instruction.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and the law, and the commandments which I have written: that thou mayst teach them.

text.NRSV: So Moses set out with his assistant Joshua, and Moses went up into the mountain of God.
text.Rotherham: And Moses rose up, and Joshua his attendant, and Moses went up into the mountain of God;
text.KJV: And Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua: and Moses went up into the mount of God.
text.NABRE: So Moses set out with Joshua, his assistant, and went up to the mountain of God.
text.DRB: Moyses rose up, and his Minister Josue: and Moyses ascending into the mount of God,

text.NRSV: To the elders he had said, “Wait here for us, until we come to you again; for Aaron and Hur are with you; whoever has a dispute may go to them.”
text.Rotherham: but, unto the elders, he said—Tarry for us here, until we return unto you. And lo! Aaron and Hur, are with you, he that hath a cause, let him draw near unto them.
text.KJV: And he said unto the elders, Tarry ye here for us, until we come again unto you: and, behold, Aaron and Hur are with you: if any man have any matters to do, let him come unto them.
text.NABRE: He told the elders, “Wait here for us until we return to you. Aaron and Hur are with you. Anyone with a complaint should approach them.”
text.DRB: said to the Ancients: Expect here till we return to you, you have Aaron and Hur with you: If any question shall rise, you shall refer it to them.

text.NRSV: Then Moses went up on the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses wont up into the mountain,—and the cloud covered the mountain.
text.KJV: And Moses went up into the mount, and a cloud covered the mount.
text.NABRE: Moses went up the mountain. Then the cloud covered the mountain.
text.DRB: And when Moyses was ascended, a cloud covered the mount,

text.NRSV: The glory of the Lord settled on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it for six days; on the seventh day he called to Moses out of the cloud.
text.Rotherham: And the glory of Yahweh rested upon Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days,—then called he unto Moses on the seventh day, out of the midst of the cloud.
text.KJV: And the glory of the Lord abode upon mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days: and the seventh day he called unto Moses out of the midst of the cloud.
text.NABRE: The glory of the Lord settled upon Mount Sinai. The cloud covered it for six days, and on the seventh day he called to Moses from the midst of the cloud.
text.DRB: and the glory of our Lord dwelt upon Sinai, covering it with a cloud six days, and the seventh day he called him out of the midst of the darkness.

text.NRSV: Now the appearance of the glory of the Lord was like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the sight of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And the appearance of the glory of Yahweh was like a consuming fire on the top of the mountain,—in the sight of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And the sight of the glory of the Lord was like devouring fire on the top of the mount in the eyes of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: To the Israelites the glory of the Lord was seen as a consuming fire on the top of the mountain.
text.DRB: And the form of the glory of our Lord, was as it were fire burning upon the top of the mount, in the sight of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Moses entered the cloud, and went up on the mountain. Moses was on the mountain for forty days and forty nights.
text.Rotherham: And Moses entered into the midst of the cloud and ascended into the mountain. And it came to pass that Moses was in the mountain forty days and forty nights.
text.KJV: And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and gat him up into the mount: and Moses was in the mount forty days and forty nights.
text.NABRE: But Moses entered into the midst of the cloud and went up on the mountain. He was on the mountain for forty days and forty nights.
text.DRB: And Moyses entering into the midst of the cloud, ascended into the mount: and he was there forty days and forty nights.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Tell the Israelites to take for me an offering; from all whose hearts prompt them to give you shall receive the offering for me.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, that they take for me a heave-offering,—of every man whose heart urgeth him, shall ye take my heave-offering.
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites: Let them receive contributions for me. From each you shall receive the contribution that their hearts prompt them to give me.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, that they take first fruits for me, of every man that offereth of his own accord, you shall take them.

text.NRSV: This is the offering that you shall receive from them: gold, silver, and bronze,
text.Rotherham: And, this is the heave-offering which ye shall take of them,—gold and silver and bronze;
text.KJV: And this is the offering which ye shall take of them; gold, and silver, and brass,
text.NABRE: These are the contributions you shall accept from them: gold, silver, and bronze;
text.DRB: And these are the things which you must take: Gold, and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: blue, purple, and crimson yarns and fine linen, goats’ hair,
text.Rotherham: and blue, and purple, and crimson and fine linen, and goat’s hair;
text.KJV: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair,
text.NABRE: violet, purple, and scarlet yarn; fine linen and goat hair;
text.DRB: hyacinth and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and silk, and the hair of goats,

text.NRSV: tanned rams’ skins, fine leather, acacia wood,
text.Rotherham: and rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins and acacia wood;
text.KJV: And rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and shittim wood,
text.NABRE: rams’ skins dyed red, and tahash skins; acacia wood;
text.DRB: and rams' skins dyed red, and janthine skins, and the wood setim;

text.NRSV: oil for the lamps, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,
text.Rotherham: oil for giving light,—perfumes for the anointing oil, and for fragrant incense;
text.KJV: Oil for the light, spices for anointing oil, and for sweet incense,
text.NABRE: oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
text.DRB: oil to make lights: spices for ointment, and for incense of good savour:

text.NRSV: onyx stones and gems to be set in the ephod and for the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: onyx stones, and setting stones,—for the ephod and for the breastpiece:
text.KJV: Onyx stones, and stones to be set in the ephod, and in the breastplate.
text.NABRE: onyx stones and other gems for mounting on the ephod and the breastpiece.
text.DRB: Onyx stones, and precious stones to adorn the ephod, and rationale.

text.NRSV: And have them make me a sanctuary, so that I may dwell among them.
text.Rotherham: So shall they make for me a sanctuary,—and I will make my habitation in their midst:
text.KJV: And let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.
text.NABRE: They are to make a sanctuary for me, that I may dwell in their midst.
text.DRB: And they shall make me a sanctuary, and I will dwell in the midst of them:

text.NRSV: In accordance with all that I show you concerning the pattern of the tabernacle and of all its furniture, so you shall make it.
text.Rotherham: according to all that, I, am shewing thee, the pattern of the habitation, and the pattern of all the furnishings thereof, even so, shall ye make it.
text.KJV: According to all that I shew thee, after the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the instruments thereof, even so shall ye make it.
text.NABRE: According to all that I show you regarding the pattern of the tabernacle and the pattern of its furnishings, so you are to make it.
text.DRB: according to all the similitude of the tabernacle which I will shew thee, & of all the vessels to the service thereof: and thus you shall make it:

text.NRSV: They shall make an ark of acacia wood; it shall be two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And they shall make an ark of acacia wood,-two cubits and a half, the length thereof and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the height thereof.
text.KJV: And they shall make an ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.
text.NABRE: Plan of the Ark. You shall make an ark of acacia wood, two and a half cubits long, one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high.
text.DRB: Frame an ark of the wood setim, the length whereof shall have two cubits & an half: the breadth, a cubit & an half: the height, likewise a cubit & an half.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside you shall overlay it, and you shall make a molding of gold upon it all around.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, within and without, shalt thou overlay it,—and thou shalt make thereon a rim of gold, round about.
text.KJV: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, within and without shalt thou overlay it, and shalt make upon it a crown of gold round about.
text.NABRE: Plate it inside and outside with pure gold, and put a molding of gold around the top of it.
text.DRB: And thou shalt plate it with most pure gold within and without: and over it thou shalt make a golden crown round about:

text.NRSV: You shall cast four rings of gold for it and put them on its four feet, two rings on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt cast for it four rings of gold, and shalt put them on the four feet thereof,—even two rings on the one side thereof, and two rings on the other side thereof.
text.KJV: And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in the four corners thereof; and two rings shall be in the one side of it, and two rings in the other side of it.
text.NABRE: Cast four gold rings and put them on the four supports of the ark, two rings on one side and two on the opposite side.
text.DRB: & four golden rings, which thou shalt put at the four corners of the ark: let two rings be on the one side, & two on the other.

text.NRSV: You shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make staves, of acacia wood,—and shalt overlay them with gold.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
text.NABRE: Then make poles of acacia wood and plate them with gold.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make bars also of the wood setim, & shalt cover them with gold.

text.NRSV: And you shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, by which to carry the ark.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt bring in the staves into the rings, on the sides of the ark,—to bear the ark therewith,
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.
text.NABRE: These poles you are to put through the rings on the sides of the ark, for carrying it;
text.DRB: And thou shalt put them in through the rings that are in the sides of the ark, that it may be carried on them:

text.NRSV: The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be taken from it.
text.Rotherham: In the rings of the ark, shall remain the staves,—they shall not be removed therefrom.
text.KJV: The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not be taken from it.
text.NABRE: they must remain in the rings of the ark and never be withdrawn.
text.DRB: the which shall be always in the rings, neither shall they at any time be drawn out of them.

text.NRSV: You shall put into the ark the covenant that I shall give you.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put into the ark,—the testimony which I will give unto thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.
text.NABRE: In the ark you are to put the covenant which I will give you.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put in the ark the testification which I will give thee.

text.NRSV: Then you shall make a mercy seat of pure gold; two cubits and a half shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a propitiatory, of pure gold,—two cubits and a half, the length thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof,
text.KJV: And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.
text.NABRE: You shall then make a cover of pure gold, two and a half cubits long, and one and a half cubits wide.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make a Propitiatory of most pure gold: the length thereof shall hold two cubits and an half, and the breadth a cubit & an half.

text.NRSV: You shall make two cherubim of gold; you shall make them of hammered work, at the two ends of the mercy seat.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make two cherubim of gold,—of beaten work, shalt thou make them, out of the two ends of the propitiatory.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make two cherubims of gold, of beaten work shalt thou make them, in the two ends of the mercy seat.
text.NABRE: Make two cherubim of beaten gold for the two ends of the cover;
text.DRB: Two Cherubims also thou shalt make of beaten gold, on both sides of the oracle.

text.NRSV: Make one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other; of one piece with the mercy seat you shall make the cherubim at its two ends.
text.Rotherham: And make thou one cherub out of this end, and the other cherub out of that end,—out of the propitiatory, shall ye make the cherubim upon the two ends thereof.
text.KJV: And make one cherub on the one end, and the other cherub on the other end: even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof.
text.NABRE: make one cherub at one end, and the other at the other end, of one piece with the cover, at each end.
text.DRB: Let one Cherub be on the one side, and the other on the other.

text.NRSV: The cherubim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings. They shall face one to another; the faces of the cherubim shall be turned toward the mercy seat.
text.Rotherham: And the cherubim shall be spreading out two wings on high—making a shelter with their wings over the propitiatory, with their faces one towards the other,—towards the propitiatory, shall be the faces of the cherubim.
text.KJV: And the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces shall look one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be.
text.NABRE: The cherubim shall have their wings spread out above, sheltering the cover with them; they shall face each other, with their faces looking toward the cover.
text.DRB: Let them cover both sides of the Propitiatory spreading their wings, and covering the oracle, and let them look one towards the other, their faces turned unto the Propitiatory wherewith the ark is to be covered,

text.NRSV: You shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark; and in the ark you shall put the covenant that I shall give you.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put the propitiatory upon the ark above,—and in the ark, shalt thou put the testimony, which I will give unto thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark; and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that I shall give thee.
text.NABRE: This cover you shall then place on top of the ark. In the ark itself you are to put the covenant which I will give you.
text.DRB: wherein thou shalt put the testimony that I will give thee.

text.NRSV: There I will meet with you, and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim that are on the ark of the covenant, I will deliver to you all my commands for the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And I will meet with thee there, and will speak with thee from off the propitiatory, from between the two cherubim, which are upon the ark of the testimony,—whatsoever I may give thee in charge, for the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims which are upon the ark of the testimony, of all things which I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: There I will meet you and there, from above the cover, between the two cherubim on the ark of the covenant, I will tell you all that I command you regarding the Israelites.
text.DRB: Thence will I command, and will speak to thee over the propitiatory & from the midst of the two Cherubims which shall be upon the ark of testimony, all things which I will command the children of Israel by thee.

text.NRSV: You shall make a table of acacia wood, two cubits long, one cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a table, of acacia wood, two cubits, the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.
text.KJV: Thou shalt also make a table of shittim wood: two cubits shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.
text.NABRE: The Table. You shall also make a table of acacia wood, two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make a table also of the wood setim, having two cubits in length, and in breadth a cubit, & in height a cubit and an half.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, and make a molding of gold around it.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt overlay it with pure gold,—and shalt make thereto a rim of gold, round about;
text.KJV: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, and make thereto a crown of gold round about.
text.NABRE: Plate it with pure gold and make a molding of gold around it.
text.DRB: And thou shalt plate it with most pure gold: and thou shalt make to it a golden ledge round about,

text.NRSV: You shall make around it a rim a handbreadth wide, and a molding of gold around the rim.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt make thereto a border of a handbreadth, round about,—and shalt make a rim of gold to the border thereof, round about;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make unto it a border of an hand breadth round about, and thou shalt make a golden crown to the border thereof round about.
text.NABRE: Make a frame for it, a handbreadth high, and make a molding of gold around the frame.
text.DRB: and to the ledge itself a crown interpolished, four fingers high: and upon the same, another golden crown.

text.NRSV: You shall make for it four rings of gold, and fasten the rings to the four corners at its four legs.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt make to it four rings of gold,—and shalt place the rings on the four corners which pertain to the four feet thereof:
text.KJV: And thou shalt make for it four rings of gold, and put the rings in the four corners that are on the four feet thereof.
text.NABRE: You shall also make four rings of gold for it and fasten them at the four corners, one at each leg.
text.DRB: Thou shalt prepare also four golden rings, and shalt put them in the four corners of the same table at every foot.

text.NRSV: The rings that hold the poles used for carrying the table shall be close to the rim.
text.Rotherham: adjoining the border, shall be the rings,—as receptacles for the staves, for lifting the table;
text.KJV: Over against the border shall the rings be for places of the staves to bear the table.
text.NABRE: The rings shall be alongside the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table.
text.DRB: Under the crown shall the golden rings be, that the bars may be put through them, and the table may be carried.

text.NRSV: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold, and the table shall be carried with these.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt make the staves of acacia wood, and shalt overlay them with gold,—and the table shall be lifted there, with;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be borne with them.
text.NABRE: These poles for carrying the table you shall make of acacia wood and plate with gold.
text.DRB: The bars also themselves thou shalt make of the wood setim, & shalt compass them with gold to bear up the table.

text.NRSV: You shall make its plates and dishes for incense, and its flagons and bowls with which to pour drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt make its dishes and its pans and its basins and its bowls, for pouring out therewith,—of pure gold, shalt thou make them.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and spoons thereof, and covers thereof, and bowls thereof, to cover withal: of pure gold shalt thou make them.
text.NABRE: You shall make its plates and cups, as well as its pitchers and bowls for pouring libations; make them of pure gold.
text.DRB: Thou shalt prepare also saucers, and phials, censers, & goblets, wherein the libaments are to be offered, of most pure gold.

text.NRSV: And you shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me always.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place upon the table Presence-bread, before me continually.
text.KJV: And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway.
text.NABRE: On the table you shall always keep showbread set before me.
text.DRB: And thou shalt set upon the table loaves of proposition in my sight always.

text.NRSV: You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The base and the shaft of the lampstand shall be made of hammered work; its cups, its calyxes, and its petals shall be of one piece with it;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a lampstand of pure gold, of beaten work, shall the lampstand be made, even its base and its shaft its cups, its apples, and its blossoms of the same, shall be:
text.KJV: And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work shall the candlestick be made: his shaft, and his branches, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, shall be of the same.
text.NABRE: The Menorah. You shall make a menorah of pure beaten gold—its shaft and branches—with its cups and knobs and petals springing directly from it.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a candle stick beaten of most pure gold, the shaft thereof, & branches, cups, and bowls, and lilies proceeding from the same.

text.NRSV: and there shall be six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;
text.Rotherham: with, six branches, coming out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand, out the one side thereof, and three branches of the lampstand, out of the other side thereof:
text.KJV: And six branches shall come out of the sides of it; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side:
text.NABRE: Six branches are to extend from its sides, three branches on one side, and three on the other.
text.DRB: Six branches shall go forth of the sides, three out of one side, and three out of the other.

text.NRSV: three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on one branch, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on the other branch—so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: three cups like almond-flowers, in the one branch apple and blossom, and, three cups like almond-flowers, in the other branch apple and blossom, so, for the six branches, coming out of the lampstand.
text.KJV: Three bowls made like unto almonds, with a knop and a flower in one branch; and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch, with a knop and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick.
text.NABRE: On one branch there are to be three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; on the opposite branch there are to be three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; and so for the six branches that extend from the menorah.
text.DRB: Three cups as it were in manner of a nut on every branch, and bowl withal, and a lily: and three-cups likewise of the fashion of a nut in another branch, and a bowl withal, and a lily. This shall be the work of the six branches, that are to be drawn forth from the shaft:

text.NRSV: On the lampstand itself there shall be four cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with its calyxes and petals.
text.Rotherham: And in the lampstand itself, four cups,—like almond-flowers—its apples, and its blossoms;
text.KJV: And in the candlestick shall be four bowls made like unto almonds, with their knops and their flowers.
text.NABRE: On the menorah there are to be four cups, shaped like almond blossoms, with their knobs and petals.
text.DRB: and in the candlestick itself shall be four cups in manner of a nut, and at every one bowls and lilies.

text.NRSV: There shall be a calyx of one piece with it under the first pair of branches, a calyx of one piece with it under the next pair of branches, and a calyx of one piece with it under the last pair of branches—so for the six branches that go out of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: and an apple under two branches of the same, and, another apple, under two other branches of the same, and another apple under two other branches, of the same,—for the six branches, coming out of the lampstand:
text.KJV: And there shall be a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick.
text.NABRE: The six branches that go out from the menorah are to have a knob under each pair.
text.DRB: Bowls under two branches in three places, which together make six coming forth out of one shaft.

text.NRSV: Their calyxes and their branches shall be of one piece with it, the whole of it one hammered piece of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: their apples and their branches, of the same, shall be,—all of it, of one beaten work, of pure gold.
text.KJV: Their knops and their branches shall be of the same: all it shall be one beaten work of pure gold.
text.NABRE: Their knobs and branches shall so spring from it that the whole will form a single piece of pure beaten gold.
text.DRB: Both the bowls therefore and the branches shall be out of it, all the whole beaten of most pure gold.

text.NRSV: You shall make the seven lamps for it; and the lamps shall be set up so as to give light on the space in front of it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the lamps thereof seven, and one shall light up its lamps, and it shall give light, over against the face thereof.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof: and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may give light over against it.
text.NABRE: You shall then make seven lamps for it and so set up the lamps that they give their light on the space in front of the menorah.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also seven lamps, and shalt set them upon the candlestick, for to give light over-against.

text.NRSV: Its snuffers and trays shall be of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And, its snuffers, and its snuff-trays of pure gold.
text.KJV: And the tongs thereof, and the snuffdishes thereof, shall be of pure gold.
text.NABRE: These, as well as the trimming shears and trays, must be of pure gold.
text.DRB: The snuffers also and where the snuffings shall be put out, let them be made of most pure gold.

text.NRSV: It, and all these utensils, shall be made from a talent of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: With a talent of pure gold, shall he make it, with all these vessels,
text.KJV: Of a talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.
text.NABRE: Use a talent of pure gold for the menorah and all these utensils.
text.DRB: The whole weight of the candlestick with all the furniture thereof shall have a talent of most pure gold.

text.NRSV: And see that you make them according to the pattern for them, which is being shown you on the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And see thou and make them, by the pattern of them, which thou wast caused to behold in the mount
text.KJV: And look that thou make them after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount.
text.NABRE: See that you make them according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
text.DRB: Look, and make it according to the pattern, that was shewed thee in the mount.

text.NRSV: Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and crimson yarns; you shall make them with cherubim skillfully worked into them.
text.Rotherham: Also the habitation shalt thou make, with ten curtains, of twined linen, and blue, and purple, and crimson, with cherubim, the work of a skilful weaver, shalt thou make them.
text.KJV: Moreover thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them.
text.NABRE: The tabernacle itself you shall make out of ten sheets woven of fine linen twined and of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, with cherubim embroidered on them.
text.DRB: And the tabernacle thou shalt make thus: Ten curtains shalt thou make of twisted silk, and hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, varied with embroidered work.

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains shall be of the same size.
text.Rotherham: The length of each curtain twenty-eight cubits, and, the breadth four cubits, of each curtain, one measure, for all the curtains.
text.KJV: The length of one curtain shall be eight and twenty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and every one of the curtains shall have one measure.
text.NABRE: The length of each shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width four cubits; all the sheets shall be of the same size.
text.DRB: The length of one curtain shall have twenty eight cubits: the breadth shall be of four cubits. All the curtains shall be of one measure.

text.NRSV: Five curtains shall be joined to one another; and the other five curtains shall be joined to one another.
text.Rotherham: The five curtains, shall be joined, one to another, and, the other five curtains, joined, one to another.
text.KJV: The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another.
text.NABRE: Five of the sheets are to be joined one to another; and the same for the other five.
text.DRB: Five curtains shall be joined one to another, & the other five shall hang together with the connection.

text.NRSV: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set; and likewise you shall make loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make loops of blue on the border of each curtain, at the edge of the set,—and, thus, shalt thou do in the border of the curtain, that is outmost in the second set:
text.KJV: And thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.
text.NABRE: Make loops of violet yarn along the edge of the end sheet in one set, and the same along the edge of the end sheet in the other set.
text.DRB: Loops of hyacinth thou shalt make in the sides & tops of the curtains, that they may be coupled one to another.

text.NRSV: You shall make fifty loops on the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set; the loops shall be opposite one another.
text.Rotherham: fifty loops, shalt thou make, in the one curtain, and fifty loops, shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain which is in the second set, the loops being opposite, one to another.
text.KJV: Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that is in the coupling of the second; that the loops may take hold one of another.
text.NABRE: Make fifty loops along the edge of the end sheet in the first set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding sheet in the second set, and so placed that the loops are directly opposite each other.
text.DRB: Fifty loops shall every curtain have on both sides, so set on, that one loop may be against another loop, and one may be fitted to the other.

text.NRSV: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and join the curtains to one another with the clasps, so that the tabernacle may be one whole.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make fifty clasps of gold,—so shalt thou join the curtains one to another, with the clasps, and thus shall the habitation be one.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty taches of gold, and couple the curtains together with the taches: and it shall be one tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Then make fifty clasps of gold and join the two sets of sheets, so that the tabernacle forms one whole.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also fifty circles of gold wherewith the veils of the curtains are to be joined, that it may be made one tabernacle.

text.NRSV: You shall also make curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; you shall make eleven curtains.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make curtains of goat’s hair, for a tent, over the habitation—eleven curtains, shalt thou make them.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make curtains of goats’ hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make.
text.NABRE: Also make sheets woven of goat hair for a tent over the tabernacle. Make eleven such sheets;
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also eleven curtains of hair, to cover the top of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains shall be of the same size.
text.Rotherham: The length of each curtain, thirty cubits, and the breadth, four cubits, of each curtain,—one measure, to the eleven curtains.
text.KJV: The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and the eleven curtains shall be all of one measure.
text.NABRE: the length of each shall be thirty cubits, and the width four cubits: all eleven sheets shall be of the same size.
text.DRB: The length of one hair curtain shall have thirty cubits: and the breadth, four: the measure of all the curtains shall be equal.

text.NRSV: You shall join five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and the sixth curtain you shall double over at the front of the tent.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt join the five curtains by themselves, and the six curtains by themselves, and thou shalt fold back the sixth curtain, in the forefront of the tent.
text.KJV: And thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Join five of the sheets into one set, and the other six sheets into another set. Use the sixth sheet double at the front of the tent.
text.DRB: Of the which, five thou shalt join apart, and the six thou shalt couple one to another, so that the sixth curtain in the front of the roof thou shalt double.

text.NRSV: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second set.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the border of the one curtain, that is outmost in the set, and fifty loops on the border of the curtain, of the second set.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outmost in the coupling, and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.
text.NABRE: Make fifty loops along the edge of the end sheet in one set, and fifty loops along the edge of the end sheet in the second set.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also fifty loops in the edge of one curtain, that it may be joined with the other: and fifty loops in the edge of the other curtains that it may be coupled with his fellow.

text.NRSV: You shall make fifty clasps of bronze, and put the clasps into the loops, and join the tent together, so that it may be one whole.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make fifty clasps of bronze, and bring the clasps into the loops, so shalt thou join the tent, and it shall be one.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make fifty taches of brass, and put the taches into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.
text.NABRE: Also make fifty bronze clasps and put them into the loops, to join the tent into one whole.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also fifty buckles of brass, wherewith the loops may be joined, that of all there may be made one covering.

text.NRSV: The part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And the overplus that remaineth in the curtains of the tent,—the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over at the back of the habitation.
text.KJV: And the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: There will be an extra half sheet of tent covering, which shall be allowed to hang down over the rear of the tabernacle.
text.DRB: And that which shall remain in the curtains, that are prepared for the roof, to wit, one curtain that is overplus, with the half thereof thou shalt cover the backside of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of what remains in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle, on this side and that side, to cover it.
text.Rotherham: And, the cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side of that which remaineth, in the length of the curtains of the tent shall be hung over the sides of the habitation on this side and on that side to make a covering.
text.KJV: And a cubit on the one side, and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, it shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.
text.NABRE: Likewise, the sheets of the tent will have an extra cubit’s length to be left hanging down on either side of the tabernacle to cover it.
text.DRB: And there shall hang down a cubit on the one side, and another on the other side, which is the overplus in the length of the curtains, fencing both sides of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: You shall make for the tent a covering of tanned rams’ skins and an outer covering of fine leather.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a covering, for the tent, of rams’ skins dyed red,—and a covering of badger’s skins, above.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering above of badgers’ skins.
text.NABRE: Over the tent itself make a covering of rams’ skins dyed red, and above that, a covering of tahash skins.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also another cover to the roof of the ram's skins dyed red: & over that again another cover of janthine skins.

text.NRSV: You shall make upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make boards for the habitation,—of acacia wood to stand up:
text.KJV: And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up.
text.NABRE: The Framework. You shall make frames for the tabernacle, acacia-wood uprights.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also the boards of the tabernacle standing upright of the wood setim,

text.NRSV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.
text.Rotherham: ten cubits, the length of the board, and a cubit and a half cubit the breadth of each board:
text.KJV: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board.
text.NABRE: The length of each frame is to be ten cubits, and its width one and a half cubits.
text.DRB: of the which let every one have ten cubits in length, and in breadth one and a half a-piece.

text.NRSV: There shall be two pegs in each frame to fit the frames together; you shall make these for all the frames of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: two tenons, to each board, united one to another,—thus, shalt thou make, for all the boards of the habitation,
text.KJV: Two tenons shall there be in one board, set in order one against another: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Each frame shall have two arms joined one to another; so you are to make all the frames of the tabernacle.
text.DRB: In the sides of the board, shall be made two mortises, whereby one board may be joined to another board: and after this manner shall all the boards be prepared.

text.NRSV: You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side;
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt make the boards for the habitation, twenty boards, on the south side southwards.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.
text.NABRE: Make the frames of the tabernacle as follows: twenty frames on the south side,
text.DRB: Of the which twenty shall be in the south side that tendeth southward.

text.NRSV: and you shall make forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs;
text.Rotherham: And forty sockets of silver, shalt thou make under the twenty boards,—two sockets under the one board for its two tenons, and two sockets, under the next board, for its two tenons.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
text.NABRE: with forty silver pedestals under the twenty frames, two pedestals under each frame for its two arms;
text.DRB: For the which thou shalt cast forty feet of silver, that there may two feet be put under every board at the two corners.

text.NRSV: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side twenty frames,
text.Rotherham: And on the second side of the habitation, towards the north, twenty boards;
text.KJV: And for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards:
text.NABRE: twenty frames on the other side of the tabernacle, the north side,
text.DRB: In the second side also of the tabernacle that looketh to the North, there shall be twenty boards,

text.NRSV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame;
text.Rotherham: and their forty sockets of silver,—two sockets, under the one board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.KJV: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
text.NABRE: with their forty silver pedestals, two pedestals under each frame.
text.DRB: having forty feet of silver, two feet shall be put under every board.

text.NRSV: and for the rear of the tabernacle westward you shall make six frames.
text.Rotherham: And for the hinderpart of the habitation, westward, shalt thou make six boards.
text.KJV: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.
text.NABRE: At the rear of the tabernacle, to the west, six frames,
text.DRB: But on the west quarter of the tabernacle thou shalt make six boards,

text.NRSV: You shall make two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear;
text.Rotherham: And, two boards, shalt thou make, for the corners of the habitation,—in the hinderpart:
text.KJV: And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
text.NABRE: and two frames for the corners of the tabernacle, at its rear.
text.DRB: and again other two which shall be erected in the corners at the back of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: they shall be separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; it shall be the same with both of them; they shall form the two corners.
text.Rotherham: that they may be double beneath, and at the same time, they shall be entire on the top thereof, into each ring,—thus, shall it be for them both, for the two corners, shall they be.
text.KJV: And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.
text.NABRE: These two shall be double at the bottom, and likewise double at the top, to the first ring. That is how both corner frames are to be made.
text.DRB: And they shall be joined together from beneath unto the top, & one jointure shall hold them all. The like jointure shall be kept for the two boards also that are to be put in the corners.

text.NRSV: And so there shall be eight frames, with their bases of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under the first frame, and two bases under the next frame.
text.Rotherham: So shall they be eight boards, and, their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets,—two sockets, under the one board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.KJV: And they shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
text.NABRE: Thus, there shall be eight frames, with their sixteen silver pedestals, two pedestals under each frame.
text.DRB: And they shall be in all eight boards, their silver feet sixteen, two feet accounted for every board.

text.NRSV: You shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make bars, of acacia wood,—five, for the boards of the one side of the habitation,
text.KJV: And thou shalt make bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.NABRE: Also make bars of acacia wood: five for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also five bars of the wood setim, to hold together the boards on the one side of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
text.Rotherham: and five bars, for the boards of the other side of the habitation, and five bars, for the boards of the side of the habitation, at the hinderpart westward;
text.KJV: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the two sides westward.
text.NABRE: five for those on the other side, and five for those at the rear, to the west.
text.DRB: and five others on the other side, & as many at the west side:

text.NRSV: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall pass through from end to end.
text.Rotherham: with the middle bar in the midst of the boards,—running along from end to end.
text.KJV: And the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end.
text.NABRE: The center bar, at the middle of the frames, shall reach across from end to end.
text.DRB: which shall be put along by the midst of the boards from one end to the other.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay the frames with gold, and shall make their rings of gold to hold the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
text.Rotherham: And the boards, shalt thou overlay with gold, and their rings, shalt thou make of gold, as receptacles for the bars,—and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.
text.KJV: And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.
text.NABRE: Plate the frames with gold, and make gold rings on them as holders for the bars, which are also to be plated with gold.
text.DRB: The boards also themselves thou shalt plate with gold, & shalt cast rings of gold to be set upon them, through which the bars may hold together the boardwork: the which thou shalt cover with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you were shown on the mountain.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou rear up the habitation, according to the fashion thereof, which thou wast caused to see in the mount.
text.KJV: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the fashion thereof which was shewed thee in the mount.
text.NABRE: You shall set up the tabernacle according to its plan, which you were shown on the mountain.
text.DRB: And thou shalt erect the tabernacle according to the pattern that was shewed thee in the Mount.

text.NRSV: You shall make a curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a veil, of blue and purple, and crimson and fine-twined linen,—of the work of a skilful weaver, shall it be made, with cherubim;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen of cunning work: with cherubims shall it be made:
text.NABRE: The Veils. You shall make a veil woven of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, with cherubim embroidered on it.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a veil of hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk, wrought with embroidered work & goodly variety:

text.NRSV: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, which have hooks of gold and rest on four bases of silver.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, with their hooks of gold,—upon four sockets of silver,
text.KJV: And thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim wood overlaid with gold: their hooks shall be of gold, upon the four sockets of silver.
text.NABRE: It is to be hung on four gold-plated columns of acacia wood, which shall have gold hooks and shall rest on four silver pedestals.
text.DRB: which thou shalt hang before four pillars of the wood setim, the which themselves also shall be plated with gold, & shall have four heads of gold, but feet of silver.

text.NRSV: You shall hang the curtain under the clasps, and bring the ark of the covenant in there, within the curtain; and the curtain shall separate for you the holy place from the most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hang up the veil under the clasps,—and shalt bring in thither, within the veil the ark of the testimony; and the veil shall make a division for you between the holy [place] and the most holy.
text.KJV: And thou shalt hang up the vail under the taches, that thou mayest bring in thither within the vail the ark of the testimony: and the vail shall divide unto you between the holy place and the most holy.
text.NABRE: Hang the veil from clasps. The ark of the covenant you shall bring inside, behind this veil which divides the holy place from the holy of holies.
text.DRB: And the veil shall be hanged on with rings, within the which thou shalt put the ark of testimony, with the which also the Sanctuary, and the Sanctuaries of the Sanctuary, shall be divided.

text.NRSV: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the covenant in the most holy place.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place the propitiatory, upon the ark of the testimony,—in the most holy [place].
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
text.NABRE: Set the cover on the ark of the covenant in the holy of holies.
text.DRB: Thou shalt set also the Propitiatory upon the ark of testimony in the Sancta sanctorum:

text.NRSV: You shall set the table outside the curtain, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table; and you shall put the table on the north side.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand over against the table, on the side of the habitation, southward,—and the table, shalt thou place on the north side.
text.KJV: And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and thou shalt put the table on the north side.
text.NABRE: Outside the veil you shall place the table and the menorah, the latter on the south side of the tabernacle, opposite the table, which is to be put on the north side.
text.DRB: and the table without the veil: and over-against the table the candlestick in the south side of the tabernacle: for the table shall stand in the north side.

text.NRSV: You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a screen for the opening of the tent, of blue, and purple and crimson, and fine-twined linen,—the work of an embroiderer.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework.
text.NABRE: For the entrance of the tent make a variegated curtain of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a hanging in the entering of the tabernacle of hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk with embroidered work.

text.NRSV: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and shalt overlay them with gold, and their hooks [shall be] of gold,—and thou shalt cast for them five sockets of bronze.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make for the hanging five pillars of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, and their hooks shall be of gold: and thou shalt cast five sockets of brass for them.
text.NABRE: Make five columns of acacia wood for this curtain; plate them with gold, with their hooks of gold; and cast five bronze pedestals for them.
text.DRB: And five pillars of the wood setim thou shalt plate with gold, before the which the hanging shall be drawn: whose heads shall be of gold, and feet of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square, and it shall be three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the altar of acacia wood,—of five cubits length and five cubits breadth four square, shall the altar be, and three cubits the height thereof.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make an altar of shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof shall be three cubits.
text.NABRE: You shall make an altar of acacia wood, on a square, five cubits long and five cubits wide; it shall be three cubits high.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also an Altar of the wood setim, which shall have five cubits in length, an as many in breadth, that is, four-square, and three cubits in height.

text.NRSV: You shall make horns for it on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it, and you shall overlay it with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make its horns upon its four corners, of the same, shall be its horns,—and thou shalt overlay it with copper.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the horns of it upon the four corners thereof: his horns shall be of the same: and thou shalt overlay it with brass.
text.NABRE: At the four corners make horns that are of one piece with the altar. You shall then plate it with bronze.
text.DRB: And there shall be at the four corners horns of the same: and thou shalt cover it with brass.

text.NRSV: You shall make pots for it to receive its ashes, and shovels and basins and forks and firepans; you shall make all its utensils of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make its pans for removing its ashes, and its shovels and its sprinkling bowls, and its flesh-hooks and its fire-pans,—all its vessels, shalt thou make of bronze.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basons, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make of brass.
text.NABRE: Make pots for removing the ashes, as well as shovels, basins, forks, and fire pans; all these utensils you shall make of bronze.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make for the uses thereof pans for to take the ashes, and tongues, and flesh-hooks, and fire pans, all the vessels thou shalt make of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall also make for it a grating, a network of bronze; and on the net you shall make four bronze rings at its four corners.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make to it a grating, a network of bronze,—and thou shalt make upon the network, four rings of bronze, upon the four corners thereof.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make for it a grate of network of brass; and upon the net shalt thou make four brasen rings in the four corners thereof.
text.NABRE: Make for it a grating, a bronze network; make four bronze rings for it, one at each of its four corners.
text.DRB: And a grate in manner of a net of brass: at the four corners whereof shall be four rings of brass,

text.NRSV: You shall set it under the ledge of the altar so that the net shall extend halfway down the altar.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place it under the margin of the altar beneath,—so shall the network reach unto the midst of the altar.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.
text.NABRE: Put it down around the altar, on the ground. This network is to be half as high as the altar.
text.DRB: which thou shalt put under the hearth of the Altar: and the grates shall be unto the midst of the Altar.

text.NRSV: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of acacia wood,—and shalt overlay them with copper.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with brass.
text.NABRE: You shall also make poles of acacia wood for the altar, and plate them with bronze.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also two bars for the Altar of the wood setim, which thou shalt cover with plates of brass:

text.NRSV: the poles shall be put through the rings, so that the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar when it is carried.
text.Rotherham: And the staves shall be brought into the rings,—so shall the staves be on the two sides of the altar in lifting it:
text.KJV: And the staves shall be put into the rings, and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar, to bear it.
text.NABRE: These poles are to be put through the rings, so that they are on either side of the altar when it is carried.
text.DRB: and thou shalt draw them through rings, & they shall be on both sides of the Altar to carry it.

text.NRSV: You shall make it hollow, with boards. They shall be made just as you were shown on the mountain.
text.Rotherham: Hollow with boards, shalt thou make it,—as it was showed thee in the mount, so, shall they make it.
text.KJV: Hollow with boards shalt thou make it: as it was shewed thee in the mount, so shall they make it.
text.NABRE: Make the altar itself in the form of a hollow box. Just as it was shown you on the mountain, so it is to be made.
text.DRB: Not massy, but empty & hollow in the inside shalt thou make it, as it was shewed thee in the Mount.

text.NRSV: You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side the court shall have hangings of fine twisted linen one hundred cubits long for that side;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the court of the habitation,—on the south side southward, hangings for the court of fine-twined linen, a hundred cubits in length to the one side;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen of an hundred cubits long for one side:
text.NABRE: Court of the Tabernacle. You shall also make a court for the tabernacle. On the south side the court shall have hangings, of fine linen twined, a hundred cubits long,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also the court of the tabernacle, in the south part whereof against the south there shall be hangings of twisted silk: one side shall hold in length an hundred cubits.

text.NRSV: its twenty pillars and their twenty bases shall be of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands shall be of silver.
text.Rotherham: and the pillars thereof twenty, with their sockets twenty, of bronze,—and the hooks of the pillars and their cross-rods of silver.
text.KJV: And the twenty pillars thereof and their twenty sockets shall be of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets shall be of silver.
text.NABRE: with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze; the hooks and bands on the columns shall be of silver.
text.DRB: And twenty pillars with as many feet of brass, which shall have heads with their engravings of silver.

text.NRSV: Likewise for its length on the north side there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long, their pillars twenty and their bases twenty, of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands shall be of silver.
text.Rotherham: And, so, for the north side in length, hangings a hundred cubits,—and the pillars thereof twenty, and their sockets twenty. of bronze, and the hooks of the pillars and their cross-rods of silver.
text.KJV: And likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings of an hundred cubits long, and his twenty pillars and their twenty sockets of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
text.NABRE: On the north side there shall be similar hangings, a hundred cubits long, with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze; the hooks and bands on the columns shall be of silver.
text.DRB: In like manner also on the north side there shall be in length hangings of an hundred cubits, twenty pillars, and feet of brass as many, and their heads with their engravings of silver.

text.NRSV: For the width of the court on the west side there shall be fifty cubits of hangings, with ten pillars and ten bases.
text.Rotherham: And, the breadth of the court on the west side, hangings fifty cubits,—their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.
text.KJV: And for the breadth of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits: their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.
text.NABRE: On the west side, across the width of the court, there shall be hangings, fifty cubits long, with ten columns and ten pedestals.
text.DRB: But in the breadth of the court, that looketh to the west, there shall be hangings of fifty cubits, and ten pillars, and as many feet.

text.NRSV: The width of the court on the front to the east shall be fifty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, the breadth of the court on the east side eastward, fifty cubits;
text.KJV: And the breadth of the court on the east side eastward shall be fifty cubits.
text.NABRE: The width of the court on the east side shall be fifty cubits.
text.DRB: In that breadth also of the court, which looketh to the east, there shall be fifty cubits.

text.NRSV: There shall be fifteen cubits of hangings on the one side, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and fifteen cubits the hangings for the side-piece,—their pillars three, and their sockets three;
text.KJV: The hangings of one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.
text.NABRE: On one side there shall be hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals;
text.DRB: In the which there shall be deputed to one side hangings of fifteen cubits, and three pillars and as many feet:

text.NRSV: There shall be fifteen cubits of hangings on the other side, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and, for the second side-piece, fifteen hangings,—their pillars three, and their sockets three,
text.KJV: And on the other side shall be hangings fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.
text.NABRE: on the other side there shall be hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals.
text.DRB: and in the other side there shall be hangings containing fifteen cubits, three pillars, and as many feet.

text.NRSV: For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework; it shall have four pillars and with them four bases.
text.Rotherham: And for the gate of the court, a screen of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen, the work of an embroiderer,—their pillars four, and their sockets four.
text.KJV: And for the gate of the court shall be an hanging of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework: and their pillars shall be four, and their sockets four.
text.NABRE: At the gate of the court there shall be a variegated curtain, twenty cubits long, woven of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined. It shall have four columns and four pedestals.
text.DRB: And in the entering of the court there shall be made an hanging of twenty cubits of hyacinth and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk, with embroidered work: it shall have four pillars, with as many feet.

text.NRSV: All the pillars around the court shall be banded with silver; their hooks shall be of silver, and their bases of bronze.
text.Rotherham: All the pillars of the court round about, to be joined with cross-rods of silver, and their hooks of silver,—and their sockets of bronze,
text.KJV: All the pillars round about the court shall be filleted with silver; their hooks shall be of silver, and their sockets of brass.
text.NABRE: All the columns around the court shall have bands and hooks of silver, and pedestals of bronze.
