text.DRB: All the pillars of the court round about shall be garnished with plates of silver, silver heads, and feet of brass.

text.NRSV: The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits, the width fifty, and the height five cubits, with hangings of fine twisted linen and bases of bronze.
text.Rotherham: The length of the court, a hundred cubits and the breadth fifty throughout and the height five cubits of fine-twined linen,—and their sockets, of bronze.
text.KJV: The length of the court shall be an hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty every where, and the height five cubits of fine twined linen, and their sockets of brass.
text.NABRE: The court is to be one hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and five cubits high. Fine linen twined must be used, and the pedestals must be of bronze.
text.DRB: In length the court shall occupy and hundred cubits, in breadth fifty, the height, shall be of five cubits, and it shall be made of twisted silk, and shall have feet of brass.

text.NRSV: All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use, and all its pegs and all the pegs of the court, shall be of bronze.
text.Rotherham: All the utensils of the habitation, throughout all the service thereof,—and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, of bronze.
text.KJV: All the vessels of the tabernacle in all the service thereof, and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, shall be of brass.
text.NABRE: All the fittings of the tabernacle, whatever be their use, as well as all its tent pegs and all the tent pegs of the court, must be of bronze.
text.DRB: All the vessels of the tabernacle for all uses and ceremonies, the pins as well of it as of the court, thou shalt make of brass.

text.NRSV: You shall further command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the light, so that a lamp may be set up to burn regularly.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, shalt command the sons of Israel that they bring unto thee—pure, beaten olive-oil, for giving light,—that the lamp may lift up its flame continually.
text.KJV: And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn always.
text.NABRE: Oil for the Lamps. You shall command the Israelites to bring you clear oil of crushed olives, to be used for the light, so that you may keep lamps burning always.
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel that they bring thee oil of the olive-trees the purest, and beaten with a pestle: that a lamp may burn always.

text.NRSV: In the tent of meeting, outside the curtain that is before the covenant, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening to morning before the Lord. It shall be a perpetual ordinance to be observed throughout their generations by the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: In the tent of meeting, outside the veil which is by the testimony, shall Aaron and his sons order it from evening until morning, before Yahweh,—a statute age-abiding, to their generations, from the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: In the tabernacle of the congregation without the vail, which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall order it from evening to morning before the Lord: it shall be a statute for ever unto their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: From evening to morning Aaron and his sons shall maintain them before the Lord in the tent of meeting, outside the veil which hangs in front of the covenant. This shall be a perpetual statute for the Israelites throughout their generations.
text.DRB: in the tabernacle of the testimony, without the veil that is drawn before the testimony. And Aaron and his sons, shall place it, that it may give light before the Lord until the morning. Is shall be a perpetual observance throughout their successions before the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then bring near to you your brother Aaron, and his sons with him, from among the Israelites, to serve me as priests—Aaron and Aaron’s sons, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore bring near unto thee—Aaron thy brother and his sons with him out of the midst of the sons of Israel, that they may minister as priests unto me,—Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar sons of Aaron.
text.KJV: And take thou unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
text.NABRE: Have your brother Aaron, and with him his sons, brought to you, from among the Israelites, that they may be my priests: Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
text.DRB: Take unto thee also Aaron thy brother with his sons, from among the children of Israel that they may do the function of priesthood unto me: Aaron, Nadab, and Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: You shall make sacred vestments for the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother,—for glory and for beauty.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother for glory and for beauty.
text.NABRE: For the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron you shall have sacred vestments made.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make an holy vesture to Aaron thy brother for glory and beauty.

text.NRSV: And you shall speak to all who have ability, whom I have endowed with skill, that they make Aaron’s vestments to consecrate him for my priesthood.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, shalt speak unto all the wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom,—and they shall make Aaron’s garments, to hallow him for ministering as priest unto me.
text.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto all that are wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron’s garments to consecrate him, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: Therefore, tell the various artisans whom I have endowed with skill to make vestments for Aaron to consecrate him as my priest.
text.DRB: And thou shalt speak to all the wise of heart, whom I have replenished with the spirit of wisdom, that they make Aaron's vestures, wherein he being sanctified may minister to me.

text.NRSV: These are the vestments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a checkered tunic, a turban, and a sash. When they make these sacred vestments for your brother Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests,
text.Rotherham: These, therefore are the garments which they shall make—a breastpiece, and an ephod and a robe, and a tunic of checker work, a turban and a girdle,—so shall they make holy garments for Aaron thy brother and for his sons, for ministering as priests unto me.
text.KJV: And these are the garments which they shall make; a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre, and a girdle: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: These are the vestments they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a brocade tunic, a turban, and a sash. In making these sacred vestments which your brother Aaron and his sons are to wear in serving as my priests,
text.DRB: And these shall be the vestments that they shall make: Rationale and an Ephod, a tunic and a strait linen garment, a mitre and a girdle. They shall make the holy vestments for thy brother Aaron and his sons, that they may do the function of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: they shall use gold, blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine linen.
text.Rotherham: They themselves, therefore, shall take the gold, and the blue and the purple,—and the crimson, and the fine linen.
text.KJV: And they shall take gold, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen.
text.NABRE: they shall use gold, violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen.
text.DRB: And they shall take gold, and hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and silk.

text.NRSV: They shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen, skillfully worked.
text.Rotherham: And they shall make the ephod,—of gold, blue and purple crimson and fine-twined linen, the work of a skilful weaver.
text.KJV: And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and of purple, of scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cunning work.
text.NABRE: The Ephod and Breastpiece. The ephod they shall make of gold thread and of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, embroidered on cloth of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: And they shall make the Ephod of gold and hyacinth and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk, embroidered with diverse colours.

text.NRSV: It shall have two shoulder-pieces attached to its two edges, so that it may be joined together.
text.Rotherham: Two joining shoulderpieces, shall there be to it upon the two extremities thereof that it may be joined together,
text.KJV: It shall have the two shoulderpieces thereof joined at the two edges thereof; and so it shall be joined together.
text.NABRE: It shall have a pair of shoulder straps joined to its two upper ends.
text.DRB: It shall have two edges joined in the top on both sides, that they may be closed together.

text.NRSV: The decorated band on it shall be of the same workmanship and materials, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And the curious band for fastening it, that is upon it, like the work thereof of the same, shall it be,—of gold blue and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen,
text.KJV: And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, shall be of the same, according to the work thereof; even of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
text.NABRE: The embroidered belt of the ephod shall extend out from it and, like it, be made of gold thread, of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: The very workmanship also and all the variety of the work shall be of gold and hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk.

text.NRSV: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take two onyx stones,—and engrave upon them the names of the sons of Israel:
text.KJV: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and grave on them the names of the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: Get two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:
text.DRB: And thou shalt take two Onyx stones, and shalt grave in them the names of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the remaining six on the other stone, in the order of their birth.
text.Rotherham: six of their names, on the one stone,—and the names of the six that remain, on the second stone according to their births:
text.KJV: Six of their names on one stone, and the other six names of the rest on the other stone, according to their birth.
text.NABRE: six of their names on one stone, and the names of the remaining six on the other stone, in the order of their birth.
text.DRB: six names in one stone, and the other six in the other, according to the order of their nativity.

text.NRSV: As a gem-cutter engraves signets, so you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel; you shall mount them in settings of gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: with the work of a stone engraver, like seal-ring engravings, shalt thou engrave the two stones, after the names of the sons of Israel,—to be encircled with settings of gold, shalt thou make them.
text.KJV: With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel: thou shalt make them to be set in ouches of gold.
text.NABRE: As a gem-cutter engraves a seal, so shall you have the two stones engraved with the names of the sons of Israel and then mounted in gold filigree work.
text.DRB: After the work of a graver and the graving of a lapidary, thou shalt grave them with the names of the children of Israel, set in gold and compassed about:

text.NRSV: You shall set the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel; and Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord on his two shoulders for remembrance.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulderpieces of the ephod, as stones of memorial for the sons of Israel,—so shall Aaron bear their names before Yahweh upon his two shoulders, for a memorial.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulders of the ephod for stones of memorial unto the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord upon his two shoulders for a memorial.
text.NABRE: Set these two stones on the shoulder straps of the ephod as memorial stones of the sons of Israel. Thus Aaron shall bear their names on his shoulders as a reminder before the Lord.
text.DRB: and thou shalt put them in both sides of the Ephod, a memorial for the children of Israel. And Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord upon both shoulders, for a remembrance.

text.NRSV: You shall make settings of gold filigree,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make ouches of gold;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make ouches of gold;
text.NABRE: Make filigree rosettes of gold,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also hooks of gold,

text.NRSV: and two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords; and you shall attach the corded chains to the settings.
text.Rotherham: and two chains of pure gold, like cord, shalt thou make them, of wreathen work, and thou shalt put the wreathen chains on the ouches.
text.KJV: And two chains of pure gold at the ends; of wreathen work shalt thou make them, and fasten the wreathen chains to the ouches.
text.NABRE: as well as two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, and fasten the cordlike chains to the filigree rosettes.
text.DRB: and two little chains of most pure gold linked one to another, which thou shalt put into the hooks.

text.NRSV: You shall make a breastpiece of judgment, in skilled work; you shall make it in the style of the ephod; of gold, of blue and purple and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen you shall make it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a breastpiece for giving sentence the work of a skilful weaver, like the work of the ephod, shalt thou make it, of gold, blue, and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen, shalt thou make it.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the breastplate of judgment with cunning work; after the work of the ephod thou shalt make it; of gold, of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.
text.NABRE: The breastpiece of decision you shall also have made, embroidered like the ephod with gold thread and violet, purple, and scarlet yarn on cloth of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: The Rationale of judgment also thou shalt make with embroidered work of diverse colours, according to the workmanship of the Ephod of gold, hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk.

text.NRSV: It shall be square and doubled, a span in length and a span in width.
text.Rotherham: Four-square, shall it be double,—a span the length thereof and a span the breadth thereof.
text.KJV: Foursquare it shall be being doubled; a span shall be the length thereof, and a span shall be the breadth thereof.
text.NABRE: It is to be square when folded double, a span high and a span wide.
text.DRB: It shall be four square and double: it shall have the measure of a palm as well in length as in breadth.

text.NRSV: You shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of carnelian, chrysolite, and emerald shall be the first row;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set therein a setting of stones, four rows of stones,—one row—a sardius, a topaz and an emerald, the first row;
text.KJV: And thou shalt set in it settings of stones, even four rows of stones: the first row shall be a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: this shall be the first row.
text.NABRE: On it you shall mount four rows of precious stones: in the first row, a carnelian, a topaz, and an emerald;
text.DRB: And thou shalt set in it four rews of stones: In the first rew shall be the stone sardius, and topazius, and the emerald:

text.NRSV: and the second row a turquoise, a sapphire and a moonstone;
text.Rotherham: and the second row a carbuncle a sapphire and a diamond;
text.KJV: And the second row shall be an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.
text.NABRE: in the second row, a garnet, a sapphire, and a beryl;
text.DRB: in the second the carbuncle, the sapphire, & the jasper:

text.NRSV: and the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.Rotherham: and the third row an opal, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.KJV: And the third row a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.
text.NABRE: in the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.DRB: in the third a ligurius, an achates, an amethyst:

text.NRSV: and the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper; they shall be set in gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: and the fourth row, a Tarshish stone, a sardonyx, and a jasper,—enclosed with gold, shall they be when they are set.
text.KJV: And the fourth row a beryl, and an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be set in gold in their inclosings.
text.NABRE: in the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper. These stones are to be mounted in gold filigree work,
text.DRB: in the fourth a chrysolith, an onyx, and berillus. They shall be set in gold by their rews.

text.NRSV: There shall be twelve stones with names corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they shall be like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.
text.Rotherham: And the stones shall be after the names of the sons of Israel—twelve, after their names,—as the engravings of a seal-ring everyone after his name, shall they be, for the twelve tribes,
text.KJV: And the stones shall be with the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, like the engravings of a signet; every one with his name shall they be according to the twelve tribes.
text.NABRE: twelve of them to match the names of the sons of Israel, each stone engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes.
text.DRB: And they shall have the names of the children of Israel: with twelve names shall they be graven, every stone with the names of every one according to the twelve tribes.

text.NRSV: You shall make for the breastpiece chains of pure gold, twisted like cords;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make, upon the breastpiece, chains like cords of wreathen work,—of pure gold.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make upon the breastplate chains at the ends of wreathen work of pure gold.
text.NABRE: When the chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, have been made for the breastpiece,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make in the Rationale chains linked one to another of the purest gold:

text.NRSV: and you shall make for the breastpiece two rings of gold, and put the two rings on the two edges of the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make, upon the breastpiece, two rings of gold,—and thou shalt place the two rings, upon the two ends of the breastpiece.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make upon the breastplate two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
text.NABRE: you shall then make two rings of gold for it and fasten them to the two upper ends of the breastpiece.
text.DRB: and two rings of gold, which thou shalt put in both the tops of the Rationale:

text.NRSV: You shall put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hang the two wreathen chains of gold upon the two rings,—upon the ends of the breastpiece;
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings which are on the ends of the breastplate.
text.NABRE: The gold cords are then to be fastened to the two rings at the upper ends of the breastpiece,
text.DRB: and the golden chains thou shalt join to the rings, that are in the edges thereof:

text.NRSV: the two ends of the two cords you shall attach to the two settings, and so attach it in front to the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: and the other two ends of the two wreathen chains, shalt thou fasten upon the two ouches, so shalt thou hang them upon the shoulderpieces of the ephod, in the forefront thereof.
text.KJV: And the other two ends of the two wreathen chains thou shalt fasten in the two ouches, and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod before it.
text.NABRE: the other two ends of the cords being fastened in front to the two filigree rosettes which are attached to the shoulder straps of the ephod.
text.DRB: and the ends of the chains themselves thou shalt couple with two hooks on both sides of the Ephod, which is toward the Rationale.

text.NRSV: You shall make two rings of gold, and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on its inside edge next to the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastpiece upon the border thereof, which is on the side of the ephod inwards.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the breastplate in the border thereof, which is in the side of the ephod inward.
text.NABRE: Make two other rings of gold and put them on the two lower ends of the breastpiece, on its edge that faces the ephod.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also two rings of gold which thou shalt put in the tops of the Rationale, in the brims, that are over against the Ephod, & look toward the back parts thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall make two rings of gold, and attach them in front to the lower part of the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod, at its joining above the decorated band of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and place them upon the two shoulderpieces of the ephod beneath, on the front of the face thereof, over against its joining, above the curious band of the ephod:
text.KJV: And two other rings of gold thou shalt make, and shalt put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart thereof, over against the other coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.
text.NABRE: Then make two more rings of gold and fasten them to the bottom of the shoulder straps next to where they join the ephod in front, just above its embroidered belt.
text.DRB: Moreover also other two rings of gold, which are to be set on both sides of the Ephod beneath, that looketh toward the nether joining, that the Rationale may be fitted with the Ephod,

text.NRSV: The breastpiece shall be bound by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord, so that it may lie on the decorated band of the ephod, and so that the breastpiece shall not come loose from the ephod.
text.Rotherham: that they may bind the breastpiece by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue, so that it may remain upon the curious band of the ephod, and the breastpiece not be removed from of the ephod.
text.KJV: And they shall bind the breastplate by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.
text.NABRE: Violet ribbons shall bind the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod, so that the breastpiece will stay right above the embroidered belt of the ephod and not swing loose from it.
text.DRB: and may be fastened by the rings thereof unto the rings of the Ephod with a lace of hyacinth, that the joining artificially wrought may continue, & the Rationale & Ephod may not be separated one from the other.

text.NRSV: So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart when he goes into the holy place, for a continual remembrance before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece for giving sentence upon his heart when he goeth into the holy place,—for a memorial before Yahweh continually.
text.KJV: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy place, for a memorial before the Lord continually.
text.NABRE: Whenever Aaron enters the sanctuary, he will thus bear the names of the sons of Israel on the breastpiece of decision over his heart as a constant reminder before the Lord.
text.DRB: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the Rationale of judgment upon his breast, when he shall enter into the Sanctuary, a memorial before the Lord for ever.

text.NRSV: In the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be on Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the Lord; thus Aaron shall bear the judgment of the Israelites on his heart before the Lord continually.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou place, in the breastpiece for giving sentence, the lights and the perfections, so shall they be upon the heart of Aaron when he goeth in before Yahweh,—so shall Aaron bear the sentence of the sons of Israel, upon his heart before Yahweh continually.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron’s heart, when he goeth in before the Lord: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the Lord continually.
text.NABRE: In this breastpiece of decision you shall put the Urim and Thummim, that they may be over Aaron’s heart whenever he enters the presence of the Lord. Thus he shall always bear the decisions for the Israelites over his heart in the presence of the Lord.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put in the Rationale of Judgment Doctrine, and Verity, which shall be on Aaron's breast, when he shall go in before the Lord: and he shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his breast, in the sight of the Lord always.

text.NRSV: You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod wholly of blue;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
text.NABRE: Other Vestments. The robe of the ephod you shall make entirely of violet material.
text.DRB: And thou shalt make the tunic of the Ephod all of hyacinth,

text.NRSV: It shall have an opening for the head in the middle of it, with a woven binding around the opening, like the opening in a coat of mail, so that it may not be torn.
text.Rotherham: and the opening for his head shall be in the midst thereof,—a border, shall there be to the opening thereof round about the work of a weaver, like the opening of a coat of mail, shall there be to it, it must not be rent.
text.KJV: And there shall be an hole in the top of it, in the midst thereof: it shall have a binding of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of an habergeon, that it be not rent.
text.NABRE: It shall have an opening for the head in the center, and around this opening there shall be a selvage, woven as at the opening of a shirt, to keep it from being torn.
text.DRB: in the midst whereof above shall be a hole for the head, and a border round about it woven, as is wont to be made in the utmost parts of garments, that it may not easily be broken.

text.NRSV: On its lower hem you shall make pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, all around the lower hem, with bells of gold between them all around—
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make upon the skirts thereof pomegranates of blue and purple and crimson, upon the skirts thereof round about—and bells of gold in the midst of them round about:
text.KJV: And beneath upon the hem of it thou shalt make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, round about the hem thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:
text.NABRE: At the hem at the bottom you shall make pomegranates, woven of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen twined, with gold bells between them;
text.DRB: And beneath at the feet of the same tunic, round about, thou shalt make as it were pomegranates, of hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, little bells interposed between,

text.NRSV: a golden bell and a pomegranate alternating all around the lower hem of the robe.
text.Rotherham: a bell of gold and a pomegranate, a bell of gold, and a pomegranate,—upon the skirts of the robe, round about.
text.KJV: A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the hem of the robe round about.
text.NABRE: a gold bell, a pomegranate, a gold bell, a pomegranate, all around the hem of the robe.
text.DRB: so that there be a bell of gold and a pomegranate; and again another bell of gold & a pomegranate.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall wear it when he ministers, and its sound shall be heard when he goes into the holy place before the Lord, and when he comes out, so that he may not die.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be upon Aaron, for ministering,—so shall be heard the sound of him—when he goeth into the holy place, before Yahweh, and cometh out, and dieth not.
text.KJV: And it shall be upon Aaron to minister: and his sound shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy place before the Lord, and when he cometh out, that he die not.
text.NABRE: Aaron shall wear it when ministering, that its sound may be heard as he enters and leaves the Lord’s presence in the sanctuary; else he will die.
text.DRB: And Aaron shall be vested with it in the office of his ministry, that the sound may be heard, when he goeth in & cometh out of the Sanctuary, in the sight of the Lord, & that he die not.

text.NRSV: You shall make a rosette of pure gold, and engrave on it, like the engraving of a signet, “Holy to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a burnished plate, of pure gold,—and shalt engrave thereupon, like the engravings of a seal-ring, Holiness to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and grave upon it, like the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.
text.NABRE: You shall also make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it, as on a seal engraving, “Sacred to the Lord.”
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a plate of the purest gold: wherein thou shalt grave after the work of a graver, Holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall fasten it on the turban with a blue cord; it shall be on the front of the turban.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put it upon a cord of blue, so shall it be upon the turban,—upon the forefront of the turban, shall it be.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put it on a blue lace, that it may be upon the mitre; upon the forefront of the mitre it shall be.
text.NABRE: This plate is to be tied over the turban with a violet ribbon in such a way that it rests on the front of the turban,
text.DRB: And thou shalt tie it with a lace of hyacinth, and it shall be upon the mitre,

text.NRSV: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall take on himself any guilt incurred in the holy offering that the Israelites consecrate as their sacred donations; it shall always be on his forehead, in order that they may find favor before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be upon the forehead of Aaron, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the hallowed things, which the sons of Israel shall hallow, for all their hallowed gift,—and it shall be upon his forehead continually, that there may be acceptance for them before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And it shall be upon Aaron’s forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the Lord.
text.NABRE: over Aaron’s forehead. Since Aaron bears whatever guilt the Israelites may incur in consecrating any of their sacred gifts, this plate must always be over his forehead, so that they may find favor with the Lord.
text.DRB: hanging over the forehead of the High-Priest. And Aaron shall carry the iniquities of those things, which the children of Israel have offered and sanctified, in all their gifts and donaries. And the plate shall be always in his forehead, that the Lord may be well pleased with them.

text.NRSV: You shall make the checkered tunic of fine linen, and you shall make a turban of fine linen, and you shall make a sash embroidered with needlework.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt weave, in checker work the tunic of fine linen, and shalt make a turban of fine linen,—and, a girdle, shalt thou make the work of an embroiderer.
text.KJV: And thou shalt embroider the coat of fine linen, and thou shalt make the mitre of fine linen, and thou shalt make the girdle of needlework.
text.NABRE: The tunic of fine linen shall be brocaded. The turban shall be made of fine linen. The sash shall be of variegated work.
text.DRB: And thou shalt gird the tunic with silk, and thou shalt make a silken mitre, and a bawdrike of embroidered work.

text.NRSV: For Aaron’s sons you shall make tunics and sashes and headdresses; you shall make them for their glorious adornment.
text.Rotherham: And, for the sons of Aaron, shalt thou make tunics, and shalt make for them girdles,—and caps, shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.
text.KJV: And for Aaron’s sons thou shalt make coats, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and bonnets shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.
text.NABRE: Likewise, for the glorious adornment of Aaron’s sons you shall have tunics and sashes and skullcaps made, for glorious splendor.
text.DRB: Moreover for the sons of Aaron thou shalt prepare linen tunics, and bawdrikes, and mitres for glory and beauty:

text.NRSV: You shall put them on your brother Aaron, and on his sons with him, and shall anoint them and ordain them and consecrate them, so that they may serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt clothe therewith Aaron thy brother, and his sons, with him,—and shalt anoint them and install them, and hallow them, so shall they minister as priests unto me.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: With these you shall clothe your brother Aaron and his sons. Anoint and install them, consecrating them as my priests.
text.DRB: And with all these things thou shalt vest Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him. And thou shalt consecrate the hands of them all, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the function of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: You shall make for them linen undergarments to cover their naked flesh; they shall reach from the hips to the thighs;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make for them breeches of linen, to cover their unseemliness, from the loins even unto the thighs, shall they be;
text.KJV: And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their nakedness; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach:
text.NABRE: You must also make linen pants for them, to cover their naked flesh from their loins to their thighs.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also linen breeches, to cover the flesh of their turpitude from the reins unto the thighs:

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons shall wear them when they go into the tent of meeting, or when they come near the altar to minister in the holy place; or they will bring guilt on themselves and die. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for him and for his descendants after him.
text.Rotherham: so shall they be upon Aaron and upon his sons, when they go into the tent of meeting, or when they approach unto the altar, to minister in the holy place, lest they bear iniquity, and die,—a statute age-abiding, to him and to his seed after him.
text.KJV: And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute for ever unto him and his seed after him.
text.NABRE: Aaron and his sons shall wear them whenever they go into the tent of meeting or approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary, lest they incur guilt and die. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for him and for his descendants.
text.DRB: and Aaron and his sons shall use them when they shall enter into the tabernacle of testimony, or when they approach to the Altar to minister in the Sanctuary, lest guilty of iniquity they die. It shall be a law for ever to Aaron and to his seed after him.

text.NRSV: Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them, so that they may serve me as priests. Take one young bull and two rams without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the thing which thou shalt do to them to hallow them, that they may minister as priests unto me,—Take one young bullock and two rams without defect;
text.KJV: And this is the thing that thou shalt do unto them to hallow them, to minister unto me in the priest’s office: Take one young bullock, and two rams without blemish,
text.NABRE: This is the rite you shall perform in consecrating them as my priests. Procure a young bull and two unblemished rams.
text.DRB: But this also shalt thou do, that they may be consecrated to me in priesthood. Take a calf from the herd, and two rams without spot,

text.NRSV: and unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers spread with oil. You shall make them of choice wheat flour.
text.Rotherham: and bread unleavened and cakes unleavened with oil poured over, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil,—of fine wheaten meal, shalt thou make them;
text.KJV: And unleavened bread, and cakes unleavened tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil: of wheaten flour shalt thou make them.
text.NABRE: With bran flour make unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers spread with oil,
text.DRB: and unleavened bread, and a cake without leaven, tempered with oil, wafers also unleavened anointed with oil: of wheaten flour thou shalt make all.

text.NRSV: You shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket, and bring the bull and the two rams.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place them in one basket, and bring them near in the basket,—and the bullock, and the two rams.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.
text.NABRE: and put them in a basket. Take the basket of them along with the bull and the two rams.
text.DRB: And: being put in a basket thou shalt offer them: and the calf and the two rams.

text.NRSV: You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and wash them with water.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and his sons, shalt thou bring near, unto the opening of the tent of meeting,—and shalt bathe them in water.
text.KJV: And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shalt wash them with water.
text.NABRE: Aaron and his sons you shall also bring to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there wash them with water.
text.DRB: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of testimony: And when thou hast washed the father with his sons in water,

text.NRSV: Then you shall take the vestments, and put on Aaron the tunic and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastpiece, and gird him with the decorated band of the ephod;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take the garments and clothe Aaron—with the tunic, and with the robe of the ephod, and with the ephod, and with the breastpiece,—and shalt gird him with the curious band of the ephod;
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the garments, and put upon Aaron the coat, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the curious girdle of the ephod:
text.NABRE: Take the vestments and clothe Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself, and the breastpiece, fastening the embroidered belt of the ephod around him.
text.DRB: thou shalt vest Aaron with his vestments, that is, with the linen garment and the tunic, and the Ephod and the Rationale, which thou shalt gird with the bawdrike.

text.NRSV: and you shall set the turban on his head, and put the holy diadem on the turban.
text.Rotherham: and shalt put the turban upon his head,—and place the holy crown upon the turban.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and put the holy crown upon the mitre.
text.NABRE: Put the turban on his head, the sacred diadem on the turban.
text.DRB: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and the holy plate upon the mitre,

text.NRSV: You shall take the anointing oil, and pour it on his head and anoint him.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour upon his head,—so shalt thou anoint him.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour it upon his head, and anoint him.
text.NABRE: Then take the anointing oil and pour it on his head, and anoint him.
text.DRB: and thou shalt pour the oil of unction upon his head: and by this rite shall he be consecrated.

text.NRSV: Then you shall bring his sons, and put tunics on them,
text.Rotherham: His sons also, shalt thou bring near,—and clothe them with tunics;
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring his sons, and put coats upon them.
text.NABRE: Bring forward his sons also and clothe them with the tunics,
text.DRB: His sons also thou shalt bring, and shalt invest them with the linen tunics, and gird them with a bawdrike,

text.NRSV: and you shall gird them with sashes and tie headdresses on them; and the priesthood shall be theirs by a perpetual ordinance. You shall then ordain Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and twist round for them caps, so shall they have priesthood by a statute age-abiding,—so shalt thou install, Aaron and his sons.
text.KJV: And thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and put the bonnets on them: and the priest’s office shall be theirs for a perpetual statute: and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.
text.NABRE: gird them with the sashes, and tie the skullcaps on them. Thus shall the priesthood be theirs by a perpetual statute, and thus shall you install Aaron and his sons.
text.DRB: to wit, Aaron and his children, and thou shalt put mitres upon them: and they shall be Priests to me by a perpetual religion. After that thou shalt have consecrated their hands,

text.NRSV: You shall bring the bull in front of the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt bring near the bullock, before the tent of meeting,—and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands upon the head of the bullock;
text.KJV: And thou shalt cause a bullock to be brought before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the bullock.
text.NABRE: Installation Sacrifices. Now bring forward the bull in front of the tent of meeting. There Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on its head.
text.DRB: thou shalt present also the calf before the tabernacle of testimony. And Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon his head,

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the bull before the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the bullock before Yahweh,—at the opening of the tent of meeting:
text.KJV: And thou shalt kill the bullock before the Lord, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Then slaughter the bull before the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and thou shalt kill him in the sight of the Lord, beside the door of the tabernacle of testimony.

text.NRSV: and shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger, and all the rest of the blood you shall pour out at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and place upon the horns of the altar, with thy finger,—and all the remaining blood, shalt thou pour out at the foundation of the altar;
text.KJV: And thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and pour all the blood beside the bottom of the altar.
text.NABRE: Take some of its blood and with your finger put it on the horns of the altar. All the rest of the blood you shall pour out at the base of the altar.
text.DRB: And that which thou takest of the blood of the calf, thou shalt put upon the horns of the Altar with thy finger, and the rest of the blood thou shalt pour at the bottom thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails, and the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and turn them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inner part, and the caul upon the liver, and the two kidneys, with the fat which is upon them, and burn as incense on the altar:
text.KJV: And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul that is above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and burn them upon the altar.
text.NABRE: All the fat that covers its inner organs, as well as the lobe of its liver and its two kidneys, together with the fat that is on them, you shall take and burn on the altar.
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also the whole fat that covereth the entrails, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and shalt offer a burnt sacrifice upon the Altar:

text.NRSV: But the flesh of the bull, and its skin, and its dung, you shall burn with fire outside the camp; it is a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: but the flesh of the bullock and its skin, and its dung, shalt thou burn up with fire, outside the camp,—a sin-bearer, it is.
text.KJV: But the flesh of the bullock, and his skin, and his dung, shalt thou burn with fire without the camp: it is a sin offering.
text.NABRE: But the meat and hide and dung of the bull you must burn up outside the camp, since this is a purification offering.
text.DRB: but the flesh of the calf and the hide and the dung, thou shalt burn abroad without the camp, because it is for sin.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take one of the rams, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: One ram also, shalt thou take, and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands, upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: Thou shalt also take one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.
text.NABRE: Then take one of the rams, and after Aaron and his sons have laid their hands on its head,
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also one ram, upon the head whereof Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the ram, and shall take its blood and dash it against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the ram,—and take its blood, and dash upon the altar round about;
text.KJV: And thou shalt slay the ram, and thou shalt take his blood, and sprinkle it round about upon the altar.
text.NABRE: slaughter it. The blood you shall take and splash on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: Which when thou hast killed, thou shalt take of the blood thereof, and pour round about the Altar.

text.NRSV: Then you shall cut the ram into its parts, and wash its entrails and its legs, and put them with its parts and its head,
text.Rotherham: but the ram itself, shalt thou cut up into its pieces,—and shalt bathe its inner part, and its legs, and lay upon its pieces and upon its head;
text.KJV: And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and wash the inwards of him, and his legs, and put them unto his pieces, and unto his head.
text.NABRE: Cut the ram into pieces; you shall wash its inner organs and shanks and put them with the pieces and with the head.
text.DRB: And the ram itself thou shalt cut into pieces, and his entrails and feet being washed, thou shalt put upon the flesh cut in pieces, and upon his head.

text.NRSV: and turn the whole ram into smoke on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the Lord; it is a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt burn the whole ram as incense on the altar, an ascending-sacrifice, it is to Yahweh,—a satisfying odour, an altar-flame to Yahweh, it is.
text.KJV: And thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar: it is a burnt offering unto the Lord: it is a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then you shall burn the entire ram on the altar, since it is a burnt offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And thou shalt offer the whole ram for a burnt sacrifice upon the Altar: it is an oblation to the Lord, a most sweet savour of the victim of the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the second ram,—and Aaron and his sons shall lean their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.
text.NABRE: After this take the other ram, and when Aaron and his sons have laid their hands on its head,
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also the other ram, upon whose head Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

text.NRSV: and you shall slaughter the ram, and take some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the lobes of the right ears of his sons, and on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet, and dash the rest of the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou slay the ram, and take of its blood, and place upon the tip of the ear of Aaron and upon the tip of the ear of his sons—the right ear, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot,—and shalt dash the blood upon the altar round about.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of his blood, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.
text.NABRE: slaughter it. Some of its blood you shall take and put on the tip of Aaron’s right ear and on the tips of his sons’ right ears and on the thumbs of their right hands and the great toes of their right feet. Splash the rest of the blood on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: Which when thou hast immolated, thou shalt take of his blood, and put upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron and of his sons, and upon the thumbs and great toes of their right hand and foot, and thou shalt pour the blood upon the Altar round about.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar, and some of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it on Aaron and his vestments and on his sons and his sons’ vestments with him; then he and his vestments shall be holy, as well as his sons and his sons’ vestments.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take of the blood that is upon the altar and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle upon Aaron and upon his garments, and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him,—so shall he be hallowed—he, and his garments, and his sons and the garments of his sons, with him.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be hallowed, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.
text.NABRE: Then take some of the blood that is on the altar, together with some of the anointing oil, and sprinkle this on Aaron and his vestments, as well as on his sons and their vestments, that he and his sons and their vestments may be sacred.
text.DRB: And when thou hast taken of the blood that is upon the Altar, and of the oil of unction, thou shalt sprinkle Aaron and his vesture, his sons and their vestments. And after they and their vestments are consecrated,

text.NRSV: You shall also take the fat of the ram, the fat tail, the fat that covers the entrails, the appendage of the liver, the two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of ordination),
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take from the ram—the fat and the fat-tail and the fat that covereth the inner part and the caul of the liver and the two kidneys with the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder,—for, a ram of installation, it is,—
text.KJV: Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat and the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder; for it is a ram of consecration:
text.NABRE: Now, from this ram you shall take its fat: its fatty tail, the fat that covers its inner organs, the lobe of its liver, its two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and its right thigh, since this is the ram for installation;
text.DRB: thou shalt take the fat of the ram, and the tail and the tallow, that covereth the lungs, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat, that is upon them, and the right shoulder, because it is the ram of consecration:

text.NRSV: and one loaf of bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer, out of the basket of unleavened bread that is before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: and one round-cake of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer,—out of the basket of unleavened cakes, which is before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened bread that is before the Lord:
text.NABRE: then, out of the basket of unleavened food that you have set before the Lord, you shall take one of the loaves of bread, one of the cakes made with oil, and one of the wafers.
text.DRB: and a piece of one loaf, a cake tempered with oil, a wafer out of the basket of azymes, which is set in the sight of the Lord:

text.NRSV: and you shall place all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and raise them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt lay the whole upon the heads of Aaron, and upon the hands of his sons,—then shalt thou wave them to and fro as a wave-offering, before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And thou shalt put all in the hands of Aaron, and in the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them for a wave offering before the Lord.
text.NABRE: All these things you shall put into the hands of Aaron and his sons, so that they may raise them as an elevated offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: and thou shalt put all upon the hands of Aaron and of his sons, and shalt sanctify them elevating before the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take them from their hands, and turn them into smoke on the altar on top of the burnt offering of pleasing odor before the Lord; it is an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and shalt take them from their hand, and burn them as incense on the altar upon the ascending-sacrifice,—as a satisfying odour, before Yahweh, an altar-flame, it is to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt receive them of their hands, and burn them upon the altar for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour before the Lord: it is an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: After you receive them back from their hands, you shall burn them on top of the burnt offering on the altar as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And thou shalt take all from their hands: and shalt burn them upon the Altar for an holocaust, a most sweet savour in the sight of the Lord, because it is his oblation.

text.NRSV: You shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s ordination and raise it as an elevation offering before the Lord; and it shall be your portion.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take the breast from the ram of installation which is for Aaron, and shalt wave it as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—so shall it be thy share,
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s consecration, and wave it for a wave offering before the Lord: and it shall be thy part.
text.NABRE: Finally, take the brisket of Aaron’s installation ram and raise it as an elevated offering before the Lord; this is to be your own portion.
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also the breast of the ram, wherewith Aaron was consecrated, and elevating it thou shalt sanctify it before the Lord, and it shall fall to thy part.

text.NRSV: You shall consecrate the breast that was raised as an elevation offering and the thigh that was raised as an elevation offering from the ram of ordination, from that which belonged to Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt hallow the breast of the wave-offering, and the shoulder of the heave-offering, which is waved to and fro, and which is heaved up, from the ram of installation, from that which is for Aaron, and from that which is for his sons;
text.KJV: And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:
text.NABRE: Thus shall you set aside the brisket of whatever elevated offering is raised, as well as the thigh of whatever contribution is raised up, whether this be the installation ram or anything else belonging to Aaron or to his sons.
text.DRB: And thou shalt sanctify both the consecrated breast, and the shoulder that thou didst separate of the ram,

text.NRSV: These things shall be a perpetual ordinance for Aaron and his sons from the Israelites, for this is an offering; and it shall be an offering by the Israelites from their sacrifice of offerings of well-being, their offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: so shall it belong to Aaron and to his sons for an age-abiding statute from the sons of Israel, for a heave-offering, it is,—and, a heave-offering, shall it remain, from the sons of Israel out of their peace-offerings, their heave-offering to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And it shall be Aaron’s and his sons’ by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, even their heave offering unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Such things are due to Aaron and his sons from the Israelites by a perpetual statute as a contribution. From their communion offerings, too, the Israelites shall make a contribution, their contribution to the Lord.
text.DRB: wherewith Aaron was consecrated and his sons, and they shall fall to Aaron's part and his sons by a perpetual right from the children of Israel: because they are the primitives and beginnings of their pacific victims which they offer to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed on to his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.
text.Rotherham: And, the holy garments which belong to Aaron, shall belong to his sons, after him,-to be anointed therein, and to be installed therein,
text.KJV: And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons’ after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.
text.NABRE: The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed down to his sons after him, that in them they may be anointed and installed.
text.DRB: And the holy vesture, which Aaron shall use, his sons shall have after him, that they may be anointed, and their hands consecrated in it.

text.NRSV: The son who is priest in his place shall wear them seven days, when he comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shall they be put on by him from among his sons who is priest in his stead,—by him who goeth into the tent of meeting, to minister in the holy place.
text.KJV: And that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy place.
text.NABRE: The son who succeeds him as priest and who is to enter the tent of meeting to minister in the sanctuary shall be clothed with them for seven days.
text.DRB: He of his sons that shall be appointed High-Priest in his stead, and that shall enter into the tabernacle of testimony to minister in the Sanctuary, shall wear it seven days.

text.NRSV: You shall take the ram of ordination, and boil its flesh in a holy place;
text.Rotherham: And, the ram of installation, shalt thou take,—and shalt boil its flesh, in a holy place;
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.
text.NABRE: You shall take the installation ram and boil its meat in a holy place.
text.DRB: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, & shalt boil the flesh thereof in a holy place;

text.NRSV: and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket,—at the opening of the tent of meeting:
text.KJV: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: At the entrance of the tent of meeting Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram and the bread that is in the basket.
text.DRB: which Aaron shalt eat and his sons. The loaves also, that are in the basket, they shall eat in the entry of the tabernacle of testimony,

text.NRSV: They themselves shall eat the food by which atonement is made, to ordain and consecrate them, but no one else shall eat of them, because they are holy.
text.Rotherham: thus shall they eat those things wherewith a propitiatory-covering hath been made, to install them, to hallow them, but, a stranger, shall not eat, for holy, they are.
text.KJV: And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
text.NABRE: They themselves are to eat of these things by which atonement was made at their installation and consecration; but no unauthorized person may eat of them, since they are sacred.
text.DRB: that it may be a placable sacrifice, & the hands of the offerers may be sanctified. A stranger shall not eat of them, because they are holy.

text.NRSV: If any of the flesh for the ordination, or of the bread, remains until the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
text.Rotherham: And, if there be left over of the flesh of installation, or of the bread until the morning, then shalt thou burn up what is left over, in the fire, it shall not be eaten for holy, it is.
text.KJV: And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
text.NABRE: If some of the meat of the installation sacrifice or some of the bread remains over on the next day, this remnant you must burn up; it is not to be eaten, since it is sacred.
text.DRB: And if there remain of the consecrated flesh, or of the bread till the morning, thou shalt burn the remains with fire: they shall not be eaten, because they are sanctified.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons, just as I have commanded you; through seven days you shall ordain them.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt do for Aaron and for his sons, in this manner, according to all which I have commanded, thee,—seven days, shalt thou install them.
text.KJV: And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all things which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.
text.NABRE: Carry out all these commands in regard to Aaron and his sons just as I have given them to you. Seven days you shall spend installing them,
text.DRB: All that I have commanded thee thou shalt do upon Aaron and his sons. Seven days shalt thou consecrate their hands:

text.NRSV: Also every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall offer a sin offering for the altar, when you make atonement for it, and shall anoint it, to consecrate it.
text.Rotherham: And a sin-bearing bullock, shalt thou offer daily, for them who are to be covered by propitiation, and shalt make a sin-cleansing for the altar, when thou puttest a propitiatory-covering thereon,—and shalt anoint it to hallow it.
text.KJV: And thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.
text.NABRE: sacrificing a bull each day as a purification offering, to make atonement. Thus you shall purify the altar by purging it, and you shall anoint it in order to consecrate it.
text.DRB: & thou shalt offer a calf for sin every day for expiation. And thou shalt cleanse the Altar when thou hast offered the host of expiation, and shalt anoint it unto sanctification.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shalt thou put a propitiatory-covering over the altar, and so shalt hallow it, thus shall the altar become most holy; whosoever toucheth the altar, must be holy.
text.KJV: Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy: whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
text.NABRE: Seven days you shall spend in purging the altar and in consecrating it. Then the altar will be most sacred, and whatever touches it will become sacred.
text.DRB: Seven days shalt thou expiate the altar and sanctify it, and it shall be most Holy, every one, that shall touch it, shall be sanctified.

text.NRSV: Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old regularly each day.
text.Rotherham: This, moreover, is what thou shalt offer upon the altar, two lambs of a year old day by day, continually:
text.KJV: Now this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.
text.NABRE: Now, this is what you shall regularly offer on the altar: two yearling lambs as the sacrifice established for each day;
text.DRB: This is it which thou shalt do upon the Altar: Two lambs of a year old every day continually,

text.NRSV: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer in the evening;
text.Rotherham: The one lamb, shalt thou offer in the morning, and the second lamb, shalt thou offer between the evenings;
text.KJV: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:
text.NABRE: one lamb in the morning and the other lamb at the evening twilight.
text.DRB: one lamb in the morning, and another at even,

text.NRSV: and with the first lamb one-tenth of a measure of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and one-fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering.
text.Rotherham: and a tenth part of fine meal mingled with a fourth part of the hin of beaten oil, and, as a drink-offering, a fourth part of the hin of wine,—with the one lamb.
text.KJV: And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering.
text.NABRE: With the first lamb there shall be a tenth of an ephah of bran flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil of crushed olives and, as its libation, a fourth of a hin of wine.
text.DRB: the tenth part of flour tempered with oil beaten, which shall have in measure the fourth part of an hin, and wine for libation of the same measure to one lamb.

text.NRSV: And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening, and shall offer with it a grain offering and its drink offering, as in the morning, for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the second lamb, shalt thou offer between the evenings,-according to the meal-offering of the morning, and according to the drink-offering thereof, shalt thou offer with it, for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, to Yahweh:
text.KJV: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The other lamb you shall offer at the evening twilight, with the same grain offering and libation as in the morning. You shall offer this as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, according to the rite of the morning oblation, and according to that which we have said, for a savour of sweetness:

text.NRSV: It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
text.Rotherham: a continual ascending-sacrifice to your generations, at the opening of the tent of meeting, before Yahweh, where will meet with you, to speak unto thee there.
text.KJV: This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the Lord: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.
text.NABRE: Throughout your generations this regular burnt offering shall be made before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting, where I will meet you and speak to you.
text.DRB: it is a sacrifice to the Lord, by perpetual oblation unto your generations, at the door of the tabernacle of testimony before the Lord, where I will appoint to speak unto thee.

text.NRSV: I will meet with the Israelites there, and it shall be sanctified by my glory;
text.Rotherham: So will I meet there with the sons of Israel, and it shall be hallowed by my glory.
text.KJV: And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory.
text.NABRE: There, at the altar, I will meet the Israelites; hence, it will be made sacred by my glory.
text.DRB: And there will I command the children of Israel, and the Altar shall be sanctified in my glory.

text.NRSV: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate, to serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I hallow the tent of meeting, and the altar,—and, Aaron and his sons, will I hallow, to minister as priests unto me.
text.KJV: And I will sanctify the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar: I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons, to minister to me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: Thus I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar, just as I also consecrate Aaron and his sons to be my priests.
text.DRB: I will sanctify also the tabernacle of testimony with the Altar & Aaron with his sons, to do the function of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: I will dwell among the Israelites, and I will be their God.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I make my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel, and will be their God;
text.KJV: And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
text.NABRE: I will dwell in the midst of the Israelites and will be their God.
text.DRB: And I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel & will be their God,

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them; I am the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: and they shall know that, I—Yahweh, am their God, who brought them forth out of the land of Egypt that I might make my habitation in their midst, I—Yahweh, their God.
text.KJV: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them: I am the Lord their God.
text.NABRE: They shall know that I, the Lord, am their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt, so that I, the Lord, their God, might dwell among them.
text.DRB: & they shall know that I am the Lord their God, that have brought them out of the Land of Ægypt, that I might abide among them, I the Lord their God.

text.NRSV: You shall make an altar on which to offer incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make an altar, for burning incense, of acacia wood, shalt thou make it:
text.KJV: And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it.
text.NABRE: For burning incense you shall make an altar of acacia wood,
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also an Altar to burn incense, of the wood setim,

text.NRSV: It shall be one cubit long, and one cubit wide; it shall be square, and shall be two cubits high; its horns shall be of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: a cubit, the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof four square, shall it be,—and two cubits, the height thereof, of the same, [shall be] the horns thereof,
text.KJV: A cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same.
text.NABRE: with a square surface, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high, with horns that are of one piece with it.
text.DRB: having a cubit of length, and another of breadth, that is, four square, and two cubits in height. The horns shall proceed out of the same.

text.NRSV: You shall overlay it with pure gold, its top, and its sides all around and its horns; and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof,-and thou shalt make to it a border of gold round about.
text.KJV: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.
text.NABRE: Its grate on top, its walls on all four sides, and its horns you shall plate with pure gold. Put a gold molding around it.
text.DRB: And thou shalt plate it with the purest gold, as well the grate thereof, as the walls round about, and the horns. And thou shalt make to it a crown of gold round about,

text.NRSV: And you shall make two golden rings for it; under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them, and they shall hold the poles with which to carry it.
text.Rotherham: And, two rings of gold, shalt thou make to it, under the border thereof upon the two corners thereof, shalt thou make them on the two sides thereof,—so shall they become receptacles for the staves, to lift it therewith.
text.KJV: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make it; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal.
text.NABRE: Underneath the molding you shall put gold rings, two on one side and two on the opposite side, as holders for the poles used in carrying it.
text.DRB: and two golden rings under the crown on either side, that the bars may be put into them, and the Altar may be carried.

text.NRSV: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make the staves of acacia wood,—and overlay them with gold.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
text.NABRE: Make the poles, too, of acacia wood and plate them with gold.
text.DRB: The bars also themselves thou shalt make of the wood setim, and shalt plate them with gold.

text.NRSV: You shall place it in front of the curtain that is above the ark of the covenant, in front of the mercy seat that is over the covenant, where I will meet with you.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place it before the veil, which is by the ark of the testimony,—before the propitiatory which is upon the testimony, where I will meet thee.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.
text.NABRE: This altar you are to place in front of the veil that hangs before the ark of the covenant where I will meet you.
text.DRB: And thou shalt set the Altar against the veil, that hangeth before the ark of the testimony before the propitiatory wherewith the testimony is covered, where I will speak to thee.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall offer fragrant incense on it; every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall offer it,
text.Rotherham: And Aaron shall burn thereon fragrant incense,—morning by morning, when he trimmeth the lamps, shall he burn it;
text.KJV: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.
text.NABRE: On it Aaron shall burn fragrant incense. Morning after morning, when he prepares the lamps,
text.DRB: And Aaron shall burn incense upon it, sweetly fragrant, in the morning. When he shall dress the lamps, he shall burn it:

text.NRSV: and when Aaron sets up the lamps in the evening, he shall offer it, a regular incense offering before the Lord throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: and, when Aaron lighteth the lamps, between the evenings, shall he burn it,—a continual incense before Yahweh, to your generations.
text.KJV: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the Lord throughout your generations.
text.NABRE: and again in the evening twilight, when he lights the lamps, he shall burn incense. Throughout your generations this shall be the regular incense offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: and when he shall place them at even, he shall burn incense everlasting before the Lord through your generations.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer unholy incense on it, or a burnt offering, or a grain offering; and you shall not pour a drink offering on it.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not cause to ascend thereupon strange incense, nor an ascending-sacrifice, nor a meal-offering,—and a drink offering, shall ye not pour out thereupon.
text.KJV: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.
text.NABRE: On this altar you shall not offer up any profane incense, or any burnt offering or grain offering; nor shall you pour out a libation upon it.
text.DRB: You shall not offer upon it incense of another composition, nor oblation, and victim, neither shall you offer libaments.

text.NRSV: Once a year Aaron shall perform the rite of atonement on its horns. Throughout your generations he shall perform the atonement for it once a year with the blood of the atoning sin offering. It is most holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But Aaron shall put a propitiatory-covering upon the horns thereof, once in the year: of the blood of the sin-bearer for propitiatory-coverings, once in the year, shall he put a propitiatory-covering thereupon to your generations, most holy, it is to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Once a year Aaron shall purge its horns. Throughout your generations he is to purge it once a year with the blood of the atoning purification offering. This altar is most sacred to the Lord.
