text.DRB: And Aaron shall pray upon the horns thereof once a year, with the blood of that which was offered for sin, & shall pacify upon it in your generations. It shall be most Holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Census Tax. The Lord also told Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: When you take a census of the Israelites to register them, at registration all of them shall give a ransom for their lives to the Lord, so that no plague may come upon them for being registered.
text.Rotherham: When thou takest the sum of the sons of Israel, by their numberings, then shall they give every man a propitiatory-covering for his soul to Yahweh, when they are numbered,—that there may be among them no plague when they are numbered.
text.KJV: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the Lord, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them.
text.NABRE: When you take a census of the Israelites who are to be enrolled, each one, as he is enrolled, shall give the Lord a ransom for his life, so that no plague may come upon them for being enrolled.
text.DRB: When thou shalt take the sum of the children of Israel according to their number, every one of them shall give a price for their souls to the Lord, & there shall be no scourge among them, when they shall be reckoned.

text.NRSV: This is what each one who is registered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs), half a shekel as an offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: This, shall they give—every one that passeth over to them that have been numbered—a half-shekel by the shekel of the sanctuary,—the shekel is twenty gerahs—the half-shekel, shall be a heave-offering to Yahweh.
text.KJV: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel shall be the offering of the Lord.
text.NABRE: This is what everyone who is enrolled must pay: a half-shekel, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel—twenty gerahs to the shekel—a half-shekel contribution to the Lord.
text.DRB: And this shall every one give that passeth to the naming, half a sicle according to the measure of the temple. A sicle hath twenty oboles. The half part of a sicle shall be offered to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Each one who is registered, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the Lord’s offering.
text.Rotherham: All who pass over to the numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, shall give the heave-offering of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Everyone who is enrolled, of twenty years or more, must give the contribution to the Lord.
text.DRB: He that is accounted in the number, for twenty years and upward, shall give price.

text.NRSV: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less, than the half shekel, when you bring this offering to the Lord to make atonement for your lives.
text.Rotherham: The rich, shall not give more, and the poor, shall not give less, than the half-shekel,—when they give the heave-offering of Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over your souls.
text.KJV: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
text.NABRE: The rich need not give more, nor shall the poor give less, than a half-shekel in this contribution to the Lord to pay the ransom for their lives.
text.DRB: The rich man shall not add to half a sicle, & the poor man shall diminish nothing.

text.NRSV: You shall take the atonement money from the Israelites and shall designate it for the service of the tent of meeting; before the Lord it will be a reminder to the Israelites of the ransom given for your lives.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt take the silver for the propitiatory-coverings from the sons of Israel, and shalt expend it upon the service of the tent of meeting,—thus shall it be for the sons of Israel as a memorial before Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over your souls.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
text.NABRE: When you receive this ransom money from the Israelites, you shall donate it to the service of the tent of meeting, that there it may be a reminder of the Israelites before the Lord of the ransom paid for their lives.
text.DRB: And the money being received, which was contributed of the children of Israel, thou shalt deliver unto the uses of the tabernacle of testimony, that it may be a monument of them before the Lord, & he may be propitious to their souls.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Basin. The Lord told Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: You shall make a bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. You shall put it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water in it;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make a laver of bronze, and its stand of bronze for bathing,—and thou shalt place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and shalt put therein water.
text.KJV: Thou shalt also make a laver of brass, and his foot also of brass, to wash withal: and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.
text.NABRE: For ablutions you shall make a bronze basin with a bronze stand. Place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make also a laver with his foot of brass, to wash in: and thou shalt set it between the tabernacle of the testimony and the Altar. And water being put into it,

text.NRSV: with the water Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron and his sons bathe thereat,—their hands, and their feet.
text.KJV: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:
text.NABRE: Aaron and his sons shall use it in washing their hands and feet.
text.DRB: Aaron and his sons shall wash therein their hands and feet,

text.NRSV: When they go into the tent of meeting, or when they come near the altar to minister, to make an offering by fire to the Lord, they shall wash with water, so that they may not die.
text.Rotherham: When they enter the tent of meeting, they shall bathe with water so shall they not die,—or when they approach unto the altar to minister, to burn as incense an altar-flame to Yahweh:
text.KJV: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the Lord:
text.NABRE: When they are about to enter the tent of meeting, they must wash with water, lest they die. Likewise when they approach the altar to minister, to offer an oblation to the Lord,
text.DRB: when they are going into the tabernacle of testimony, and when they are to come unto the Altar, to offer on it incense to the Lord,

text.NRSV: They shall wash their hands and their feet, so that they may not die: it shall be a perpetual ordinance for them, for him and for his descendants throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: so then they shall bathe their hands and their feet and shall not die,—and it shall be to them an age-abiding statute, to him and to his seed to their generations.
text.KJV: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations.
text.NABRE: they must wash their hands and feet, lest they die. This shall be a perpetual statute for him and his descendants throughout their generations.
text.DRB: lest perhaps they die. It shall be an everlasting law to him, and to his seed by succession.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: Moreover the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Anointing Oil. The Lord told Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses,

text.NRSV: Take the finest spices: of liquid myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet-smelling cinnamon half as much, that is, two hundred fifty, and two hundred fifty of aromatic cane,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, take to thee—principal spices,—self-flowing myrrh, five hundred, and fragrant cinnamon, half as much, two hundred and fifty, and, fragrant cane, two hundred and fifty;
text.KJV: Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much, even two hundred and fifty shekels, and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty shekels,
text.NABRE: Take the finest spices: five hundred shekels of free-flowing myrrh; half that amount, that is, two hundred and fifty shekels, of fragrant cinnamon; two hundred and fifty shekels of fragrant cane;
text.DRB: saying: Take spices, of principal and chosen myrrh five hundred sicles, and of cinnamon half so much, that is, two hundred fifty sicles, of calamus in like manner two hundred fifty,

text.NRSV: and five hundred of cassia—measured by the sanctuary shekel—and a hin of olive oil;
text.Rotherham: and, cassia, five hundred, by the shekel of the sanctuary,—and, oil olive, a hin.
text.KJV: And of cassia five hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin:
text.NABRE: five hundred shekels of cassia—all according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel; together with a hin of olive oil;
text.DRB: and of cassia five hundred sicles after the weight of the Sanctuary, of oil of olives the measure hin:

text.NRSV: and you shall make of these a sacred anointing oil blended as by the perfumer; it shall be a holy anointing oil.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make it an oil for holy anointing, a compounded perfume the work of a perfumer,—an oil for holy anointing, shall it be.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.
text.NABRE: and blend them into sacred anointing oil, perfumed ointment expertly prepared. With this sacred anointing oil
text.DRB: and thou shalt make the holy oil of unction, an ointment compounded by the art of an unguentary,

text.NRSV: With it you shall anoint the tent of meeting and the ark of the covenant,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt anoint therewith the tent of meeting,—and the ark of the testimony;
text.KJV: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,
text.NABRE: you shall anoint the tent of meeting and the ark of the covenant,
text.DRB: and thereof thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of testimony, and the ark of the testament,

text.NRSV: and the table and all its utensils, and the lampstand and its utensils, and the altar of incense,
text.Rotherham: and the table and all its utensils, and the lampstand, and its utensils,—and the incense altar,
text.KJV: And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense,
text.NABRE: the table and all its utensils, the menorah and its utensils, the altar of incense
text.DRB: and the table with the vessel thereof, the candlestick, and the furniture thereof, the Altars of incense,

text.NRSV: and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: and the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and all its utensils,-and the laver, and its stand.
text.KJV: And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot.
text.NABRE: and the altar for burnt offerings with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand.
text.DRB: and of holocaust, and all the furniture that pertaineth to the service of them.

text.NRSV: you shall consecrate them, so that they may be most holy; whatever touches them will become holy.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou hallow them, and they shall be most holy,—whosoever toucheth them, shall be holy.
text.KJV: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.
text.NABRE: When you have consecrated them, they shall be most sacred; whatever touches them shall be sacred.
text.DRB: And thou shalt sanctify all, and they shall be most Holy: he that shall touch them, shall be sanctified.

text.NRSV: You shall anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, in order that they may serve me as priests.
text.Rotherham: Aaron also, and his sons, shalt thou anoint,—so shalt thou hallow them to minister as priests unto me.
text.KJV: And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: Aaron and his sons you shall also anoint and consecrate as my priests.
text.DRB: Thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the function of priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: You shall say to the Israelites, “This shall be my holy anointing oil throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak, saying, An oil for holy anointing, shall this be to me, to your generations.
text.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: As sacred anointing oil this shall belong to me throughout your generations.
text.DRB: To the children of Israel also thou shalt say: This oil of unction shall be holy unto me through your generations.

text.NRSV: It shall not be used in any ordinary anointing of the body, and you shall make no other like it in composition; it is holy, and it shall be holy to you.
text.Rotherham: Upon the flesh of a common man, shall it not be poured, and according to the proportions thereof, shall ye not make any like it,—holy, it is, holy, shall it be to you.
text.KJV: Upon man’s flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you.
text.NABRE: It may not be used in any ordinary anointing of the body, nor may you make any other oil of a like mixture. It is sacred, and shall be treated as sacred by you.
text.DRB: The flesh of man shall not be anointed therewith, and you shall make none other after the composition of it, because it is sanctified, and shall be holy unto you.

text.NRSV: Whoever compounds any like it or whoever puts any of it on an unqualified person shall be cut off from the people.”
text.Rotherham: Whosoever compoundeth any like it, and whosoever bestoweth thereof upon a stranger, shall be cut off from among his people.
text.KJV: Whosoever compoundeth any like it, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: Whoever prepares a perfume like this, or whoever puts any of this on an unauthorized person, shall be cut off from his people.
text.DRB: What man soever shall compound such, and shall give thereof to a stranger, shall be abandoned out of his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Take sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum, sweet spices with pure frankincense (an equal part of each),
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Take to thee aromatic spices stacte and onycha, and galbanum,—aromatic spices, and pure frankincense,—weight for weight, shall it be.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; these sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like weight:
text.NABRE: The Incense. The Lord told Moses: Take these aromatic substances: storax and onycha and galbanum, these and pure frankincense in equal parts;
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Take unto thee spices, stacte, and onycha, galbanum of sweet savour, and the clearest frankincense, all shall be of equal weight:

text.NRSV: and make an incense blended as by the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy;
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt make of it an incense, a perfume the work of a perfumer,—salted, pure, holy.
text.KJV: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy:
text.NABRE: and blend them into incense. This fragrant powder, expertly prepared, is to be salted and so kept pure and sacred.
text.DRB: and thou shalt make incense compounded by the work of an unguentary, exactly tempered, and pure, and most worthy of sanctification.

text.NRSV: and you shall beat some of it into powder, and put part of it before the covenant in the tent of meeting where I shall meet with you; it shall be for you most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt beat some of it to powder, and place thereof before the testimony, in the tent of meeting, where I am to meet with thee,—most holy, shall it be to you.
text.KJV: And thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.
text.NABRE: Grind some of it into fine dust and put this before the covenant in the tent of meeting where I will meet you. This incense shall be treated as most sacred by you.
text.DRB: And when thou hast beaten all into very small powder, thou shalt set of it before the tabernacle of testimony, in the place where I will appear to thee. Most Holy shall the incense be unto you.

text.NRSV: When you make incense according to this composition, you shall not make it for yourselves; it shall be regarded by you as holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the incense which thou shalt make, according to the proportions thereof, ye shall not make for yourselves,—holy, shall it be to thee, to Yahweh.
text.KJV: And as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the Lord.
text.NABRE: You may not make incense of a like mixture for yourselves; you must treat it as sacred to the Lord.
text.DRB: Such confection you shall not make unto your own uses, because it is holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Whoever makes any like it to use as perfume shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall make like it, to smell of it shall be cut off from among his people.
text.KJV: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: Whoever makes an incense like this for his own enjoyment of its fragrance, shall be cut off from his people.
text.DRB: What man soever shall make the like, to enjoy the smell thereof, shall perish out of his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: See, I have called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:
text.Rotherham: See, I have called by name, Bezaleel, son of Uri, son of Hur of the tribe of Judah;
text.KJV: See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:
text.NABRE: See, I have singled out Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,
text.DRB: Behold, I have called by name Beseleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda,

text.NRSV: and I have filled him with divine spirit, with ability, intelligence, and knowledge in every kind of craft,
text.Rotherham: and have filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom and in understanding, and in knowledge and in all manner of workmanship;
text.KJV: And I have filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship,
text.NABRE: and I have filled him with a divine spirit of skill and understanding and knowledge in every craft:
text.DRB: and I have replenished him with the spirit of God, with wisdom, & understanding, and knowledge in all work,

text.NRSV: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
text.Rotherham: to devise skilful designs, to work in gold and in silver, and in bronze;
text.KJV: To devise cunning works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,
text.NABRE: in the production of embroidery, in making things of gold, silver, or bronze,
text.DRB: to devise whatsoever may be artificially made of gold, and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: in cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, in every kind of craft.
text.Rotherham: and in the cutting of stones for setting, and in the carving of wood,—to work in all manner of workmanship,
text.KJV: And in cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of timber, to work in all manner of workmanship.
text.NABRE: in cutting and mounting precious stones, in carving wood, and in every other craft.
text.DRB: of marble, and precious stones, and diversity of wood.

text.NRSV: Moreover, I have appointed with him Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and I have given skill to all the skillful, so that they may make all that I have commanded you:
text.Rotherham: I myself, therefore, lo! I have given with him Oholiab, son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, and in the heart of every one that is wise-hearted, have I put wisdom,—so shall they make all that I have commanded thee—
text.KJV: And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan: and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee;
text.NABRE: As his assistant I myself have appointed Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan. I have also endowed all the experts with the necessary skill to make all the things I have commanded you:
text.DRB: And I have given him for his fellow, Ooliab the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan. And in the heart of every skillful man have I put wisdom: that they may make all things which I have commanded thee,

text.NRSV: the tent of meeting, and the ark of the covenant, and the mercy seat that is on it, and all the furnishings of the tent,
text.Rotherham: the tent of meeting, and the ark of the testimony; and the propitiatory which is thereupon, and all the utensils of the tent;
text.KJV: The tabernacle of the congregation, and the ark of the testimony, and the mercy seat that is thereupon, and all the furniture of the tabernacle,
text.NABRE: the tent of meeting, the ark of the covenant with its cover, all the furnishings of the tent,
text.DRB: the tabernacle of covenant, and the ark of testimony, and the propitiatory, that is over it, and all the vessels of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: the table and its utensils, and the pure lampstand with all its utensils, and the altar of incense,
text.Rotherham: and the table, and its utensils, and the pure lampstand, and all its utensils,—and the incense altar;
text.KJV: And the table and his furniture, and the pure candlestick with all his furniture, and the altar of incense,
text.NABRE: the table with its utensils, the pure gold menorah with all its utensils, the altar of incense,
text.DRB: and the table and the vessels thereof, the candlestick most pure with the vessels thereof, and the Altars of incense,

text.NRSV: and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand,
text.Rotherham: and the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and all its utensils, and the laver, and its stand;
text.KJV: And the altar of burnt offering with all his furniture, and the laver and his foot,
text.NABRE: the altar for burnt offerings with all its utensils, the basin with its stand,
text.DRB: and of holocaust, and all their vessels, the laver with his foot,

text.NRSV: and the finely worked vestments, the holy vestments for the priest Aaron and the vestments of his sons, for their service as priests,
text.Rotherham: and the cloths of variegated stuff, and the holy garments, for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, for ministering as priests;
text.KJV: And the cloths of service, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office,
text.NABRE: the service cloths, the sacred vestments for Aaron the priest, the vestments for his sons in their ministry,
text.DRB: the holy vestments in the ministry for Aaron the Priest, and for his sons, that they may execute their office, about the sacred things:

text.NRSV: and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the holy place. They shall do just as I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the holy place, according to all which I have commanded thee, shall they do.
text.KJV: And the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the holy place: according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do.
text.NABRE: the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense for the sanctuary. According to all I have commanded you, so shall they do.
text.DRB: the oil of unction, and the incense of spices in the Sanctuary, all things which I have commanded thee, shall they make.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Sabbath Laws. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: You yourself are to speak to the Israelites: “You shall keep my sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, given in order that you may know that I, the Lord, sanctify you.
text.Rotherham: Thou thyself, therefore, speak unto the sons of Israel, saying, Surely, my sabbaths, shall ye keep, for, a sign it is betwixt me and you to your generations, that ye may know that I Yahweh do hallow you.
text.KJV: Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that ye may know that I am the Lord that doth sanctify you.
text.NABRE: You must also tell the Israelites: Keep my sabbaths, for that is to be the sign between you and me throughout the generations, to show that it is I, the Lord, who make you holy.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: See that you keep my sabbath: because it is a sign between me and you in your generations: that you may know that I am the Lord, which sanctify you.

text.NRSV: You shall keep the sabbath, because it is holy for you; everyone who profanes it shall be put to death; whoever does any work on it shall be cut off from among the people.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye keep the sabbath, for holy, it is unto you, he that profaneth it shall be surely put to death; verily whosoever doeth therein any work that soul shall be cut off from the midst of its people:
text.KJV: Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it is holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: Therefore, you must keep the sabbath for it is holiness for you. Whoever desecrates it shall be put to death. If anyone does work on that day, that person must be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: Keep you my sabbath: for it is holy unto you: he that shall pollute it, dying shall die: he that shall do work in it, his soul shall perish out of the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a sabbath of solemn rest, holy to the Lord; whoever does any work on the sabbath day shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but on the seventh day, is a holy sabbath-keeping of rest unto Yahweh,—whosoever doeth work on the sabbath day, shall be, surely put to death.
text.KJV: Six days may work be done; but in the seventh is the sabbath of rest, holy to the Lord: whosoever doeth any work in the sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Six days there are for doing work, but the seventh day is the sabbath of complete rest, holy to the Lord. Anyone who does work on the sabbath day shall be put to death.
text.DRB: Six days shall you do work: in the seventh day is the sabbath, the holy rest to the Lord. Every one that shall do any work in this day, shall die.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Israelites shall keep the sabbath, observing the sabbath throughout their generations, as a perpetual covenant.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the sons of Israel keep the sabbath,—by making it a Day of Rest to your generations, as an age-abiding covenant:
text.KJV: Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations, for a perpetual covenant.
text.NABRE: So shall the Israelites observe the sabbath, keeping it throughout their generations as an everlasting covenant.
text.DRB: Let the children of Israel keep the Sabbath, and celebrate it in their generations. It is an everlasting covenant

text.NRSV: It is a sign forever between me and the people of Israel that in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.”
text.Rotherham: between me and the sons of Israel, a sign it is unto times age-abiding,—for in six days, did Yahweh make the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day, he rested, and was refreshed.
text.KJV: It is a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever: for in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.
text.NABRE: Between me and the Israelites it is to be an everlasting sign; for in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, but on the seventh day he rested at his ease.
text.DRB: between me and the children of Israel, and a sign perpetual. For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and in the seventh he ceased from work.

text.NRSV: When God finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the covenant, tablets of stone, written with the finger of God.
text.Rotherham: Then gave he unto Moses, as he finished speaking with him in Mount Sinai, the two tables of the testimony,—tables of stone, written with the finger of God,
text.KJV: And he gave unto Moses, when he had made an end of communing with him upon mount Sinai, two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God.
text.NABRE: When the Lord had finished speaking to Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the covenant, the stone tablets inscribed by God’s own finger.
text.DRB: And our Lord, when he had ended such speeches in mount Sinai, gave unto Moyses two stone tables of testimony, written with the finger of God.

text.NRSV: When the people saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mountain, the people gathered around Aaron, and said to him, “Come, make gods for us, who shall go before us; as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.”
text.Rotherham: And, when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him—Up, make for us gods, who shall go before us, for, as for this Moses—the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we know not what hath befallen him.
text.KJV: And when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him, Up, make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
text.NABRE: When the people saw that Moses was delayed in coming down from the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and said to him, “Come, make us a god who will go before us; as for that man Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.”
text.DRB: And the people seeing that Moyses made tarriance ere he came down from the mount, being assembled against Aaron, they said: Arise, make us gods, that may go before us: for what hath chanced to this Moyses the man that brought us out of the Land of Ægypt, we know not.

text.NRSV: Aaron said to them, “Take off the gold rings that are on the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.”
text.Rotherham: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the rings of gold, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons and of your daughters,—and bring them unto me.
text.KJV: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them unto me.
text.NABRE: Aaron replied, “Take off the golden earrings that your wives, your sons, and your daughters are wearing, and bring them to me.”
text.DRB: And Aaron said to them: Take the golden earlets from the ears of your wives, and sons, and daughters, & bring them to me.

text.NRSV: So all the people took off the gold rings from their ears, and brought them to Aaron.
text.Rotherham: And all the people of themselves brake off the rings of gold which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.
text.KJV: And all the people brake off the golden earrings which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.
text.NABRE: So all the people took off their earrings and brought them to Aaron.
text.DRB: And the people did that he had commanded, bringing the earlets to Aaron.

text.NRSV: He took the gold from them, formed it in a mold, and cast an image of a calf; and they said, “These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!”
text.Rotherham: And he received [the gold] at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, and made it a molten calf, and they said—These, are thy gods, O Israel, who brought thee up, out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And he received them at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, after he had made it a molten calf: and they said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: He received their offering, and fashioning it with a tool, made a molten calf. Then they cried out, “These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.”
text.DRB: Which when he had received, he formed them by founder's work, and made of them a molten calf. And they said: These are thy gods Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation and said, “Tomorrow shall be a festival to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it. And Aaron made proclamation, and said, A festival to Yahweh, to-morrow!
text.KJV: And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation, and said, To morrow is a feast to the Lord.
text.NABRE: On seeing this, Aaron built an altar in front of the calf and proclaimed, “Tomorrow is a feast of the Lord.”
text.DRB: Which when Aaron had seen, he builded an altar before it, and by a crier's voice proclaimed saying: Tomorrow is the solemnity of the Lord.

text.NRSV: They rose early the next day, and offered burnt offerings and brought sacrifices of well-being; and the people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.
text.Rotherham: So they rose up early, on the morrow, and offered ascending-sacrifices, and brought near peace-offerings, and the people sat down, to eat and to drink, and then rose up—to make sport!
text.KJV: And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.
text.NABRE: Early the next day the people sacrificed burnt offerings and brought communion sacrifices. Then they sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.
text.DRB: And rising in the morning, they offered holocausts, and pacific hosts, and the people sat down to eat, and to drink, and they rose up to play.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go down at once! Your people, whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, have acted perversely;
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—Go get thee down, for thy people whom thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt have corrupted themselves.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go, get thee down; for thy people, which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves:
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: Go down at once because your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt, have acted corruptly.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying: Go, get thee down: thy people, which thou hast brought out of the Land of Ægypt, hath sinned.

text.NRSV: they have been quick to turn aside from the way that I commanded them; they have cast for themselves an image of a calf, and have worshiped it and sacrificed to it, and said, ‘These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!’”
text.Rotherham: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them, they have made for themselves—a molten calf, and have bowed themselves down thereto and have sacrificed thereto, and have said. These, are thy gods, O Israel, who have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them: they have made them a molten calf, and have worshipped it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: They have quickly turned aside from the way I commanded them, making for themselves a molten calf and bowing down to it, sacrificing to it and crying out, “These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the land of Egypt!”
text.DRB: They have quickly revolted from the way that thou didst shew them: and they have made to themselves a molten calf, and have adored, and immolating hosts unto it, have said: These are thy gods Israel, that have brought thee out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “I have seen this people, how stiff-necked they are.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh said unto Moses,—I have looked on this people, and lo! a stiff-necked people, it is.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, I have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people:
text.NABRE: I have seen this people, how stiff-necked they are, continued the Lord to Moses.
text.DRB: And again our Lord said to Moyses: I see that this people is stiff-necked:

text.NRSV: Now let me alone, so that my wrath may burn hot against them and I may consume them; and of you I will make a great nation.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let me alone, that mine anger may kindle upon them and that I may consume them, and may make of thee a great nation.
text.KJV: Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may wax hot against them, and that I may consume them: and I will make of thee a great nation.
text.NABRE: Let me alone, then, that my anger may burn against them to consume them. Then I will make of you a great nation.
text.DRB: suffer me, that my fury may be angry against them, & that I may destroy them, and I will make thee into a great nation.

text.NRSV: But Moses implored the Lord his God, and said, “O Lord, why does your wrath burn hot against your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?
text.Rotherham: And Moses besought the face of Yahweh his God,—and said—Wherefore, O Yahweh, should thine anger kindle upon thy people, whom thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great might and with a firm hand?
text.KJV: And Moses besought the Lord his God, and said, Lord, why doth thy wrath wax hot against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power, and with a mighty hand?
text.NABRE: But Moses implored the Lord, his God, saying, “Why, O Lord, should your anger burn against your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a strong hand?
text.DRB: But Moyses besought the Lord his God, saying: Why Lord, is thy fury angry against thy people, whom thou hast brought forth of the Land of Ægypt, in great power, and in a strong hand?

text.NRSV: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘It was with evil intent that he brought them out to kill them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth’? Turn from your fierce wrath; change your mind and do not bring disaster on your people.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak saying—For mischief, hath he taken them forth to slay them among the mountains, and to make an end of them from off the face of the ground? Turn thou from the kindling of thine anger, and be grieved over the calamity to thy people,
text.KJV: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak, and say, For mischief did he bring them out, to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from thy fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against thy people.
text.NABRE: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘With evil intent he brought them out, that he might kill them in the mountains and wipe them off the face of the earth’? Turn from your burning wrath; change your mind about punishing your people.
text.DRB: Let not the Ægyptians say I beseech thee: He hath craftily brought them forth, that he might kill them in the mountains, and destroy them from the earth: let thine anger cease, and be pacified upon the wickedness of thy people.

text.NRSV: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, how you swore to them by your own self, saying to them, ‘I will multiply your descendants like the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have promised I will give to your descendants, and they shall inherit it forever.’”
text.Rotherham: Have remembrance of Abraham, of Isaac and of Israel thy servants to whom thou didst swear by thyself, and didst speak unto them saying, I will multiply your seed, as the stars of the heavens,—and, all this land of which I have spoken, will I give to your seed, and they shall inherit it to times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.
text.NABRE: Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, and how you swore to them by your own self, saying, ‘I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky; and all this land that I promised, I will give your descendants as their perpetual heritage.’”
text.DRB: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, saying: I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven: and this whole land, whereof I have spoken, I will give to your seed, & you shall possess it always.

text.NRSV: And the Lord changed his mind about the disaster that he planned to bring on his people.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh was grieved,—over the calamity—which he had spoken of inflicting on his people.
text.KJV: And the Lord repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people.
text.NABRE: So the Lord changed his mind about the punishment he had threatened to inflict on his people.
text.DRB: And our Lord was pacified from doing the evil which he had spoken against his people.

text.NRSV: Then Moses turned and went down from the mountain, carrying the two tablets of the covenant in his hands, tablets that were written on both sides, written on the front and on the back.
text.Rotherham: And Moses turned and went down out of the mount, with the two tables of testimony in his hand,—tables written upon on both sides of them, on this and on that, were they written.
text.KJV: And Moses turned, and went down from the mount, and the two tables of the testimony were in his hand: the tables were written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other were they written.
text.NABRE: Moses then turned and came down the mountain with the two tablets of the covenant in his hands, tablets that were written on both sides, front and back.
text.DRB: And Moyses returned from the mount, carrying the two tables of testimony in his hand, written on both sides,

text.NRSV: The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved upon the tablets.
text.Rotherham: Now as for, the tables, the work of God, they were,—and, as for the writing, the writing of God, it was, graven upon the tables.
text.KJV: And the tables were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, graven upon the tables.
text.NABRE: The tablets were made by God; the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.
text.DRB: and made by the work of God: the writing also of God was graven in the tables.

text.NRSV: When Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, “There is a noise of war in the camp.”
text.Rotherham: And Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted,—so he said unto Moses—The noise of war, in the camp!
text.KJV: And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses, There is a noise of war in the camp.
text.NABRE: Now, when Joshua heard the noise of the people shouting, he said to Moses, “That sounds like a battle in the camp.”
text.DRB: And Josue hearing the tumult of the people crying out, said to Moyses: The noise of battail is heard in the camp.

text.NRSV: But he said, “It is not the sound made by victors, or the sound made by losers; it is the sound of revelers that I hear.”
text.Rotherham: But he said—Not the noise of the cry of heroism, nor yet the noise of the cry of defeat,—the noise of alternate song, do I hear.
text.KJV: And he said, It is not the voice of them that shout for mastery, neither is it the voice of them that cry for being overcome: but the noise of them that sing do I hear.
text.NABRE: But Moses answered, “It is not the noise of victory, it is not the noise of defeat; the sound I hear is singing.”
text.DRB: Who answered: It is not the cry of men encouraging of fight, nor the shout of men compelling to fly: but I do hear the voice of singers.

text.NRSV: As soon as he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moses’ anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets from his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when he drew near unto the camp, and saw the calf, and the dancings, that the anger of Moses kindled, and he cast out of his hands the tables, and brake them in pieces at the foot of the mountain.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as soon as he came nigh unto the camp, that he saw the calf, and the dancing: and Moses’ anger waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and brake them beneath the mount.
text.NABRE: As he drew near the camp, he saw the calf and the dancing. Then Moses’ anger burned, and he threw the tablets down and broke them on the base of the mountain.
text.DRB: And when he approached to the camp, he saw the calf, and the dances: and being very wrath, he threw the tables out of his hand, and brake them at the foot of the mount.

text.NRSV: He took the calf that they had made, burned it with fire, ground it to powder, scattered it on the water, and made the Israelites drink it.
text.Rotherham: Then took he the calf which they had made and burned it in the fire, and ground it till it became powder, and strewed it on the face of the waters, and made the sons of Israel drink.
text.KJV: And he took the calf which they had made, and burnt it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and strawed it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it.
text.NABRE: Taking the calf they had made, he burned it in the fire and then ground it down to powder, which he scattered on the water and made the Israelites drink.
text.DRB: And catching the calf which they had made, he burnt it, & beat it into powder, which he strawed into water, and gave thereof drink to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Aaron, “What did this people do to you that you have brought so great a sin upon them?”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron, What had this people done to thee,—that thou shouldst have brought upon them a great sin?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?
text.NABRE: Moses asked Aaron, “What did this people do to you that you should lead them into a grave sin?”
text.DRB: And he said to Aaron: What hath this people done to thee, that thou shouldest bring upon them an heinous sin?

text.NRSV: And Aaron said, “Do not let the anger of my lord burn hot; you know the people, that they are bent on evil.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord kindle,—thou thyself, knowest the people, that ready for mischief, they are.
text.KJV: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord wax hot: thou knowest the people, that they are set on mischief.
text.NABRE: Aaron replied, “Do not let my lord be angry. You know how the people are prone to evil.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Let not my Lord be offended for thou knowest this people, that it is prone to evil:

text.NRSV: They said to me, ‘Make us gods, who shall go before us; as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.’
text.Rotherham: So they said to me—Make for us gods, who shall go before us,—for, as for this, Moses—the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we know not what hath befallen him.
text.KJV: For they said unto me, Make us gods, which shall go before us: for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
text.NABRE: They said to me, ‘Make us a god to go before us; as for this man Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’
text.DRB: they said to me: Make us gods, that may go before us: for unto this same Moyses, that brought us forth out of the Land of Ægypt, we know not what is chanced.

text.NRSV: So I said to them, ‘Whoever has gold, take it off!’; so they gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf!”
text.Rotherham: And I said to them—Whosoever hath gold let them break it off, So they gave it to me,—and I cast it into the fire, and there came out—this calf.
text.KJV: And I said unto them, Whosoever hath any gold, let them break it off. So they gave it me: then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.
text.NABRE: So I told them, ‘Whoever is wearing gold, take it off.’ They gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and this calf came out.”
text.DRB: To whom I said: Which of you hath gold? They took, & brought to me: & I cast it into the fire, and this calf came forth.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw that the people were running wild (for Aaron had let them run wild, to the derision of their enemies),
text.Rotherham: And Moses saw the people, that unbridled, they were,—for Aaron had given them the rein, for a whispering, among their enemies.
text.KJV: And when Moses saw that the people were naked; (for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame among their enemies:)
text.NABRE: Moses saw that the people were running wild because Aaron had lost control—to the secret delight of their foes.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore seeing the people that they were made naked (for Aaron had spoiled them for the ignominy of filth, & had set them naked among their enemies).

text.NRSV: then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, “Who is on the Lord’s side? Come to me!” And all the sons of Levi gathered around him.
text.Rotherham: So Moses took his stand in the gate of the camp, and said—Who is for Yahweh? …To me! Then gathered unto him all the sons of Levi,
text.KJV: Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, Who is on the Lord’s side? let him come unto me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him.
text.NABRE: Moses stood at the gate of the camp and shouted, “Whoever is for the Lord, come to me!” All the Levites then rallied to him,
text.DRB: & standing in the gate of the camp, he said: If any man be our Lord's, let him join to me, And there gathered unto him all the sons of Levi:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Put your sword on your side, each of you! Go back and forth from gate to gate throughout the camp, and each of you kill your brother, your friend, and your neighbor.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said to them—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, Put ye every man his sword upon his thigh,—pass through and return from gate to gate in the camp, and slay ye every man his brother and every man his friend and every man his neighbour.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Put every man his sword by his side, and go in and out from gate to gate throughout the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbour.
text.NABRE: and he told them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Each of you put your sword on your hip! Go back and forth through the camp, from gate to gate, and kill your brothers, your friends, your neighbors!”
text.DRB: to whom he said: This saith the Lord God of Israel: Put every man his sword upon his thigh: go, and return from gate to gate through the midst of the camp, and every man kill his brother, and friend, and neighbour.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi did as Moses commanded, and about three thousand of the people fell on that day.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Levi did, according to the word of Moses,—and there fell from among the people on that day, about three thousand men.
text.KJV: And the children of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.
text.NABRE: The Levites did as Moses had commanded, and that day about three thousand of the people fell.
text.DRB: And the sons of Levi did according to the saying of Moyses, and there were slain in that day about three and twenty thousand men.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Today you have ordained yourselves for the service of the Lord, each one at the cost of a son or a brother, and so have brought a blessing on yourselves this day.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—Install yourselves to-day, unto Yahweh, yea every man for his son and for his brother,—so will he bestow upon you to-day, a blessing.
text.KJV: For Moses had said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the Lord, even every man upon his son, and upon his brother; that he may bestow upon you a blessing this day.
text.NABRE: Then Moses said, “Today you are installed as priests for the Lord, for you went against your own sons and brothers, to bring a blessing upon yourselves this day.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said: You have consecrated your hands this day to our Lord, every man in his son & in his brother, that blessing may be given to you.

text.NRSV: On the next day Moses said to the people, “You have sinned a great sin. But now I will go up to the Lord; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye, have sinned a great sin,—Now, therefore, I must go up unto Yahweh, Peradventure, I may make a propitiatory-covering for your sin.
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye have sinned a great sin: and now I will go up unto the Lord; peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin.
text.NABRE: The Atonement. On the next day Moses said to the people, “You have committed a grave sin. Now I will go up to the Lord; perhaps I may be able to make atonement for your sin.”
text.DRB: And when the next day was come, Moyses spake to the people: You have sinned a very great sin: I will go up to our Lord, if by any means I may be able to entreat him for your sinful fact.

text.NRSV: So Moses returned to the Lord and said, “Alas, this people has sinned a great sin; they have made for themselves gods of gold.
text.Rotherham: So Moses returned unto Yahweh, and said,—Oh now, this people hath sinned a great sin, and they have made for themselves—gods of gold.
text.KJV: And Moses returned unto the Lord, and said, Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made them gods of gold.
text.NABRE: So Moses returned to the Lord and said, “Ah, this people has committed a grave sin in making a god of gold for themselves!
text.DRB: And returning to our Lord, he said: I beseech thee, this people hath sinned a heinous sin, and they have made to themselves gods of gold; either forgive them this trespass,

text.NRSV: But now, if you will only forgive their sin—but if not, blot me out of the book that you have written.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if thou wilt forgive their sin, …but if not, blot me I pray thee out of thy book, which thou hast written,
text.KJV: Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin—; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.
text.NABRE: Now if you would only forgive their sin! But if you will not, then blot me out of the book that you have written.”
text.DRB: or if thou do not, strike me out of the book that thou hast written.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against me I will blot out of my book.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses,—Him who hath sinned against me, must I blot out of my book.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Whosoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: Only the one who has sinned against me will I blot out of my book.
text.DRB: To whom our Lord answered: He that hath sinned to me, him will I strike out of my book:

text.NRSV: But now go, lead the people to the place about which I have spoken to you; see, my angel shall go in front of you. Nevertheless, when the day comes for punishment, I will punish them for their sin.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, go lead the people to the place of which I spake to thee, lo! my messenger, shall go before thee,—But, in the day when I do visit, then will I visit upon them their sin.
text.KJV: Therefore now go, lead the people unto the place of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.
text.NABRE: Now, go and lead the people where I have told you. See, my angel will go before you. When it is time for me to punish, I will punish them for their sin.
text.DRB: but go thou, and lead this people wither I have told thee: mine Angel shall go before thee. And I in the day of revenge will visit this sin also of theirs.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord sent a plague on the people, because they made the calf—the one that Aaron made.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh plagued the people,—for what they had done with the calf, which Aaron made.
text.KJV: And the Lord plagued the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made.
text.NABRE: Thus the Lord struck the people for making the calf, the one that Aaron made.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore smote the people for the fault concerning the calf, which Aaron had made.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Go, leave this place, you and the people whom you have brought up out of the land of Egypt, and go to the land of which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying, ‘To your descendants I will give it.’
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses—Get thee away, go up from hence, thou, and the people whom thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt,—into the land of which I sware to Abraham to Isaac and to Jacob, saying, To thy seed, will I give it;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Depart, and go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it:
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses: Go! You and the people whom you have brought up from the land of Egypt are to go up from here to the land about which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: I will give it to your descendants.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying: Go, get thee up from this place, thou and thy people which thou hast brought out of the Land of Ægypt, into the land whereof I sware to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: To thy seed I will give it:

text.NRSV: I will send an angel before you, and I will drive out the Canaanites, the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.Rotherham: And I will send before thee a messenger,-and will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite;
text.KJV: And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:
text.NABRE: Driving out the Canaanites, Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites, I will send an angel before you
text.DRB: and I will send an Angel thy precursor, that I may cast out the Chananeite, and Amorrheite, and Hetheite, and Pherezeite, and Heveite, and Jebuseite,

text.NRSV: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey; but I will not go up among you, or I would consume you on the way, for you are a stiff-necked people.”
text.Rotherham: into a land flowing with milk and honey,—for I will not go up in thy midst in that a stiff-necked people, thou art, lest I consume thee in the way.
text.KJV: Unto a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up in the midst of thee; for thou art a stiffnecked people: lest I consume thee in the way.
text.NABRE: to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I myself will not go up in your company, because you are a stiff-necked people; otherwise I might consume you on the way.
text.DRB: and thou mayest enter into the land that floweth with milk and honey: for I will not go up with thee, because thou art a stiff-necked people: lest perhaps I destroy thee in the way.

text.NRSV: When the people heard these harsh words, they mourned, and no one put on ornaments.
text.Rotherham: And when the people heard these evil tidings, they afflicted themselves,—and they put not, any man, his ornaments upon him.
text.KJV: And when the people heard these evil tidings, they mourned: and no man did put on him his ornaments.
text.NABRE: When the people heard this painful news, they mourned, and no one wore any ornaments.
text.DRB: And the people hearing this very ill saying, mourned: and no man put on his ornaments after the custom.

text.NRSV: For the Lord had said to Moses, “Say to the Israelites, ‘You are a stiff-necked people; if for a single moment I should go up among you, I would consume you. So now take off your ornaments, and I will decide what to do to you.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Say unto the sons of Israel, Ye, are a stiff-necked people, in one moment—if I should go up in thy midst, should I make an end of thee,—Now, therefore lay down thine ornaments from off thee, that I may see what I must do with thee.
text.KJV: For the Lord had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye are a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses: Speak to the Israelites: You are a stiff-necked people. Were I to go up in your company even for a moment, I would destroy you. Now off with your ornaments! Let me think what to do with you.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Speak to the children of Israel: Thou art a stiff-necked people, once I shall go up in the midst of thee, and shall destroy thee. Now presently lay away thy ornaments, that I may know what to do unto thee.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Israelites stripped themselves of their ornaments, from Mount Horeb onward.
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments, [and moved on] from Mount Horeb.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments by the mount Horeb.
text.NABRE: So, from Mount Horeb onward, the Israelites stripped off their ornaments.
text.DRB: Therefore the children of Israel laid away their ornaments from mount Horeb.

text.NRSV: Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far off from the camp; he called it the tent of meeting. And everyone who sought the Lord would go out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: And, Moses, proceeded to take a tent, and pitch it by itself outside the camp, afar off from the camp, and he called it, The Tent of Meeting,—and so it came to pass that whosoever was seeking Yahweh went out unto the tent of meeting, which was on the outside of the camp.
text.KJV: And Moses took the tabernacle, and pitched it without the camp, afar off from the camp, and called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. And it came to pass, that every one which sought the Lord went out unto the tabernacle of the congregation, which was without the camp.
text.NABRE: Moses’ Intimacy with God. Moses used to pitch a tent outside the camp at some distance. It was called the tent of meeting. Anyone who wished to consult the Lord would go to the tent of meeting outside the camp.
text.DRB: Moyses also taking the tabernacle, pitched it without the camp a far off, and called the name thereof, The Tabernacle of covenant. And all the people, that had any question, went forth to the Tabernacle of covenant, without the camp.

text.NRSV: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise and stand, each of them, at the entrance of their tents and watch Moses until he had gone into the tent.
text.Rotherham: And it came about, that, when Moses went out unto the tent, all the people used to rise up, and station themselves every man at the opening of his tent,—and they watched Moses, until he entered the tent.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, that all the people rose up, and stood every man at his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, the people would all rise and stand at the entrance of their own tents, watching Moses until he entered the tent.
text.DRB: And when Moyses went forth to the tabernacle, all the people rose up, and every one stood in the door of his pavilion, and they beheld the back of Moyses, till he entered into the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: When Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the Lord would speak with Moses.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that when Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud came down, and stood at the opening of the tent,—and spake with Moses.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as Moses entered into the tabernacle, the cloudy pillar descended, and stood at the door of the tabernacle, and the Lord talked with Moses.
text.NABRE: As Moses entered the tent, the column of cloud would come down and stand at its entrance while the Lord spoke with Moses.
text.DRB: And when he was entered into the Tabernacle of covenant, the pillar of the cloud came down, and stood at the door, & he spake with Moyses,

text.NRSV: When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise and bow down, all of them, at the entrance of their tent.
text.Rotherham: And when all the people beheld the pillar of cloud, standing at the opening of the tent, then all the people rose up and bowed themselves down, every man at the entrance of his tent.
text.KJV: And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand at the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man in his tent door.
text.NABRE: On seeing the column of cloud stand at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise and bow down at the entrance of their own tents.
text.DRB: all they beholding that the pillar of the cloud stood at the door of the Tabernacle. And they stood, & adored at the doors of their tabernacles.

text.NRSV: Thus the Lord used to speak to Moses face to face, as one speaks to a friend. Then he would return to the camp; but his young assistant, Joshua son of Nun, would not leave the tent.
text.Rotherham: Thus Yahweh used to speak unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend,—and when he returned unto the camp, his attendant Joshua son of Nun, a young man, moved not from the midst of the tent.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And he turned again into the camp: but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not out of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: The Lord used to speak to Moses face to face, as a person speaks to a friend. Moses would then return to the camp, but his young assistant, Joshua, son of Nun, never left the tent.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake unto Moyses face to face, as a man is wont to speak to his friend. And when he returned into the camp, his minister Josue the son of Nun, a young man, departed not from the Tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Moses said to the Lord, “See, you have said to me, ‘Bring up this people’; but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.’
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses, unto Yahweh, See! thou art saying unto me—Take up this people, and yet thou hast no let me know whom thou wilt send with me,—though thou thyself, didst say—I acknowledge thee by name, Yea, moreover, thou hast found favour in mine eyes.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the Lord, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.
text.NABRE: Moses said to the Lord, “See, you are telling me: Lead this people. But you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said: You are my intimate friend; You have found favor with me.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to our Lord: Thou commandest me to lead forth this people, & doest not shew me whom thou wilt send with me, especially whereas thou hast said: I know thee by name, and thou hast found grace in my sight.

text.NRSV: Now if I have found favor in your sight, show me your ways, so that I may know you and find favor in your sight. Consider too that this nation is your people.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, let me know, I beseech thee, thy way, that I may acknowledge thee, to the intent I may find favour in thine eyes,—see thou, therefore, that this nation is thine own people.
text.KJV: Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation is thy people.
text.NABRE: Now, if I have found favor with you, please let me know your ways so that, in knowing you, I may continue to find favor with you. See, this nation is indeed your own people.
text.DRB: If therefore I have found grace in thy sight, shew me thy face, that I may know thee, and may find grace before thine eyes, look upon thy people this nation.

text.NRSV: He said, “My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”
text.Rotherham: So then he said,—Mine own presence, shall go on thus will I give thee rest.
text.KJV: And he said, My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered: I myself will go along, to give you rest.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: My face shall go before thee, & I will give thee rest.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “If your presence will not go, do not carry us up from here.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, If thy presence is not going on, do not take us up from hence.
text.KJV: And he said unto him, If thy presence go not with me, carry us not up hence.
text.NABRE: Moses replied, “If you are not going yourself, do not make us go up from here.
text.DRB: And Moyses said: If thyself doest not go before, bring us not out of this place.

text.NRSV: For how shall it be known that I have found favor in your sight, I and your people, unless you go with us? In this way, we shall be distinct, I and your people, from every people on the face of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Whereby, indeed, shall it be known then that I have found favour in thine eyes, I, and thy people? Shall it not be, by thy going on with us? So shall we be more distinguished, I, and thy people, than any people that are on the face of the ground.
text.KJV: For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? is it not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that are upon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: For how can it be known that I and your people have found favor with you, except by your going with us? Then we, your people and I, will be singled out from every other people on the surface of the earth.”
text.DRB: For whereby shall we be able to know I and thy people, that we have found grace in thy sight, unless thou walk with us that we may be glorified of all peoples, that dwell upon the earth?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “I will do the very thing that you have asked; for you have found favor in my sight, and I know you by name.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses: Even this thing, which thou hast spoken, will I do—For thou hast found favour in mine eyes, And I have acknowledged thee by name.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast spoken: for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: This request, too, which you have made, I will carry out, because you have found favor with me and you are my intimate friend.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: This word also, which thou hast spoken, will I do: for thou hast found grace before me, and thyself I have known by name.

text.NRSV: Moses said, “Show me your glory, I pray.”
text.Rotherham: And he said,—Show me, I pray thee, thy glory.
text.KJV: And he said, I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.
text.NABRE: Then Moses said, “Please let me see your glory!”
text.DRB: Who said: Shew me thy glory.

text.NRSV: And he said, “I will make all my goodness pass before you, and will proclaim before you the name, ‘The Lord’; and I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will show mercy on whom I will show mercy.
text.Rotherham: And he said,—I, will cause all my goodness to pass before thee. And will proclaim myself by the name Yahweh, before thee, And will show favour, unto whom I may show favour, And will have compassion on whom I may have compassion.
text.KJV: And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thee, and I will proclaim the name of the Lord before thee; and will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered: I will make all my goodness pass before you, and I will proclaim my name, “Lord,” before you; I who show favor to whom I will, I who grant mercy to whom I will.
text.DRB: He answered: I will shew thee all good, and will call in the name of the Lord before thee: and I will have mercy on whom I will, and I will be merciful to whom it shall please me.

text.NRSV: But,” he said, “you cannot see my face; for no one shall see me and live.”
text.Rotherham: But he said—Thou canst not see my face, For no son of earth can see me, and live
text.KJV: And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live.
text.NABRE: But you cannot see my face, for no one can see me and live.
text.DRB: And again he said: Thou canst not see my face: for man shall not see me, & live.

text.NRSV: And the Lord continued, “See, there is a place by me where you shall stand on the rock;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, said, Lo! a place near me,—So then thou shalt station thyself upon the rock:
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Behold, there is a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock:
text.NABRE: Here, continued the Lord, is a place near me where you shall station yourself on the rock.
text.DRB: And again: Behold, quoth he, there is a place with me, and thou shalt stand upon the rock.

text.NRSV: and while my glory passes by I will put you in a cleft of the rock, and I will cover you with my hand until I have passed by;
text.Rotherham: And it shall be while my glory passeth by, I will put thee in the cleft of the rock,—and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, while my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:
text.NABRE: When my glory passes I will set you in the cleft of the rock and will cover you with my hand until I have passed by.
text.DRB: And when my glory shall pass, I will set thee in a hole of the rock, & protect thee with my right hand, until I pass:

text.NRSV: then I will take away my hand, and you shall see my back; but my face shall not be seen.”
text.Rotherham: Then will I take away my hand, and thou shalt see behind me, But, my face, must not be seen.
text.KJV: And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen.
text.NABRE: Then I will remove my hand, so that you may see my back; but my face may not be seen.
text.DRB: and I will take away my hand, and thou shalt see my back-parts: but my face thou canst not see.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Cut two tablets of stone like the former ones, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the former tablets, which you broke.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses—Hew thee two tables of stone, like the first,—and I will write upon the tables the words which were upon the first tables which thou didst break in pieces;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first: and I will write upon these tables the words that were in the first tables, which thou brakest.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: “Cut two stone tablets like the former, that I may write on them the words which were on the former tablets that you broke.
text.DRB: And after this he said: Cut thee two tables of stone like unto the former, and I will write upon them the words, which the tables had which thou hast broken.

text.NRSV: Be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai and present yourself there to me, on the top of the mountain.
text.Rotherham: and be thou ready by the morning,—and thou shalt come up. in the morning, into Mount Sinai; then shalt thou station thyself for me there upon the top of the mount;
text.KJV: And be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning unto mount Sinai, and present thyself there to me in the top of the mount.
text.NABRE: Get ready for tomorrow morning, when you are to go up Mount Sinai and there present yourself to me on the top of the mountain.
text.DRB: Be ready in the morning, that thou mayest forthwith go up into the mount Sinai, and thou shalt stand with me upon the top of the mount.
