text.NRSV: No one shall come up with you, and do not let anyone be seen throughout all the mountain; and do not let flocks or herds graze in front of that mountain.”
text.Rotherham: and, no man, may come up with thee, and let no man so much as be seen in all the mount,—and let not even the flocks or the herds, feed in front of that mountain.
text.KJV: And no man shall come up with thee, neither let any man be seen throughout all the mount; neither let the flocks nor herds feed before that mount.
text.NABRE: No one shall come up with you, and let no one even be seen on any part of the mountain; even the sheep and the cattle are not to graze in front of this mountain.”
text.DRB: Let no man go up with thee, neither let any man be seen throughout the whole mount: the oxen also and the sheep let them not feed over-against.

text.NRSV: So Moses cut two tablets of stone like the former ones; and he rose early in the morning and went up on Mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tablets of stone.
text.Rotherham: So he hewed two tables of stone like the first and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up into Mount Sinai, as Yahweh had commanded him,—and took in his hand two tables of stone.
text.KJV: And he hewed two tables of stone like unto the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up unto mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tables of stone.
text.NABRE: Moses then cut two stone tablets like the former, and early the next morning he went up Mount Sinai as the Lord had commanded him, taking in his hand the two stone tablets.
text.DRB: He cut out therefore two tables of stone, such as had been before: and rising very early he went up into mount Sinai, as our Lord had commanded him, carrying with him the tables.

text.NRSV: The Lord descended in the cloud and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name, “The Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh descended in the cloud, and took his station with him there,—and proclaimed himself by the name Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the Lord descended in the cloud, and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Lord came down in a cloud and stood with him there and proclaimed the name, “Lord.”
text.DRB: And when our Lord was descended in a cloud, Moyses stood with him, calling upon the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord passed before him, and proclaimed, “The Lord, the Lord, a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh passed before him, and proclaimed,—Yahweh, Yahweh, A God of compassion and favour,—Slow to anger and abundant in lovingkindness and faithfulness:
text.KJV: And the Lord passed by before him, and proclaimed, The Lord, The Lord God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth,
text.NABRE: So the Lord passed before him and proclaimed: The Lord, the Lord, a God gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in love and fidelity,
text.DRB: Who passing before him said: Dominatour Lord God, merciful and clement, patient and of much compassion, and true,

text.NRSV: keeping steadfast love for the thousandth generation, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, yet by no means clearing the guilty, but visiting the iniquity of the parents upon the children and the children’s children, to the third and the fourth generation.”
text.Rotherham: Keeping lovingkindness to a thousand generations, Forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, Though he leave not, utterly unpunished, Visiting the iniquity of fathers, Upon sons, And upon sons’ sons, Unto a third and unto a fourth generation.
text.KJV: Keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and that will by no means clear the guilty; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, and upon the children’s children, unto the third and to the fourth generation.
text.NABRE: continuing his love for a thousand generations, and forgiving wickedness, rebellion, and sin; yet not declaring the guilty guiltless, but bringing punishment for their parents’ wickedness on children and children’s children to the third and fourth generation!
text.DRB: which keepest mercy unto thousands: which takest away iniquity, and wicked facts, and sins, and no man of himself is innocent before thee. Which doest render the iniquity of the fathers to the children, and to the nephews unto the third and fourth generation.

text.NRSV: And Moses quickly bowed his head toward the earth, and worshiped.
text.Rotherham: Then Moses made haste,—and bent his head towards the earth and bowed himself down;
text.KJV: And Moses made haste, and bowed his head toward the earth, and worshipped.
text.NABRE: Moses at once knelt and bowed down to the ground.
text.DRB: And Moyses making haste, bowed flat unto the earth, & adoring,

text.NRSV: He said, “If now I have found favor in your sight, O Lord, I pray, let the Lord go with us. Although this is a stiff-necked people, pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: and said—If, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, O My Lord, I pray thee let My Lord go on in our midst,—although a stiff-necked people, it is, so wilt thou pardon our iniquity and our sin and take us as thine own.
text.KJV: And he said, If now I have found grace in thy sight, O Lord, let my Lord, I pray thee, go among us; for it is a stiffnecked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for thine inheritance.
text.NABRE: Then he said, “If I find favor with you, Lord, please, Lord, come along in our company. This is indeed a stiff-necked people; yet pardon our wickedness and sins, and claim us as your own.”
text.DRB: he said: If I have found grace in thy sight, o Lord, I beseech thee that thou wilt go with us (for it is a stiff-necked people) and take away our iniquities & sins, & possess us.

text.NRSV: He said: I hereby make a covenant. Before all your people I will perform marvels, such as have not been performed in all the earth or in any nation; and all the people among whom you live shall see the work of the Lord; for it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Lo! I, am about to solemnise a covenant,-in presence of all thy people, will I do marvellous things, which have not been created in any part of the earth nor among any of the nations,—so shall all the people in whose midst thou, art see the work of Yahweh for a fearful thing, it is, which I, am doing with thee.
text.KJV: And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels, such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation: and all the people among which thou art shall see the work of the Lord: for it is a terrible thing that I will do with thee.
text.NABRE: Religious Laws. The Lord said: Here is the covenant I will make. Before all your people I will perform marvels never before done in any nation anywhere on earth, so that all the people among whom you live may see the work of the Lord. Awe-inspiring are the deeds I will perform with you!
text.DRB: Our Lord answered: I will make a covenant in the sight of all. I will do signs that were never seen upon the earth, nor in any nations: that this people may see, in the midst of whom thou art, the terrible work of the Lord, which I will do.

text.NRSV: Observe what I command you today. See, I will drive out before you the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
text.Rotherham: Take thou heed what, I, am commanding thee this day,—behold me! driving out from before thee—the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
text.KJV: Observe thou that which I command thee this day: behold, I drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
text.NABRE: As for you, observe what I am commanding you today. See, I am about to drive out before you the Amorites, Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites.
text.DRB: Observe all things which this day I command thee: I myself will cast out before thy face the Amorrheite, & Chananeite, & Hetheite, the Pherezeite also, and Heveite, and Jebuseite.

text.NRSV: Take care not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land to which you are going, or it will become a snare among you.
text.Rotherham: Take heed to thyself, Lest thou solemnise a covenant with the inhabitant of the land, whereupon, thou, art about to enter,—Lest he become a snare in thy midst;
text.KJV: Take heed to thyself, lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land whither thou goest, lest it be for a snare in the midst of thee:
text.NABRE: Take care not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land that you are to enter; lest they become a snare among you.
text.DRB: Beware thou never join amity with the inhabitants of that land, which may be thy ruin:

text.NRSV: You shall tear down their altars, break their pillars, and cut down their sacred poles
text.Rotherham: For their altars, shall ye smash, And their pillars, shall ye shiver,—And their sacred-stems, shall ye fell.
text.KJV: But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves:
text.NABRE: Tear down their altars; smash their sacred stones, and cut down their asherahs.
text.DRB: but destroy their altars, break their statues, and cut down their groves:

text.NRSV: (for you shall worship no other god, because the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God).
text.Rotherham: For thou shalt not bow thyself down to another GOD,—For as for Yahweh, Jealous, is his name, A jealous GOD, he is:
text.KJV: For thou shalt worship no other god: for the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God:
text.NABRE: You shall not bow down to any other god, for the Lord—“Jealous” his name—is a jealous God.
text.DRB: adore not a strange God. The Lord his name is Jealous, God is an emulator.

text.NRSV: You shall not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, for when they prostitute themselves to their gods and sacrifice to their gods, someone among them will invite you, and you will eat of the sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: Lest thou solemnise a covenant with the inhabitant of the land,—And then as surely as they go unchastely after their gods and sacrifice to their gods, So surely will he invite thee, And thou wilt eat of his sacrifice;
text.KJV: Lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they go a whoring after their gods, and do sacrifice unto their gods, and one call thee, and thou eat of his sacrifice;
text.NABRE: Do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land; else, when they prostitute themselves with their gods and sacrifice to them, one of them may invite you and you may partake of the sacrifice.
text.DRB: Enter no traffic with the men of those regions: lest when they have fornicated with their gods, & have adored their idols, some man call thee to eat of the things immolated.

text.NRSV: And you will take wives from among their daughters for your sons, and their daughters who prostitute themselves to their gods will make your sons also prostitute themselves to their gods.
text.Rotherham: And thou wilt take of his daughters for thy sons,—And his daughters will go unchastely after their gods, And will cause thy sons to go unchastely after their gods.
text.KJV: And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after their gods, and make thy sons go a whoring after their gods.
text.NABRE: And when you take their daughters as wives for your sons, and their daughters prostitute themselves with their gods, they will make your sons do the same.
text.DRB: Neither shalt thou take a wife for thy sons of their daughters: lest after themselves have fornicated they make thy sons also to fornicate with their gods.

text.NRSV: You shall not make cast idols.
text.Rotherham: Molten gods, shalt thou not make to thee.
text.KJV: Thou shalt make thee no molten gods.
text.NABRE: You shall not make for yourselves molten gods.
text.DRB: Molten gods thou shalt not make to thee.

text.NRSV: You shall keep the festival of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month of Abib; for in the month of Abib you came out from Egypt.
text.Rotherham: The festival of unleavened cakes, shalt thou keep, seven days, shalt thou eat unleavened cakes, which I commanded thee, at the set time, in the month Abib,—for in the month Abib, camest thou forth out of Egypt.
text.KJV: The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep. Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, in the time of the month Abib: for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.
text.NABRE: You shall keep the festival of Unleavened Bread. For seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib you are to eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you; for in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.
text.DRB: Thou shalt keep the solemnity of the azymes. Seven days shalt thou eat azymes, as I have commanded thee, in the time of the month of new corn: for in the month of spring time thou didst go out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: All that first opens the womb is mine, all your male livestock, the firstborn of cow and sheep.
text.Rotherham: Every one that is the first to be born, is mine,—and every one of thy cattle that is born a male, firstling of oxen or sheep.
text.KJV: All that openeth the matrix is mine; and every firstling among thy cattle, whether ox or sheep, that is male.
text.NABRE: To me belongs every male that opens the womb among all your livestock, whether in the herd or in the flock.
text.DRB: All of the male kind, that openeth the matrice, shall be mine. Of all beasts, as well oxen as of sheep, it shall be mine.

text.NRSV: The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or if you will not redeem it you shall break its neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: But the firstling of an ass, thou mayst redeem with a lamb, and if thou wilt not redeem it, then shalt thou break its neck. Every firstborn of thy sons, shalt thou redeem, and he must not see my face, with empty hand.
text.KJV: But the firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb: and if thou redeem him not, then shalt thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem. And none shall appear before me empty.
text.NABRE: The firstling of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb; if you do not redeem it, you must break its neck. The firstborn among your sons you shall redeem. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.
text.DRB: The first-born of an ass thou shalt redeem with a sheep: but if thou wilt not give a price for it, it shall be slain. The first-born of thy sons thou shalt redeem: neither shalt thou appear empty in my sight.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even in plowing time and in harvest time you shall rest.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shalt thou labour, but on the seventh day, shalt thou rest,—in plowing time and in harvest, shalt thou rest.
text.KJV: Six days thou shalt work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest: in earing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.
text.NABRE: Six days you may labor, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even during the seasons of plowing and harvesting you must rest.
text.DRB: Six days shalt thou work, the seventh day thou shalt cease to ear & reap.

text.NRSV: You shall observe the festival of weeks, the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the festival of ingathering at the turn of the year.
text.Rotherham: And the festival of weeks, shalt thou make to thee, the firstfruits of the wheat harvest,—and the festival of ingathering, at the closing in of the year:
text.KJV: And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year’s end.
text.NABRE: You shall keep the feast of Weeks with the first fruits of the wheat harvest, likewise, the feast of the Ingathering at the close of the year.
text.DRB: The solemnity of weeks thou shalt make to thee, in the first fruits of corn of thy wheat harvest, and the solemnity, when the time of the year returneth that all things are laid up.

text.NRSV: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord God, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: three times in the year, shall every one of thy males see the face of the Lord Yahweh the God of Israel.
text.KJV: Thrice in the year shall all your men children appear before the Lord God, the God of Israel.
text.NABRE: Three times a year all your men shall appear before the Lord, the Lord God of Israel.
text.DRB: Three times of the year all thy male shall appear in the sight of the omnipotent Lord God of Israel.

text.NRSV: For I will cast out nations before you, and enlarge your borders; no one shall covet your land when you go up to appear before the Lord your God three times in the year.
text.Rotherham: For I will root out nations from before thee, and will enlarge thy borders,—and no man shall covet thy land, when thou goes up to see the face of Yahweh thy God, three times in the year.
text.KJV: For I will cast out the nations before thee, and enlarge thy borders: neither shall any man desire thy land, when thou shalt go up to appear before the Lord thy God thrice in the year.
text.NABRE: Since I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your territory, no one will covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: For when I shall have taken away the nations from thy face, and shall have dilated thy borders, no man shall lie in wait against thy land, when thou doest go up, and appear in the sight of the Lord thy God thrice in a year.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven, and the sacrifice of the festival of the passover shall not be left until the morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not slay, along with anything leavened, the blood of my sacrifice,—neither shall remain to the morning, the sacrifice of the festival of the passover.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left unto the morning.
text.NABRE: You shall not offer me the blood of sacrifice with anything leavened, nor shall the sacrifice of the Passover feast be kept overnight for the next day.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not immolate the blood of my host upon leaven: neither shall there remain in the morning of the victim of the solemnity of the Phase.

text.NRSV: The best of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother’s milk.
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the firstfruits of thy ground, shalt thou bring into the house of Yahweh, thy God, Thou shalt not boil a kid, in the milk of its dam.
text.KJV: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring unto the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
text.NABRE: The choicest first fruits of your soil you shall bring to the house of the Lord, your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.
text.DRB: The first of the fruits of thy ground thou shalt offer in the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of his dam.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Write these words; in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Write for thyself these words, for according to the tenor of these words, have I solemnized with thee, a covenant, and with Israel.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Write thou these words: for after the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel.
text.NABRE: Radiance of Moses’ Face. Then the Lord said to Moses: Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: write thee these words, in which I have made a covenant both with thee and with Israel.

text.NRSV: He was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.
text.Rotherham: So then he was there, with Yahweh, forty days and forty nights, bread, did he not eat and, water, did he not drink, and he wrote upon the tables, the words of the covenant, the ten words.
text.KJV: And he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he did neither eat bread, nor drink water. And he wrote upon the tables the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.
text.NABRE: So Moses was there with the Lord for forty days and forty nights, without eating any food or drinking any water, and he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten words.
text.DRB: Therefore he was there with our Lord forty days & forty nights: he did not eat bread, and he drunk no water, & he wrote in the tables the words of the covenant, ten.

text.NRSV: Moses came down from Mount Sinai. As he came down from the mountain with the two tablets of the covenant in his hand, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Moses came down out of Mount Sinai, with the two tables of testimony in the hand of Moses, when he came down out of the mount, that, Moses, knew not that the skin of his face shone, through his having spoken with him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai with the two tables of testimony in Moses’ hand, when he came down from the mount, that Moses wist not that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.
text.NABRE: As Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the covenant in his hands, he did not know that the skin of his face had become radiant while he spoke with the Lord.
text.DRB: And when Moyses came down from the mount Sinai, he held the two tables of testimony, and he knew not that his face was horned by the conversation of the talk of our Lord.

text.NRSV: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, the skin of his face was shining, and they were afraid to come near him.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and all the sons of Israel saw Moses, and lo! the skin of his face shone, and they stood in awe of drawing nigh unto him.
text.KJV: And when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come nigh him.
text.NABRE: When Aaron, then, and the other Israelites saw Moses and noticed how radiant the skin of his face had become, they were afraid to come near him.
text.DRB: And Aaron & the children of Israel seeing the face of Moyses horned, they were afraid to come near.

text.NRSV: But Moses called to them; and Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke with them.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses called unto them, and Aaron and all the princes in the assembly returned unto him,—and Moses spake unto them.
text.KJV: And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.
text.NABRE: Only after Moses called to them did Aaron and all the leaders of the community come back to him. Moses then spoke to them.
text.DRB: And being called of him, they returned as well Aaron as the Princes of the Synagogue. And after that he spake to them,

text.NRSV: Afterward all the Israelites came near, and he gave them in commandment all that the Lord had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: And afterwards, came nigh, all the sons of Israel, and he gave them in commandment all that Yahweh had spoken with him in Mount Sinai.
text.KJV: And afterward all the children of Israel came nigh: and he gave them in commandment all that the Lord had spoken with him in mount Sinai.
text.NABRE: Later, all the Israelites came up to him, and he enjoined on them all that the Lord had told him on Mount Sinai.
text.DRB: all the children of Israel also came to him: whom he commanded all things that he had heard of our Lord in mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished speaking with them, he put a veil on his face;
text.Rotherham: And when Moses had made an end of speaking with them, he put over his face a veil:
text.KJV: And till Moses had done speaking with them, he put a vail on his face.
text.NABRE: When Moses finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.
text.DRB: And having ended his talk, he put a veil upon his face.

text.NRSV: but whenever Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he would take the veil off, until he came out; and when he came out, and told the Israelites what he had been commanded,
text.Rotherham: but, as often as Moses went in before Yahweh to speak with him, he put aside the veil until he came out,—and, as often as he came out and spake unto the sons of Israel, that which he had been commanded,
text.KJV: But when Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he took the vail off, until he came out. And he came out, and spake unto the children of Israel that which he was commanded.
text.NABRE: Whenever Moses entered the presence of the Lord to speak with him, he removed the veil until he came out again. On coming out, he would tell the Israelites all that he had been commanded.
text.DRB: Which going in to our Lord, and speaking with him, he took away until he went forth, and then he spake to the children of Israel all things that had been commanded him,

text.NRSV: the Israelites would see the face of Moses, that the skin of his face was shining; and Moses would put the veil on his face again, until he went in to speak with him.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses face shone,—so Moses again put the veil over his face, until he went in to speak with him.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses’ face shone: and Moses put the vail upon his face again, until he went in to speak with him.
text.NABRE: Then the Israelites would see that the skin of Moses’ face was radiant; so he would again put the veil over his face until he went in to speak with the Lord.
text.DRB: who saw that the face of Moyses coming forth was horned, but he covered his face again, if at any time he spake to them.

text.NRSV: Moses assembled all the congregation of the Israelites and said to them: These are the things that the Lord has commanded you to do:
text.Rotherham: And Moses called together all the assembly of the sons of Israel and said unto them,—These are the things which Yahweh hath commanded to do them.
text.KJV: And Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These are the words which the Lord hath commanded, that ye should do them.
text.NABRE: Moses assembled the whole Israelite community and said to them, “These are the words the Lord has commanded to be observed.
text.DRB: Therefore all the multitude of the children of Israel being gathered together, he said to them: These are the things which our Lord hath commanded to be done.

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day you shall have a holy sabbath of solemn rest to the Lord; whoever does any work on it shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but on the seventh day, shall there be to you a holy sabbath-keeping, unto Yahweh, whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.
text.KJV: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the Lord: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: On six days work may be done, but the seventh day shall be holy to you as the sabbath of complete rest to the Lord. Anyone who does work on that day shall be put to death.
text.DRB: Six days you shall do work: the seventh day shall be holy unto you, the sabbath, and rest of our Lord: he that shall do any work in it, shall be slain.

text.NRSV: You shall kindle no fire in all your dwellings on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not kindle a fire, in any of your dwellings,—on the sabbath day.
text.KJV: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: You shall not even light a fire in any of your dwellings on the sabbath day.”
text.DRB: You shall not kindle fire in all your habitations on the sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Moses said to all the congregation of the Israelites: This is the thing that the Lord has commanded:
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, saying, This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, saying,
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord commanded, saying,
text.NABRE: Collection of Materials. Moses said to the whole Israelite community, “This is what the Lord has commanded:
text.DRB: And Moyses said to all the assembly of the children of Israel: This is the word that our Lord hath commanded, saying:

text.NRSV: Take from among you an offering to the Lord; let whoever is of a generous heart bring the Lord’s offering: gold, silver, and bronze;
text.Rotherham: Take ye from among you, a heave-offering to Yahweh, every one whose heart is willing shall bring it in, even the heave-offering of Yahweh, gold and silver and bronze:
text.KJV: Take ye from among you an offering unto the Lord: whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the Lord; gold, and silver, and brass,
text.NABRE: Receive from among you contributions for the Lord. Everyone, as his heart prompts him, shall bring, as a contribution to the Lord, gold, silver, and bronze;
text.DRB: Separate with you first fruits to the Lord. Let every one that is willing & hath a ready heart, offer them to the Lord: gold and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine linen; goats’ hair,
text.Rotherham: and blue and purple and crimson and fine linen and goat’s-hair;
text.KJV: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair,
text.NABRE: violet, purple, and scarlet yarn; fine linen and goat hair;
text.DRB: hyacinth and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and silk, the hair of goats,

text.NRSV: tanned rams’ skins, and fine leather; acacia wood,
text.Rotherham: and rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and acacia wood;
text.KJV: And rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and shittim wood,
text.NABRE: rams’ skins dyed red, and tahash skins; acacia wood;
text.DRB: and rams' skins dyed red, and janthine skins, the wood setim,

text.NRSV: oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,
text.Rotherham: and oil for giving light, and aromatic spices for the anointing oil, and for fragrant incense;
text.KJV: And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense,
text.NABRE: oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
text.DRB: and oil to maintain lights, and to make ointment, and most sweet incense.

text.NRSV: and onyx stones and gems to be set in the ephod and the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: and onyx stones and setting stones, for the ephod, and for the breastpiece.
text.KJV: And onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate.
text.NABRE: onyx stones and other gems for mounting on the ephod and on the breastpiece.
text.DRB: Onyx stones, and precious stones, for the adorning of the Ephod and the Rationale.

text.NRSV: All who are skillful among you shall come and make all that the Lord has commanded: the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And, all the wise-hearted among you, shall come in, that they may make, all that which Yahweh hath commanded:—
text.KJV: And every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the Lord hath commanded;
text.NABRE: Call for Artisans. “Let every artisan among you come and make all that the Lord has commanded:
text.DRB: Whosoever of you is wise, let him come, and make that which our Lord hath commanded:

text.NRSV: its tent and its covering, its clasps and its frames, its bars, its pillars, and its bases;
text.Rotherham: the habitation, its tent, and its covering,—its clasps its boards, and its bars its pillars, and its sockets:
text.KJV: The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets,
text.NABRE: the tabernacle, with its tent, its covering, its clasps, its frames, its bars, its columns, and its pedestals;
text.DRB: to wit, the Tabernacle, and the roof thereof, and the cover, the rings, and the boardwork with the bars, the pins & the feet:

text.NRSV: the ark with its poles, the mercy seat, and the curtain for the screen;
text.Rotherham: the ark with its staves the propitiatory, and the screening veil;
text.KJV: The ark, and the staves thereof, with the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering,
text.NABRE: the ark, with its poles, the cover, and the curtain veil;
text.DRB: the Ark and the staves, the propitiatory, and the veil, that is drawn before it:

text.NRSV: the table with its poles and all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence;
text.Rotherham: the table with its staves and all its utensils, and the Presence-bread:
text.KJV: The table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread,
text.NABRE: the table, with its poles and all its utensils, and the showbread;
text.DRB: the Table with the bars and the vessels, and the loaves of proposition:

text.NRSV: the lampstand also for the light, with its utensils and its lamps, and the oil for the light;
text.Rotherham: the lampstand for giving light, with its utensils, and its lamps, and the oil for giving light;
text.KJV: The candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light,
text.NABRE: the menorah, with its utensils, the lamps, and the oil for the light;
text.DRB: the Candlestick to bear up the lights, the vessels thereof and the lamps, and the oil to the nourishing of fires:

text.NRSV: and the altar of incense, with its poles, and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance, the entrance of the tabernacle;
text.Rotherham: and the incense altar, with its staves, and the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense,—and the entrance screen, for the entrance of the habitation;
text.KJV: And the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle,
text.NABRE: the altar of incense, with its poles; the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense; the entrance curtain for the entrance of the tabernacle;
text.DRB: the Altar of incense, and the bars, and the oil of unction and the incense of spices: the Hanging at the door of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: the altar of burnt offering, with its grating of bronze, its poles, and all its utensils, the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: the altar for the ascending-sacrifice, and the bronze grating which pertaineth thereto, its staves and all its utensils, the laver and its stand;
text.KJV: The altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,
text.NABRE: the altar for burnt offerings, with its bronze grating, its poles, and all its utensils; the basin, with its stand;
text.DRB: the Altar of holocaust, and his grate of brass, with the bars and vessels thereof: the Laver and his feet:

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, its pillars and its bases, and the screen for the gate of the court;
text.Rotherham: the hangings for the court, its pillars and its sockets,—and the screen for the gate of the court:
text.KJV: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court,
text.NABRE: the hangings of the court, with their columns and pedestals; the curtain for the gate of the court;
text.DRB: the Curtains of the court with the pillars and the feet, the hanging in the doors of the entry,

text.NRSV: the pegs of the tabernacle and the pegs of the court, and their cords;
text.Rotherham: the pins of the habitation, and the pins of the court, and their cords;
text.KJV: The pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords,
text.NABRE: the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the court, with their ropes;
text.DRB: the pins of the tabernacle and of the court with their little cords:

text.NRSV: the finely worked vestments for ministering in the holy place, the holy vestments for the priest Aaron, and the vestments of his sons, for their service as priests.
text.Rotherham: the cloths of variegated stuff for ministering in the sanctuary,—the sacred garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons for ministering as priests.
text.KJV: The cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: the service cloths for use in the sanctuary; the sacred vestments for Aaron, the priest, and the vestments for his sons in their ministry.”
text.DRB: the Vestments, that are to be used in the ministry of the sanctuary, the vesture of Aaron the high-Priest, and of his sons, to do the function of Priesthood unto me.

text.NRSV: Then all the congregation of the Israelites withdrew from the presence of Moses.
text.Rotherham: So all the assembly of the sons of Israel went forth from the presence of Moses;
text.KJV: And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.
text.NABRE: The Contribution. When the whole Israelite community left Moses’ presence,
text.DRB: And all the multitude of the children of Israel going forth from the sight of Moyses,

text.NRSV: And they came, everyone whose heart was stirred, and everyone whose spirit was willing, and brought the Lord’s offering to be used for the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the sacred vestments.
text.Rotherham: and they brought in—every man whose heart lifted him up,—and, every one whose spirit made him willing, brought in the heave-offering of Yahweh, for the construction of the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the holy garments.
text.KJV: And they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, and they brought the Lord’s offering to the work of the tabernacle of the congregation, and for all his service, and for the holy garments.
text.NABRE: all, as their hearts moved them and their spirit prompted, brought a contribution to the Lord for the work of the tent of meeting, for all its services, and for the sacred vestments.
text.DRB: offered first fruits to our Lord with a most prompt and devout mind, to make the work of the tabernacle of the testimony. Whatsoever was necessary to the service thereof and to the holy vestments,

text.NRSV: So they came, both men and women; all who were of a willing heart brought brooches and earrings and signet rings and pendants, all sorts of gold objects, everyone bringing an offering of gold to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea they brought in—the men with the women,—all who were willing-hearted, brought in—bracelets and nose-ornaments and rings, and buckles, all manner of jewels of gold, even every man who waved a wave-offering of gold unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, and brought bracelets, and earrings, and rings, and tablets, all jewels of gold: and every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Both the men and the women, all as their heart prompted them, brought brooches, earrings, rings, necklaces, and various other gold articles. Everyone who could presented an offering of gold to the Lord.
text.DRB: both men and women did give, tablets and earlets, rings and bracelets: every vessel of gold was separated for the donaries of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And everyone who possessed blue or purple or crimson yarn or fine linen or goats’ hair or tanned rams’ skins or fine leather, brought them.
text.Rotherham: And, every man with whom was found blue and purple and crimson and fine linen and goat’s-hair,—and rams’ skins dyed red and badgers’ skins brought them in.
text.KJV: And every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair, and red skins of rams, and badgers’ skins, brought them.
text.NABRE: Everyone who happened to have violet, purple, or scarlet yarn, fine linen or goat hair, rams’ skins dyed red or tahash skins, brought them.
text.DRB: If any man had hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed red, and janthine skins,

text.NRSV: Everyone who could make an offering of silver or bronze brought it as the Lord’s offering; and everyone who possessed acacia wood of any use in the work, brought it.
text.Rotherham: Every one who would heave a heave-offering of silver or bronze, brought in the heave-offering of Yahweh,—and, every one with whom was found acacia wood for any construction for the service, brought it in.
text.KJV: Every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the Lord’s offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it.
text.NABRE: Whoever could make a contribution of silver or bronze offered it to the Lord; and everyone who happened to have acacia wood for any part of the work, brought it.
text.DRB: metal of silver and brass, they offered to our Lord, and the wood setim for diverse uses.

text.NRSV: All the skillful women spun with their hands, and brought what they had spun in blue and purple and crimson yarns and fine linen;
text.Rotherham: And, all the women who were wise-hearted, with their hands, did spin,—and brought in that which they had spun—the blue, and the purple, the crimson, and the fine linen.
text.KJV: And all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, both of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine linen.
text.NABRE: All the women who were expert spinners brought hand-spun violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen thread.
text.DRB: But the skillful women also gave such things as they had spun, hyacinth, purple, and scarlet, and silk,

text.NRSV: all the women whose hearts moved them to use their skill spun the goats’ hair.
text.Rotherham: And, all the women whose hearts lifted them up with wisdom, span the goat’s-hair.
text.KJV: And all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats’ hair.
text.NABRE: All the women, as their hearts and skills moved them, spun goat hair.
text.DRB: and goats' hair, giving all of their own accord.

text.NRSV: And the leaders brought onyx stones and gems to be set in the ephod and the breastpiece,
text.Rotherham: And the princes, brought in the onyx stones and the setting stones,—for the ephod and for the breastpiece;
text.KJV: And the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate;
text.NABRE: The tribal leaders brought onyx stones and other gems for mounting on the ephod and on the breastpiece;
text.DRB: But the Princes offered onyx stones, and precious stones, for the Ephod and the Rationale,

text.NRSV: and spices and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.
text.Rotherham: and the aromatic spices, and the oil,—for giving light, and for anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.
text.KJV: And spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.
text.NABRE: as well as spices, and oil for the light, anointing oil, and fragrant incense.
text.DRB: and spices and oil to maintain the lights, and for the preparing of ointment, and to make the incense of most sweet savour.

text.NRSV: All the Israelite men and women whose hearts made them willing to bring anything for the work that the Lord had commanded by Moses to be done, brought it as a freewill offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: [In the case of], every male and woman whose heart, made them willing, to bring in for any construction which, Yahweh had commanded them to make, by the hand of Moses, [in their case] did the sons of Israel bring in a free-will offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: The children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the Lord, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work, which the Lord had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: Every Israelite man and woman brought to the Lord such voluntary offerings as they thought best, for the various kinds of work which the Lord, through Moses, had commanded to be done.
text.DRB: All men and women with devout mind offered donaries, that the works might be made which our Lord had commanded by the hand of Moyses. All the children of Israel did dedicate voluntary things to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to the Israelites: See, the Lord has called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the sons of Israel—See! Yahweh hath called by name,—Bezaleel son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the Lord hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
text.NABRE: The Artisans. Moses said to the Israelites: “See, the Lord has singled out Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,
text.DRB: And Moyses said to the children of Israel: Behold, our Lord hath called by name Beseleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda.

text.NRSV: he has filled him with divine spirit, with skill, intelligence, and knowledge in every kind of craft,
text.Rotherham: and filled him with the spirit of God,—in, wisdom in understanding and in knowledge, and in all manner of execution;
text.KJV: And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship;
text.NABRE: and has filled him with a divine spirit of skill and understanding and knowledge in every craft:
text.DRB: And hath filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom and intelligence, and science and all learning,

text.NRSV: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
text.Rotherham: to devise skilful designs,—to work in gold and in silver and in bronze;
text.KJV: And to devise curious works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,
text.NABRE: in the production of embroidery, in making things of gold, silver, or bronze,
text.DRB: to devise and to make work in gold and silver, and brass,

text.NRSV: in cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, in every kind of craft.
text.Rotherham: and in the cutting of stones for setting, and in the cutting of wood,—to work in any manner of skilful workmanship,
text.KJV: And in the cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of wood, to make any manner of cunning work.
text.NABRE: in cutting and mounting precious stones, in carving wood, and in every other craft.
text.DRB: and in graving stones, and in carpenter's work. Whatsoever can be devised artificially,

text.NRSV: And he has inspired him to teach, both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.
text.Rotherham: To teach also, hath he put in his heart, [the heart of] him, and of Oholiab—son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan;
text.KJV: And he hath put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.
text.NABRE: He has also given both him and Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, the ability to teach others.
text.DRB: he hath given in his heart: Ooliab also the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan:

text.NRSV: He has filled them with skill to do every kind of work done by an artisan or by a designer or by an embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen, or by a weaver—by any sort of artisan or skilled designer.
text.Rotherham: hath filled them with wisdom of heart to work all manner of workmanship of cutter, and deviser and embroiderer, in blue and in purple, in crimson and in fine linen and of the weaver,—workers of any manner of workmanship, and devisers of skilful designs.
text.KJV: Them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of them that do any work, and of those that devise cunning work.
text.NABRE: He has endowed them with skill to execute all types of work: engraving, embroidering, the making of variegated cloth of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen thread, weaving, and all other arts and crafts.
text.DRB: both hath he instructed with wisdom, to make the works of a carpenter, a tapester, an embroiderer of hyacinth and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and silk, and to weave all things, and to invent all new things.

text.NRSV: Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful one to whom the Lord has given skill and understanding to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Bezaleel and Oholiab work, and every man that is wise-hearted, in whom Yahweh hath put wisdom and understanding, to know how to work any manner of workmanship for the holy service,—according to all that Yahweh hath commanded.
text.KJV: Then wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whom the Lord put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that the Lord had commanded.
text.NABRE: “Bezalel, therefore, will set to work with Oholiab and with all the artisans whom the Lord has endowed with skill and understanding in knowing how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary, just as the Lord has commanded.”
text.DRB: Beseleel therefore, and Ooliab, and every wise man, to whom our Lord gave wisdom and understanding, to know how to work artificially, made the things that are necessary for the uses of the Sanctuary, and which our Lord did command.

text.NRSV: Moses then called Bezalel and Oholiab and every skillful one to whom the Lord had given skill, everyone whose heart was stirred to come to do the work;
text.Rotherham: So then Moses called upon Bezaleel, and upon Oholiab, and upon every man that was wise-hearted, in whose heart Yahweh had put wisdom,—every one whose heart lifted him up, to draw near unto the work, to do it.
text.KJV: And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whose heart the Lord had put wisdom, even every one whose heart stirred him up to come unto the work to do it:
text.NABRE: Moses then called Bezalel and Oholiab and all the other artisans whom the Lord had endowed with skill, men whose hearts moved them to come and do the work.
text.DRB: And when Moyses had called them, and every cunning man, to whom our Lord had given wisdom, and such as of their own accord had offered themselves to the making of the work,

text.NRSV: and they received from Moses all the freewill offerings that the Israelites had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning,
text.Rotherham: And they took, from the presence of Moses, all the heave-offering which the sons of Israel had brought in for the work of the holy service to do it,—howbeit, they, brought in unto him yet more free-will offerings, morning by morning.
text.KJV: And they received of Moses all the offering, which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it withal. And they brought yet unto him free offerings every morning.
text.NABRE: They received from Moses all the contributions which the Israelites had brought for the work to be done for the sanctuary service. Still, morning after morning the people continued to bring their voluntary offerings to Moses.
text.DRB: he delivered all the donaries of the children of Israel unto them. Who being earnest about the work, the people daily in the morning did offer their vows.

text.NRSV: so that all the artisans who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came, each from the task being performed,
text.Rotherham: Then came in all the wise men who were doing all the holy work,—man by man from his work which, they, were doing;
text.KJV: And all the wise men, that wrought all the work of the sanctuary, came every man from his work which they made;
text.NABRE: Thereupon all the artisans who were doing the work for the sanctuary came from the work each was doing,
text.DRB: Whereupon the artificers being constrained to come,

text.NRSV: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing much more than enough for doing the work that the Lord has commanded us to do.”
text.Rotherham: and spake unto Moses, saying, More than enough, are the people bringing in,—[more] than is needed for the service pertaining to the work; which Yahweh hath commanded to be done.
text.KJV: And they spake unto Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work, which the Lord commanded to make.
text.NABRE: and told Moses, “The people are bringing much more than is needed to carry out the work which the Lord has commanded us to do.”
text.DRB: said to Moyses: The people offereth more than is necessary.

text.NRSV: So Moses gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp: “No man or woman is to make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing;
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave commandment and they passed a proclamation throughout the camp, saying, Let, neither man nor woman, make any more material for the holy heave-offering. So the people were restrained from bringing in.
text.KJV: And Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the offering of the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.
text.NABRE: Moses, therefore, ordered a proclamation to be made throughout the camp: “Let neither man nor woman make any more contributions for the sanctuary.” So the people stopped bringing their offerings;
text.DRB: Moyses therefore commanded proclamation to be made by the crier's voice: Let neither man nor woman offer any more in the work of the Sanctuary. And so they ceased from offering gifts,

text.NRSV: for what they had already brought was more than enough to do all the work.
text.Rotherham: For the material was enough for all the work, to make it,—and to spare.
text.KJV: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.
text.NABRE: there was already enough at hand, and more than enough, to complete the work to be done.
text.DRB: because the things that were offered did suffice and were over-much.

text.NRSV: All those with skill among the workers made the tabernacle with ten curtains; they were made of fine twisted linen, and blue, purple, and crimson yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked into them.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the wise-hearted among the workers of the material make the habitation of ten curtains, of fine-twined linen, and blue and purple and crimson, with cherubim the work of the skilful weaver, did he make them.
text.KJV: And every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work made he them.
text.NABRE: The Tent Cloth and Coverings. The various artisans who were doing the work made the tabernacle with its ten sheets woven of fine linen twined, having cherubim embroidered on them with violet, purple, and scarlet yarn.
text.DRB: And all the wise hearted men, to accomplish the work of the tabernacle, made ten curtains of twisted silk, and hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, with varied work, and the art of embroidering:

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains were of the same size.
text.Rotherham: The length of each curtain, was twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth four cubits, for each curtain,—one measure, had all the curtains.
text.KJV: The length of one curtain was twenty and eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: the curtains were all of one size.
text.NABRE: The length of each sheet was twenty-eight cubits, and the width four cubits; all the sheets were the same size.
text.DRB: of which one had in length twenty eight cubits, and in breadth four: there was one measure of all the curtains.

text.NRSV: He joined five curtains to one another, and the other five curtains he joined to one another.
text.Rotherham: And he joined the [first] five curtains, one to another,—and the [other] five curtains, joined he one to another,
text.KJV: And he coupled the five curtains one unto another: and the other five curtains he coupled one unto another.
text.NABRE: Five of the sheets were joined together, edge to edge; and the other five sheets likewise, edge to edge.
text.DRB: And he joined five curtains, one to another, and the other five he coupled to themselves one with another.

text.NRSV: He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain of the first set; likewise he made them on the edge of the outermost curtain of the second set;
text.Rotherham: And he made loops of blue upon the border of the first curtain, at the edge of the set,—thus, made he in the border of the other curtain at the edge of the second set:
text.KJV: And he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling: likewise he made in the uttermost side of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.
text.NABRE: Loops of violet yarn were made along the edge of the end sheet in the first set, and the same along the edge of the end sheet in the second set.
text.DRB: He made also loops of hyacinth in the edge of one curtain on either side, and in the edge of the other curtain in like manner,

text.NRSV: he made fifty loops on the one curtain, and he made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that was in the second set; the loops were opposite one another.
text.Rotherham: Fifty loops, made he in the first curtain, and fifty loops, made he in the edge of the curtain that was in the second set,—the loops being, opposite, one to another.
text.KJV: Fifty loops made he in one curtain, and fifty loops made he in the edge of the curtain which was in the coupling of the second: the loops held one curtain to another.
text.NABRE: Fifty loops were thus put on one inner sheet, and fifty loops on the inner sheet in the other set, with the loops directly opposite each other.
text.DRB: that the loops might meet one against another, and might be joined each with other.

text.NRSV: And he made fifty clasps of gold, and joined the curtains one to the other with clasps; so the tabernacle was one whole.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty clasps of gold,—and joined the curtains one to another with the clasps, and so the habitation became, one.
text.KJV: And he made fifty taches of gold, and coupled the curtains one unto another with the taches: so it became one tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Then fifty clasps of gold were made, with which the sheets were joined so that the tabernacle formed one whole.
text.DRB: Whereupon also he did cast fifty rings of gold, that might catch the loops of the curtains, and might be made one tabernacle.

text.NRSV: He also made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains.
text.Rotherham: And he made curtains of goat’s-hair, for the tent over the habitation, eleven curtains, did he make them:
text.KJV: And he made curtains of goats’ hair for the tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains he made them.
text.NABRE: Sheets of goat hair were also woven as a tent over the tabernacle. Eleven such sheets were made.
text.DRB: He made also eleven curtains of goats hair to cover the roof of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The length of each curtain was thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains were of the same size.
text.Rotherham: the length of each curtain, was thirty cubits, and, four cubits, was the breath of each curtain,—one measure, had the eleven curtains.
text.KJV: The length of one curtain was thirty cubits, and four cubits was the breadth of one curtain: the eleven curtains were of one size.
text.NABRE: The length of each sheet was thirty cubits and the width four cubits; all eleven sheets were the same size.
text.DRB: one curtain in length had thirty cubits, & in breadth four cubits: all the curtains were of one measure:

text.NRSV: He joined five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.
text.Rotherham: And he joined five of the curtains by themselves,—and the six curtains, by themselves.
text.KJV: And he coupled five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.
text.NABRE: Five of these sheets were joined into one set, and the other six sheets into another set.
text.DRB: of which five he joined apart, & the other six apart.

text.NRSV: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the other connecting curtain.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty loops upon the border of the curtain that was outmost in the one set and fifty loops, made he upon the border of the curtain of the second set.
text.KJV: And he made fifty loops upon the uttermost edge of the curtain in the coupling, and fifty loops made he upon the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.
text.NABRE: Fifty loops were made along the edge of the end sheet in one set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding sheet in the other set.
text.DRB: And he made fifty loops in the edge of one curtain, and fifty in the edge of another curtain, that they might be joined one to another.

text.NRSV: He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it might be one whole.
text.Rotherham: And he made fifty clasps of bronze,—to join together the tent, that it might become one.
text.KJV: And he made fifty taches of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be one.
text.NABRE: Fifty bronze clasps were made with which the tent was joined so that it formed one whole.
text.DRB: And fifty buckles of brass wherewith the roof might be knit together, that of all the curtains there might be made one covering.

text.NRSV: And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams’ skins and an outer covering of fine leather.
text.Rotherham: And he made a covering to the tent, of rams’ skins dyed red,—and a covering of badgers’ skins, above.
text.KJV: And he made a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers’ skins above that.
text.NABRE: A covering for the tent was made of rams’ skins dyed red and, above that, a covering of tahash skins.
text.DRB: He made also a cover for the tabernacle of rams' skins dyed red: & another cover over that of janthine skins.

text.NRSV: Then he made the upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
text.Rotherham: And he made the boards for the habitation, of acacia wood, for standing up:
text.KJV: And he made boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood, standing up.
text.NABRE: The Framework. Frames were made for the tabernacle, acacia-wood uprights.
text.DRB: He made also the boards of the tabernacle of the wood setim standing.

text.NRSV: Ten cubits was the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the width of each frame.
text.Rotherham: ten cubits, the length of each board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of each board:
text.KJV: The length of a board was ten cubits, and the breadth of a board one cubit and a half.
text.NABRE: The length of each frame was ten cubits, and the width one and a half cubits.
text.DRB: The length of one board was ten cubits: and the breadth contained one cubit & an half.

text.NRSV: Each frame had two pegs for fitting together; he did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: two tenons to each board, united one to another,—thus, made he for all the boards of the habitation.
text.KJV: One board had two tenons, equally distant one from another: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Each frame had two arms, fastening them one to another. In this way all the frames of the tabernacle were made.
text.DRB: There were two mortises throughout every board, that one might be joined to the other. So made he in all the boards of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The frames for the tabernacle he made in this way: twenty frames for the south side;
text.Rotherham: And he made the boards for the habitation, twenty boards, for the south side southwards;
text.KJV: And he made boards for the tabernacle; twenty boards for the south side southward:
text.NABRE: The frames for the tabernacle were made as follows: twenty frames on the south side,
text.DRB: Of the which twenty were at the south side against the south,

text.NRSV: and he made forty bases of silver under the twenty frames, two bases under the first frame for its two pegs, and two bases under the next frame for its two pegs.
text.Rotherham: and, forty sockets of silver, made he, under the twenty boards, two sockets, under the first board, for its two tenons, and, two sockets, under the next board for its two tenons.
text.KJV: And forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
text.NABRE: with forty silver pedestals under the twenty frames, two pedestals under each frame for its two arms;
text.DRB: with forty feet of silver. Two feet were put under one board on either side of the corners, where the mortises of the sides end in the corners.

text.NRSV: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, he made twenty frames
text.Rotherham: And for the second side of the habitation to the north quarter, made he twenty boards;
text.KJV: And for the other side of the tabernacle, which is toward the north corner, he made twenty boards,
text.NABRE: twenty frames on the other side of the tabernacle, the north side,
text.DRB: At that side also of the tabernacle, that looked toward the north, he made twenty boards,

text.NRSV: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under the first frame and two bases under the next frame.
text.Rotherham: and their forty sockets of silver, two sockets, under the first board, and, two sockets, under the next board.
text.KJV: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
text.NABRE: with their forty silver pedestals, two pedestals under each frame.
text.DRB: with forty feet of silver, two feet for every board.

text.NRSV: For the rear of the tabernacle westward he made six frames.
text.Rotherham: And, for the hinder part of the habitation westward, made he six boards;
text.KJV: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.
text.NABRE: At the rear of the tabernacle, to the west, six frames were made,
text.DRB: But against the west, to wit, at that side of the tabernacle, which looketh to the sea, he made six boards,

text.NRSV: He made two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear.
text.Rotherham: and two boards, made he for the corners of the habitation,—in the hinderpart.
text.KJV: And two boards made he for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
text.NABRE: and two frames were made for the corners of the tabernacle, at its rear.
text.DRB: and two other at each corner of the tabernacle behind:

text.NRSV: They were separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; he made two of them in this way, for the two corners.
text.Rotherham: Thus were they to be double beneath, and at the same time, should they be entire, at the top thereof, into each ring, thus, did he for them both, for the two corners.
text.KJV: And they were coupled beneath, and coupled together at the head thereof, to one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners.
text.NABRE: These were double at the bottom, and likewise double at the top, to the first ring. That is how both corner frames were made.
text.DRB: which were also joined from beneath unto the top, & they grew together into one connexion. So he made on either side at the corners:

text.NRSV: There were eight frames with their bases of silver: sixteen bases, under every frame two bases.
text.Rotherham: Thus were there to be eight boards, and, their sockets of silver, were sixteen sockets, two sockets apiece under each board.
text.KJV: And there were eight boards; and their sockets were sixteen sockets of silver, under every board two sockets.
text.NABRE: Thus, there were eight frames, with their sixteen silver pedestals, two pedestals under each frame.
text.DRB: that there were in all eight boards, and had sixteen feet of silver, to wit, two feet under every board.

text.NRSV: He made bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And he made bars of acacia wood,—five, for the boards of the first side of the habitation,
text.KJV: And he made bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
text.NABRE: Bars of acacia wood were also made, five for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,
text.DRB: He made also bars of the wood setim, five to hold together the boards of one side of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
text.Rotherham: and five bars, for the boards of the other side of the habitation, and, five bars, for the boards of the habitation at the hinderpart westward.
text.KJV: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward.
text.NABRE: five for those on the other side, and five for those at the rear, to the west.
text.DRB: and five other to join together the boards of the other side: and besides these, five other bars at the west side of the tabernacle against the sea.

text.NRSV: He made the middle bar to pass through from end to end halfway up the frames.
text.Rotherham: And he made the middle bar,—to run along in the midst of the boards, from end to end.
text.KJV: And he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other.
text.NABRE: The center bar, at the middle of the frames, was made to reach across from end to end.
text.DRB: He made also another bar, that might come by the midst of the boards from corner unto corner.

text.NRSV: And he overlaid the frames with gold, and made rings of gold for them to hold the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
text.Rotherham: And the boards, overlaid he with gold and their rings, made he of gold, as receptacles for the bars, and he overlaid the bars with gold.
text.KJV: And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold to be places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
text.NABRE: The frames were plated with gold, and gold rings were made on them as holders for the bars, which were also plated with gold.
text.DRB: And the boardwork itself he plated with gold. And their rings he made of gold, through which the bars might be drawn: the which also themselves he covered with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine twisted linen, with cherubim skillfully worked into it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the veil, of blue and purple and crimson and fine-twined linen,—of the work of a skilful weaver, made he it, with cherubim.
text.KJV: And he made a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: with cherubims made he it of cunning work.
text.NABRE: The Veil. The veil was made of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, with cherubim embroidered on it.
text.DRB: He made also a veil of hyacinth, and purple, scarlet, and twisted silk, with embroidered work, varied and distinguished:

text.NRSV: For it he made four pillars of acacia, and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold, and he cast for them four bases of silver.
text.Rotherham: And he made for it four pillars of acacia, and overlaid them with gold their hooks of gold, and cast for them, four sockets of silver.
text.KJV: And he made thereunto four pillars of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold: their hooks were of gold; and he cast for them four sockets of silver.
text.NABRE: Four gold-plated columns of acacia wood, with gold hooks, were made for it, and four silver pedestals were cast for them.
text.DRB: and four pillars of the wood setim, which with their heads he plated with gold, casting their feet of silver.

text.NRSV: He also made a screen for the entrance to the tent, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and fine twisted linen, embroidered with needlework;
text.Rotherham: And he made a screen for the opening of the tent, of blue and purple and crimson and fine twined linen, the work of an embroiderer;
text.KJV: And he made an hanging for the tabernacle door of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, of needlework;
text.NABRE: The curtain for the entrance of the tent was made of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, woven in a variegated manner.
text.DRB: He made also a hanging in the entry of the tabernacle of hyacinth, purple, scarlet, and twisted silk, with the work of an embroiderer:

text.NRSV: and its five pillars with their hooks. He overlaid their capitals and their bases with gold, but their five bases were of bronze.
text.Rotherham: and the five pillars thereof and their hooks, and he overlaid their capitals and their cross-rods, with gold, but, their five sockets, were of bronze.
text.KJV: And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets were of brass.
text.NABRE: Its five columns, with their hooks as well as their capitals and bands, were plated with gold; their five pedestals were of bronze.
text.DRB: and five pillars with their heads, which he covered with gold, and their feet he did cast of brass.

text.NRSV: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; it was two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And Bezaleel made the ark, of acacia wood, two cubits and a half, the length thereof and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the height thereof;
text.KJV: And Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half was the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it:
text.NABRE: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood, two and a half cubits long, one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high.
text.DRB: And Beseleel made also the ark of the wood setim, having two cubits & an half in length, and a cubit and an half in breadth, the height also was of one cubit and an half: and he plated it with the purest gold within & without.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a molding of gold around it.
text.Rotherham: and he overlaid it with pure gold within and without,-and made for it a rim of gold, round about;
text.KJV: And he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about.
text.NABRE: The inside and outside were plated with gold, and a molding of gold was put around it.
text.DRB: And he made to it a crown of gold round about,

text.NRSV: He cast for it four rings of gold for its four feet, two rings on its one side and two rings on its other side.
text.Rotherham: and he cast for it four rings of gold, upon the four feet thereof,—even, two rings, on the one side thereof and, two rings, on the other side thereof;
text.KJV: And he cast for it four rings of gold, to be set by the four corners of it; even two rings upon the one side of it, and two rings upon the other side of it.
text.NABRE: Four gold rings were cast for its four supports, two rings on one side and two on the opposite side.
text.DRB: casting four rings of gold at the four corners thereof: two rings in the one side, and two in the other.

text.NRSV: He made poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold,
text.Rotherham: and he made staves of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold;
text.KJV: And he made staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.
text.NABRE: Poles of acacia wood were made and plated with gold;
text.DRB: Bars also he made of the wood setim, which he plated with gold,

text.NRSV: and put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark.
text.Rotherham: and he brought the staves into the rings, upon the sides of the ark, for lifting the ark.
text.KJV: And he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.
text.NABRE: these poles were put through the rings on the sides of the ark, for carrying it.
text.DRB: and which he put into the rings, that were at the sides of the ark to carry it.

text.NRSV: He made a mercy seat of pure gold; two cubits and a half was its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
text.Rotherham: And he made a propitiatory of pure gold,—two cubits and a half, the length thereof, and, a cubit and a half, the breadth thereof:
text.KJV: And he made the mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half was the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof.
text.NABRE: The cover was made of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.
text.DRB: He made also the Propitiatory, that is, the Oracle, of the purest gold, two cubits & an half in length, and a cubit & an half in breadth.

text.NRSV: He made two cherubim of hammered gold; at the two ends of the mercy seat he made them,
text.Rotherham: and he made two cherubim, of gold, of beaten work, did he make them, out of the two ends of the propitiatory:
text.KJV: And he made two cherubims of gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat;
text.NABRE: Two cherubim of beaten gold were made for the two ends of the cover;
text.DRB: Two Cherubins also of beaten gold, which he set on either side of the propitiatory:

text.NRSV: one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end; of one piece with the mercy seat he made the cherubim at its two ends.
text.Rotherham: one cherub, out of this end, and, one cherub, out of that end, out of the propitiatory itself, made he the cherubim, out of the two ends thereof:
text.KJV: One cherub on the end on this side, and another cherub on the other end on that side: out of the mercy seat made he the cherubims on the two ends thereof.
text.NABRE: one cherub was at one end, the other at the other end, made of one piece with the cover, at each end.
text.DRB: One Cherub in the top of one side, and the other Cherub in the top of the other side: two Cherubins in each top of the Propitiatory,

text.NRSV: The cherubim spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings. They faced one another; the faces of the cherubim were turned toward the mercy seat.
text.Rotherham: and the cherubim were spreading out their wings on high making a shelter with their wings over the propitiatory, with, their faces, one towards the other, towards the propitiatory, were the faces of the cherubim,
