text.KJV: And the cherubims spread out their wings on high, and covered with their wings over the mercy seat, with their faces one to another; even to the mercy seatward were the faces of the cherubims.
text.NABRE: The cherubim had their wings spread out above, sheltering the cover. They faced each other, with their faces looking toward the cover.
text.DRB: spreading their wings, and covering the Propitiatory, and looking one toward the other and toward it.

text.NRSV: He also made the table of acacia wood, two cubits long, one cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.Rotherham: And he made the table of acacia wood, two cubits, the length thereof and, a cubit, the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof,
text.KJV: And he made the table of shittim wood: two cubits was the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof:
text.NABRE: The Table. The table was made of acacia wood, two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.
text.DRB: He made also the table of the wood setim in length two cubits, and in breadth one cubit, which had in height a cubit and an half.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold, and made a molding of gold around it.
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid it with pure gold,—and he made thereto a rim of gold round about:
text.KJV: And he overlaid it with pure gold, and made thereunto a crown of gold round about.
text.NABRE: It was plated with pure gold, and a molding of gold was put around it.
text.DRB: And he did compass it with the finest gold, and he made to it a golden ledge round about,

text.NRSV: He made around it a rim a handbreadth wide, and made a molding of gold around the rim.
text.Rotherham: and he mane thereto a border of a handbreadth, round about, and he made a rim of gold to the border thereof round about:
text.KJV: Also he made thereunto a border of an handbreadth round about; and made a crown of gold for the border thereof round about.
text.NABRE: A frame a handbreadth high was also put around it, with a molding of gold around the frame.
text.DRB: and to the ledge itself a golden crown interpolished of four fingers, & upon the same another golden crown.

text.NRSV: He cast for it four rings of gold, and fastened the rings to the four corners at its four legs.
text.Rotherham: and he cast for it, four rings of gold, and placed the rings upon the four corners, which pertained to the four feet thereof:
text.KJV: And he cast for it four rings of gold, and put the rings upon the four corners that were in the four feet thereof.
text.NABRE: Four rings of gold were cast for it and fastened at the four corners, one at each leg.
text.DRB: And he cast four rings of gold, which he put in the four corners at every foot of the table

text.NRSV: The rings that held the poles used for carrying the table were close to the rim.
text.Rotherham: near to the border, were the rings, as receptacles for the staves, for lifting the table;
text.KJV: Over against the border were the rings, the places for the staves to bear the table.
text.NABRE: The rings were alongside the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table.
text.DRB: against the crown: & he put the bars into them, that the table might be carried.

text.NRSV: He made the poles of acacia wood to carry the table, and overlaid them with gold.
text.Rotherham: and he made the staves of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold,—for lifting the table;
text.KJV: And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold, to bear the table.
text.NABRE: These poles for carrying the table were made of acacia wood and plated with gold.
text.DRB: The bars also themselves he made of the wood setim, and compassed them with gold.

text.NRSV: And he made the vessels of pure gold that were to be on the table, its plates and dishes for incense, and its bowls and flagons with which to pour drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: and he made the utensils which were to be upon the table, its dishes and its pans and its bowls, and its basins for pouring out therewith, of pure gold.
text.KJV: And he made the vessels which were upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold.
text.NABRE: The vessels that were set on the table, its plates and cups, as well as its pitchers and bowls for pouring libations, were made of pure gold.
text.DRB: And the vessels for the diverse uses of the table, saucers, phials, and goblets, & censers, of pure gold, wherein the libaments are to be offered.

text.NRSV: He also made the lampstand of pure gold. The base and the shaft of the lampstand were made of hammered work; its cups, its calyxes, and its petals were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the lampstand, of pure gold, of beaten work, made he the lampstand, its base and its shafts, its cups, its apples and its blossoms were, of the same;
text.KJV: And he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same:
text.NABRE: The Menorah. The menorah was made of pure beaten gold—its shaft and branches—with its cups and knobs and petals springing directly from it.
text.DRB: He made also the candlestick beaten of the finest gold. From the shaft whereof the branches, cups, & bowls & lilies did proceed:

text.NRSV: There were six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;
text.Rotherham: with, six branches coming out of its sides,—three branches of the lampstand out of its one side and three branches of the lampstand out of its other side:
text.KJV: And six branches going out of the sides thereof; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof:
text.NABRE: Six branches extended from its sides, three branches on one side and three on the other.
text.DRB: six on both sides, three branches on one side, & three on the other:

text.NRSV: three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on one branch, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with calyx and petals, on the other branch—so for the six branches going out of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: three cups like almond-flowers in the one branch—apple and blossom, and, three cups like almond-flowers, in the next branch, apple and blossom, so for the six branches coming out of the lampstand;
text.KJV: Three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower; and three bowls made like almonds in another branch, a knop and a flower: so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick.
text.NABRE: On one branch there were three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; on the opposite branch there were three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; and so for the six branches that extended from the menorah.
text.DRB: three cups in manner of a nut on every branch, and bowls withal and lilies, and three cups of the fashion of a nut in another branch, and bowls withal and lilies. The work of the six branches, that proceeded from the shaft of the candlestick, equal.

text.NRSV: On the lampstand itself there were four cups shaped like almond blossoms, each with its calyxes and petals.
text.Rotherham: and in the lampstand itself, four cups, like almond-flowers, its apples and its blossoms:
text.KJV: And in the candlestick were four bowls made like almonds, his knops, and his flowers:
text.NABRE: On the menorah there were four cups, shaped like almond blossoms, with their knobs and petals.
text.DRB: And in the shaft itself were four cups after the manner of a nut, and bowls withal at every one and lilies:

text.NRSV: There was a calyx of one piece with it under the first pair of branches, a calyx of one piece with it under the next pair of branches, and a calyx of one piece with it under the last pair of branches.
text.Rotherham: with, an apple, under the two branches of the same, and, an apple, under the next two branches of the same, and, an apple, under the next two branches of the same, for the six branches coming out of the same:
text.KJV: And a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches going out of it.
text.NABRE: The six branches that went out from the menorah had a knob under each pair.
text.DRB: and bowls under two branches in three places, which together make six branches proceeding from one shaft.

text.NRSV: Their calyxes and their branches were of one piece with it, the whole of it one hammered piece of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: their apples and their brunches were of the same, all of it one beaten work, of pure gold.
text.KJV: Their knops and their branches were of the same: all of it was one beaten work of pure gold.
text.NABRE: The knobs and branches so sprang from it that the whole formed but a single piece of pure beaten gold.
text.DRB: Both the bowls therefore, & the branches were out of it, all beaten of the purest gold.

text.NRSV: He made its seven lamps and its snuffers and its trays of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And he made the lamps thereof seven,—with its snuffers and its snuff-trays, of pure gold.
text.KJV: And he made his seven lamps, and his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, of pure gold.
text.NABRE: Its seven lamps, as well as its trimming shears and trays, were made of pure gold.
text.DRB: He made also the seven lamps with their snuffers, and the vessels, where the snuffings should be put out, of most pure gold.

text.NRSV: He made it and all its utensils of a talent of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: Of a talent of pure gold, made he it,—and all the utensils thereof.
text.KJV: Of a talent of pure gold made he it, and all the vessels thereof.
text.NABRE: A talent of pure gold was used for the menorah and its various utensils.
text.DRB: The candlestick withal the vessels thereof did weigh a talent of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the altar of incense of acacia wood, one cubit long, and one cubit wide; it was square, and was two cubits high; its horns were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And he made the incense altar, of acacia wood,—a cubit, the length thereof, and, a cubit, the breadth thereof, foursquare, and two cubits the height thereof, of the same, were the horns thereof.
text.KJV: And he made the incense altar of shittim wood: the length of it was a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; it was foursquare; and two cubits was the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same.
text.NABRE: The Altar of Incense. The altar of incense was made of acacia wood, on a square, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high, having horns that sprang directly from it.
text.DRB: He made also the altar of incense of the wood setim, having a cubit every way four square, and in height two: from the corners whereof the horns did proceed.

text.NRSV: He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, and its sides all around, and its horns; and he made for it a molding of gold all around,
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid it with pure gold—the top thereof and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof,-and he made thereto a rim of gold, round about.
text.KJV: And he overlaid it with pure gold, both the top of it, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns of it: also he made unto it a crown of gold round about.
text.NABRE: Its grate on top, its walls on all four sides, and its horns were plated with pure gold; and a gold molding was put around it.
text.DRB: And he plated it with the purest gold, with the grate and the walls and the horns.

text.NRSV: and made two golden rings for it under its molding, on two opposite sides of it, to hold the poles with which to carry it.
text.Rotherham: And two rings of gold, made he thereto—beneath the rim thereof, upon the two corners thereof, upon the two sides thereof,—as receptacles for the staves, to lift it therewith.
text.KJV: And he made two rings of gold for it under the crown thereof, by the two corners of it, upon the two sides thereof, to be places for the staves to bear it withal.
text.NABRE: Underneath the molding gold rings were placed, two on one side and two on the opposite side, as holders for the poles used in carrying it.
text.DRB: And he made to it a crown of gold round about, and two golden rings under the crown at either side, that the bars may be put into them, and the altar may be carried.

text.NRSV: And he made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.
text.Rotherham: And he made the staves of acacia wood,—and overlaid them with gold.
text.KJV: And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.
text.NABRE: The poles, too, were made of acacia wood and plated with gold.
text.DRB: And the bars themselves he made also of the wood setim, and covered them with plates of gold.

text.NRSV: He made the holy anointing oil also, and the pure fragrant incense, blended as by the perfumer.
text.Rotherham: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure fragrant incense,—the work of a perfumer.
text.KJV: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary.
text.NABRE: The sacred anointing oil and the fragrant incense were prepared in their pure form by a perfumer.
text.DRB: He compounded also oil for the ointment of sanctification, and incense of the purest spices with the work of a pigmentary.

text.NRSV: He made the altar of burnt offering also of acacia wood; it was five cubits long, and five cubits wide; it was square, and three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made the altar for the ascending-sacrifice of acacia wood,—five cubits, the length thereof and, five cubits, the breadth thereof foursquare, and, three cubits, the height thereof.
text.KJV: And he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood: five cubits was the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; it was foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof.
text.NABRE: The altar for burnt offerings was made of acacia wood, on a square, five cubits long and five cubits wide; its height was three cubits.
text.DRB: He made also the Altar of Holocaust of the wood setim, five cubits four square, and three in height:

text.NRSV: He made horns for it on its four corners; its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made the horns thereof, upon the four corners thereof, of the same, were the horns thereof,—and he overlaid it with bronze.
text.KJV: And he made the horns thereof on the four corners of it; the horns thereof were of the same: and he overlaid it with brass.
text.NABRE: At the four corners horns were made that sprang directly from the altar. It was then plated with bronze.
text.DRB: the horns whereof did proceed from the corners, and he covered it with plates of brass.

text.NRSV: He made all the utensils of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the firepans: all its utensils he made of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made all the utensils of the altar—the pans and the shovels, and the sprinkling bowls, the flesh-hooks and the fire-pans,—all the utensils thereof, made he of bronze.
text.KJV: And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass.
text.NABRE: All the utensils of the altar, the pots, shovels, basins, forks and fire pans, were likewise made of bronze.
text.DRB: And for the uses thereof he prepared of brass diverse vessels, cauldrons, tongs, flesh-hooks, pot-hooks, and fire pans.

text.NRSV: He made for the altar a grating, a network of bronze, under its ledge, extending halfway down.
text.Rotherham: And he made to the altar a grating, of network of bronze,—under the margin thereof beneath as far as the midst thereof.
text.KJV: And he made for the altar a brasen grate of network under the compass thereof beneath unto the midst of it.
text.NABRE: A grating, a bronze network, was made for the altar and placed around it, on the ground, half as high as the altar itself.
text.DRB: And the grate thereof in manner of a net he made of brass, and under it in the midst of the altar an hearth,

text.NRSV: He cast four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating to hold the poles;
text.Rotherham: And he cast four rings in the four corners to the grating of bronze,—as receptacles for the staves,
text.KJV: And he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, to be places for the staves.
text.NABRE: Four rings were cast for the four corners of the bronze grating, as holders for the poles,
text.DRB: casting four rings at as many tops of the net, to put in bars to carry it:

text.NRSV: he made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with bronze.
text.Rotherham: And he made the staves of acacia wood,—and overlaid them with bronze.
text.KJV: And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with brass.
text.NABRE: which were made of acacia wood and plated with bronze.
text.DRB: the which themselves also he made of the wood setim, and covered them with plates of brass:

text.NRSV: And he put the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar, to carry it with them; he made it hollow, with boards.
text.Rotherham: And he brought the staves into the rings, upon the sides of the altar, to lift it therewith,—hollow with boards, did he make it.
text.KJV: And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar, to bear it withal; he made the altar hollow with boards.
text.NABRE: The poles were put through the rings on the sides of the altar for carrying it. The altar was made in the form of a hollow box.
text.DRB: and he drew them through the rings, that stood out in the sides of the altar. And the altar itself was not massy, but hollow of boards, and within empty.

text.NRSV: He made the basin of bronze with its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And he made the laver of bronze, and the stand thereof of bronze,—with the mirrors of the female hosts who did service at the opening of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And he made the laver of brass, and the foot of it of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: The bronze basin, with its bronze stand, was made from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: He made also the laver of brass, with the foot thereof, of women's glasses, that watched in the door of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: He made the court; for the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits long;
text.Rotherham: And he made the court,—on the south side southward, the hangings of the court of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits;
text.KJV: And he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, an hundred cubits:
text.NABRE: The Court of the Tabernacle. The court was made as follows. On the south side the hangings of the court were of fine linen twined, a hundred cubits long,
text.DRB: He made also the court, in the south side whereof were hangings of twisted silk, of an hundred cubits,

text.NRSV: its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: their pillars, twenty, and their sockets twenty, of bronze,—the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver:
text.KJV: Their pillars were twenty, and their brasen sockets twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
text.NABRE: with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze, the hooks and bands of the columns being of silver.
text.DRB: twenty pillars of brass with their feet, the heads of the pillars, and the whole graving of the work, of silver.

text.NRSV: For the north side there were hangings one hundred cubits long; its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: and, on the north side, a hundred cubits, their pillars, twenty, and, their sockets twenty, of bronze,—the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.KJV: And for the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits, their pillars were twenty, and their sockets of brass twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
text.NABRE: On the north side there were similar hangings, a hundred cubits long, with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze; the hooks and bands of the columns were of silver.
text.DRB: In like manner at the north side the hangings, pillars, and feet & the heads of the pillars were of the same measure, and work & metal.

text.NRSV: For the west side there were hangings fifty cubits long, with ten pillars and ten bases; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
text.Rotherham: And on the west side, hangings, fifty cubits, their pillars, ten, and, their sockets, ten,—and, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.KJV: And for the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
text.NABRE: On the west side there were hangings, fifty cubits long, with ten columns and ten pedestals; the hooks and bands of the columns were of silver.
text.DRB: But on that side that looketh to the west, there were hangings of fifty cubits, ten brazen pillars with their feet, and the heads of the pillars, and all the graving of the work, of silver.

text.NRSV: And for the front to the east, fifty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And on the east side towards the dawn, fifty cubits:
text.KJV: And for the east side eastward fifty cubits.
text.NABRE: On the east side the court was fifty cubits.
text.DRB: Moreover against the east he prepared hangings of fifty cubits:

text.NRSV: The hangings for one side of the gate were fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: hangings, fifteen cubits, for the side-piece,—their pillars, three, and, their sockets three;
text.KJV: The hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
text.NABRE: On one side there were hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals;
text.DRB: of the which, one side contained fifteen cubits of three pillars, with their feet:

text.NRSV: And so for the other side; on each side of the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
text.Rotherham: and, for the second sidepiece on this side and on that of the gate of the court, hangings, fifteen cubits,—their pillars three, and their sockets, three.
text.KJV: And for the other side of the court gate, on this hand and that hand, were hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
text.NABRE: on the other side, beyond the gate of the court, there were likewise hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals.
text.DRB: & on the other side (because between both he made the entry of the tabernacle) there were hangings equally of fifteen cubits, & three pillars, & feet as many.

text.NRSV: All the hangings around the court were of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen.
text.KJV: All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen.
text.NABRE: The hangings on all sides of the court were woven of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: All the hangings of the court were woven of twisted silk.

text.NRSV: The bases for the pillars were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver; the overlaying of their capitals was also of silver, and all the pillars of the court were banded with silver.
text.Rotherham: And, the sockets for the pillars, were of bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver,—and the overlaying of their capitals, was silver,—and, they themselves, were filleted with silver, even all the pillars of the court,
text.KJV: And the sockets for the pillars were of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.
text.NABRE: The pedestals of the columns were of bronze, while the hooks and bands of the columns were of silver; the capitals were silver-plated, and all the columns of the court were banded with silver.
text.DRB: The feet of the pillars were of brass, & their heads with all their gravings of silver: but the pillars also of the court themselves he plated with silver.

text.NRSV: The screen for the entrance to the court was embroidered with needlework in blue, purple, and crimson yarns and fine twisted linen. It was twenty cubits long and, along the width of it, five cubits high, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
text.Rotherham: And, the screen of the gate of the court was the work of an embroiderer, blue and purple and crimson, and fine-twined linen,—and, twenty cubits, in length, and the height, according to the breadth, of five cubits, to match the curtains of the court;
text.KJV: And the hanging for the gate of the court was needlework, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: and twenty cubits was the length, and the height in the breadth was five cubits, answerable to the hangings of the court.
text.NABRE: At the gate of the court there was a variegated curtain, woven of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined, twenty cubits long and five cubits wide, in keeping with the hangings of the court.
text.DRB: And in the entry thereof he made with embroidered work a hanging of hyacinth, purple, scarlet, and twisted silk, that had twenty cubits in length, but the height was five cubits, according to the measure which all the hangings of the court had.

text.NRSV: There were four pillars; their four bases were of bronze, their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their bands of silver.
text.Rotherham: and, their pillars, were four, and, their sockets, four, of bronze,—their hooks, of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their connecting-rods, of silver.
text.KJV: And their pillars were four, and their sockets of brass four; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their chapiters and their fillets of silver.
text.NABRE: There were four columns and four pedestals of bronze for it, while their hooks were of silver, and their capitals and their bands silver-plated.
text.DRB: And the pillars in the entry were four with feet of brass, and their heads and gravings of silver.

text.NRSV: All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court all around were of bronze.
text.Rotherham: And, all the pins for the habitation, and for the court round about, of bronze.
text.KJV: And all the pins of the tabernacle, and of the court round about, were of brass.
text.NABRE: All the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the court around it were of bronze.
text.DRB: The pins also of the tabernacle and of the court round about he made of brass.

text.NRSV: These are the records of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the covenant, which were drawn up at the commandment of Moses, the work of the Levites being under the direction of Ithamar son of the priest Aaron.
text.Rotherham: These, are the details of the habitation—the habitation of testimony, which were detailed at the bidding of Moses,—the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
text.KJV: This is the sum of the tabernacle, even of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son to Aaron the priest.
text.NABRE: Amount of Metal Used. The following is an account of the various amounts used on the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the covenant, drawn up at the command of Moses by the Levites under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
text.DRB: These are the instruments of the tabernacle of testimony, which were numbered according to the precept of Moyses, in the ceremonies of the Levites by the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the Priest:

text.NRSV: Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the Lord commanded Moses;
text.Rotherham: So then Bezaleel son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that Yahweh had commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: However, it was Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, who made all that the Lord commanded Moses,
text.DRB: which Beseleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Juda had accomplished, as our Lord commanded by Moyses,

text.NRSV: and with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen.
text.Rotherham: And, with him, was Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an artificer and skilful weaver,—and an embroiderer—in blue, and in purple and in crimson and in fine linen.
text.KJV: And with him was Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen.
text.NABRE: and he was assisted by Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, who was an engraver, an embroiderer, and a weaver of variegated cloth of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen.
text.DRB: having joined to himself for his companion Ooliab the son of Achisamech of the tribe of Dan: who was himself also an egregious artificer in wood, and a tapester, and embroiderer of hyacinth, purple, scarlet, and silk.

text.NRSV: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, the gold from the offering, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred thirty shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel.
text.Rotherham: As for all the gold that was used for, the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, it came to pass that the gold of the wave-offering was nine-and-twenty talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary.
text.KJV: All the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place, even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: All the gold used in the entire construction of the sanctuary, having previously been given as an offering, amounted to twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel.
text.DRB: All the gold that was spent in the work of the Sanctuary, and that was offered in donaries, was nine and twenty talents, and seven hundred thirty sicles according to the measure of the Sanctuary.

text.NRSV: The silver from those of the congregation who were counted was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel;
text.Rotherham: And, the silver of them who were numbered of the assembly, was one hundred talents,—and one thousand, seven hundred and seventy-five shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary;
text.KJV: And the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:
text.NABRE: The silver of those of the community who were enrolled was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel;
text.DRB: And it was offered of them that passed to the number, from twenty years and upward, of six hundred three thousand, & five hundred fifty able men to bear arms.

text.NRSV: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, measured by the sanctuary shekel), for everyone who was counted in the census, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand, five hundred fifty men.
text.Rotherham: a bekah per head, that is a half shekel, by the shekel of the sanctuary, for all that passed over to them who had been numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, for six hundred and three thousand, and five hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: A bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men.
text.NABRE: one bekah apiece, that is, a half-shekel, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel, was received from everyone who was enrolled, of twenty years or more, namely, six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men.
text.DRB: There were moreover an hundred talents of silver, whereof were cast the feet of the Sanctuary, and of the entry where the veil hangeth.

text.NRSV: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary, and the bases of the curtain; one hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent for a base.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the hundred talents of silver were used for casting the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the veil,—one hundred sockets with the hundred talents a talent for a socket.
text.KJV: And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
text.NABRE: One hundred talents of silver were used for casting the pedestals of the sanctuary and the pedestals of the veil, one talent for each pedestal, or one hundred talents for the one hundred pedestals.
text.DRB: An hundred feet were made of an hundred talents, one talent being accounted for every foot.

text.NRSV: Of the thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their capitals and made bands for them.
text.Rotherham: And with the thousand, seven hundred and seventy-five, made the hooks for the pillars,—and overlaid their capitals and united them with connecting-rods.
text.KJV: And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them.
text.NABRE: The remaining one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels were used for making the hooks on the columns, for plating the capitals, and for banding them with silver.
text.DRB: And of the thousand seven hundred, and seventy five he made the heads of the pillars, which themselves he also plated with silver.

text.NRSV: The bronze that was contributed was seventy talents, and two thousand four hundred shekels;
text.Rotherham: And the bronze of the wave-offering, was seventy talents,—and two thousand and four hundred shekels.
text.KJV: And the brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels.
text.NABRE: The bronze, given as an offering, amounted to seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.
text.DRB: Of brass also there were offered seventy two thousand talents, and four hundred sicles besides,

text.NRSV: with it he made the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar and the bronze grating for it and all the utensils of the altar,
text.Rotherham: And he made therewith, the sockets for the opening of the tent of meeting, and the altar of bronze and the grating of bronze which pertained thereto,—and all the utensils of the altar,
text.KJV: And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar,
text.NABRE: With this were made the pedestals at the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar with its bronze gratings, and all the utensils of the altar,
text.DRB: of the which were cast the feet in the entry of the tabernacle of testimony, and the altar of brass with the grate thereof, and all the vessels, that pertain to the use thereof,

text.NRSV: the bases all around the court, and the bases of the gate of the court, all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs around the court.
text.Rotherham: and the sockets for the court round about, and the sockets for the gate of the court,—and all the pins for the habitation and all the pins for the court round about.
text.KJV: And the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about.
text.NABRE: the pedestals around the court, the pedestals at the gate of the court, and all the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the court around it.
text.DRB: and the feet of the court as well in the circuit as in the entry thereof, and the pins of the tabernacle and of the court round about.

text.NRSV: Of the blue, purple, and crimson yarns they made finely worked vestments, for ministering in the holy place; they made the sacred vestments for Aaron; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And of the blue and the purple, and the crimson, made they cloths of variegated stuff for ministering in the sanctuary,—and they made the holy garments which were for Aaron,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And of the blue, and purple, and scarlet, they made cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, and made the holy garments for Aaron; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: With violet, purple, and scarlet yarn were woven the service cloths for use in the sanctuary, as well as the sacred vestments for Aaron, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: Moreover of hyacinth and purple, scarlet and silk he made the vestures, that Aaron should wear when he ministered in the holy places, as our Lord commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: He made the ephod of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: So he made the ephod,—of gold, blue, and purple, and crimson, and fine twined linen,
text.KJV: And he made the ephod of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
text.NABRE: The ephod was woven of gold thread and of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: He made therefore an Ephod of gold, hyacinth, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and twisted silk,

text.NRSV: Gold leaf was hammered out and cut into threads to work into the blue, purple, and crimson yarns and into the fine twisted linen, in skilled design.
text.Rotherham: And they did beat out the plates of gold and he proceeded to cut them into threads, to work into the midst of the blue and into the midst of the purple and into the midst of the crimson, and into the midst of the fine linen,—with the work of a skilful weaver.
text.KJV: And they did beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it into wires, to work it in the blue, and in the purple, and in the scarlet, and in the fine linen, with cunning work.
text.NABRE: Gold was first hammered into gold leaf and then cut up into threads, which were woven with the violet, purple, and scarlet yarn into an embroidered pattern on the fine linen.
text.DRB: with embroidered work, and he did cut thin plates of gold, and drew them small into threads, that they might be twisted with the woof of the former colours,

text.NRSV: They made for the ephod shoulder-pieces, joined to it at its two edges.
text.Rotherham: Shoulder-pieces, made they thereto, for joining,—at its two edges, was it joined.
text.KJV: They made shoulderpieces for it, to couple it together: by the two edges was it coupled together.
text.NABRE: Shoulder straps were made for it and joined to its two upper ends.
text.DRB: and two edges coupled one to the other in the top on either side,

text.NRSV: The decorated band on it was of the same materials and workmanship, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And as for the curious fastening girdle which was thereon, of the same, it was according to the working thereof, gold blue, and purple, and crimson and fine twined linen,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And the curious girdle of his ephod, that was upon it, was of the same, according to the work thereof; of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: The embroidered belt on the ephod extended out from it, and like it, was made of gold thread, of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: and a bawdrike of the same colours, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: The onyx stones were prepared, enclosed in settings of gold filigree and engraved like the engravings of a signet, according to the names of the sons of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And they wrought the onyx stones, enclosed in ouches of gold,—graven with the engravings of a seal-ring, after the names of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And they wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold, graven, as signets are graven, with the names of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: The onyx stones were prepared and mounted in gold filigree work; they were engraved like seal engravings with the names of the sons of Israel.
text.DRB: He prepared also two Onyx stones, fast set and closed in gold, & graven by the art of a lapidary, with the names of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: He set them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, to be stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And he put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod, as stones of memorial for the sons of Israel,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he put them on the shoulders of the ephod, that they should be stones for a memorial to the children of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: These stones were set on the shoulder straps of the ephod as memorial stones of the sons of Israel, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: & he set them in the sides of the Ephod for a monument of the children of Israel, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: He made the breastpiece, in skilled work, like the work of the ephod, of gold, of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And he made the breastpiece the work of a skilful weaver like the work of the ephod,—of gold blue and purple and crimson, and fine twined linen.
text.KJV: And he made the breastplate of cunning work, like the work of the ephod; of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
text.NABRE: The breastpiece was embroidered like the ephod, with gold thread and violet, purple, and scarlet yarn on cloth of fine linen twined.
text.DRB: He made also a Rationale with embroidered work according to the work of the Ephod, of gold, hyacinth, purple, and scarlet twice dyed, & twisted silk:

text.NRSV: It was square; the breastpiece was made double, a span in length and a span in width when doubled.
text.Rotherham: Foursquare, it was doubled, made they the breastpiece,—a span, was the length thereof, and, a span, the breadth thereof, doubled.
text.KJV: It was foursquare; they made the breastplate double: a span was the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof, being doubled.
text.NABRE: It was square and folded double, a span high and a span wide in its folded form.
text.DRB: four square, double, of the measure of a palm.

text.NRSV: They set in it four rows of stones. A row of carnelian, chrysolite, and emerald was the first row;
text.Rotherham: And they set therein four rows of stones,—one row a sardius, a topaz and an emerald, the first row;
text.KJV: And they set in it four rows of stones: the first row was a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: this was the first row.
text.NABRE: Four rows of precious stones were mounted on it: in the first row a carnelian, a topaz, and an emerald;
text.DRB: And he set four rews of precious stones. In the first rew was sardius, topazius, an emerald.

text.NRSV: and the second row, a turquoise, a sapphire, and a moonstone;
text.Rotherham: and the second row,—a carbuncle a sapphire, and a diamond;
text.KJV: And the second row, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.
text.NABRE: in the second row, a garnet, a sapphire, and a beryl;
text.DRB: In the second, a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a jasper.

text.NRSV: and the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.Rotherham: and the third row,—an opal an agate and an amethyst;
text.KJV: And the third row, a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.
text.NABRE: in the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
text.DRB: In the third, a ligurius, an achates, and an amethyst.

text.NRSV: and the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper; they were enclosed in settings of gold filigree.
text.Rotherham: and the fourth row, a Tarshish stone, a sardonyx, and a jasper,—enclosed in ouches of gold, when they were set
text.KJV: And the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper: they were inclosed in ouches of gold in their inclosings.
text.NABRE: in the fourth row a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper. They were mounted in gold filigree work.
text.DRB: In the fourth a chrysolith, an onyx, and berillus, compassed & enclosed in gold by their rews.

text.NRSV: There were twelve stones with names corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they were like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.
text.Rotherham: And as for the stones, after the names of the sons of Israel, they were twelve, after their names,—with the engravings of a seal-ring, each one, after his name, for the twelve tribes,
text.KJV: And the stones were according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, like the engravings of a signet, every one with his name, according to the twelve tribes.
text.NABRE: These stones were twelve, to match the names of the sons of Israel, and each stone was engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes.
text.DRB: And the twelve stones themselves were graven with the names of the twelve tribes of Israel, every one with his several name.

text.NRSV: They made on the breastpiece chains of pure gold, twisted like cords;
text.Rotherham: And they made, upon the breastpiece, chains like cords of wreathen work,—of pure gold.
text.KJV: And they made upon the breastplate chains at the ends, of wreathen work of pure gold.
text.NABRE: Chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, were made for the breastpiece,
text.DRB: They made also in the rationale little chains linked one to another of the purest gold,

text.NRSV: and they made two settings of gold filigree and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two edges of the breastpiece;
text.Rotherham: And they made two ouches of gold, and two rings of gold,—and they placed the two rings upon the two ends of the breastpiece;
text.KJV: And they made two ouches of gold, and two gold rings; and put the two rings in the two ends of the breastplate.
text.NABRE: together with two gold filigree rosettes and two gold rings. The two rings were fastened to the two upper ends of the breastpiece.
text.DRB: and two hooks, and as many rings of gold. Moreover the rings they set on either side of the Rationale,

text.NRSV: and they put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.
text.Rotherham: and they placed the two wreathen chains of gold upon the two rings,-upon the ends of the breastpiece;
text.KJV: And they put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate.
text.NABRE: The two gold chains were then fastened to the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece.
text.DRB: on the which the two golden chains should hang, which they put into the hooks, that stood out in the corners of the Ephod.

text.NRSV: Two ends of the two cords they had attached to the two settings of filigree; in this way they attached it in front to the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: and the other two ends of the two wreathen chains, placed they upon the two ouches,—and placed them upon the shoulder-pieces of the ephod in the forefront thereof.
text.KJV: And the two ends of the two wreathen chains they fastened in the two ouches, and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod, before it.
text.NABRE: The other two ends of the two chains were fastened in front to the two filigree rosettes, which were attached to the shoulder straps of the ephod.
text.DRB: These both before and behind did so agree with themselves, that the Ephod and the Rationale might be knit one to the other,

text.NRSV: Then they made two rings of gold, and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on its inside edge next to the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And they made two rings of gold, and put on the two ends of the breastpiece, upon the border thereof, which is towards the ephod, inwards.
text.KJV: And they made two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastplate, upon the border of it, which was on the side of the ephod inward.
text.NABRE: Two other gold rings were made and put on the two lower ends of the breastpiece, on the edge facing the ephod.
text.DRB: tied to the bawdrike & with rings strongly coupled, which a lace of hyacinth joined, lest they should flag loosely, and be moved one from the other, as our Lord commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: They made two rings of gold, and attached them in front to the lower part of the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod, at its joining above the decorated band of the ephod.
text.Rotherham: And they made two other rings of gold, and placed them upon the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod below on the front of the face thereof, to match the joining thereof.—above the curious girdle of the ephod,
text.KJV: And they made two other golden rings, and put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart of it, over against the other coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.
text.NABRE: Two more gold rings were made and fastened to the bottom of the two shoulder straps next to where they joined the ephod in front, just above its embroidered belt.
text.DRB: They made also the tunic of the Ephod all of hyacinth,

text.NRSV: They bound the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord, so that it should lie on the decorated band of the ephod, and that the breastpiece should not come loose from the ephod; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they bound the breastpiece by the rings thereof into the rings of the ephod, with a cord of blue that it might remain upon the curious girdle of the ephod, and the breastpiece not be removed from upon the ephod,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And they did bind the breastplate by his rings unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it might be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate might not be loosed from the ephod; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Violet ribbons bound the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod, so that the breastpiece stayed right above the embroidered belt of the ephod and did not swing loose from it. All this was just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: and a hole for the head in the upper part against the midst, and the border of the hole round about woven:

text.NRSV: He also made the robe of the ephod woven all of blue yarn;
text.Rotherham: And he made the robe of the ephod, the work of a weaver, wholly of blue;
text.KJV: And he made the robe of the ephod of woven work, all of blue.
text.NABRE: Other Vestments. The robe of the ephod was woven entirely of violet yarn,
text.DRB: and beneath at the feet pomegranates of hyacinth, purple, scarlet, and twisted silk:

text.NRSV: and the opening of the robe in the middle of it was like the opening in a coat of mail, with a binding around the opening, so that it might not be torn.
text.Rotherham: with the opening of the robe in the midst thereof, like the opening of a coat of mail,—a border to the opening thereof round about it might not be rent open.
text.KJV: And there was an hole in the midst of the robe, as the hole of an habergeon, with a band round about the hole, that it should not rend.
text.NABRE: with an opening in its center like the opening of a shirt, with selvage around the opening to keep it from being torn.
text.DRB: and little bells of the purest gold, which they did put between the pomegranates in the utmost part of the tunic round about:

text.NRSV: On the lower hem of the robe they made pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and of fine twisted linen.
text.Rotherham: And they made upon the skirts of the robe, pomegranates—of blue and purple and crimson,—twined.
text.KJV: And they made upon the hems of the robe pomegranates of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined linen.
text.NABRE: At the hem of the robe pomegranates were made of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined;
text.DRB: to wit, a bell of gold, & a pomegranate, wherewith the high-Priest went adorned, when he executed his ministry, as our Lord had commanded Moyses:

text.NRSV: They also made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates on the lower hem of the robe all around, between the pomegranates;
text.Rotherham: And they made bells of pure gold, and placed the bells in the midst of the pomegranates upon the skirts of the robe, roundabout, in the midst of the pomegranates:
text.KJV: And they made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates upon the hem of the robe, round about between the pomegranates;
text.NABRE: bells of pure gold were also made and put between the pomegranates all around the hem of the robe:
text.DRB: They made also silken tunics with woven work for Aaron and his sons:

text.NRSV: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate all around on the lower hem of the robe for ministering; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, upon the skirts of the robe, round about, to minister in—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: A bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, round about the hem of the robe to minister in; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: a bell, a pomegranate, a bell, a pomegranate, all around the hem of the robe which was to be worn in performing the ministry—all this, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: and mitres with their little crowns of silk:

text.NRSV: They also made the tunics, woven of fine linen, for Aaron and his sons,
text.Rotherham: And they made the tunics of fine linen the work of a weaver, for Aaron and for his sons:
text.KJV: And they made coats of fine linen of woven work for Aaron, and for his sons,
text.NABRE: For Aaron and his sons there were also woven tunics of fine linen;
text.DRB: Linen breeches also, of fine linen:

text.NRSV: and the turban of fine linen, and the headdresses of fine linen, and the linen undergarments of fine twisted linen,
text.Rotherham: and the turban of fine linen, and the ornamental caps of fine linen,—and the linen drawers, of fine twined linen:
text.KJV: And a mitre of fine linen, and goodly bonnets of fine linen, and linen breeches of fine twined linen,
text.NABRE: the turban of fine linen; the ornate skullcaps of fine linen; linen pants of fine linen twined;
text.DRB: & a girdle of twisted silk, hyacinth, purple, & scarlet twice dyed, with the art of embroidering, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: and the sash of fine twisted linen, and of blue, purple, and crimson yarns, embroidered with needlework; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and the girdle of fine twined linen, and blue and purple and crimson the work of an embroiderer,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And a girdle of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, of needlework; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: and sashes of variegated work made of fine linen twined and of violet, purple, and scarlet yarn, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: They made also the plate of sacred veneration of most pure gold, & they wrote in it with the work of a lapidary: The Holy of our Lord:

text.NRSV: They made the rosette of the holy diadem of pure gold, and wrote on it an inscription, like the engraving of a signet, “Holy to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And they made the burnished plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote thereupon a writing, with the engravings of a seal-ring Holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote upon it a writing, like to the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.
text.NABRE: The plate of the sacred diadem was made of pure gold and inscribed, as on a seal engraving: “Sacred to the Lord.”
text.DRB: and they tied it to the mitre with a lace of hyacinth, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: They tied to it a blue cord, to fasten it on the turban above; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they placed there, upon a cord of blue, to fasten it upon the turban up above, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And they tied unto it a lace of blue, to fasten it on high upon the mitre; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: It was tied over the turban with a violet ribbon, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: Therefore all the work of the tabernacle & of the roof of testimony was perfected: & the children of Israel did all things which our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: In this way all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished; the Israelites had done everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus was finished all the work of the habitation of the tent of meeting,—thus did the sons of Israel, according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they.
text.KJV: Thus was all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation finished: and the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses, so did they.
text.NABRE: Presentation of the Work to Moses. Thus the entire work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was completed. The Israelites did the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses; so it was done.
text.DRB: And they offered the tabernacle & the roof & the whole furniture, rings, boards, bars, pillars and their feet,

text.NRSV: Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent and all its utensils, its hooks, its frames, its bars, its pillars, and its bases;
text.Rotherham: And they brought in the habitation unto Moses, the tent, and all its furniture,—its clasps, its boards, its bars, and its pillars and its sockets;
text.KJV: And they brought the tabernacle unto Moses, the tent, and all his furniture, his taches, his boards, his bars, and his pillars, and his sockets,
text.NABRE: They then brought to Moses the tabernacle, the tent with all its furnishings, the clasps, the frames, the bars, the columns, the pedestals,
text.DRB: the cover of rams' skins dyed red, and the other cover of janthine skins,

text.NRSV: the covering of tanned rams’ skins and the covering of fine leather, and the curtain for the screen;
text.Rotherham: and the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers’ skins, and the screening veil;
text.KJV: And the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers’ skins, and the vail of the covering,
text.NABRE: the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, the covering of tahash skins, the curtain veil;
text.DRB: the veil, the ark, the bars, the propitiatory,

text.NRSV: the ark of the covenant with its poles and the mercy seat;
text.Rotherham: the ark of the testimony, with its staves,—and the propitiatory;
text.KJV: The ark of the testimony, and the staves thereof, and the mercy seat,
text.NABRE: the ark of the covenant with its poles, the cover,
text.DRB: the table with the vessels thereof and the loaves of proposition:

text.NRSV: the table with all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence;
text.Rotherham: the table all its utensils, and the Presence-bread;
text.KJV: The table, and all the vessels thereof, and the shewbread,
text.NABRE: the table with all its utensils and the showbread,
text.DRB: the candlestick, the lamps, and the furniture of them with the oil:

text.NRSV: the pure lampstand with its lamps set on it and all its utensils, and the oil for the light;
text.Rotherham: the pure lampstand the lamps thereof the lamps to be put in order, and all the utensils thereof,—and the oil for giving light;
text.KJV: The pure candlestick, with the lamps thereof, even with the lamps to be set in order, and all the vessels thereof, and the oil for light,
text.NABRE: the pure gold menorah with its lamps set up on it and with all its utensils, the oil for the light,
text.DRB: the altar of gold, and the ointment, and the incense of spices:

text.NRSV: the golden altar, the anointing oil and the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance of the tent;
text.Rotherham: and the altar of gold, and the oil of anointing, and the fragrant incense,—and the screen for the opening of the tent;
text.KJV: And the golden altar, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the tabernacle door,
text.NABRE: the golden altar, the anointing oil, the fragrant incense; the curtain for the entrance of the tent,
text.DRB: and the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: the bronze altar, and its grating of bronze, its poles, and all its utensils; the basin with its stand;
text.Rotherham: the altar of bronze and the grating of bronze which pertaineth thereto, the staves thereof, and all the utensils thereof,—the laver, and its stand,
text.KJV: The brasen altar, and his grate of brass, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,
text.NABRE: the altar of bronze with its bronze grating, its poles and all its utensils, the basin with its stand,
text.DRB: the altar of brass, the grate, the bars, and all the vessels thereof: the laver with the foot thereof: the hangings of the court, and the pillars with their feet:

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, its pillars, and its bases, and the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, and its pegs; and all the utensils for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: the hangings of the court its pillars and its sockets, and the screen for the gate of the court, its cords and its pins,—and all the utensils for the service of the habitation for the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and his sockets, and the hanging for the court gate, his cords, and his pins, and all the vessels of the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of the congregation,
text.NABRE: the hangings of the court with their columns and pedestals, the curtain for the gate of the court with its ropes and tent pegs, all the equipment for the service of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting;
text.DRB: the hanging in the entry of the court, and the little cords, and the pins thereof. Nothing wanted of the vessels, that was commanded to be made for the ministry of the tabernacle, and for the roof of covenant.

text.NRSV: the finely worked vestments for ministering in the holy place, the sacred vestments for the priest Aaron, and the vestments of his sons to serve as priests.
text.Rotherham: them cloths of variegated stuff, for ministering in the sanctuary,—the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons for ministering as priests.
text.KJV: The cloths of service to do service in the holy place, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and his sons’ garments, to minister in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: the service cloths for use in the sanctuary, the sacred vestments for Aaron the priest, and the vestments to be worn by his sons in their ministry.
text.DRB: The vestments also, which the priests use in the Sanctuary, to wit, Aaron and his sons,

text.NRSV: The Israelites had done all of the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel make all the work.
text.KJV: According to all that the Lord commanded Moses, so the children of Israel made all the work.
text.NABRE: Just as the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites had carried out all the work.
text.DRB: the children of Israel offered, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw that they had done all the work just as the Lord had commanded, he blessed them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses looked upon all the structure and lo! they had made it, as Yahweh had commanded, so, had they made it,—and Moses blessed them.
text.KJV: And Moses did look upon all the work, and, behold, they had done it as the Lord had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.
text.NABRE: So when Moses saw that all the work was done just as the Lord had commanded, he blessed them.
text.DRB: Which things after that Moyses saw all finished, he blessed them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: On the first day of the month, in the first month, shalt thou rear the habitation, of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: On the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation.
text.NABRE: On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: The first month, the first day of the month, thou shalt erect the tabernacle of the testimony,

text.NRSV: You shall put in it the ark of the covenant, and you shall screen the ark with the curtain.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt put therein, the ark of the testimony,—and shalt screen the ark with the veil;
text.KJV: And thou shalt put therein the ark of the testimony, and cover the ark with the vail.
text.NABRE: Put the ark of the covenant in it, and screen off the ark with the veil.
text.DRB: & shalt put in it the ark, and shalt let down before it the veil:

text.NRSV: You shall bring in the table, and arrange its setting; and you shall bring in the lampstand, and set up its lamps.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order what is to be arranged thereupon,—and thou shalt bring in the lampstand, and mount the lamps thereof;
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it; and thou shalt bring in the candlestick, and light the lamps thereof.
text.NABRE: Bring in the table and set it. Then bring in the menorah and set up the lamps on it.
text.DRB: & bringing in the table, thou shalt set upon it the things that are commanded after the rite. The candlestick shall stand with the lamps thereof,

text.NRSV: You shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the covenant, and set up the screen for the entrance of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the altar of gold for incense, before the ark of the testimony,—and put up the screen for the opening of the habitation;
text.KJV: And thou shalt set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Put the golden altar of incense in front of the ark of the covenant, and hang the curtain at the entrance of the tabernacle.
text.DRB: and the altar of gold whereon the incense is burned, before the ark of testimony. Thou shalt put the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the altar for the ascending-sacrifice,—before the opening of the habitation of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: And thou shalt set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Put the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and before it the altar of holocaust:

text.NRSV: and place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt place the laver between the tent of meeting, and the altar, and shalt put therein water,
text.KJV: And thou shalt set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and shalt put water therein.
text.NABRE: Place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it.
text.DRB: the laver between the altar and the tabernacle, which thou shalt fill with water.

text.NRSV: You shall set up the court all around, and hang up the screen for the gate of the court.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put up the court round about, and hang up the screen of the gate of the court.
text.KJV: And thou shalt set up the court round about, and hang up the hanging at the court gate.
text.NABRE: Set up the court round about, and put the curtain at the gate of the court.
text.DRB: And thou shalt compass about the court with hangings, & the entry thereof.

text.NRSV: Then you shall take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and consecrate it and all its furniture, so that it shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take the oil of anointing, and anoint the habitation and all that is therein,—and shalt hallow it. and all the furniture thereof so shall it be holy.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is therein, and shalt hallow it, and all the vessels thereof: and it shall be holy.
text.NABRE: Take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and everything in it, consecrating it and all its furnishings, so that it will be sacred.
text.DRB: And taking the oil of unction thou shalt anoint the tabernacle with the vessels thereof, that they may be sanctified:

text.NRSV: You shall also anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and consecrate the altar, so that the altar shall be most holy.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt anoint the altar of ascending sacrifice, and all its utensils,—and hallow the altar, so shall the altar be most holy:
text.KJV: And thou shalt anoint the altar of the burnt offering, and all his vessels, and sanctify the altar: and it shall be an altar most holy.
text.NABRE: Anoint the altar for burnt offerings and all its utensils, consecrating it, so that it will be most sacred.
text.DRB: the altar of holocaust and all the vessels thereof:

text.NRSV: You shall also anoint the basin with its stand, and consecrate it.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt anoint the laver and its stand,—and shalt hallow it.
text.KJV: And thou shalt anoint the laver and his foot, and sanctify it.
text.NABRE: Likewise, anoint the basin with its stand, and thus consecrate it.
text.DRB: the laver with the foot thereof; all shalt thou consecrate with the oil of unction, that they may be most Holy.

text.NRSV: Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shall wash them with water,
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt bring near Aaron and his sons, unto the opening of the tent of meeting,—and shalt bathe them in the water;
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and wash them with water.
text.NABRE: Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there wash them with water.
text.DRB: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of testimony, and having washed them with water,

text.NRSV: and put on Aaron the sacred vestments, and you shall anoint him and consecrate him, so that he may serve me as priest.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt clothe Aaron with the holy garments,—and shalt anoint him and hallow him so shall he minister as priest unto me.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put upon Aaron the holy garments, and anoint him, and sanctify him; that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: Clothe Aaron with the sacred vestments and anoint him, thus consecrating him as my priest.
text.DRB: thou shalt revest them with the sacred vestments, that they may minister to me, and the unction of them may prosper to an everlasting priesthood.

text.NRSV: You shall bring his sons also and put tunics on them,
text.Rotherham: His sons also, shalt thou bring near,—and shalt clothe them with tunics;
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring his sons, and clothe them with coats:
text.NABRE: Bring forward his sons also, and clothe them with the tunics.
text.DRB: And Moyses did all things which our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: and anoint them, as you anointed their father, that they may serve me as priests: and their anointing shall admit them to a perpetual priesthood throughout all generations to come.
text.Rotherham: and shalt anoint them as thou didst anoint their father, and they shall minister as priests unto me,—so shall their anointing remain to them for an age-abiding priesthood, to their generations.
text.KJV: And thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father, that they may minister unto me in the priest’s office: for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.
text.NABRE: As you have anointed their father, anoint them also as my priests. Thus, by being anointed, shall they receive a perpetual priesthood throughout all future generations.
text.DRB: Therefore the first month of the second year, the first day of the month, the tabernacle was placed.

text.NRSV: Moses did everything just as the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Moses did,—according to all that Yahweh had commanded him, so did he.
text.KJV: Thus did Moses: according to all that the Lord commanded him, so did he.
text.NABRE: Moses did just as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: And Moyses erected it, and put the boards and feet and bars, and reared the pillars,

text.NRSV: In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle was set up.
text.Rotherham: Thus came it to pass, in the first month, in the second year, on the first of the month, that the habitation was reared.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, that the tabernacle was reared up.
text.NABRE: On the first day of the first month of the second year the tabernacle was set up.
text.DRB: and spread the roof over the tabernacle, putting over it a cover, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Moses set up the tabernacle; he laid its bases, and set up its frames, and put in its poles, and raised up its pillars;
text.Rotherham: Yea Moses reared the habitation, and placed its sockets, and fixed its boards, and fastened its bars,—and reared its pillars.
text.KJV: And Moses reared up the tabernacle, and fastened his sockets, and set up the boards thereof, and put in the bars thereof, and reared up his pillars.
text.NABRE: It was Moses who set up the tabernacle. He placed its pedestals, set up its frames, put in its bars, and set up its columns.
text.DRB: He put also the testimony in the ark, thrusting bars underneath, and the oracle above.

text.NRSV: and he spread the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent over it; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Then spread he abroad the tent upon the habitation, and put the covering of the tent upon it, above,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he spread abroad the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent above upon it; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: He spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering on top of the tent, as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: And when he had brought the ark into the tabernacle, he drew before it the veil to fulfill the commandment of our Lord.

text.NRSV: He took the covenant and put it into the ark, and put the poles on the ark, and set the mercy seat above the ark;
text.Rotherham: And he took and placed the testimony within the ark, and put the staves upon the ark,—and placed the propitiatory upon the ark, above;
text.KJV: And he took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the staves on the ark, and put the mercy seat above upon the ark:
text.NABRE: He took the covenant and put it in the ark; he placed poles alongside the ark and set the cover upon it.
text.DRB: He set the table also in the tabernacle of testimony at the north side without the veil,

text.NRSV: and he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the curtain for screening, and screened the ark of the covenant; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and brought in the ark into the habitation, and put up the screening veil, and screened over the ark of the testimony,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the vail of the covering, and covered the ark of the testimony; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: He brought the ark into the tabernacle and hung the curtain veil, thus screening off the ark of the covenant, as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: ordering the bread of proposition before it, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: He put the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the curtain,
text.Rotherham: And he placed the table in the tent of meeting,—on the side of the habitation northward,—outside the veil;
text.KJV: And he put the table in the tent of the congregation, upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the vail.
text.NABRE: He put the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the veil,
text.DRB: He set the candlestick also in the tabernacle of testimony over against the table on the south side,

text.NRSV: and set the bread in order on it before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and set in order thereupon the arrangement of bread before Yahweh,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he set the bread in order upon it before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: and arranged the bread on it before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: placing the lamps in order, according to the precept of our Lord.

text.NRSV: He put the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: And he put the lampstand, in the tent of meeting, over against the table,—on the side of the habitation southward;
text.KJV: And he put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.
text.NABRE: He placed the menorah in the tent of meeting, opposite the table, on the south side of the tabernacle,
text.DRB: He set also the altar of gold under the roof of testimony against the veil,

text.NRSV: and set up the lamps before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and mounted the lamps before Yahweh,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he lighted the lamps before the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: and he set up the lamps before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: and burned upon it the incense of spices, as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the curtain,
